(First Scepter)
|
Requires a Scroll of Forbidden Rights |
Trade a Rusty Dagger to the Mine Keeper from your KD with 999 runes

(you lose the runes in the process). Your SoFR becomes part of the Scepter so it will not show as a separate item in your inventory anymore.

When waging war, you will simultaneously attack the next 2 kingdoms (as opposed to 1) in the chosen direction doing full damage to each and taking half damage from each.

You are granted 1 extra attack/transport turn.

Allows you to assail enemy kingdoms (see /ann for 2/13/2004)

Also gives the ability to use some kingdom commands :

/rep = Repairs damage to your armies as much as possible with your existing gold without any waste.

/fort = Repairs damage to your fortifications as much as possible with your existing gold without any waste.

/bf = Buys as much food as possible with your existing gold without any waste.

/ref = Repairs and buys as much food as possible without any waste.

Also used to get the Scepter of Unending Repulsion.
|

Scepter of Unending Repulsion
(Second Scepter)
|
Requires a Scepter of Harnessed Hatred, (see line above) and a pure DDD or D*D relic. A D*Devoid will not work. |
Trade a pure DDD or D*D relic to the Mine Keeper. You lose the relic and get the upgraded Scepter.

Has all the powers of the Scepter of Harnessed Hatred plus:

When waging war, you will simultaneously attack the next 3 kingdoms (as opposed to 2) in the chosen direction doing full damage to each and taking half damage from each.

Allows you to assail and besiege enemy kingdoms (see /ann for 2/13/2004)

You are granted 1 more extra attack/transport turn (for a total of 2 extra attacks from having the Scepters)

You gain the /alch ability which converts runes back into gold (1 rune to 1 bil gold).

When you are defending you do 50% more damage to your attacker(s).
|

Harbingers Scepter of Oppression
(Third Scepter)
|
Requires a Scepter of Unending Repulsion (see line above) and 2 Tears of the Fallen |
You need to have 2 pure Tears Of The Fallen , trade one of them to the Mine Keeper. He converts both ToTF and your Scepter of Unending Repulsion (see above) into a Harbingers Scepter of Oppression.

Has all the powers of Scepter of Unending Repulsion plus is the equivalent of having 2 Tears of the Fallen equipped, without taking up relic slots.

You are granted an extra attack/transport turn (for a total of 3 extra attacks from having the Scepters)

Also gives you more commands , the double alchemize ability, which grants you the ability to alchemize from distant kingdoms, 2 runes at a time (/da xxx,plane,xxx) or if you also have a DaD and DsD you can alch 1 rune at a time (/sa xxx,plane,xxx)
|

Harbingers Scepter of Delegation
(Fourth Scepter)
|
Requires a Harbingers Scepter of Oppression, See line above,
drops off a Polymorphic Beetle |
Kill a Polymorphic Beetle after you get a Harbingers Scepter of Oppression (see line above).

Allows you to use the Public Embezzle command, so other players can help you embezzle your kingdoms

Note : You will need access to at least one plane (off Surface) to reach a Polymorphic Beetle (PB) or someone with permanent Templar , Aqualungs , and Hell access to summon a private one for you.

The random spawns do not appear on Surface but all private beasts do. Remember, a private beast might appear in a water kingdom so having Aqualungs is advised if you want to fight them.
|

Captains Staff of Valor
(CSoV)
|
Sail the ports and it will eventually drop randomly.

It may take many trips to get it. See note on right |
The Staff doubles the defensive effectiveness of Runes. This quest also gives attackers the ability to enrage the armies of their kingdoms resulting in double the war damage. It costs 10 runes to /nrage a kingdoms armies which will last 1 hour.

Also grants the /chance ability. If you have an EEoT you can type /chance and the kingdom where you are located gets 10 runes. However, there is a 20% chance of losing the EEoT in the process. The odds balance out so over time you would expect to break even, no net gain or loss.

Note: Crazy Gizmo (S2) pointed out you can enter a port and keep sailing TO that same one. It costs 0 gold and you will eventually find the staff.

I tried this with a mule and got it in 5 voyages but tried again more recently (August 2018) and the staff still did not drop after 30 "voyages".

Wizardofhatred (S1) has suggested its quicker and cheaper to just keep teleporting to the same port. Its faster than sailing, costs no gold, and he managed to get his staff that way in 4 or 5 trips. I tried it with two mules and didn't get the drop after 200 trips, so I can't verify that it works but Darklordbladeflame on S1 said he also got one by teleporting, so perhaps I was just unlucky.
|

Free Fortifier Relic
(Freefort)
|
Drops off a Goblin King BEAST on Surface |
The King has only 410 dur, but each attack can only do 1 damage per attack type, i.e. if you cast twice and also do some relic damage your total damage in that attack is only 3 points.

Allows a player to use the /freefort command and add 250 million to all fortifications at no cost. It requires you have a pure Free Fortify relic in your inventory, which has a small and very random chance of breaking with every use. Killing a King is a matter of luck.

The Goblin King which drops freeforts is a Beast, not the normal Goblin King you can create using the New Fight menu. Gob King beasts are random Sur spawns.
|

Quest Items for Waging War

Essence Element of Tecton
(Tec)
|
Pm the Quake Keeper (cost is 999 runes) from any location |
Stand in YOUR KD containing 999 runes and trade the Quake Keeper a rusty dagger (use /add Quake Keeper to get him on your trade list). You lose the runes, and get the Tecton.

To use the tecton, move to the kingdom where you want to do severe damage (not your own kingdom) and while there trade the tecton to the Quake Keeper.

The location where you are at that time will suffer immediate damage, and for the rest of the current game hour (type /t to see the time) your armys attacks in any kingdom will do 25x more damage than normal (aftershock).

Dropping a Tecton also gives you a chance to find a sleeved poker card, which can be used to help with dueling. You can get a card even if you drop the Tecton on an NPC kingdom, like the Pub or one of the Keeps, where you run no risk of an irate player retaliating on your kingdoms.

Reduces cannons, walls, and moats to rubble. Does massive random damage to catapults, soldiers, archers, trebuchets, food, population, and runes.

Morale is not damaged by Quakes.

While under the influence of a Tecton you cannot accept kingdoms from another player or give them to anyone other then "NOBODY", which makes them available for settling (thanks to Ramza, Orden and Red193193 for that tip).

Alundra Mischef on S2 shared an interesting tip. The hourly actions limit (6,000/hr) is reset by dropping a Tecton so it is a way to perform more actions in an hour. A little extreme, but interesting game behavior.
|

Essence Element of Greater Tecton

Thanks to Obsidian Night on S1 for clarifying the details for this item.

(The July 24, 2022 /ann has the first announcement about this essence.)

Cannot be used in the Forge plane
|
The cost is 10 "regular" Tectons

(so equivalent to 10 trillion gold) |
Create 10 Tectons with runes in your own kingdoms (use /add Quake Keeper to get him on your trade list) and then trade 1 Tecton to the Quake Keeper. You will lose the 10 Tectons and get 1 Greater Tecton in return ("You find a Essence Element of Greater Tecton"). You also get a pm "Quake Keeper: Simply hand it back when standing in the plane of your choice to activate" .

To use the Greater Tecton, move to the plane where you want to cause damage and trade it to the Quake Keeper. It will then damage some random location on that plane, removing the king, Trs, runes, pop and armies and lowering morale to 0%. The food and forts are not affected. You will be automatically teleported to that location and can settle it. The (now former) king will get a pm alert about the attack "Quake Keeper: Your kingdom at x,y,z was sucked into the void" (courtesy of
Messier on S1) and a system-wide announcement (yellow text) will show "Someone causes a random kingdom to be consumed by the void" . The specific location will not be announced.

When using a Greater Tecton there is very small chance you might find a DD relic . There is a system message when this happens "Someone found a powerful relic in the cracked earth!!!" (thanks to Logi on S2 for that information). Also, Grunkin (S1) pointed out that Greater Tectons do trigger aftershock for the remaining hour like a regular Tecton (see /ann for 7/24/2022).

|

Essence Element of Stone
(Stone)
|
Pm the Quake Keeper (cost is 999 runes) from any location |
Stand in YOUR KD containing 999 runes and trade the Quake Keeper an old knife (use /add Quake Keeper to get him on your trade list). You lose the runes, and get the Stone.

To use the stone trade it to the Quake Keeper and all your kingdoms will gain extra protection against attack for the rest of the current game hour (type /t to see the time). I'm not certain whether you need to be in one of your own kingdoms when trading the stone or if any location will still key it to your protection, so be in your own kingdom at the time to be sure.

Vastly increases the defense of all your Kingdoms for the remainder of the game hour. The Stone is the defensive answer to a Tecton

While under the influence of a Stone you cannot accept kingdoms from another player or give them to anyone other then "NOBODY", which makes them available for settling (thanks to Ramza, Orden and Red193193 for that tip).
|

STAT, DEFENSIVE AND DAMAGE BOOSTING QUESTS (you might also think of these as dueling power quests)

Totem of Second Chances

Sailor’s Crimp

(requires Mountain Tamer or Transcendental Sanction) |
Specific Sky cloud (130,Sky,140) |
Take the Crimp to any port on Surface, it Transforms into a Totem of Second Chances

Note : You will need a Mountain Tamer or Transcendental Sanction to enter Sky

Despite what is on /help , Forsaken Dream (S2) mentioned finding one at 85,Sky,84 and Xxzeroxx (S2) at 66,Sky,66 so several locations seem to work. Perhaps anywhere in Sky will do the trick.
|

Totem of Second Chances
(Totem)
|
Requires a Sailors Crimp, See line above |
Makes it impossible to be fatally wounded by relics when attacking |

Tears of the Fallen (ToTF) |
Trade the Pub Keeper a Caster's Revenge , Melee's Rage , or Vampire's Scorn super relic. |
You lose the super relic when you trade it to the Pub Keeper. ToTF gives you a 50% chance of being saved from death for first ToTF equipped, 10% more for each one equipped thereafter. |

Detangler |
Kill an Almighty Avatar in Heaven Keep
(66, Hev, 66) |
Halves your chance of being affected by your enemys confusion relics.

Note : You will need a Transcendental Sanction or Omega Sanction to enter Heaven
|

Scepter of Annulment (SoA) |
Either a $50 Purchase or 50k ash from the Ash Keeper (150,Sur,150) |
It is equivalent to having an additional 26 annulment relics equipped, required for anyone seriously thinking of killing non-surface beasts.

You can pm the Ash Keeper from any location for details. |

Attackers Balance |
Random Drop Found Anywhere. |
Prevents your enemy from using relics to get their health above 1,999,999,998 so that you are sure to kill them with two max damage hits unless they have used a heal spell or manage to revive.

Also allows Theurgists to equip a single heal spell.
|

Sash of the Blood Rogue
(Sash)

Caution: some glitch in the game allows players to accidentially collect more than one of these (Thanks to Surzerker for sharing that information). They do not stack so if you get a second it does not function, it takes up space, and like all quest items you can't destroy it. The only way to remove the second Sash is by emailing Glitchless and having Jeff do that for you.
|
Need at least 1 Essence Element of Great Treasure |
Gives some defensive bonuses if you have sleeved cards. You get it by giving an EEoGT to A Citizen in the port of Carella (129.Sur,51), you can chat up A Citizen there to get some details. Success appears to be random, with the chance of getting a Sash only 1 in 20 against you. Some players have used many treasures trying for a Sash. Good luck

3/17/2005 Announcement:

The sash greatly increases the power of the attack bonuses gained from sleeved black cards and also allows you to gain an Ambush ability from any sleeved red card. The Ambush ability when activated gives you a 50 percent chance of ambushing someone who attacks you, causing their attack to be useless (their relics don’t fire and they can’t attack) and still allowing you to perform your counter attack as always. Note that you only leave Ambush mode after it has once successfully fired, guaranteeing some usage out of every sleeved red card. Having the sash also allows your Devastating Blow, Crippling Blow, Mana Rush, and Mana Surge to work on beasts.

Quote A Citizen: Think you’re luckier than me? Well give me an EEoGT and I’ll roll my 20 sided die, if it rolls a 10 you can have my sash.
|

Shadow Scale Quest |
Upgrades for Demonic, Earthen and Crystal items, downgrades for Shadow Scale |
Visit A Citizen at 150,Hev,150. The citizen will upgrade Demonic , Earthen or Crystal items in exchange for ash, or downgrade Shadow Scale for ash and 999 runes. The quest is detailed here . Shadow Scale items can not be traded. |

QUESTS THAT COMBINE SEVERAL PREVIOUS QUEST ITEMS INTO ONE

Transcendental Sanction (access to all planes). Prerequisites – Mountain Tamer and Lava Splicer

Transcendental Sanction
(Trans)
|
Temple (40,Sur,225) |
Once you have both the Tamer and Splicer take them to the Temple. The Transcendental Sanction combines the power of the Lava Splicer and Mountain Tamer into a single far more powerful item that grants you access to all planes EXCEPT The Forge: Hell, Dungeon, Surface, Sky, and Heaven. You also no longer have to access those planes from a specific location. |

Omega Sanction Prerequisites : 1. Transcendental Sanction, 2. Aqua Lungs, 3. Demonic Pinions, 4. White Wings, 5. Totem of Second Chances, 6. Wand of Transportation, 7. Haste of Hell, 8. Wand of Swift Winds, 9. Experience Gravitator

Omega Sanction
(Omega)
|
Temple (40,Sur,225) |
Once you have all 9 required items, take them to the Temple. The Omega Sanction combines all the power of the 9 required quest items into one, freeing 8 storage slots for other items |

Archers Pouch of Secrecy (also helps crafters) Prerequisites : 1. Tradeskill Manifesto, 2. Theurgal Damnation Staff, 3. Detangler, 4. Quickening, 5. Scepter of Annulment, 6. Bounteous Nock

Archers Pouch of Secrecy
(APoS)
|
Kill Yoros anywhere on Sur until it drops |
If you have all of the following items: Tradeskill Manifesto, Theurgal Damnation Staff, Detangler, Quickening, Scepter of Annulment, and Bounteous Nock, then killing a Yoro La HiHi Samurai will give you a chance to have all of the above items morph into the Archers Pouch of Secrecy, which in addition to being the combination to the above items, gives archers a 75% of having 2 attacks, and also doubles the speed of chanting and crafting. |

Sanction of Surpassment Prerequisites : 1. Omega Sanction, 2. Fists of Flames, 3. Templars Wand, 4. Skill Binder, 5. RDADMS Lessonbook, 6. Backpack, 7. Vamp Horns, 8. Coif of Refulgence, 9. Cloak of Debilitation

Sanction of Surpassment
(SoS)
|
Lake in Heaven
(256,Hev,256)
|
Allows players to level to the current level cap and combines the power of the 9 required items into one.When you have all the items just go to 256,Hev,256 and they will automatically convert into an SoS when you enter that kingdom. Without the SoS you will reach a hard level cap of 1,000 and not move up from there. |

Sanction of Serenity
(SoSe) ( Prerequisites : 1. Sanction of Surpassment, 2. Skill Potencifier, 3. Harbingers Scepter of Delegation, 4. Goblin Hide Backpack, 5. Fangs of the Bloodletter, 6. Beast Bane

Sanction of Serenity (SoSe) |
Obtained from the Rune Keeper

Required to access The Forge plane (entrance at 141,Hel,146 then move down) |
If you have the 6 prerequisite items and 50,000 ash, you trade your SoS to the Rune Keeper and he converts them all into an SoSe. Players with a Quickness (/q ability) only need 30k ash.

Combines the power of 6 quest items into one with other major benefits:

- 50% chance of being revived (stacks with ToTF).

- Haste improvement for arrows better than DT is greatly increased.

- Grandmaster Bowmen get an additional 10% mez and void resistance, plus dodge ability that is as effective as equipping two shields but only works against melee attacks.

- Magicians, Master Magicians, Armsmen and Master of Arms have a 75% chance of a double attack with their single spell or weapon in addition to their normal bonuses.

- Bloodletters get the 10x FotB bonus PLUS an additional 10x (100x total) damage to beasts and have a 10% mez and void resistance.

- Theurgal Diviniators get an extra 50% chance of a double cast, a 5x damage bonus, and 10% mez and void resistance.

- Masters of Fists receive an additional 10% mez and void resistance as well as the same dodge ability as Grandmaster Bowmen.

- Required for access to The Forge

|

Tracking Quests You can find a much fuller explanation of these quests on this reddit post.

Goldblum Goo

Completing this quest costs either 5,000+ ash or 999 runes and some minor ash. |
Obtained by killing a Nightmare Fly beast |
After getting the Goo trade it to the Shrine Keeper, from your kingdom with 999 runes if you want to pay that way, and he will give you a clue about which kingdom you must enter and kill any random enemy. Every 5 minutes you can message the Keeper with "track" and for 10 ash you'll get a clue where the target kingdom is relative to your current location. The process can get tedious.

When you eventually find the right kingdom and kill something there you complete the quest and your stats will include a chance to not consume a black sleeved card when you trigger one before fighting. The quest is repeatable and the bonus keeps stacking. You can see it in your /stats output. |

Deformed Wing

Completing this quest costs either 5,000+ ash or 999 runes and some minor ash. |
Obtained by killing a Dragonling Zombie beast |
After getting the Wing trade it to the Shrine Keeper, from your kingdom with 999 runes if you want to pay that way, and he will give you a clue about which kingdom you must enter and kill any random enemy. Every 5 minutes you can message the Keeper with "track" and for 10 ash you'll get a clue where the target kingdom is relative to your current location. The process can get tedious.

When you eventually find the right kingdom and kill something there you complete the quest and your stats will include a chance to not consume a red sleeved card when you trigger one before fighting. The quest is repeatable and the bonus keeps stacking. You can see it in your /stats output. |
|

Master of Class Quests were added on 1 June 2018. These quests require access to the Forge plane is required so it is necessary to first have a Sanction of Serenity to do them.
I have only done the Vampire's Shade quest but I have chatted with the Forge Hermit and thanks to a tip from Tris on S1 I believe the instructions below are correct.

The description for these quests was a little confusing to me but Randall Flagg on S2 helped sort it out. When he tried to get the Fighter's Gambeson while showing as an Armsman, not a Master, the Hermit refused to take his gold or ash and gave the response "Only the best fighters, casters, and vampires need apply". Same when he equipped as a Berserker, but not a Master. After adjusting his stats to show as a Master Berserker or Master At Arms he was able to complete the quest for the Gambeson. He then shifted his stats away from what is needed to be a Master Berserker but with the Gambeson he still showed as one.

So, it seems these items do not help you initially qualify as a Master of class with a stat distribution that is different from normal, but they do allow you to retain that status even after moving some stats away from the initial requirements. I'm quessing that characters with much more accuracy than they need to hit everything will use these quests to shift more points towards damage (or maybe evasion) while keeping their status as Masters.

The Vampire, Fighter and Caster items each cost 25,000 ash.
The Theurgist and Monk Staffs cost 2 DaD or DaDev relics each!

An interesting observation came from Mordred on S1. He found that without getting the Warder or Squire he was able to jump those quests and get a Fighter's Gambeson once he shifted enough stats to show as a Master of Class.

Fighter's Gambeson
Costs 25,000 ash |
Go to 24,For,32 and chat with the Forge Hermit |
1. Equip as a fighter
2. Use /st to confirm the server sees you as a master fighter and not as any other class.
3. Give the hermit 25 gold, he will take 25,000 ash, and you will get a Fighter's Gambeson.

Removes stat requirements to be Master Berserker or Master of Arms(?). Gives Master Berserkers and Master of Arms a 20% chance to negate any kind of vampire's void or devoid resist with a stake through the heart. |

Caster's Robes
Costs 25,000 ash |
Go to 24,For,32 and chat with the Forge Hermit
|
1. Equip as a caster
2. Use /st to confirm the server sees you as a master caster and not as any other class.
3. Give the hermit 25 gold, he will take 25,000 ash, and you will get a Caster's Robes.

Removes stat requirements to be Master Wizard or Master Magician. Gives Master Wizards and Master Magicians a 50% chance to burn their enemy's arrows mid-flight. |

Vampire's Shade
Costs 25,000 ash |
Go to 24,For,32 and chat with the Forge Hermit |
1. Equip as a vampire
2. Use /st to confirm the server sees you as a master vampire and not as any other class.
3. Give the hermit 25 gold, he will take 25,000 ash, and you will get a Vampire's Shade.

Removes stat requirements to be Bloodletter. Gives Bloodletters a 10% chance to resist their enemy's voids and devoids. |

Theurgist's Staff of Domination
Costs two DaDev relics.

Thanks to Eyemredy09 on S2 for clarifying that you can not use plain DaD's, only DaDevoids will work as for the Monk's Staff below. Trying to use DaD relics prompted the reply Forge Hermit: "Hmmm, ya. That`s a DaD, but I think I`d prefer a better one. Two, actually." |
Go to 24,For,32 and chat with the Forge Hermit |
1. Equip as a theurgist
2. Use /st to confirm the server sees you as a theurgist and not as any other class. I thought it required being a Master of the Arcane but Mydnitetoker on S2 (thanks for sharing) was able to get the staff when he showed as a Theurgal Divinator.
3. Give the hermit a pure DaDev relic then he will take another, and you will get a Theurgist's Staff of Domination.

Gives Theurgal Divinators the effects of having two extra DLDev relics equipped and increases their damage bonus gained from Shadows from 1.7x to 2.2x and a 50% chance to burn their enemy's arrows mid-flight.

If you get this item and then decide you do not want it the hermit will can remove it and give a refund of your relics but that refund will cost (presumably) 50,000 ash. Thanks to Evincarvolrath for reminding me about the refund option and providing the ash cost. |

Friar's Staff of Preeminence
(For Monks)
Costs two DaDev relics

Seiyakou clarified that only DaDevoids work for getting the Staff. The hermit will not accept plain DaD relics as seen in his comments if you try to use them:

When attempting to trade a DaD - “Forge Hermit: Hmmm, ya. That's a DaD, but I think I'd prefer a better one. Two, actually.”

When trading a DaDev but the second relic is only a DaD - “Forge Hermit: That's funny. I don't see two of them.” |
Go to 24,For,32 and chat with the Forge Hermit
|
1. Equip as a monk
2. Use /st to confirm the server sees you as a master monk and not as any other class.
3. Give the hermit a pure DaDev relic then he will take another, and you will get a Friar's Staff of Preeminence.

Gives Master of Fists the effects of having an extra DaDev relic equipped and allows them to put arrows in their hands to take advantage of effects such as Shadow bonuses and relics. Also provides 50% chance of pummeling an enemy, preventing their attack.

If you get this item and then decide you do not want it the hermit will can remove it and give a refund of your relics but that refund will cost 50,000 ash. Thanks to Evincarvolrath for reminding me about the refund option and providing the ash cost. |

Two Convenience Items Were added on 1 July 2018. Both are only available for cash, not as random drops or through using ash.
While just buying something is not realy a "quest" these will appear in your inventory under "Others" with all the other quest items.

The Descender |
Allows those who ALREADY HAVE SoSe to travel to the Forge from any Hell location instead of always using 141,Hel,146 as their access point. |
Purchase directly from Glitchless for $25

Remember, you must already have SoSe to make use of this item. |

The Expander |
Increases your inventory space by an additional 75 slots, but also takes up one slot itself giving a maximum capacity of 164 (thanks to Anubis for confirming that factoid). Only one Expander can be had on a character. |
Purchase directly from Glitchless for $75 |

Innate Class Resists (These stack with all quest items and relics)

The Master Bowman class now receives an additional 10% resistance to voidance.

The Grandmaster Bowman class now recieves an additional 20% resistance to voidance.

The Armsman class now recieves an additional 10% resistance to voidance.

The Master of Arms class now recieves an additional 20% resistance to voidance.

The Berserker class now receives an additional 5% resistance to voidance.

The Master Berserker class now receives an additional 10% resistance to voidance.

The Magician class now recieves an additional 5% resistance to voidance.

The Master Magician class now recieves an additional 10% resistance to voidance.

The Wizard class now receives an additional 3% resistance to voidance.

The Master Wizard class now receives an additional 5% resistance to voidance.

The Blood Tyrant and Bloodletter class now receives an additional 3% resistance to voidance.

The Master of Fists class now receives an additional 5% resistance to mesmerization.

The Armsman class now recieves an additional 10% resistance to mesmerization.

The Master of Arms class now recieves an additional 20% resistance to mesmerization.

The Berserker class now receives an additional 5% resistance to mesmerization.

The Master Berserker class now receives an additional 10% resistance to mesmerization.

The Magician class now recieves an additional 5% resistance to mesmerization.

The Master Magician class now recieves an additional 10% resistance to mesmerization.

The Wizard class now receives an additional 3% resistance to mesmerization.

The Master Wizard class now receives an additional 5% resistance to mesmerization.

* The only way to get your fists to a power greater than a Mace of Resurrection is through pure skill training, special quest items lack the power.

When in Sky, you get DOUBLE the amount of experience. Which means you level twice as fast as on the surface.

When in Dungeon, you get DOUBLE the amount of gold per creature you kill.

When in Heaven, you get TRIPLE the amount of gold per creature you kill.

When in Hell, you get TRIPLE the amount of experience.

Return to home

pages.title: Relics
pages.text:
## Relics


This information is taken right off the official help site , it’s been reformatted and some notes have been added for clarification.


Relics shown in red text can’t be annulled


Common abbreviation for relics which are commonly abbreviated in the game are shown in parentheses next to the name.


All relics stack with more of the same and with different relics unless explicitly stated otherwise.


NOTE: The /help site shows some relics as doing “unstoppable damage” which confuses some players. What this means is that those relics can not be annulled. You can stop them using voidance or devoidance relics. Also, against players with defensive skill, or in special kingdoms like the Pub, Puddle, Palace, Keeps, Shrine and Insane Dungeon, the damage they do will be much less than against normal critters. Defensive skill reduces all damage, including that from “unstoppable” relics. On this page I more correctly list them as being unannullable, not unstoppable.







Relic (common in-game abbreviation) | 
Effect | 
Verbose Relic Display Message | 






Pure Boost Relics (these just increase your own stats) These are all player craftable




Strong Arm (SA) | 
Boosts strength by 40% | 
your muscles bulge. | 

Archer's Eye (AE) | 
Boosts dexterity by 40% | 
your eyesight sharpens. | 



Cat's Feet (CF) | 
Boosts agility by 40% | 
your feet quicken. | 



Merlin's Insight (MI) | 
Boosts intelligence by 40% | 
your mind tingles | 



Mental Focus (MF) | 
Boosts concentration by 40% | 
your mind clears | 



Impediment (Imped) | 
Boosts contravention by 40% | 
your mind takes guard | 



Priest's Touch (PT) | 
Restores lost health by 40% if you have taken damage . If you have full health, it does nothing. | 
you feel rejuvinated. | 



The Calling of Titus (Titus) | 
All of the above by 20% (not 40%) | 
you are beset with the spirit of Titus! | 









Pure Deduction Relics (there lower your enemy’s stats) These are all player craftable




Debilitator | 
Lowers enemy strength by a factor of 1.4 | 
your enemy looks weak. | 



Maladroit | 
Lowers enemy dexterity by a factor of 1.4 | 
your enemy looks groggy | 



Dawdle | 
Lowers enemy agility by a factor of 1.4 | 
your enemy looks sluggish | 



Stupidify | 
Lowers enemy intelligence by a factor of 1.4 | 
your enemy looks foolish | 



Distraction | 
Lowers enemy concentration by a factor of 1.4 | 
your enemy looks distracted | 



Mind Pierce | 
Lowers enemy contravention by a factor of 1.4 | 
your enemy looks concerned | 



Touch of the Damned (TotD) | 
Lowers enemy health by 40% OR by 40% of your durability, whichever is lower | 
your enemy looks lifeless | 



The Calling of Cassius (Cassius) | 
All of the above by a factor of 1.2 (not by a factor of 1.4) | 
your enemy is plagued by the spirit of Cassius! | 








Pure Plundering Relics (these take from your enemy and give to you) These are all player craftable




Siphon Strength | 
Increases your strength by 20% and decreases your enemy's strength by that amount | 
you steal your enemy's strength | 



Stolen Hands | 
Increases your dexterity by 20% and decreases your enemy's dexterity by that amount | 
you steal your enemy's dexterity | 



Filched Feet | 
Increases your agility by 20% and decreases your enemy's agility by that amount | 
you steal your enemy's agility | 



Pilfered Intellect | 
Increases your intelligence by 20% and decreases your enemy's intelligence by that amount | 
you steal your enemy's intelligence | 



Lifted Thoughts | 
Increases your concentration by 20% and decreases your enemy's concentration by that amount | 
you steal your enemy's concentration | 



Purloinment | 
Increases your contravention by 20% and decreases your enemy's contravention by that amount | 
you steal your enemy's contravention | 



Blood Pinch (BP)
Formerly unannullable, now has a 50% chance of beign annulled | 
Steals health from your enemy in the amount of 20% of your durability. This is a “kill” relic, meaning it can do damage even when your enemy’s dur is down to 1 | 
you steal your enemy's health | 



The Calling of Lestat (Lestat)
Formerly unannullable, now has a 50% chance of beign annulled | 
All of the above by 10% (not 20%). This is a “kill” relic, meaning it can do damage even when your enemy’s dur is down to 1 | 
the spirit of Lestat possesses you! | 








Special Relics These are all player craftable




Confusion | 
Randomly (10% of the time) mesmerizes enemy | 
your enemy looks confused | 


Life Roulette | 50% chance of healing you to full health. | You take your chances with the wheel |


Annulment (Annul) | 
Cancels two of enemy’s relics/enchantments | 
you annul [one or two] of your enemy's relics.
OR
but there was nothing to annul | 



Damage Shield | 
Increases effectiveness of healing and armor by 15% | 
you feel an aura of protection | 



Damage Capacitor | 
Increases effectiveness of weapons and casts by 15% | 
you feel dangerous | 



The Calling of Cara (Cara) | 
Boosts all stats except Dur by 20% plus incorporates the relics Confusion & Damage Shield & Damage Capacitor | 
your body pulses with the blessings of Cara! | 



Dexterous Hoist (DH) | 
Boosts dexterity by 60% | 
time stops as your body excels | 



Stream of Conscious (SoC) | 
Boosts concentration by 60% | 
time stops as your mind excels | 



Blood Bath (BB) | 
Lowers enemy health by 60% OR by 60% of your durability, whichever is lower | 
your enemy cringes in pain | 



Vampiric Leech (VL) | 
Steals 50% enemy health OR 50% of your durability, whichever is lower | 
your enemy collapses and you feel refreshed | 



Death Spike (DS) | 
Does 1,000,000 unannullable damage to normal enemies. This is a “kill” relic, meaning it can do damage even when your enemy’s dur is down to 1 | 
your enemy is stricken by calamity | 



Denial of the Believer (DotB) | 
Boosts all stats except Dur by 20% plus incorporates the relics Confusion & Damage Shield & Damage Capacitor + lowers enemy health by 90% OR by 90% of your durability, whichever is lower | 
your enemy reaches into his chest and squeezes his own heart | 








Eternal Relics (Those which are active all the time, thus do not need to fire and cannot be voided or annulled). These relics can only boost your modified skill to a max value of 99. To get skills above that you need a Skill Potencifier and have to train your base skill from 100 to its max of 125.
All but Sacrificial Skillation and Tears of the Fallen are player craftable




Theurgal Equilizer (TE) | 
Raises Theurgist damage by a factor of 2.5, this is player craftable | 
the fury of Hell boils in your eyes | 



Theurgal Conquest (TC) | 
Raises Theurgist damage by a factor of 6.25 + Voidance (Not including Confusion) , this is player "craftable" by using the Chained Libertine NPC.
First craft a Theurgal Equilizer, then type /add Chained Libertine to get it on your trade list, then trade the pure TE to the Libertine, you get back a pure TC) | 
flames leap from your heart and engulf your foe | 



Unerring Edge | 
Raises Attacking Accuracy by 5, this is player craftable | 
angels fall glowing tears on your target to guide your weapon. | 



Faithful Severity | 
Raises Attacking Precision by 5, this is player craftable | 
your weapon glows with intense acuity | 



Clarity | 
Raises Casting Accuracy by 5, this is player craftable | 
the magic of Hades immobolizes your victim | 



Mental Virtue | 
Raises Casting Precision by 5, this is player craftable | 
your mind divides space and time | 



Devestation | 
Raises Criticality by 5, this is player craftable | 
you feel a rush of ferocity | 



Rectification | 
Raises Regeneration by 5, this is player craftable | 
angels breathe life into your veins | 



Allegiance (Alleg) | 
Raises Kingmanship by 5, this is player craftable | 
you call on your brethren for support | 



Impetuosity (Impet) | 
Raises Haste by 5, this is player craftable | 
the spinning of the world slows to a crawl | 



Preservation | 
Raises Defensive by 5, this is player craftable | 
fibers of angelic hair enshroud your body | 



Monasticism | 
Raises Monk Discipline by 5, this is player craftable | 
you cleanse your mind of all things | 



Apex
(Does not stack with more Apex so DO NOT equip more than one. You will just get more delay, no benefits) | 
Doubles leveling speed but also doubles your delay factor (not stackable, beast drop, crafted, or obtained from the Hell Hermit for 100 runes, pm the Hermit), also player craftable .
Apex does not change your experience per kill (yellow exp bar) so you still kill as many times per training. What apex does is double your stat points when you train, so the purple level bar moves twice as far. | 
you feel a burning desire to fulfill your destiny of superiority | 



Sacrificial Skillation (SS) | 
Increases skill chance by 10% but increases your delay factor by 10% (only dropped off beasts or gotten from the Hell Hermit for 100 runes, pm the Hermit) | 
you take your time and concentrate on proper technique | 



Tears of the Fallen (TotF) | 
50% chance of being saved from death for 1st TotF equipped, 10% for each one equipped thereafter.
Created by trading a pure super relic to the Pub Keeper at 150,Sur,145. You lose the super and get one ToTF in return.
If you only have an upgraded super relic (MUR, VAS, CER) you can downgrade it by trading the pure relic to the Palace Keeper . You get back the super relic, the palace element used to upgrade the super is lost. | 
you hear the tears of a wingless angel strike the ground | 






Quest/Drop/Purchase Only Relics




The Super Relics MR, CR and VS can be purchased from Glitchless for $50 or obtained from the Puddle Keeper by sacrificing a DD relic. It is also possible, but very, very rare, for MR, CR or VS to drop randomly when normal enemies are killed.




Melee's Rage (MR) | 
Boosts DEX and STR by 80%, all other stats except Dur by 20%, incorporates the relics Confusion & Damage Shield & Damage Capacitor

. | 
you go mad | 



Caster's Revenge (CR) | 
Boosts CNC and NTL by 80%, all other stats except Dur by 20%, incorporates the relics Confusion & Damage Shield & Damage Capacitor .

| 
your mana surges | 



Vampire's Scorn (VS) | 
20% durability boost + 10 Death Spikes + **Double Defense + Confusion + Blood Pinch .

| 
your enemy's veins collapse | 



Devil's Deliverance (DD) | 
Boosts all stats except Dur by 80%, boosts DUR by 50%, incorporates the relics Confusion & Damage Shield & Damage Capacitor + lowers enemy health by 90% OR by 90% of your durability, whichever is lower + 10 Death Spikes (10 mil fixed damage to normal enemies) + **Double Defense + Voidance + Annulment + Doubles your damage output.

This is a “kill” relic, meaning it can do damage even when your enemy’s dur is down to 1.

I do not think these drop off kills at all anymore, however you can get one from the Sky Citizen for 250,000 ash. It is possible to accumulate 250k ash by destroying drops with attached relics, most likely in kingdoms with the highest drop rates but with a lot of time it would be possible while playing anywhere.


There is also a small chance of finding a DD when a Greater Tecton is dropped. The system message when this happens is "Someone found a powerful relic in the cracked earth!!!" (thanks to Logi on S2 for that information).


I would guess that most DDs are now probably obtained through buying 5 ash elements from Glitchless for $50 each to make 250k ash, then using that ash for the Sky Citizen quest.
| 









These are the upgraded versions of the basic super relics (above), see the quests for details




Melee's Undying Rage (MUR) | 
Boosts DEX and STR by 92%, all other stats except Dur by 20%, incorporates the relics Confusion & Damage Shield & Damage Capacitor + 2% Voidance resistance.


Made from a Melee’s Rage Super relic using an Essence Element of Lava (drops off Palace LA beasts). Trade the pure MR relic to the Palace Keeper and he will take the Lava and convert your MR into an MUR. | 
rage fills your heart and veins as you pulse with the combined force of all who seek revenge | 



Caster's Eternal Revenge (CER) | 
Boosts CNC and NTL by 92%, all other stats except Dur by 20%, incorporates the relics Confusion & Damage Shield & Damage Capacitor + 2% Voidance resistance.


Made from a Caster’s Revenge Super relic using an Essence Element of Storm (drops off Palace LA beasts). Trade the pure CR relic to the Palace Keeper and he will take the Storm and convert your CR into an CER. | 
lightning fills the sky as your pupils turn red with the blood of your past foes | 



Vampire's Amaranthine Scorn (VAS) | 
25% durability boost + 15 Death Spikes + **Double Defense + Confusion + Blood Pinch + 2% Voidance resistance.


Made from a Vampire’s Scorn Super relic using an Essence Element of Blood (drops off Palace LA beasts). Trade the pure VS relic to the Palace Keeper and he will take the Blood and convert your VS into a VAS. | 
you watch your enemy attempt to scream in pain as his tongue melts into boiling blood and scalds his insides | 



Devil's Deleterious Deliverance (DDD) | 

Boosts all stats except Dur by 92%, boosts DUR by 60%, incorporates the relics Confusion & Damage Shield & Damage Capacitor + lowers enemy health by 90% OR by 90% of your durability, whichever is lower + 20 Death Spikes (20 mil fixed damage to normal enemies) + **Double Defense + Voidance + Annulment + 4% Voidance resistance + 2% mesmerization resistance + Doubles your damage output. With 20 Death Spikes this is a kill relic.


Made from a Devil’s Deliverance relic using all three palace elements (drops off Palace LA beasts), so you need to have an Essence Element of Lava plus an Essence Element of Storm plus an Essence Element of Blood . Trade the pure DD relic to the Palace Keeper and he will take the Lava, Storm and Blood and convert your DD into a DDD. | 
the eyes of your enemy roll back in his head as he falls to his knees and begins to remove his own flesh | 








The relics below are upgrades to the DDD relic, collectively referred to as D*D relics. Each requires a dark essence, a pure DDD, a pure super relic (MR, CR, or VS) and 999 runes (verified by Loveless on S2).

While in YOUR kingdom that has 999 runes, trade the dark essence to the Surface Hermit. He takes the runes and relics and returns the D*D to you. Each dark essence makes a different D*D, for example you would use a Dark Resistance to convert a DDD into a DrD. The Dark Elements are: Dark Mutilation, Dark Litheness, Dark Putridity, Dark Resistance, Dark Skill, Dark Acceleration, Dark War, and Dark Opulence. At one time these were available from Glitchless for $250 each, but now they must be put together from their component parts.

Using the Ash Collector to add a Devoidance to a D*D relic converts it into a D*Devoid (D*Dv) which gives the same benefits as both the D*D and Devoid but only uses one slot. I believe the firing messages are the same for both the D*D and D*Dv version of each relic, with the Devoid message added as well.

NOTE: For 2,000 ash the Shrine Keeper will convert one type of D*Dv relic into another. The sequence of conversions is a wheel, any version of D*Dv can be converted into any other version with a maximum of 7 trades (thats 14,000 ash). You just trade him the D*Dv you have and he takes the ash and converts it into the next one. The sequence is:

DMDv to DLDv to DPDv to DRDv to DSDv to DADv to DWDv to DODv to DMDv

You can pm the Shrine Keeper (/m Shrine Keeper: hello) to see his dialog about converting your D*D relics. PLEASE REMEMBER Tyraelthefallen was good enough to point out that you can do the conversions with relics still attached to items, no need to first disenchant them .






Devil's Mutilating Deliverance (DmD or DMD) | 
Devil's Deleterious Deliverance but with a 150% boost to STR and NTL + Raises Theurgist damage by a factor of 6.25 | 
your enemy begins to twitch and convulse as his eyes collapse | 



Devil's Lithe Deliverance (DlD or DLD) | 
Devil's Deleterious Deliverance but with a 150% boost to DEX and CNC + Raises Theurgist damage by a factor of 6.25 | 
your enemy spasms in such severity that his spinal cord twists and breaks | 



Devil's Putrid Deliverance (DpD or DPD) | 
Devil's Deleterious Deliverance but with a 150% boost to AGI and CNT , 76% boost to DUR | 
your enemy vomits his own bath of acid and allows it to melt his flesh | 



Devil's Resistant Deliverance (DrD or DRD) | 
Devil's Deleterious Deliverance + 10% Voidance resistance + 10% mesmerization resistance | 
your enemy's bones become weakened and shatter from the vibrations caused by his shrieks of horror and pain | 



Devil's Skillful Deliverance (DsD or DSD) | 
Devil's Deleterious Deliverance + increase relative skill chance by 15% | 
your enemy's attempt to avoid your eyes fail him as he becomes entranced with fear seeing the essence of death within you | 



Devil's Accelerated Deliverance (DaD or DAD)

(Acceleration effect does not stack with more DaD) | 
Devil's Deleterious Deliverance + doubles leveling speed (the leveling effect is not stackable with itself, however it will stack with an Apex) | 
your enemy flinches uncontrollably, biting down through his tongue, shattering his teeth which penetrate his gums | 



Devil's Warring Deliverance (DwD or DWD)

(Rune effect does not stack with more DwD) | 
Devil's Deleterious Deliverance + halves the effective value of the runes of kingdoms when attacking them (not stackable).


Swp Devil (Server 2) asked Glitchless about whether the rune defense reduction still applied when the DwD is in a Goblin Hide Backpack and was told it must be equipped for that benefit to apply. So it looks like you can have the rest of the DRC in your GHB but need to equip the DwD to reduce enemy rune defense when attacking. | 
your enemy kneels before you as your black horses of war surround him | 



Devil's Opulent Deliverance (DoD or DOD) | 
Devil's Deleterious Deliverance + grants the /br ability allowing you to buy runes for a distant kingdom and the /sr ability to purchase 1 rune at a time instead of 2 | 
your enemy pleads before your throne that you spare him as you spill his blood on your piles of treasure below | 









Relics and the GHB
A special case exists for characters with a Goblin Hide Backpack and the DaD, DsD, DoD and DwD. Relics usually need to be equipped to have any effect but these four can still give benefits when they are unequipped and in pure form if your character has a GHB . In that case some of the non-combat benefits will still work for these specific reilcs even when they are not equipped. You won't get the stat boosts, the rune defense reduction of the DwD or the skilling boost from the DsD but you will get other effects from the DwD, DoD and DaD and the 4-relic combo effect if you have all four.




Voidance (Void) | 
Each yields a 10% chance to void all enemy enchantments + Confusion , this is player craftable . | 
the walls of space collapse as you attempt to void all your enemies enchantments | 



Devoidance (Devoid , only drops off beasts) | 
Each yields a 10% chance to void all enemy enchantments + Confusion + 10% mesmerization resistance (see notes below). Devoids can block enemy Voids from firing, but not enemy devoids | 
you call upon the powers below to render your enemy's relics useless | 



D*Devoidance (D*Dv) | 
Any of the D*D relics that has been upgraded by Ash Collector with the addition of a Devoid | 
Same messages as corresponding D*D plus Devoid? | 



Shirt Ripper (SR) | 
Doubles your Strength (only dropped off beasts ) | 
your muscles bulge as your shirt tears into pieces and bursts into flames as your enemy cowers before you | 



Brain Swell (BS) | 
Doubles your Intelligence only dropped off beasts )) | 

your brain expands to the point of shattering your skull, sending fragments that tear through your enemy's flesh | 



Encephalon Decay (ED) | 
Does 2,000,000 unannullable damage to normal enemies (only found on surface), this is player craftable . This is a “kill” relic, meaning it can do damage even when your enemy’s dur is down to 1 | 
your enemy staggers as his brains leak from his ears | 



Vogar's Vice (VV) | 
Does 3,000,000 unannullable damage to normal enemies (only found on surface), this is player craftable . This is a “kill” relic, meaning it can do damage even when your enemy’s dur is down to 1 | 
your enemy twitches and falls to the ground as Vogar compresses their spine | 



Putrefaction (Put) | 
Does 5,000,000 unannullable damage to normal enemies (send a pm to Vogar if you want to see his dialog). You need a pure Encephalon Decay , a pure Vogar's Vice and a kingdom having at least 5 runes (you need to be its king). With those in hand, trade Vogar the VV and he’ll combine it with an ED into a Putrefaction (you can trade to an NPC for 0 gold). You will lose the 5 runes. This is a “kill” relic, meaning it can do damage even when your enemy’s dur is down to 1. You can get Vogar on your pm and trade lists using the command /add Vogar.


I think the 7 billion gold cost (assuming you craft the VV and ED) of the relic is more than the 5 mil damage is worth, but some players like them. | 
putrescent fluids begin to accumulate at the feet of your petrified foe, seeping into his skin and invading his veins with death | 



Leather of Delegation (LoD) | 
If you have a Scepter of Delegation this relic allows you to embezzle gold to your own remote kingdoms. It does not need to be equipped and only works when pure. This relic is player craftable .


Prior to use you set locations to receive the embezzled gold by using the command "/pea amount" without the quotes while your character is located in the destination kingdom (i.e. /pea 150000000). You then move through your other kingdoms and in each one type “/pe location” without the quotes, where ‘location’ is the destination for the gold (i.e. /pe 123,Sur,123).


The amount specified by the /pea command will be moved from your current location Trs to the destination Trs. As the destination kingdom Trs becomes full additional runes will automatically be purchased and placed in it. | 
Not equipped, no firing message | 



Free Fortifier (freefort or FF) | 
Drops off the Goblin King Sur beast, gives free improvements to kingdoms. With each use of the /freefort command each kingdom fortification (walls, moats, cannons) on each side is increased by 250 million, so each use gives you 3 billion of improvements for free.


Since December 2016 it also adds 250 mil gold to the treasury per use (thanks to Dragon Eater on S1 for telling me the amount), raising the value per use to 3.25 billion.


For example, IF you buy one for an EEoGT (250 bil) and IF you manage to get 77 uses out of it before it breaks, you will break even (77 uses would give you 250 bil worth of kingdom improvements). However, each time it's used, a freefort has a chance of breaking, so you don’t know in advance if you will lose money, break even, or come out ahead. Breakage is random, so it may happen on the first use, the 100th, or the 1,000th. | 
Not equipped, no firing message but you do get warning that's it's creaking and a system message goes out when one breaks.


This relic does not work for kingdoms in The Forge | 








This information is taken right off the official help site , it’s been reformatted and some notes have been added for clarification.



Special Relic Combos


A player with Devil's Opulent Deliverance and Devil's Warring Deliverance equipped will have up to 48 hours of interest accrued as opposed to the standard 24.


A player with Devil's Opulent Deliverance, Devil's Warring Deliverance, Devil's Skillful Deliverance, and Devil's Accelerated Deliverance equipped doubles the effectiveness of all your kingdom's runes and unlocks the potential of high kingmanship (110, 120, 125) to give you 1, 2, or 3 extra hourly attacks respectively.


A player with at least one Devil's Accelerated Deliverance and any number of Devil's Skillful Deliverances equipped will have each Devil's Skillful Deliverance yield a 20% increase in skill chance as opposed to the standard 15%.


A player with at least one Devil's Resistant Deliverance equipped will change the effects of any number of Devil's Putrid, Lithe, and/or Mutilating Deliverances equipped as follows: 


Devil's Putrid Deliverance yields +40% Agi and +40% Cnt instead of +30% and +15% Dur instead of +10% (overall boost of 169% to your base Agi and Cnt, 84% boost to base Dur).


Devil's Lithe Deliverance yields +40% Dex and +40% Cnc instead of +30% (overall of 169% to your base Dex and Cnc). 


Devil's Mutilating Deliverance yields +40% Str and +40% Ntl instead of +30% (overall boost of 169% to your base Str and Ntl).




Notes

All resistances are considered chance based effects


The probability of a % chance based effect of firing when stacked is:


( 1 - (1 - ( Chance / 100 ))^Amount ) * 100


So if you had 8 Voidances relics equipped:


( 1 - ( 1 - ( 10/100))^8 ) * 100 = 56.95% chance of voiding!


Voidance cannot be voided by another Voidance. Enchantments that have Voidance as a component, however, can be partially voided (meaning everything is voided except for the Voidance component)


Devoidance is better than the Voidance relic. Unlike the standard Voidance, a Devoidance can void an enemy's Voidance, but not an enemy's Devoidance. A Devoidance cannot be voided by a Voidance. Any resistance you have toward Voidance works against Devoidance as well.


Although many relics do damage, only Blood Pinch and Death Spike can kill an opponent without a euphoric rush or surge. They are commonly known as finishing relics.


Although Devil's Deliverance contains 2 elements that both have Confusion, the DD only has 1 confusion.


All relic effects stack unless otherwise noted in the description of the relic.


* means they cannot be annulled by a regular Annulment relic

**Doubles your ability to defend, not your defensive skill




This page last updated 20 October 2025











Return to home

pages.title: Beasts
pages.text:
## Beasts Of RWK




## Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved


Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.




NOTE : The Ash Collector is on magma in Hell, so you need both hell access and a Templar's Wand (meaning you need to be a permanent Templar) to summon beasts, plus as mentioned below the summoner needs to have Aqualungs too as of 6/14/2008 . . Players with 10-day trial access alone can't do it, but those with permanent templar, aqualungs, AND 10-day trial can. 



You can get a list of what beasts your character has killed so far in the current month by typing /beast in game.

RWK Beasts are special critters that are not always available like the ones you can fight using the Actions, New Fight menu. Beasts either appear randomly when awakened by a player action (almost any action will do it, fighting, chatting, crafting, walking) or are summoned with ash and gold by players who can reach the Ash Collector (location 141,Hell,174, requires Hell access and permanent Templar ). Even playing poker can awaken a beast (courtesy of Spirk on S1). When a beast appears there is a system message, yellow text all players see, announcing its location. Whoever can reach the beast before it is killed can use the Actions, Battle menu to fight it. They are usually much harder to kill than normal creatures and can drop unique and valuable items.


Beasts are more resistant to damage than normal creatures and even more resistant than the critters in the keeps, puddle and pub. For relic damage that's fixed or based on your own dur (using relics like VV or BP ), you will do 25x less damage in the pub, etc. than in normal kingdoms. The "regular" approbation beasts ( Widow , Corpse and EHA ) will take around 8x less damage than that, and the higher beasts like Rogues 10x less than regulars. So, if you can do exactly 1 billion damage to a normal Yoro, you will do 40 million to Pub Yoros, 5 million to an EHA, and 500k to a Goblin Rogue. There may be some play in the damage reduction to Rogues and I have no data on other beasts, but the overall trend is probably accurate. Polymorphic Beetles are easier than approbation beasts, but I only have an estimate of about 3x less damage than in the Pub, so for our example above around 13 mil damage to a Beetle. I don't have any data on damage done by other classes or against LA's, DE's or other newer beasts. DotB seems to begin doing less damage as a beast dies even when its remaining health is well above yours, which seems at odds with how that relic should work ("...lowers enemy health by 90% OR by 90% of your durability, whichever is lower).


Fire Chicken (Server 2) has reported that relic damage to beast guards is the same as that to regular beasts for Bloodletters (vamps with 67% or more of base points in Dur and having Fangs of the Bloodletter ) and "uber" beasts take 400x less relic damage than regular ones. My own data on relic damage to the harder beasts is scattered and inconclusive so I'll report his conclusions as I got them: "A good way to calculate relic damage for vamps is attacking beast guards. Damage against these is exactly the same as damage to regs. Also ubers take 400x less (relic) damage than regs, beast guards = regulars with fangs." This information should be useful to vamps who are thinking about getting a private beast but not sure if they can do enough damage to kill it.


Some beasts give an approbation or blessing, which is a relic permanently attached to a body part. These approbations don not take up any storage space and are always equipped, so they can greatly increase
your damage. Players can have a total of 10 approbations and blessings (10 in total, not 10 of each). Blessings and approbations don't show anywhere on your character. You can see if you have any blessings or approbations on your character by typing /app in game.


The Polymorphic Beetle was the first beast added to the game, it's the easiest non-surface beast to kill, and it gives a blessing instead of an approbation. You get approbations from: Crucified Corpse, Darkest Widow, Eclyptic Hell Angel, "normal" Lord Alchemar, and Rogue Goblin.


Many beasts don't give either a blessing or approbation: Palace Alchemars, Ancient Alchemar, Master Templar, Goblin Shaman, Golem of Living Rock, Unborn Torments and Grandmaster Templars, Dragon Eaters, plus the
3 easy Sur beasts (Goblin Miner, Goblin King and Goblin Thief).


Since the Beast Bane (BB) and Beast Noobane (BNB) were added to the game it has become nearly impossible to kill these public beasts without one. Players with a BB or BNB can instantly teleport to 1 kingdom away from the beast location and usually will kill it within seconds. If you have to teleport manually your chances of getting there before it's dead are slim, although it can be done if you are quick and lucky. Your chances are also better at the end of the month than the beginning. Only 4 beasts can ever appear as public ones on Sur, the rest will only appear on the four other planes unless summoned as privates by the Ash Collector. Three of these surface beasts are very easy to kill (Goblin King, Thief and Miner) and are intended to allow newbies and any non-access player a chance to get a beast kill. The only rub is that you really need at least a Noobane to have any chance of reaching them in time.


Access players who can reach the Ash Collector (not to be confused with the Ash Keeper at 150,Sur,150) can use their ash and gold to summon beasts. There is some control of what will appear and you can choose whether it will be a public beast that everyone will see and can try to kill, or a private one whose location is only given to the summoner. Private beasts require a LOT more ash than public ones. As of June 2008 all private beasts appear on the Surface. Public summoned beasts still appear on the various planes.


Here are all the beasts currently in the game. The ones in blue text are the oldest ones, they can be tough to kill because their defensive skill lowers your damage quite a bit. You also need enough annuls to stop them from healing. The best way to do that is with a Scepter of Annulment ( SoA ) but it is possible to kill them using enough annulment relics. If you keep attacking any beast without enough annuls or an SoA, and it keeps healing as a result, you will eventually be automatically muted. When muted, you can't attack any beasts so this lets those who are prepared to fight them get a kill. Also, each time it heals off you everyone in the KD will get a message about it, so you will make enemies quickly if you repeatedly heal beasts and may end up being chainkilled yourself. These old beasts are easy to hit because they have almost no evasion skill, Agility or Contravention, so you can manage to hit them with a modified accuracy stat (Dex, Agi or Cnc) of only around 100k.


The beasts in red text are the only ones that appear randomly as public beasts on the Surface (all privately summoned beasts now appear on surface, but only the summoner sees the locations for those). The Rogue is hard to kill and it's unlikely any non-access player is going to get one. You also will need an SoA to kill a Rogue. The Miner, Thief and King are easy to kill and any newbie who manages to reach one before it dies can potentially kill it. Getting those is as much luck at anything else.


The beasts in black text are the hardest to kill. As of the 5/17/04 announcement "the Lord Alchemar and all beasts more powerful than him have been upgraded. They now have Scepters of Annulment which will make attacking them with annul-able relics futile". Some also have a lot of evasion stat (Agi or Cnt) so you need a lot more accuracy to hit them. Figure 3 bil modified Dex, Cnc or Agi (depending on your class) to hit them with 5 bil being a better goal for hitting them every attack. You will also need an SoA of your own to succeed with these beasties. I've been told that the Ancient Alchemar , Lord Alchemar and Palace LA do not have high evasion stats and can be attacked by players with low accuracy (courtesy of Dragonmage on S2). I've only ever killed one LA and one PLA in all the time I've played, and that was years ago, so I don't have more details about them. More accuracy never hurts, but you may need to test and see what balance of accuracy vs. damage you need to kill them.


The "kill message" gives you the text to identify which beast was just killed. I don't show the entire message, it's something like "Joe Schmo has slain the **** Beast" possibly with more details afterwards. The keywords for telling the old beasts apart are shown. A plain "Beast" is a Beetle, an Ultra beast is an EHA, etc. The newer beasts give a much more explicit kill message that make what they are obvious. The 3 different types of UT all give the same kill message, as do the 3 types of PLA and 3 types of Dragon Eater. You can't tell from the kill message whether it was an Ice, Storm, etc., you only know which type it is if you target the beast and have animated graphics enabled. The beast image will have flames, lightning, or snow superimposed on it showing which flavor of beast it is.





## Restrictions

There are some limits on killing beasts. First, you can only kill one of each beast per month. So, once you kill a DW you will need to wait until after the Acumen to kill DW, the server will not allow you to even attack them until the beast lists are reset. You can kill other beasts, just not more Widows that month. If you kill an Ancient Alchemar you not kill any "regular" beasts like Beetle, Cropse, Widow, EHA, UT, or other flavors of Alchemar. Conversely, once you kill a regular beast you can't attack an Ancient Alchemar until the beast lists are reset. For these reasons you should think before summoning a beast. If you already killed one and you summon another of that same beast you will not be able to attack it. The three 7,500 ash beasts (DW, CC and EHA) are randomly summoned as privates, so you can't predict which you will get. If you kill a DW and want an EHA next there is no guarantee you won't get a second DW that you cannot attack. Kill an AA and then summon a regular beast and you will not be able to attack it. As of early 2018 the /beasts command was added to provide a list of all the beasts you have killed that month. It should eliminate summoning a beast you then can not fight.


As I understand it, the newer beasts (I suppose these would be the "irregular" ones) are separate from the AA vs. Regular rule. I believe you can still attack Dragon Eaters, Golems and Shamans even after killing an AA but I'm not certain about that so don't summon one based on this information being accurate. I don't know how Master Templars, Grandmaster Templars and Rogues fit in with the AA kill restrictions. I never spent enough to be much of a beast killer so I'm not a good source for details of how the newer ones work. If you need to know, ask someone who kills them regularly.


Blue = Older beasts that are easier to kill than some newer ones

Red = Public Surface beasts (all private beasts are also on Sur)

Black = Hardest ones to kill



REMEMBER, ALL private beasts now appear on Sur and as mentioned below the summoner needs to have Aqualungs as of 6/14/2008.

(common abbreviation for it) |

(an SoA is always enough) |

Gold and Ash Needed to Summon Private |

BLESSING |

May drop an Ocean Bubble (confirmed by Metalg316 on S2) but the drop isn't a sure thing (Swp Devil on S2). Can also drop Shirt Ripper (thanks to Niizandar for that) and Apex (thanks to Sarenrae on S2 for that). |

Lord Alchemar
(LA)

APPROBATION | 
2 bil | 
Possible to kill without an SoA
(Mav73 got a kill with 15 annuls as relics and approbations) but easier if you have one. I have been told they don't have high evasion so you can attack with low accuracy. They DO have an SoA however so your annullable relics won't work.

Palace Lord Alchemar
(PLA)

NO APPROBATION OR BLESSING | 
2 bil | 
Mav73 from S1 did some testing with an alt character and found he needed 13 annuls equipped to kill it. With fewer annuls the beast would heal often enough to recover all the damage that character was doing. He did not get any in-game warnings, so apparently the heals did not risk an automatic mute but they did keep the critter from dying. I have been told the PLA does not require high accuracy to hit but you do need those annuls to completely put it down rather than dancing with it indefinitely.


You need a Scroll of Forbidden Rights to fight these.


You can't attack this if you have already killed an AA that month. 

| 
Spawn message "A Winged beast is spotted flying from the Palace!"

(updated messages 9 Aug 21 after seeing one spawn)


Drops Palace Elements, Storm, Blood or Lava , and "amazing wealth" (4 EEoGT ) |

Ancient Alchemar
Ancient or AA)

NO APPROBATION OR BLESSING | 
2 bil | 
Requires an SoA or 1 Annulment relic to prevent healing.


You need a Scroll of Forbidden Rights and a Beastbane to attack these beasts


I have been told they don't have high evasion so you can attack with low accuracy. They DO have an SoA however so your annullable relics won't work.


If you killed a regular beast (beetle, CC, DW, EHA, UT, LA or PLA) you won't be able to fight AA's that month. Since September 2006, you can now only kill one AA a month. |

(updated messages 10 Aug 21 after seeing one spawn)

|

Rogue Goblin
(Rogue)

APPROBATION | 
2 bil | 
They have 25 relics to annul, so it requires an SoA or 13 Annulment relics to prevent healing. They apparently have low evasion stats (like beetles, widows, corpses and EHAs) so you don't need much accuracy to hit.


There has been some conflicting information about whether these prevent fighting an AA but both Cagedangel and Bravo Mischef on S2 found they did not


Almez (S2) killed an AA after a Rogue, but on a different occasion could not attack a Rogue after killing an AA so it looks like you can't fight a Rogue if you already killed an AA that same month. In that case do NOT summon a private Rogue after you have killed an AA that month. |

Master Templar
(MT)

NO APPROBATION OR BLESSING | 
2 bil |

They can drop Earthen (Manchester S2) and and Crystal (Tyraelthefallen S1) items, and most likely also Demonic as well.

(GMT)

NO APPROBATION OR BLESSING | 
2 bil |

Not excluded by killing an AA)
(courtesy of Nsane on S1) | 
Awakened A Beast At...
Killed the Grandmaster Templar at...

Gives 1 point increases to various tradeskills (runeworking, weapon crafting, etc.). Which skills and how many are helped will vary randomly. The most I've gotten myself was 12 skills and my average for 94 GMTs between both servers is 9 skills per beast. Bernie Sanders (S2) reported unfortunately getting only 5 skills from a GMT (condolences).

| 
1,999,999,998 gold and 7,000 ash | 



Goblin Shaman
(Shaman)

NO APPROBATION OR BLESSING | 
2 bil | 
Does not require an SoA to kill (demonstrated by Archangel Michael on S2 with a level 1,000 character) but apparently they do have some relics to annul.

The Forsaken Dark (S1) reported he was annulling 15 relics and doing 9x9 damage while Averlily, without an SoA, had the Shaman heal fully every time he attacked.

(Not excluded by killing an AA, courtesy of Archer Of Crosis on S1) | 
The Stench Of Rotting Flesh Fills The Air... (courtesy of Macca123)
Killed the Goblin Shaman at...


Drops Warrior's Squire , Caster's Familiar . Can drop a hide to make the Goblin Hide Backpack or its head, which can be converted into 999 runes. Sometimes drops Earthen (James2773 on S2) or Crystal items (Swp Devil S2), probably demonic as well. | 
1,999,999,999 gold and 8,500 ash | 



Golem Of Living Rock
(Golem or GoLR)

NO APPROBATION OR BLESSING | 
2 bil | 
Best to use an SoA to prevent healing.


(Not excluded by killing an AA, courtesy of Archer Of Crosis on S1) | 
Spawn message "The ground shakes and the clamouring of chains can be heard in the distance"

Kill message "[playername] has slain a Golem Of Living Rock at..."

(updated messages 10 Aug 21 after seeing one spawn)
