Can drop Crystal , may drop demonic or earthen and devoids (courtesy of Lord Ragnarok) as well. | 
1,999,999,997 gold and 8,500 ash | 



Dragon Eater
(DE)

NO APPROBATION OR BLESSING | 
2 bil | 
Best to use an SoA to prevent healing.

However, Shiny Pete (S2) fought 3 different Dragon Eaters and in each case needed on;y 8 Annuls to prevent them from healing so it looks like that's an option for players who want a DE but do not yet have an SoA. Thank you for sharing, Pete.


Not excluded by killing an AA (courtesy of Dr Zues on S1) | 
Spawn message "A shrill screech sounds in the distance followed by a horrendous growl...and then all is silent" (courtesy of Jmo - Server 2)

Kill message "[Playername] has slain a Dragon Eater at...!"

(confirmed messages 10 Aug 21 after seeing one spawn)


Will drop an Essence Element of Dragon Flames, Storm or Shards depending on which type randomly spawns. May also drop Crystal , Demonic or Earthen items as well. | 
1,999,999,993 gold and 30,000 ash

I am informed they do not spawn from 1,000 and 2,250 ash public summonings. | 



Goblin Thief
(Thief)

NO APPROBATION OR BLESSING | 
10 | 
None


(Not excluded by killing an AA)
(courtesy of Nsane on S1) | 
Awakened a Beast At...
Killed the Goblin Thief at...

Sur Beast, anyone can kill it, drops treasure elements , can drop demonic items or devoids (verified by Macca123, S2). | 
Can not be summoned, only randomly awakened.

I am informed they do not spawn from 1,000 and 2,250 ash public summonings. | 



Goblin Miner
(Miner)

NO APPROBATION OR BLESSING | 
10 | 
None


(Not excluded by killing an AA)
(courtesy of Nsane on S1) | 
Awakened a Beast At...
Killed the Goblin Miner at...


Sur Beast, anyone can kill it, drops Ash and can drop Demonic items (verified). | 
Can not be summoned, only randomly awakened.

I am informed they do not spawn from 1,000 and 2,250 ash public summonings. | 



Goblin King
(King)

NO APPROBATION OR BLESSING | 
410 | 
None

Not excluded by killing an AA | 
Awakened a Beast At...
Killed the Goblin King...


Sur Beast, anyone can kill it, drops Free Fortifier , can also drop Devoid (thanks to Agromon for that) or SS relics and Demonic items.



This beast has a damage cap of 3 damage per attack per player (only 1 for pure vamps but you can get in 3 if you also equip a weapon and damage spell, 2 damage spells or 2 weapons in addition to your relic damage. Each offensive item (weapon, spell and relics) doing 1 point.


For Vamps you likely have 2 shields and a rapid damage spell for speed. If you also equip a second damage spell instead of a heal you can get 3 damage points per attack using the fireball icon or "C" key to attack.


Getting the kill if others are also attacking is a matter of luck since you can't tell exactly when it's down to only 1-3 hitpoints. Keep attacking and hope for the best. | 
Can not be summoned, only randomly awakened.

I am informed they do not spawn from 1,000 and 2,250 ash public summonings. | 


Nightmare Fly
(Fly)

NO APPROBATION OR BLESSING | 
2 Billion?. It only takes small damage like the other slow-kill Sur beasts | 
Probably no SoA needed like a Zombie, probably no evasion skill but likely to heal off a low-level attacker like a Zombie. | 
A waking nightmare fills your vision (must be more to it with the location but that's all I copied when I saw one spawn).


"Player" has killed the Nightmare Fly and took some body parts at xxx,Sur,yyy!


Sur Beast, anyone can kill it(?), drops Goldblum Goo , which begins a somewhat tedious quest, and can drop Demonic items (Drakken Deathlord got one on S1 12Mar2018) and probably Devoids .


This beast seems to has a damage cap of around 150 damage per attack per player if relics and two attacks are combined. Using relics and 2 spells the max I've done was 40 relic damage, 46 with a surge and 30 per cast, 35 with a surge. Keep
attacking and hope for the best.

I doubt the dur is 2 billion since only 150 damage per attack would mean more than 13 million attacks.


Can drop Sacrificial Skillation | 
Not available as private, just 1 bil gold, 1,000 ash and 1.5 bil gold, 2,250 ash public beasts. | 





Dragonling Zombie
(Zombie)

NO APPROBATION OR BLESSING | 
2 Billion?. It only takes small damage like the other slow-kill Sur beasts | 
No SoA Needed (courtesy of Maz on S1)


Does not appear to have any evasion skill, a low-level mule cast without fizzling but it also allowed the Zombie to heal most times. | 
An abomination fails to take flight.
"Player" has killed the Dragonling Zombie and took some body parts at xxx,Sur,yyy!

Sur Beast, anyone can kill it(?), drops Deformed Wing , which begins a somewhat tedious quest. If it follows the same rules as the Zombie it can likely drop Demonic items. They can drop Devoids (courtesy of Badjuju on S2).

This beast seemed to work the same as a Fly with a damage cap of about 150 damage per attack per player. Keep attacking and hope for the best.

I doubt the dur is 2 billion since only 150 damage per attack would mean more than 13 million attacks. | 
Not available as private, just 1 bil gold, 1,000 ash and 1.5 bil gold, 2,250 ash public beasts. | 








So, in summary you can get Blessings from a PB , Approbations from a CC, DW, EHA, plain LA, and Rgue . No Blessing or Approbation from a UT, PLA, AA, MT, GMT, Shaman, GoLR, DE, Thief, Miner, King, Fly or Zombie. Looks like most beasts do not give a gift that keeps on giving.

## Beast Summoning

NOTE: The Ash Collector is on magma in Hell, so you need both hell access and a Templar's Wand (meaning you need to be a permanent Templar) to summon beasts. Players with 10-day trial access alone can't do it, but those with permanent templar AND 10-day trial can.


***All private beasts will appear on Surface as of June 2008 and the player summoning them must have Aqualungs*** If you are a Sur player and someone with access is summoning a private for you it's a good idea for the intended killer to also have aqualungs since the beasts can appear in surface water kingdoms. If your beast appears on water and you don't have lungs you will not be able to reach its location and fight it. 


Here is the thumbnail summary of using the Ash Collector to summon beasts for those who don't already know how it's done. You can go to the AC at 141,Hel,174 and begin the dialog as usual by giving him 1 gold. He eventually mentions, " For slightly less than 2 billion and a hunk of ash I could summon different types of beasts. Offer me an amount and I'll tell you what I can do ." At this point you can offer him various amounts of gold just less than 2 bil and he will summon a specific private beast which will appear somewhere on Sur. If you have more than about 7,000 ash do NOT try this just to find out the details. If you have enough ash and offer the right amount of gold the AC just summons the beast, he doesn't just "tell you what he can do". I found this the hard way but at least I did summon the beast I was interested in and got my Theurgal Orb.


Anyway, the information you need to know is what amount of gold summons which specific private beast, which I show in the table below. If there are others I have not included here please update me on them so I can added them to my table.




Beast to Summon | 
Gold to Give the AC | 
Ash Used | 



Goblin Shaman | 
1,999,999,999 | 
8,500 | 



Grandmaster Templar | 
1,999,999,998 | 
7,000 | 



Golem of Living Rock | 
1,999,999,997 | 
8,500 | 



Master Templar | 
1,999,999,996 | 
8,000 | 



Rogue Goblin | 
1,999,999,995 | 
7,200 | 



Polymorphic Beetle | 
1,999,999,994 | 
7,500 | 



Dragon Eater
( Note that summoning this beast will consume 30,000 ash) | 
1,999,999,993 | 
30,000 | 



Randomly summons either a Crucified Corpse, Darkest Widow or Eclyptic Hell Angel, you do NOT get to choose which one appears. Keep in mind that even if you have already killed one of those 3 that month you can still end up getting the same beast when you summon and you will not be able to attack it. | 
2,000,000,000 | 
7,500 | 



Any random public beast, success guaranteed | 
1,500,000,000 | 
2,250 | 



Any random public beast, success NOT guaranteed | 
1,000,000,000 | 
1,000 | 






The complete list of all beasts as of March 2018 is: 

(Keep in mind some come in different flavors, like Ice, Fire and Lightning Unborn Torments.)


Polymorphic Beetle, Crucified Corpse,
Darkest Widow, Eclyptic Hell Angel, Rogue Goblin , Unborn Torment (3 flavors, Fire, Lightning and Ice), Lord Alchemar (2 flavors, plain and Palace), Ancient Alchemar, Master Templar, Grandmaster Templar, Goblin Shaman, Golem Of Living Rock, Dragon Eater (3 flavors, Fire,
Thunder and Ice), Goblin Thief , Goblin Miner , Goblin King, Nightmare Fly, Dragonling Zombie. .




The Rogue Goblin, Goblin Miner, Goblin Thief and Goblin King all appear on the surface and can be reached instantly using the /bn command (provided you have found a Beast Noobane ). The Miner and Thief and only have 10 dur and are easily killed by anyone IF you can reach them on time, the Rogue is much harder to kill, the King has only 410 HP but has a damage cap of 1 per attack per attack type (weps or hands, spells and relics can each do 1 point damage), so the kill is more a matter of good luck than with other beasts.


The Fly and Zombie have much higher dur than Goblins but like them take very little damage per attack. They can also heal even if you have an SoA. Killing those beasts requires persistent attacking over a relatively long time (compared to other beast kills) and if other players are also attacking one it will be a matter of chance who finally gets the kill.


Page updated 27 April 2026





Back to home

pages.title: Crafting Special Items

Crafting Special Items 







## Crafting "Special" Items





Most of these values are taken off Cyril's website, which seems to no longer exist, and used with his permission.

I think items at the upper end of the skill levels (like Demonics and Superior Treasures) can only become trivial by killing Grandmaster Templars. You can get close by making items but those last points to make them trivial never seem to trigger for me solely by crafting. Mine have only tripped over with GMT kills. Mert on S1 observed the same thing with Great Tresaures before Superior ones were added to the game.
Page last updated 9 March 2025

Other Forge locations will still gain interest and drop gems.



Forge Risen

Note: Forged items are relatively cheap and easy to ENCHANT(only 10 runes and trivial at 125 chantcraft skill). However , DISENCHANTING them is expensive , 999 runes and 10,000 ash, so try to make the first relic you put on one the last one you will what there. | Forge Talon | Expurgation of the Forge | Forge Risen | 765

Initial cost is 10mil Forge Trs (10 trillion normal gold), 5 Greater Gems, and takes 5 days to make. The cost then increases for each additional item as shown below.

You can use a mix of different Great Gems for the upgrade.

NOTE: These items can NOT be traded between characters and having one prevents name swaps. |

Heal Spell - 15% Cripple and Crippling Resist, 25% Damage Mitigation
Courtesy of Obsidian Night)


From the RWK Forum: "The effects of cripple are severe for a round of a fight. All stats are reduced by 80%, including attack and heal ratings. The effect of all ruby, sapphire, emerald, and void resists innate to armors is halved.

There is a less important side effect that will permanently reduce your stats by 1,000 each. This will always happen in Inquest, but will only happen outside of Inquest if you are above the hard cap and have been active for that hour."

pages.title: Character Creation Guide
pages.text:
Character Creation Guide 





## Jimcav�s Character Creation Guide



Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright.

Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is prohibited.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.



Page last updated 6 September 2022



If you are new to RWK and find this site sufficiently helpful to warrant it, you can say "thanks" by typing the command "/mentor Jimcav" (without the quotes") at any point from levels 1 through 5. It will cost you nothing but my character will receive 500 ash for helping you as a guide. This action will also trigger a humorous system message .






Archer | Caster | Fighter | Monk | Theurgist | Vampire | Hybrid |






The choices you make when you begin playing RWK can influence how easy, or hard, it is for you to develop your character. Choosing the right race and class combination, then putting your points into the correct stats, can mean the difference between a character that is powerful and one that is weak. One piece of advice for all players is to equip one light weapon or rapid spell, the choice varies by class, to reduce your delay between actions. You will level and skill much faster this way. Archers without an Attacker�s Balance will suffer a significant drop in Cnc using any spells but for routine leveling, skilling or collecting drops the increased speed is worth more than the reduction in your damage.


Classes

The classes to choose from are vampire, spell caster (2 types), fighter (2 types), monk, archer and theurgist. There are some variations in names as you develop but these are your basic choices. Vampires kill using damage relics, spell casters use damage spells, fighters use weapons, monks use their fists, archers use a bow and arrow, and theurgists cast without spells (that seems counter intuitive but it�s how the class is programmed). Each class can perform well if developed properly. If you want to become very powerful you need to spend a lot of money, in the thousands of dollars, but the basic needs for any class are the same whether it is a free account with no purchased upgrades or one with $5,000 worth of items on the character.


Statistics

All classes and races start with the same 140 total stat points per training. These are split between 70 points fixed by your race, and 70 you can choose to put into any stat you like. We train 100 times per level (without any items to speed the process) and the total number of stat points will always be 14,000 x your level (you have to estimate partial levels using the purple exp bar). This is true whether you are leveling or have reached the level cap and shifted. I�ll repeat this, you never have more than 14,000 total points per level !! For every character in the game, if you total all the points in all stats and divide by level it will give 14,000 as the result. You can double the number of points you get per training with a race upgrade to Templar , getting a Quickening , and when equipping an Apex relic or Devil�s Accelerated Deliverance relic. Each of these items can be used with the others and each doubles your points per training. With all 4 you will get 16x the base points per training, so you only need to train 6.25 times per level. At level 10 you start collecting girlfriend points and with Transcend you can double your leveling again, for a grand total of 32x base with all the toys. For all classes except vampires, there are two stats you need to develop. One determines your accuracy, whether you can successfully hit your target when you attack, and another that determines the amount of damage you can deliver. You want enough accuracy to reliably hit the target you wish to kill, then everything else should be in your damage stat. Fully developed characters have a modified damage stat that is much higher than their accuracy. You can find the stats for normal creatures by looking at the �Kingdom� menu, �Guard�. The price of a critter as a guard equals all its stats, including its dur, and is also the base value of all its drops. So, a Yoro costs 1 bil to buy as a guard, all its stats are 1 bil, and all its drops have a base value of 1 bil gold. Beasts, and creatures in special kingdoms such as the Puddle, Pub, Keeps, etc., can also have defensive skills and voids / devoids so you need more accuracy and a lot more damage stat to kill them.


Stat Use

The key to doing well is focus , keeping as many points as possible in the stats your class actually needs for delivering damage. If you reach the level cap you can then transfer your points between stats, which allows you to really focus where it counts. The level caps are 1,000 without a Sanction of Surpassment (SoS), and 1,710 with an SoS as of June, 2006. The SoS level cap increases by 10 each month. A transferred character is one that has reached the level cap and completed transferring his or her points into the 1 or 2 stats that count. Skills are also important for developing your character but that is explained in the �Skills� section of the help site.


Concentrating your available points in as few stats as possible maximizes your accuracy and damage, so trying to combine more than one class into a single character is not a good strategy. Some players will try to simultaneously play as a fighter/vamp or theurgist/vamp/archer by spreading points in more than 2 stats and equipping an odd combination of equipment. You end up being ineffective in several areas instead of good in one. Fighters can easily change between berserker and armsman and stay effective, as can casters between magician and wizard. Those transitions only involve adding or dropping one spell or weapon. Theurgs and vampires can be fairly good at switching between those roles since theurgs are almost all dur anyway. It�s mostly a question of relics. Theurgs who try to double as archers will find they don�t have the Dex to hit anything with much Agi, or if they build up Dex they will not have enough dur to deliver a lot of casting damage.


Accuracy Stats .

Accuracy means hitting your target reliably, something that affects all classes except Vampires. Archers, Berserkers and Armsmen use Dex as their accuracy stat, Theurgists, Magicians and Wizards use Cnc, Monks use Agi. Whether you hit or miss your enemy (casters will fizzle) depends on how your modified accuracy stat compares to your enemy�s evasion stat. Evasion from Archers, Fighters and Monks is Agi, while evasion from Casters is Cnt. You want more accuracy than your enemy has in evasion and a general rule of thumb is to have 2x or more the accuracy of your enemy�s evasion. So, a fighter would want the ratio of his Dex to enemy Agi to be 2 or more, a caster his Cnc to enemy Cnt to be 2 or more. As mentioned above, you can see the stats for any normal critter by using the �Kingdom� menu. Expand it to �Guard� then find the creature you wish to attack on the fight list. The number next to the enemy is how much it will cost (in gold) to buy as a guard and it is also the value for each of that critter�s stats (they are all the same) as well as the base value of all its drops. You will see that a Magician�s apprentice costs 10 gold, so each of its stats is also 10 and all the items it will drop have a base value of 10 gold. Yoros cost 1 bil gold to buy as guards, so each stat is also 1 bil. To reliably fight a Yoro you would like your modified accuracy stat (type /st to see your modded stats) to be 2 billion or higher, thus giving you an accuracy/evasion ratio of 2 or more. You can hit enemies with less accuracy, but you will miss more as the ratio goes lower. If you miss too much against a particular critter you can improve your accuracy by either increasing your modified accuracy stat with relics, items or skills, or lowering enemy evasion stat using relics ( Dawdle and Filched Feet to lower Agi, Mind Pierce and Purloinment to lower Cnt), or fight a creature higher on the �New Fight� list so your enemy will have less evasion. All of these will increase your accuracy/evasion ratio so you miss less often.



Damage

The damage you can deliver usually depends on at least two factors and often on more. The quality of your weapon or spell is one component. Better quality, meaning higher base value, means more damage. A weapon sold by the store for 1 billion gold (a Rustless Lifeceaser) will do more damage than one costing 10 gold (a Rusty dagger) all other factors being equal. The same is true for spells, monk�s fists, and archer�s bows. How much more damage will depend on your modified damage stat value. As your damage stat increased the damage you can deliver with a given weapon or spell will also increase. The same Rustless Lifeceaser used by a character with 2 billion modified Str will do more damage than in the hands of a character with 1 bil modded Str. Skills can also factor into damage (this is especially true for Monks ) and certain items will boost the overall damage above what stats can do, such as Demonic and better items for Theurgists . There are also quest drops for every class that will boost damage. Taken together all these factors can be used to give a huge overall damage boost.


The number of hits you can make per attack can also vary by class, equipment, and quest status. Armsmen and Magicians get one hit per attack because they only equip one weapon/damage spell. Berserkers and Wizards equip two weapons/damage spells and so can do damage twice per attack. Monks fight with both hands and so get double attack. Archers begin with one attack but can get quest items giving them a chance for a second while Theurgists accomplish the same thing if they use Demonic or better equipment. Spells, weapons, bows, fists and theurgal casting are capped at 999,999,999 per hit (usually abbreviated as 9x9). With two attacks it�s possible to do this damage twice, usually expressed as 2x9x9 meaning just a hair short of 2 bil damage overall. Relic damage isn�t capped at 9x9 so Vamps can potentially deliver 2 bil damage in a single attack, although it requires a lot of pricy items to do that much damage to beasts (the only enemies with 2 bil dur).


Choosing A Race

For all the available classes in the game you only need to consider a few races . Each class has a best race to choose, but not every race is the best for any class. You only need to consider between Ogre, Mesmer, Solon, and Balace to pick the optimum race for any class. Your best approach is to first decide which class you want to play, then pick the best race for that class, then put all your points into the stats needed by that class. Templar is a race upgrade you can either purchase from Glitchless for $50 or obtain in game using 50,000 ash . Templars have the same even stat distribution as Humans but they get twice as many points each time they train, so they level twice as fast as non-Templars. You can also change your race once you become a Templar to get a different stat distribution while still leveling twice as fast as a non-Templar.



Leveling

Many players seem to forget that the whole reason to play RWK is for entertainment, it�s actually supposed to be fun. A method I�d recommend for characters moving up the level ladder is to set attainable goals you can reach in a short time and then raise them as they are completed. For example, if you only focus on killing Yoros in one hit it will take you quite a while to reach that objective through normal play and you will likely get bored long before you get there. Pick a critter several levels above what you can currently kill, work on your accuracy until you can hit it most of the time, then your damage until you can usually kill it in one attack. Objective completed, pick a harder critter to work towards and repeat the process. You get some satisfaction in reaching goals, you build your character, and you may have some fun in the process.


Master of Class Status

If you play long enough to get certain levels, stat points and/or quest items your class status will change to Master of something. The benefits are some resists for certain classes and the ability to obtain some quest items only available to master of class players. The exact criteria vary with the different classes and I�m not certain about all of them. Fighters, Casters and Vamps are based on stat ratio so you need to have a percentage of your total base (unmodified) point in the stat or stats used by your class. Monks seem to be based on quest items , with master status achieved when they get Fists of Flames . Archers are based on quest items with an SoS plus one stat training after reaching level 1,000 being enough for Master (courtesy of Obsidian Night) and probably a Archer�s Pouch of Secrecy (APoS) to be a Grandmaster. Theurgist�s seem to become Masters of the Arcane with a certain % of their base stat points in Dur but I don�t know how much is required. There are additional levels you can reach with some harder to find quest items, like a Blood Tyrant becoming a Bloodletter once he gets Fangs of the Bloodletter or Masters of the Arcane becoming Theurgal Divinators when they get a Theurgal Orb . If you develop your character properly you should fall into master status along the way even if you don�t set that as a specific target.


If someone has a better understanding of class status than what I have here I'd appreciate being updated on what parts are wrong so they can be corrected, with attribution to whoever provided the correct information. Quests can't be undone and repeated to get better data, and with just two SoS characters I can only personally investigate a limited number of character classes.


Now for some specific advice by player class:



## Vampires

Vampires
use damage relics to kill and they are unusual in needing points in only one stat, Dur. Most damage relics are Dur based, so the other stats don�t come into play. Their stats and skills are:



- Damage Stat = Durability (Dur)


- Accuracy Stat = None


- Damage Skill = Criticality


- Accuracy Skill = None


- Regeneration adds to Dur when the skill is 100 and higher


The best race for a Vampire is Ogre . They receive a total of 20 points of Dur per training, more than any other race. You would put all of your 70 elective points per training into Dur. Vamps are the simplest race to develop since they only use one stat and can be entirely focused on getting equipment and relics to boost that stat alone.


Modified Dur along with your relics determines your damage. Vampires only

Equipment

Shadow Scale equipment helps all classes but is expensive and not something a new player can afford unless he�s a twink of someone with a lot already invested in the game. Items of interest to Vamps are all Dur armors, preferably Dur Enlightened Everbearing (DEE) or higher with its 21% Dur boost per piece for body armor, and 2 Dur Imbued Perfection (DIP) shields (130% Dur boost). With DIP armor only the shields give the big 130% boost , the other pieces (helm, gaunts, mantle, sleeves, leggings and boots) only give the standard 15% dur boost, same as you get with the cheapest dur armor Durability Dirty Rag (DDR) which is just 20 gold from the store. Demonic (DDLD), Earthen , Crystal and Shadow armors really give a huge boost to Vamp damage but Earthen and Crystal are only available from beast drops or upgrades using a lot of ash and all four are more expensive than the usual new player will be able to afford. For spells you want at least one Rapid spell for the speed boost, many vamps use two for simplicity even though it doesn�t add more speed. Below Demonic spells your best choice is Rapid Body Singe Durability (RBSD) for the 2x dur boost. The corresponding heal spell is Goshimhappy.


Relics

Vamps are also always interested in better damage relics . Relatively cheap damage relics include Touch of the Damned (TotD), Blood Pinch (BP), The Calling of Lestat (Lestat), Blood Bath (BB), Vampiric Leech (VL), Death Spike (DS), Denial of the Believer (DotB), Encephalon Decay (ED), Vogar's Vice (VV) and Putrefaction (Put). Of all the player craftable relics, some combination of Denial of the Believer (DotB) and Blood Pinch (BP) will probably work best. I have a page showing relic damage as a function of modified dur and combinations of DotB and BP relics. As you increase Dur the advantage of using BP becomes greater. In the special kingdoms like the Pub and Puddle you will find BP more helpful than DotB at even lower modified Dur levels. Super relics like Vampire's Scorn (VS) and it�s upgraded version Vampire's Amaranthine Scorn (VAS) are better since they boost Dur by 20 and 25% each, respectively, and include BP in them but they�re expensive (think trillions of gold or around $50 each). Devil�s Deliverance (DD) and it�s various upgrades ( DDD , D*D relics) are great but those are very, very expensive. VS and DD can potentially drop randomly but are very rare.


Class Upgrades

You become a Blood Tyrant when 67% of your total base (unmodified) stat points are in Dur. It is not possible to get to this point while still leveling, although Ogres can come close with 64% of their going into Dur. You will need to reach the level cap (1,000 without a Sanction of Surpassment (SoS), or 3120 with an SoS as of July 2018) then transfer points from other stats into Dur bring it up to 67% of your base total. Vampires with Aqualungs can do the Vamp Horns quest to increase their modded Dur and thus their relic damage. Beast damage is significantly increased for Blood Tyrants by getting Fangs for 999 runes.


Vamps who have a Skill Potencifier can get an additional Dur boost by training Regeneration skill above 99. A skill of 125 adds another 26% to your modded dur.
Bloodletter and Blood Tyrant have an innate voidance resistance of 20%.




## Fighters


Fighters equip one or two weapons (One weapon makes you a Armsman, two weapons makes you an Berserker) , their stats and skills are:



- Damage Stat = Strength (Str)


- Accuracy Stat = Dexterity (Dex)


- Damage Skill = Attacking Precision


- Accuracy Skill = Attacking Accuracy (boosts Dex)


The best race for a fighter is Ogre . They only receive 7 points of Dex per level, but get 25 Str, which you will appreciate at higher levels. You would put more of your 70 elective points per training into Dex than Str while leveling, then go with all Str for damage once your Dex is high enough to hit reliably with your weapons.


Weapon quality (read that as base price) along with your modified Str determines your damage.


The difference between an Armsman (one weapon) and a Berserker (two weapons) is that Armsmen get a 2x weapon damage boost (doubles your modified Str). You get this bonus even if you equip a shield instead of a second weapon so you can equip 16 relics (10 on items and 6 pure) and still get the damage bonus. Armsmen can also do the Coif quests to add a Dex bonus to their inherent damage boost, which seems to give them an edge over Berserkers. You can easily experiment with the difference between Berserker and Armsman by simply changing between equipping two weapons and one weapon with shield and see which configuration works best for you. Do the Coif quests when you can manage the kills if you have any interest in getting the most from being an Armsman.


Equipment

Shadow Scale equipment helps all classes but is expensive and not something a new player can afford unless he�s a twink of someone with a lot already invested in the game. Items of interest to fighters are Enlightened Everbearing (EE) or higher armor because each piece will boost Str by 20% so they increase weapon damage. The Dur version of this armor ( DEE ) is preferred for the 21% Durability it provides along with 15% more armor defense, but it is nearly twice as expensive as plain EE. Vivacity Arbitration (VA) or higher damage spells (preferably the rapid version), and Swords of Heavenly Ascension (SoHA) weapons. Each of these items also provides a 20% boost to your Str, along with 5% more Dur. Heavy weapons do twice the damage of plain weapons, while Precise weapons will double your Dex, and Light weapons will double your attack speed. I recommend using Heavy and/or Precise weapons and getting your speed boost from a rapid damage spell. Any cheap rapid spell will give you all the speed you can get from an item, while your damage will increase if you use a more expensive weapon. Put your money where it does the most good to your class.


Relics

On the low end, relics for fighters include Archer�s Eye (AE) and Dexterous Hoist (DH) for Dex and Strong Arm (SA) for Str. You can also equip Dawdle to lower enemy Agi, which has the same effect on accuracy as increasing your own Dex (so you don�t miss your hits). These relics are relatively cheap but they also get annulled in locations like the Pub, Puddle, Keeps, etc. Denial of the Believer (DotB) only boosts each stat by 20%, but it boosts all of them except Dur, includes Damage Capacitor for more damage, can�t be annulled, and adds some relic damage. DotB is the best general purpose craftable relic. Shirt Ripper (SR) is a beast drop relic that doubles Str and can be helpful when you have enough Dex to use a relic that only boosts your damage stat. It is not annullable. The super relic Melee�s Rage (MR) or its upgraded version Melee's Undying Rage (MUR) are better since they boost both Dex and Str but they�re expensive (think trillions of gold or around $50 each). Devil�s Deliverance (DD) and it�s various upgrades ( DDD , D*D relics) are great but those are very, very expensive. MR and DD can potentially drop randomly but are very rare.


Class Upgrades
You become a Master Armsman or Master Berserker when 75% of your total base (unmodified) stat points are in a combination of Dex and Str. It is not possible to get to this point while still leveling. You will need to reach the level cap (1,000 without a Sanction of Surpassment (SoS), or 1680 with an SoS as of 19 Mar 2006) then transfer points from other stats into Dex and Str to bring those up to 75% of your base total. Master Berserkers and Armsmen can get a Warrior�s Companion , Warrior�s Warder , and Warrior�s Squire which are quest items that boost Dex and Str dramatically. The enemies you need to kill for these are tough so you will need to deliver a lot of damage to complete those quests.



## Theurgists

Theurgists are to Wizards as Monks are to Berserkers, they cast with no spells equipped. Theurg damage can be very high and highly variable. One way to improve both damage and consistency
is to equip Theurgal Conquest relics. Their stats and skills are:



- Damage Stat = Durability (Dur)


- Accuracy Stat = Concentration (Cnc)


- Damage Skill = Casting Precision


- Accuracy Skill = Casting Accuracy (boosts Cnc)


- Regeneration adds to Dur when the skill is 100 and higher


The best race for a Theurgist is Ogre . They only get 3 points of Cnc each time they train, but they also receive 20 Dur which is critical for Theurgist damage. You would probably put most of your 70 elective points per training into Cnc while leveling, at least until your casting became reliable, then switch to Dur. Theurgists get a 100x Cnc bonus from not equipping any spells, so Cnc is relatively easy to develop, while high Dur is harder to find. If you find an Attacker�s Balance you can equip a heal spell and still play as a Theurgist. Using Goshimhappy (2.2 bil heal) will double your dur, and gives you way to equip another relic.


Modified Dur along with your relics determines your damage. Theurgists begin with only one cast per attack but can get a chance for a second cast by equipping Demonic (and higher) quality items. Each piece of Demonic (and better) equipment provides a 10% chance for a second attack, so with a full set of 9 you get an overall 61% chance for two casts per attack. Demonic and better items also boost theurgist casting damage by 70% per item. With 9 pieces you get an overall damage boost of 119 times more damage!


Equipment

Shadow Scale equipment helps all classes but is expensive and not something a new player can afford unless he�s a twink of someone with a lot already invested in the game. Items of interest to Theurgs are all Dur armors, preferably Dur Enlightened Everbearing (DEE) or higher with its 21% Dur boost per piece for body armor, and a Dur Imbued Perfection (DIP) shield (130% Dur boost). With DIP armor only the shields give the big 130% boost , the other pieces (helm, gaunts, mantle, sleeves, leggings and boots) only give the standard 15% dur boost, same as you get with the cheapest dur armor Durability Dirty Rag (DDR) which is just 20 gold from the store. Demonic (DDLD), Earthen , Crystal and Shadow armors really give a huge boost to Theurg damage but Earthen and Crystal are only available from beast drops or upgrades using a lot of ash and all four are more expensive than the usual new player will be able to afford. Theurgs can�t equip damage spells so your speed boost will need to come from a light weapon. Below Demonic items, using a Light Double Axe Of Durability (DaoD) will give you a 2x dur boost, and as mentioned above Goshimhappy is the godly heal spell giving the same 2x dur boost.


Relics

Cheap relics for theurgs are Mental Focus (MF) and Stream of Conscious (SoC) for Cnc. These relics are relatively cheap but they also get annulled in locations like the Pub, Puddle, Keeps, etc. Denial of the Believer (DotB) only boosts each stat by 20%, but it boosts all of them except Dur, includes Damage Capacitor for more damage, can�t be annulled, and adds some relic damage. DotB is the best general purpose craftable relic. The best craftable relics specifically for Theurgs is Theurgal Conquest (TC) which boosts Theurg casting damage by a factor of 6.25x. See the relics section for details. Super relics like Vampire's Scorn (VS) and it�s upgraded version Vampire's Amaranthine Scorn (VAS) are better since they boost Dur by 20 and 25% each, respectively, but they�re expensive (think trillions of gold or around $50 each). Devil�s Deliverance (DD) and it�s various upgrades ( DDD , D*D relics) are great but those are very, very expensive. VS and DD can potentially drop randomly but are very rare.


Class Upgrades

I haven�t been able to verify myself what it takes to become a Master of the Arcane but according to Aldaract (S1) it involves getting the Theurgal Damnation Staff and having enough stat points in Dur and Cnc. More recently (January 2024) Mav73 checked a level 1000 character as it obtained the Theurg staffs and as soon as the third dropped (Theurgal Damnation Staff) the character showes as a Master of the Arcane. He moved 2 million stat points out of Cnc and Dur and still showed as Master, so it's looking like Master status just requires having the third staff and being equipped as a Theurgist. Thank you Mav73 for sharing that information. Theurgists with Aqualungs can do the staffs quests to increase their casting damage. A Master of the Arcane can get a Theurg Orb to increase casting damage by 4x.


Theurgs who have a Skill Potencifier can get an additional Dur boost by training Regeneration skill above 99. A skill of 125 adds another 26% to your modded dur.



## Archers

Archers equip both a Bow and Arrow, their stats and skills are:



- Damage Stat = Concentration (Cnc)


- Accuracy Stat = Dexterity (Dex)


- Damage Skill = Casting Accuracy (boosts Cnc)


- Accuracy Skill = Attacking Accuracy (boosts Dex)


The best race for an archer is Mesmer . They only get 5 points of Dex each time they train, but they get a whopping 30 points of Cnc, which translates to damage. You would put most of your 70 elective points per training into Dex while leveling but once you have enough Dex to hit your enemies, you will want to put all your points into Cnc for maximum damage.


Archers get a 5x Cnc and 5x Dex Bonus. If you do not equip any spells, you also get the 100x Cnc bonus of a Theurgist.


Bow quality (read that as base price) along with your modified Cnc determines damage, while arrow quality controls attacking speed. Some data on how arrow quality relates to action delay is shown here .


Archers begin with only one hit per attack, which makes sense with a single arrow to equip. They can get quest items to give them a chance for two attacks each turn. An Enhanced Nock gives archers a 25% chance of having 2 attacks, a Bounteous Nock gives a 50% chance for two attacks and an Archer�s Pouch of Secrecy (APoS) gives a 75% chance of a second hit in one attack.


Overall, in my opinion, archers are a waste of time for surface-bound players. The only advantages to being an archer are the ability to deliver high damage to non-surface beasts and high resists in the acumens. The penalty is being very slow unless the character has completed the Sanction of Serenity quest. Without access to the other planes you can�t kill any difficult beasts and are unlikely to come close to winning an acumen. All you are left with is the huge attack delay which means you level very slowly and get fewer drops than any other class. If you still want to be an archer, do not equip your bow and arrow while leveling or focusing on skills, you want to be fast in both those situations. Just equip some damage relics and use a light weapon or rapid spell. Save the archery equipment for those occasions when you need high damage and not higher speed. On the other hand, players with Crystal or Shadow Scale arrows and the SoSe quest will be as fast as or faster than other classes and likely have no disadvantage from equipping a bow and arrow all the time.


Equipment

Although Shadow Scale equipment helps all classes, no armor or weapons give a boost to either Dex or Cnc, so relics are an Archer�s best friend. Armor for Archers is mainly to reduce damage received in battle and to equip more relics. High level archers who can kill a Golem of Living Rock (Beast) can get an Archer�s Aid (AA), which is a great help. With an AA you can equip spells without your Cnc being reduced, so you can equip two accurate damage spells for the 2d Cnc boost each gives, or use an accurate spell for Cnc and a rapid spell for speed.


Relics

At low levels and against enemies in normal kingdoms you can get by using Archer�s Eye (AE) or Dexterous Hoist (DH) for Dex and Mental Focus (MF) or Stream of Conscious (SoC) for Cnc. You can also equip Dawdle to lower enemy Agi, which has the same effect on accuracy as increasing your own Dex (so you don�t miss your hits). The problems with these relics is that they only boost a single stat and they are annulled, so in the Pub, Puddle, Keeps, and most other special kingdoms they are useless (the enemies there will annul them so they don�t fire). Denial of the Believer (DotB) only boosts each stat by 20%, but it boosts all of them except Dur, includes Damage Capacitor for more damage, can�t be annulled, and adds some relic damage. DotB is the best general purpose craftable relic. Super relics like Melee's Rage (MR) and Caster's Revenge (CR) or their upgraded versions are better but much more expensive (think trillions of gold or around $50 each). Devil�s Deliverance (DD) and it�s various upgrades ( DDD , D*D relics) are great but those are very, very expensive. MR, CR and DD can potentially drop randomly but are very rare.


Class Upgrades

It looks like all you need to become a Master Bowman (courtesy of Obsidian Night) is to equip any bow and arrow, have a Sanction of Surpassment , and train once after reaching level 1,000. Stat distribution does not seem to be a factor. Grandmaster Bowman requires getting an Archer�s Pouch of Secrecy (confirmed by Servant of Spike on S1). Stats also don't seem to factor into Grandmaster status. A Master or Grandmaster Bowman can quest for an Archer�s Aide which increases Cnc by 500x and raises the chance for a double attack to 90% and allows him to equip spells without suffering a Cnc loss. The 9/10/244 announcement also mentions that �The Archer's Aide will drop for Archer Theurgists, however, it will only work for Master and Grandmaster Bowmen.�



## Casters

Casters equip one or two spells (One spell makes you a Magician, two spells makes you a Wizard), their stats and skills are:



- Damage Stat = Intelligence (Ntl)


- Accuracy Stat = Concentration (Cnc)


- Damage Skill = Casting Precision


- Accuracy Skill = Casting Accuracy (boosts Cnc)


The best race for an caster is Solon . They receive15 points of Cnc and 25 points of Ntl each time they train. You can alternate putting your 70 elective points per training into both stats while leveling since the Solon is pretty well balanced by nature. At higher levels you will want to go all Ntl for the damage.


Damage spell quality (read that as base price)
along with your modified Ntl determines your damage.


The difference between an Magician (one damage spell) and a Wizard (two damage spells) is that Magicians get a 2x spellcasting damage boost (doubles your modified Ntl). You get this bonus even if you equip a heal spell instead of a second damage spell so you can equip 16 relics (10 on items and 6 pure) and still get the damage bonus. Magicians can also do the Cloak quests to add a Cnc bonus to their inherent damage boost, which seems to give them an edge over Wizards. You can easily experiment with the difference between Magician and Wizard by simply changing between equipping two damage spells and one damage, one heal spell to see which configuration works best for you. Do the Cloak quests when you can manage the kills if you have any interest in getting the most from being an Magician.


Equipment

Shadow Scale equipment helps all classes but is expensive and not something a new player can afford unless he�s a twink of someone with a lot already invested in the game. Items of interest to casters are Enlightened Everbearing (EE) or higher armor because each piece will boost Ntl by 20% so they increase casting damage. The Dur version of this armor ( DEE ) is preferred for the 21% Durability it provides along with 15% more armor defense, but it is nearly twice as expensive as plain EE. Vivacity Arbitration (VA) or higher damage spells, and Swords of Heavenly Ascension (SoHA) weapons (preferably the light version). Each of these items also provides a 20% boost to your Ntl, along with 5% more Dur. Major spells do twice the damage of plain damage spells, while Accurate spells will double your Cnc, and Rapid spells will double your attack speed. I recommend using Major and/or Accurate damage spells and getting your speed boost from a light weapon. Any cheap light weapon will give you all the speed you can get from an item, while your damage will increase if you use a more expensive spell. Put your money where it does the most good to your class.


Relics

On the low end, relics for casters include Mental Focus (MF) and Stream of Conscious (SoC) for Cnc and Merlin's Insight (MI) for Ntl. You can also use Mind Pierce to lower enemy Cnt, which helps your accuracy (so you do not fizzle) the same as raising your own Cnc. These relics are relatively cheap but they also get annulled in locations like the Pub, Puddle, Keeps, etc. Denial of the Believer (DotB) only boosts each stat by 20%, but it boosts all of them except Dur, includes Damage Capacitor for more damage, can�t be annulled, and adds some relic damage. DotB is the best general purpose craftable relic. Brain Swell (BS) is a beast drop relic that doubles Ntl and can be helpful when you have enough Cnc to use a relic that only boosts your damage stat. It is not annullable. The super relic Caster's Revenge (CR) or its upgraded version Caster's Eternal Revenge (CER) are better since they boost both Cnc and Ntl, but they�re expensive (think trillions of gold or around $50 each). Devil�s Deliverance (DD) and it�s various upgrades ( DDD , D*D relics) are great but those are very, very expensive. CR and DD can potentially drop randomly but are very rare.


Class Upgrades

You become a Master Magician or Master Wizard when 75% of your total base (unmodified) stat points are in a combination of Cnc and Ntl. It is possible to get to this point while still leveling if you chose Mesmer or Solon for your race. Other races will need to reach the level cap (1,000 without a Sanction of Surpassment (SoS), 1680 with an SoS as of 19 Mar 2006) then transfer points from other stats into Cnc and Ntl to bring those up to 75% of your base total. Master Wizards and Magicians can get a Caster�s Minion and Caster�s Familiar , quest items that boost Cnc and Ntl dramatically. The enemies you need to kill for these are tough so you will need to deliver a lot of damage to complete those quests.



## Monks

Monks fight with empty hands, they do not equip any weapons, bows, or shields. Oddly though monks who complete the quest for a Friar's Staff of Preeminence CAN equip arrows and still be recognized as monks by the server. While the arrows are not used as weapons and so in themselves will not cause any damage they do allow monks to equip a full compliment of 16 items, and 16 relics when you add 2 enchanted arrows to your kit. Plus, any stat boosts from the arrow itself, like higher Dur or mez resistance when using Demo and better items, will be applied. Without using arrows monks can only equip 14 relics in total (6 pure and 8 on armor and spells), which applies to any monk without the Friar's Staff. Monks rely primarily on their Monk Discipline skill for damage, their stats and skills are:



- Damage Stat = Contravention (Cnt) [note: this is Cnt, NOT Cnc]



- Accuracy Stat = Agility (Agi)


- Primary Damage Skill = Monk Discipline


- Secondary Damage Skill = Attacking Precision


- Accuracy Skill = Attacking Accuracy (boosts Agi)


The best race for a monk is Balace , it is the only sensible choice. They receive a total of 20 points each of Agi and Cnt when they train. Only 40 points doesn�t seem like much but no other race gets even that much combined Agi and Cnt. You could alternate putting your 70 elective points per training into each stat while leveling, then go with all Cnt once you have enough Agi for good accuracy.


Monk Discipline (MD) level, along with your modified Cnt determines your damage. Monks fight with both hands so they can hit two times per attack.


Equipment

Shadow Scale equipment helps all classes but is expensive and not something a new player can afford unless he�s a twink of someone with a lot already invested in the game. Items of interest to Monks are Revived Organismal (RO) armor (50% Agi boost per piece), Spirit Bind (SB) damage spells (50% Agi boost each), Clarified Creation armor (CC) (50% Cnt boost per piece) and Eternal Yearning (EY) damage spells (50% Cnt boost each). The Dur versions of those armors are preferred for the extra 15% Dur and armor defense but they are nearly twice as expensive, while the Rapid versions of the spells should be used for the extra speed. If gold is tight you can do as well with one rapid and one normal spell.


Relics

On the low end, relics for monks include Cat�s Feet (CF) for Agi and Impediment (Imped) for Cnt. You can also equip Dawdle to lower enemy Agi, which has the same effect on accuracy as increasing your own Agi (so you don�t miss your hits). These relics are relatively cheap but they also get annulled in locations like the Pub, Puddle, Keeps, etc. Denial of the Believer (DotB) only boosts each stat by 20%, but it boosts all of them except Dur, includes Damage Capacitor for more damage, can�t be annulled, and adds some relic damage. DotB is the best general purpose craftable relic. Monks don�t have any relics like Brain Swell, Shirt Ripper or super relics to help their stats. Monasticism will increase Monk Discipline by 5 points each to increase your damage. Monasticism can stack with your raw MD skill (type /raw in game) to get your overall MD above 70, but the modifiers from the monk fists quests won�t stack with raw and Monasticism above an MD of 70. If that confuses you don�t worry about it, monks want to train their MD skill as high as possible and ultimately want the raw skill to be 99 without an SP or 125 with one so just keep training it. Devil�s Deliverance (DD) and it�s various upgrades ( DDD , D*D relics) are great but those are very, very expensive. DD can potentially drop randomly but are very rare.


Class Upgrades

You become a Master of Fists when you get the Fists of Flames quest item in Hell (thanks to Etryginn on S2 for verifying that). Even with MD at 50 he didn�t move from Novice Monk to Master of Fists until he picked up the Flames, and Blevi on S2 immediately went from hybrid to Master of Fists when the Flames dropped.


Master of Fists have an innate mesmerization resistance of 20%.



## Hybrids

You show as being a hybrid when the server can�t place you into one of the other classes based on your stats and equipment, you�ve sort of fallen through the cracks. If your stats and equipment are actually configured to be one of the other classes, the label may just be a fluke of the server�s naming rules and won�t have much impact on your effectiveness in the game. If you are there because you have intentionally mixed your stats and gear to serve several different class needs, I think you should reevaluate your development plan. If you still want to play as a Hybrid, I can�t offer any useful advice on how best to proceed, all I can do is wish you good luck. I think you�ll need it.



## Base Stat Points By Race

## These are the fixed points a character gains each training. We also direct an additional 70 points to a single stat each training.


Race | Str | Dex | Agi | Dur | Ntl | Cnc | Cnt |

Human | 10 | 10 | 10 | 10 | 10 | 10 | 10

Dwarf | 12 | 10 | 10 | 13 | 14 | 3 | 8

Drow | 10 | 15 | 12 | 7 | 12 | 9 | 5

Sylvian | 7 | 15 | 20 | 7 | 12 | 4 | 5

Galatai | 14 | 12 | 12 | 12 | 4 | 11 | 5

Troll | 18 | 10 | 10 | 15 | 9 | 4 | 4

Ogre | 25 | 7 | 5 | 20 | 3 | 3 | 7

Goblin | 5 | 7 | 13 | 7 | 15 | 10 | 13

Gnome | 3 | 7 | 10 | 9 | 17 | 15 | 9

Solon | 4 | 6 | 5 | 5 | 25 | 15 | 10

Mesmer | 4 | 5 | 6 | 5 | 10 | 30 | 10

Balace | 4 | 4 | 20 | 14 | 4 | 4 | 20

Templar | 10 | 10 | 10 | 10 | 10 | 10 | 10




Innate Class Resists (These stack with all quest items and relics) 



The Master Bowman class now receives an additional 10% resistance to voidance.

The Grandmaster Bowman class now recieves an additional 20% resistance to voidance.

The Armsman class now recieves an additional 10% resistance to voidance.

The Master of Arms class now recieves an additional 20% resistance to voidance.

The Berserker class now receives an additional 5% resistance to voidance.

The Master Berserker class now receives an additional 10% resistance to voidance.

The Magician class now recieves an additional 5% resistance to voidance.

The Master Magician class now recieves an additional 10% resistance to voidance.

The Wizard class now receives an additional 3% resistance to voidance.

The Master Wizard class now receives an additional 5% resistance to voidance.

The Blood Tyrant and Bloodletter class now receives an additional 20% resistance to voidance.



The Master of Fists class now receives an additional 20% resistance to mesmerization.

The Armsman class now recieves an additional 10% resistance to mesmerization.

The Master of Arms class now recieves an additional 20% resistance to mesmerization.

The Berserker class now receives an additional 5% resistance to mesmerization.

The Master Berserker class now receives an additional 10% resistance to mesmerization.

The Magician class now recieves an additional 5% resistance to mesmerization.

The Master Magician class now recieves an additional 10% resistance to mesmerization.

The Wizard class now receives an additional 3% resistance to mesmerization.

The Master Wizard class now receives an additional 5% resistance to mesmerization.

* The only way to get your fists to a power greater than a Mace of Resurrection is through pure skill training, special quest items lack the power.



When in Sky, you get DOUBLE the amount of experience. Which means you level twice as fast as on the surface.



When in Dungeon, you get DOUBLE the amount of gold per creature you kill.



When in Heaven, you get TRIPLE the amount of gold per creature you kill.



When in Hell, you get TRIPLE the amount of experience.


This page last updated 09 January 2024
