Welcome to Race War Kingdoms, which you will always see referred to in the game as RWK. It�s a typical multiplayer role-playing game where participants compete to become wealthy and powerful so they can dominate everyone else. In many ways it�s a lot like real life, except you get to revive when you die and the money you lose is only virtual gold. You work to gain levels so you can kill harder creatures and possibly earn enough gold to get all the best toys.



You can access the official RWK help site by typing /help while in the game. Other useful in-game commands are /info , /faq and by clicking on the word "actions" on your screen to open the actions page . You can also type /guide to send out a global request for another player to act as a guide and help you get started. You can also try sending me a pm in the game for help if I�m on at the time, just type "/m Jimcav: I have a question" in the chat line. If I don�t reply it means I�m not logged into the game at that time, but I will see your message when I do log in and I will reply.



Interaction with other players doesn�t seriously begin until you reach level 10. Below 10 you cannot accept any trades or gold, settle kingdoms, or trade items to other players for more than 1 gold each. However, even at level 0 you can be given a kingdom by another character of yours who is its king. Both characters need to be playing from the same IP address so it's likely only your own characters who can take advantage of this feature. On the new character in the "Actions" menu, scroll to "Options" then set "Trivial Gold/Kingdom Filtering" to "No Filtering" before the kingdom is offered, unless it is developed enough to be non-trivial (more than 45 billion gold worth of improvements).



This approach of using a higher-level character to give a kingdom to a new character is a way for that new character to get a Death Spike relic to level faster as pointed out to me by Celtic Mercenary on Server 2. Put at least 2 runes into the kingdom before or after the new character is its king. The new character can then use the /na command twice to alch 2 runes into 1,999,999,999 gold. Kill anything to bring the gold to 2 billion and then buy a Death Spike. The new character can now inflict 1 million relic damage and can kill Peddled Parasites (960,000 dur) in one attack to train every 3 kills.



At level 10 you can finally use auction to sell items to others and start making some gold, settle kingdoms, and fully participate in RWK. Getting to level 10 is slow and tedious since you can�t do much to boost your character, but stick with it until you get through that initial slow period.








## General Information




- When you die, click on the word "Revive" to revive and get back to playing.


- There is no experience, gold or item penalty for dying in RWK, all you lose is the time it takes to revive.


- Do not click "All" when you train, it just wastes a lot of stat points. Know what class you want to play and only train the 1 or 2 stats relevant to that class. Clicking "All" is like deciding to paint a room in 1 or 2 colors but buying equal amounts of 7 different ones for the job. It�s wasteful, and if you paint with all 7 colors you�ll have to go back afterwards and spend time painting over the ones you didn�t want. Plan ahead and you will save time and be more effective while leveling.


- The game has no friends list. If you want to remember the names of players either write them down on paper or create a file on your computer and copy the names into it. The way many people behave in RWK an enemies list would probably be more useful.


- You can disable chat using the "Actions" menu, expand to "Options", "Shout Text" and choose "Disable". The same applies to auction and emotes (used for roleplaying).


- You can ignore specific players by either clicking on the name 4 times, then clicking on "Submit" or by typing /ignore Playername (where Playername would be the name you want to ignore). You can view your ignore list by typing /ignore. The list resets when you log out so you may find yourself adding the same people to ignore every time you log in.


- Chat is not moderated and there is no restriction on offensive language. Players are free to curse or make insults about race, gender, religion, nationality or anything else they choose. You can disable chat or ignore specific players as described above.


- Complaining about what players say will usually result in their intentionally making more offensive remarks of the same type to taunt you, so it�s best to not try to control what others post in chat.


- Check the tax in your location, left side a bit above center of the page in the kingdom attributes section. If the tax is set for more than 0% you should move . Tax is the percentage of the gold from your kills that�s put in the kingdom treasury (Trs), which belongs to the king and not you. Don�t give away your gold to another player, you need it yourself. Click on "Map" and use the arrows under the little map that opens on the upper right of your page to move your character. Find a location with 0% tax and play there so you can keep all your gold.





## READ THE RULES


The first thing you should do is read the rules so you don�t break them inadvertently and get your character banned before you even learn the game. You can find two versions of them, one in the game FAQ and the other in the chat section of the official RWK help site. I also collected all the rules I could find and put them on a single page here . The rules are short and simple, and easy to follow. Take the short time to learn what they are and your time in the game will be better for all of us.




## Abusive Players


The Internet is the best thing to happen to bullies since the invention of the school bus. �� RWK is like every other online game I�ve played. It has its share of jerks who seem to only play because it gives them an opportunity to insult, demean and abuse other people without any fear of retribution. If you insult someone in real life you risk getting beaten senseless (assuming you had any sense to begin with) while on the Web there is little recourse for your target to take.



When someone begins to abuse you in chat because you asked a simple question, your best response is to either click on that player�s name 4 times to make the /ignore command come up, or just type /ignore Name (where name is the player�s game handle) and hit submit. This will block any posts by that person until you exit the game, which clears your ignore list. If you try to respond in chat you may get involved in a pointless argument and it�s just a waste of your time. There are pathetic individuals who think that reaching a high level in a pointless game is some sort of great achievement which gives them the right to crap all over newbies. Perhaps is really IS the best thing they�ve done in their lives (sad thought isn�t it?), but your best response is to just ignore them. If the abuse is bad enough to make you want to leave the game, I suggest you take a minute to email abuse@glitchless.com and just mention the player�s name and that his or her behavior has lost Glitchless a potential customer. Odds are nothing will come from it, but it�s worth a try and you have nothing to lose.




## The RWK World


The virtual world of RWK consists of 6 planes stacked one atop the other like plates. Each plane is a grid of 90,000 kingdoms arranged 300 wide (East-West) by 300 high (North-South). The planes are named, from bottom to top, Forge, Hell, Dungeon, Surface, Sky, Heaven. We all start on Surface and to reach other planes you will need some quest items . A Mountain Tamer will get you into Sky and a Lava Splicer will get you to Dungeon. Each of these will require you to either spend $50 (that is 50 US dollars) or save up 50,000 ash . If you get access to both Dun and Sky you can complete a quest for access to both Hell and Heaven. Getting into the Forge, which is the most recent addition, requires completing the quest to obtain a Sanction of Serenity .




## Quests


The game has many quests and I have a separate page that lists them and tries to explain how they are done. Many begin with random items you get from kills, they appear in your inventory under "Others" and those items cannot be sold, traded to other players, or destroyed. Some quests require you to visit specific locations or kill specific creatures, and it�s all in the tables. Many quests require you to leave the surface plane. I have a separate page showing the quests possible with only Surface access and another listing the quests possible with 10-day trial access .



## Class and Race


If you haven�t already created your first character, start by reading the character creation guide . Classes are playing styles, whether you kill using weapons, spells, relics, etc. and each class has one race that�s the best choice. Try to begin with a good combination of race and class, it will help you to get a better combination of stats as you level. Once you reach the maximum level, the level cap, you can begin transferring points from the stats your class does not use into those it does. Once a player has fully transferred his stat points race is really no longer significant.




## Kingdoms


You can�t settle kingdoms (thus becoming a king) until you reach level 10. Building, defending, and waging war with a kingdom (usually referred to in the game simply as KD) is part of the game, but it�s a very expensive activity now. I recommend that new players NOT settle any kingdoms until after they learn the game fairly well. For one thing your kingdom will lose morale if it�s total improvements (armies, fortifications, food) come to a total of less than 10 billion. Below that value you will need to enter the kingdom (referred in the game as walking kingdoms) at least once every 24 hours to keep up the morale. If you wander away for long enough the morale will drop low enough (below 5%) for some to coup the kingdom and take it away from you.



Another factor to consider is that it is very easy for many players with upgrades to destroy kingdoms even if they are strongly defended, and when you lose a kingdom you also lose any gold you spent on improving it. A kingdom you spent 50 billion gold fortifying can be destroyed in a couple of minutes. There are plenty of players who will destroy your kingdom just because they can, in some cases because it amuses them to watch their targets complain about it in chat. Kingship in the current state of RWK is a hobby for the wealthiest players. Join in only if you won�t be upset to lose what you build. I�ve owned kingdoms and lost them so I don�t engage in that aspect of the game anymore and I�m not a good source of advice on how it�s done. You can find some information on it at Goof�s site and Ehlmaris� nuking guide .



## Fighting and Creature Stats


Most of your time in the game will be spent killing creatures in the Actions, New Fight list. The critters at the top of the list are easiest and the ones at the bottom hardest. Except for vampires your fighting ability will depend on two stats, one for accuracy and the other for damage. See the character creation guide for the specifics on class and stats. In general, you need enough accuracy stat to overcome your target�s evasion stat, and enough damage stat to reduce the target�s health enough to kill it. For normal critters you can use the Kingdom menu, Guard menu to see their stats . The price of the creature is also the same value of each of its stats. So, a Magician�s Apprentice costs 10 gold to buy as a guard, it also has 10 Str, 10 Dex, 10 health, etc. and all the items it drops have a base value of 10 gold, so they are things like rusty daggers and dirty rag armor. A Yoro La Hi Hi Samurai costs 1 billion gold as a guard, it has all stats at 1 billion, and it will drop items with a 1 bil base like Rustless Lifeceasers and Timeless Fortitude armor. A rough rule of thumb for choosing what to fight is to have an accuracy stat that�s 2x or more that of your enemy�s evasion. So, a fighter would want 2 mil modified Dex to fight a creature with 1 mil Agi a caster 2 mil Cnc to counter an enemy with 1 mil Cnt. If you can�t hit a particular creature your choices are to increase your accuracy stat , decrease your enemy�s evasion with relics , or fight an easier critter.



If you want to see all the creature names and stats, they are listed on Cyril�s web site along with the gold per kill for each one. The names change as you move through Hell, Dungeon, Surface, Sky and Heaven but the critters are really the same in all normal kingdoms on any plane. In the special kingdoms the creatures are harder to kill. They have defensive skill of 99 so all damage against them is reduced by 25x, plus they have damage relics and annuls to fight back when attacked. Those locations are: Stallions Pub (150,Sur,145) and Le Puddle Deep (140,Sur,190), the Heaven and Hell Keeps (both at 66, 66 on the respective planes), the Sky Shrine (100,Sky,250), Forbidden Palace (162,Hev,159) and Insane Dungeon (100,Dun,200). That last location, the ID, has much harder critters than any other location. I don�t know about other classes� damage, but relic damage is 25 million times less than against normal critters. So if you are just able to kill normal Yoros (1 billion health) you will do all of 40 points of damage in the ID. You will need both Dungeon access (a Lava Splicer , Transcendental Sanction or Trial Access ) and Demonic Pinions to reach the ID.


Do not waste your time killing guards when you are new . For one thing they are much tougher than the same critter in the "New Fight" list and as a newbie even the Magician�s Apprentice guards will probably kick your ass. They also seldom drop any items and when they do the items are crap. Killing a guard is something to do when you have leveled and can deliver enough damage to kill one before it kills you. Even then the only reasons to kill guards are to lower morale enough to coup the kingdom (morale in a KD with no runes drops 4% each time the guard is killed, and you can only kill the guard once per game hour) or to simply annoy the kingdom owner.




## Reaching Level 10 Quickly (Revised 6 Sept 2017)


Here is the short version for those who don�t like to read:



Check the tax in your location, left side a bit above center of the page in the kingdom attributes section. If the tax is set for more than 0% you should move . Click on "Map" and use the arrows under the little map that opens on the upper right of your page to move your character. Find a location with 0% tax and play there so you can keep all your gold.


Celtic Mercenary on Server 2 pointed out that someone starting a mule could reduce the time to reach level 10 using a Sur kingdom. Settle the kingdom with a higher level character you also own, add 2 runes, give the kingdom to the mule. It can then /na the runes to get 2 bil gold and buy a Death Spike to begin leveling as the max rate. This approach eliminates the time required killing low gold and experience critters for cheap relics until damage is high enough to level with the fewest kills.


This approach is only useful if you own another character developed enough to settle a kingdom and provide the runes. Both characters apparently have to be connected from the same IP address. Also, finding a Surface kingdom can take time since the plane is almost completely settled. Still, it's a method that will save time for those who can do it.



Now that Glitchless starts new players with 0 gold, your first challenge is to earn at least 10,000 gold and buy your first relic. You can use your weapon and damage spell to kill weak enemies but it will likely be Magician�s Apprentices at the start and they only pay 5 gold per kill. A much faster way to get started is to spend a few minutes playing another Glitchless game, Dragon Wizard (thanks to Captain Anarchy for that suggestion). It�s not terribly interesting but you don�t need to stay there long. Just play enough to earn a couple hundred gold then let your character die to end the game, don�t waste much time there. Five minutes should be more than enough to get what�s needed. When the game ends you designate the payout to your RWK character ( be sure to set it to the right server ), which will help buy your initial set of relics.


You get 1,000 RWK gold per 1 Dragon Wizard gold so earning 200 gold in Dragon Wizard gets your RWK character 200,000, enough for all the relics and equipment you need to start leveling efficiently. You can only get one payout per day from Dragon Wizard, and I personally would not recommend playing more than once ever per new character. Back in RWK, fight the hardest enemy you can always kill in one attack, don�t waste time on things that either kill you or routinely need several attacks for you to kill. Figure roughly 3.5 to 4 hours of total RWK playing time to reach level 10. If you earn your initial gold by killing enemies in RWK instead of using Dragon Wizard to get started more quickly, figure on adding another 40 minutes or so to reaching level 1.


As for leveling quickly, vampires level a bit faster than any other class, archers are slower than any other class. To level fastest equip as a vampire, at least until you reach level 10 and can then get the benefits of using auction and collecting points for a girlfriend . DO NOT set your skills above 1% during this time, leveling is much more important than any skills you will earn in the first 10 levels. The information above gives you some guidance on how to earn your initial gold so you can buy your first relics. The Durabilty Dirty Rag armor and Rapid Acid Drop damage spell only cost 180 gold in total (9 items at 20 gold each).


1. Start with buying a full set of Durability Dirty Rag armor, including 2 shields, for 20 gold each. Use the Actions menu, Buy, Durability Armor. Equip the armor, using the Actions menu, Equip.


2. Buy a Rapid Acid Drop damage spell, also 20 gold. Actions menu, Buy, Rapid Damage Spells. Equip the spell.

3. Buy 2 relics : Blood Pinch (BP) and Touch of the Damned (ToTD), they cost 10,000 gold each. Actions menu, Buy, Relics. Equip the BP in relic slot 4 and ToTD in relic slot 1. When you can afford more relics (you can equip 6 pure ones) put the ToTD in slots 1, 2, 3 and the BP in slots 4, 5, 6.


4. Start fighting, Actions menu, New Fight. As you move down the list each creature is harder than the ones above it. Find the hardest critter you can always kill in one attack and train on that for some time while you develop your Dur stat. When your damage increases enough, move down the list as your ability allows, always killing in one attack.


5. When you can train a stat, always choose Dur to increase your relic damage.


6. Buy and equip 2 more ToTD and 2 more BP when you can afford them. The second of each will cost 100,000 gold and the third 1 million.


If you find items with �vampire� relics on them while leveling equip them and compare your damage with the drop equipped vs what you had before finding it. Go with the item that gives the most damage. You can find what all relics do here . Direct damage relics commonly found as drops include: TotD and BP mentioned above, The Calling of Cassius (Cassius), The Calling of Lestat (Lestat). Less common are Blood Bath (BB), Vampiric Leech (VL), Death Spike (DS), Encephalon Decay (ED), and Vogar's Vice (VV). It�s possible but very, very unlikely but you can also find a "Super" relic as a drop and the vamp one is Vampire's Scorn (VS). It�s worth about $50 (that is what it would cost to buy directly from Glitchless). The other super relics you likely will never find are Melee's Rage (MR) and Caster's Revenge (CR). Those aren�t directly useful for vamps but their value makes they useful as items to trade for vamp gear.


Follow this procedure and with a decent connection and computer a new character with no twinks can earn a level every 15-30 minutes once you get enough gold to buy your first couple of relics. Good luck and good hunting :)


Long Version of leveling to 10 quickly, with a more detail:


Like many role-playing games, RWK measures progress by level. You need to reach level 1 before you can use shout, where everyone in the game can see your messages. You need to reach level 10 before you can trade items to other players for more than 1 gold each, accept any trades from other players, settle kingdoms, or be given gold. With that in mind, reaching level 10 as quickly as possible should be your first priority . There are probably many methods that work equally well but I find that equipping as a vampire lets me reach level 1 in about 20 minutes and should bring a character to level 10 within 4 hours. Once you reach level 10 you can change your setup to play as any class. If you have a faster method by all means use it.


You used to start with only 50,000 gold to spend. Now you need to make kills or play Dragon Wizard to earn some gold for equipment, so the first level can take around 30 minutes longer than in the past. Figure around 30 minutes to reach level 1 if you jumpstart with Dragon Wizard or 1 hour if you don�t. Once you are equipped and have trained some dur it will take about 20 minutes per level from 2 to 10, so around 3.5 to 4 hours from initial start to reaching level 10. That�s actual playing time, if you chat or engage in other non-leveling activities it will obviously take more time overall to hit level 10. Begin by buying Durability Dirty Rag (DDR) armor for all slots, including two shields. Also buy a Rapid Acid Drop (RAD) damage spell. When you have 10,000 gold buy 1 pure Touch of the Damned (TotD) relic. When you earn a second 10,000 gold get 1 pure Blood Pinch (BP) relic. The Rapid spell will help you make your kills more quickly than if you did not have one equipped, and the Dur armor will increase your relic damage. With TotD and BP more durability means more relic damage .


Equip the armor, shields, and rapid spell in the appropriate slots and put your BP in relic slot 6, the TotD in relic slot 1. This initial setup will cost you 20,180 gold. Begin killing creatures on the "New Fight" list and move down to find the hardest one you can kill in one attack each time, then keep fighting that and put your points into Dur each time you train. After you train a few times, recheck what you can kill in one attack and keep moving down the fight list as you can manage to kill harder critters. You will quickly be training Dur every 3 fights, which is generally the best you can manage on Sur. If the critter you kill generated the max experience you will occasionally roll over enough exp from one training to the next to allow you to train again after only 2 kills, but this won�t happen very often. You will accumulate more gold along the way and can buy another TotD once you have 100,000 gold, then a second BP with another 100k gold. If you want to use 3 of each relic, the last two will cost you 1 mil gold each, so it may take a while.


You will reach level 1 in around 20 minutes and should have 700k to 800k of gold when you do. Keep fighting this way until you hit level 10, and then if you want to play a different class make your changes. An advantage of leveling as a vamp is that your enemy never blocks your attacks and you will never miss . Level while equipped as a different class and you will occasionally have an attack not result in a clean kill, so the total time needed to reach level 10 will be longer. If you happen to find an item with a damage relic attached (like Touch of the Damned, you can see all the relics here ) equip it and test how it affects your damage. Sometimes equipping a non-durability item with a vamp relic on it will give more damage than using a piece of plain durability armor in that slot. The exact result will depend on several factors and it�s easiest to just equip and see what it does for you. Go with whichever piece gives you the most
damage.



If you want to level as a different class you should do nearly as well if you stick with the most important parts of this approach:


1. Equip an item to decrease the delay between actions. Fighters, vamps, and monks use a cheap rapid damage spell (like a Rapid Acid Drop), spell casters and Theurgs equip a cheap light weapon (like a Light Rusty Dagger). If you want to level quickly, just forget about equipping as an archer, you will always be too slow.


2. Equip the relics appropriate for increasing the accuracy and damage of your class .


3. Fight the hardest creature on the "New Fight" list that you can kill in a single attack . If you need more than one attack to kill something, move back to an easier critter. For a new and inexperienced player, more kills per hour will level you faster than fewer kills of harder creatures.


4. Spend your time getting kills and not chatting or crafting. Do those after you reach level 10.


5. Keep your skills at 0% so you will level quickly. Start working on skills after you reach level 10.


Something that Crazy Gizmo (S2) pointed out that before you begin leveling you can spend some time playing Dragon Wizard to earn some gold for their new character. This approach would give you more gold to buy your relics sooner, and you could also get some better quality dur armor to help with killing tougher critters.




## More About Leveling


At this time (September 2022) the highest possible level you can reach is 3,620 and many players are there. The highest level you can reach with only surface access is 1,000, getting above that requires a Sanction of Surpassment . Each kill you make gives you some experience, and with enough experience you gain extra stat points to distribute. Every 100 times you train you will gain 1 level unless you are a Templar ($50 purchase or 50,000 ash race upgrade) or use apex or DaD relics ( one of each), or have Quickening to gain levels faster. Each of these items (Templar upgrade, apex, DaD and quickening) doubles the number of stat points you get with each training, so with all 4 you can level 16x as much as a character with none of them. In the upper right part of your screen there are two horizontal bars, a yellow above a purple.


The yellow bar shows your exp progressing towards your next stat training, the purple bar your progress towards your next level. The easiest creatures, Magician�s Apprentices on Surface, don�t give a lot of exp so killing those won�t let you level once you move beyond your first few trainings. As you move down the "New Fight" list the critters become harder and give you more exp for each kill. At some point, as you move down the list, you get to a creature that allows you to train every 3 kills (assuming your skills are set to 0%) and at this point you have hit the surface experience cap. Killing harder critters won�t give you any additional exp, the best you can ever do on Sur without any experience-increasing upgrades (like experience egg or experience grav ) is to level every 3 kills with only an occasional training in 2. Once you reach this experience cap there isn�t any real incentive to kill harder creatures for routine leveling since they won�t let you level faster, and harder ones will give you fewer drops over time.


The gold from kills is insignificant in the game these days, so killing harder monsters is really only something to do for your own entertainment. Yoros (the hardest creature on the "New Fight" menu) only give 24,500 gold in normal kingdoms, and in this game items of any value cost billions or trillions of gold. Killing the hardest creatures doesn�t help you in a material way unless you need to kill something specific to get a quest item. After you reach level 10 there is an incentive to fight the hardest creature possible for your first 100 kills each day as that earns you the most points towards a girlfriend . Once you have made those initial 100 kills, high damage is probably not important for the rest of the day unless you need it for some specific reason, like completing a quest step or killing a beast .


In my own opinion, the best way to level is to fight the hardest creature you can kill in a single attack every time, at least until you hit the exp cap described above. Later in your RWK career you may want to play differently, but this should work best for newbies. You also want to make kills quickly , since more kills per hour mean more levels per hour. Equip a rapid damage spell or light weapon, even the cheapest ones without relics will be a huge help. Rapid spells and light weapons cut your game delay in half, so you can make nearly twice as many attacks in a given time as without one of these items. The speed boost does not stack, so you only need ONE light weapon OR rapid spell. Using more won�t hurt, they just won�t make you any faster. Since you only need the speed component of these items, you can use cheap ones that aren�t functional for damage by your class. I recommend fighters, monks and vamps to use a rapid spell and casters to use a light weapon. Archers will take a big hit in Cnc using a spell, but they are so dreadfully slow that anyone who actually wants to level as one will still benefit more from the speed of a rapid spell than the extra damage of not equipping any spells.


You will also level faster if you equip an Apex relic. You only use one since the leveling effect does not stack. With low haste you won�t see a huge benefit from an Apex but it is still there. Assuming you are using a light weapon or rapid spell, as already recommended, adding an apex will let you level around 33% faster with a haste skill of 0, and if your haste is at 99 adding an apex increases your leveling speed by about 50%. The disadvantage of using an apex is that it makes your actions slower and that can make the game less interesting, or cause you to be slow enough to miss getting to a beast if one appears. You can see a more detailed analysis of delay effects (probably too detailed for many) on this page .



The delay bar (purple bar that moves across your screen after you take any action) is intended to help level the game for players with slower connections. You may be able to speed your own actions by disconnecting the bar from the delay built into the server. If you type /dis (or /disable if you like to type more) will let you take action before the bar has entirely cleared your screen. You still have the server delay, there is no way around that, but now you aren�t being delayed by any factors that specifically slow the bar from moving, like a slow graphics card. The down side of this procedure is not knowing for certain when the server delay is completed, which means you might jump the gun on taking another action and thus earn a 10 second delay. If you use the /dis command it may take some practice to develop a rhythm for clicking that lets you click faster but not get timeouts. If the timeouts become a problem after using /dis you can re-enable the delay bar link by typing /ena (or /enable). I find that using the /dis command with an Apex equipped makes me much more likely to get timeouts than when I /dis without an Apex. The delay added by this relic changes the timing enough to cause me more problems. It may not be an issue for others, but it�s something to keep in mind when you do use the /dis command. Equipping several Sacrificial Skillation relics would probably have the same effect. Try /dis, see if it helps you, and type /enable if it causes you problems. Also, the /dis command does not carry over from one login to the next to you will need to type it every time you log in if you want to use it.


Level Milestones (courtesy of Crazy Gizmo on Server 2 with some revision by Metalg316)

There aren�t a huge number of specific changes that occur based on your level, but the game does have some points where level increases do add more options to what you can do. Here is a summary of those:






Level 0 | 
Able to use the /guide command to request assistance | 



Level 1 | 
Able to talk in open chat | 



Level 1 through 5 | 
Able to use the /mentor [Playername] command to reward someone (level 100 or higher) who helped you get started. Don�t just use this for some random player who tells you to give it to him, name someone who has honestly helped you. The command costs you nothing, you can only use it once, and the player you name as mentor gets 500 ash as his or her reward for helping a newbie. Once you reach level 6 you can no longer name a mentor. | 



Level 5 | 
Daily ash lotto begins, each day you have a chance to randomly win some ash. Entry is automatic when you log in, you can collect for up to 10 days of absence from the game, and the jackpot is 10,000 ash | 



Level 10 | 
Able to trade freely with other players and settle kingdoms, you begin to earn girlfriend points for your fist 100 kills each day. | 



Level 10 plus 1 stat training | 
Able to post a swimsuit picture (see Actions menu, options, Swimsuit Picture). You earn 50 ash per vote for the first 199, then 10 ash per vote up to1200 total votes per month) | 



Level 50 | 
Able to use /bnb and teleport to Surface beasts if you found a Beast Noobane and you can put 99% of your experience into skills instead of the 20% cap up to this point. Keep in mind that setting will completely stop your leveling. | 



Level 100 | 
You can become a guide to see requests from new players for help and qualify as a mentor (500 ash bonus each time someone names you his or her mentor). Use the Actions menu, Options, Be A Guide to see guide requests. | 



Level 200 | 
You will find a Temple Stone automatically after you hit level 200 if you have not found it yet | 



Level 500 | 
The location of players without the Templar upgrade will begin to show whenever they post messages in chat | 



Max Level minus 500 | 
Soft cap , you need 3x as many kills to level from here on up, then even more for the last 10 levels if you have an SoS and thus can reach it. This Soft Cap will increase each month along with the SoS Hard Cap. | 



Level 1000 (only significant for players without an SoS .) | 
Hard cap (maximum possible level) for players WITHOUT an SoS . If you do not have an SoS, you can begin to transfer points from one stat to another at this level. | 



Level 3620 (as of September 2022) | 
Hard cap (maximum possible level in the game) for players WITH an SoS . The hard cap is 3620 as of September 2022 but it increases by 10 every month after the Acumen Inquest . If you do have an SoS you can begin transferring points at this level from the stats you don�t use into those you do after each kill, so it�s faster than transferring at level 1,0000. See paragraph below for more detail. | 



Hard cap minus 10 | 
Slow cap for players WITH an SoS , the last 10 levels to the hard cap require 100 kills per training. | 



Level 0-9 | 
Your private messages (pm) to other players are Gray | 



Level 10- 499 | 

Your private messages (pm) to other players are Blue

| 



Level 500-1499 | 
Your private messages (pm) to other players are Purple

| 



Level 1500 - hard cap | 
Your private messages (pm) to other players are Red

| 










## Transferring Stat Points


You will eventually notice players refer to transferring stat points. They may call it just that, or just" transfer", or "cap and trans", or maybe something completely different. �� Whatever the name it�s all the same process. Once you reach the hard cap, either level 1,000 without an SoS or the current SoS hard cap (3620 in September 2022, increases by 10 each month) you can begin moving points out of useless stats into the ones your class uses for fighting well. The process is the same as leveling, you kill things to gain experience and when the yellow exp bar is full you can choose a statistic to train. The difference at the cap is that now you don�t gain any additional stat points, your overall total is fixed at 14,000 x level, so the stat you choose to train will get points taken away from all your other stats. Leveling aids like Transcend , Templar , Apex , Quickening and DaD each double the number of points transferred just as they each double the number of total points gained below the hard cap, so using these items will help you transfer faster than without them. If you have access to Hell you can transfer every fight at level 1,000. On Sur it will take more because that plane gives less experience per kill. Some players, for their own reasons, might choose to transfer either completely or partially at level 1,000 even if they intend to get an SoS. It�s a matter of individual need and situation. If you completely or partially transfer at level 1,000, and then get an SoS, your level cap will be raised to whatever the hard cap is that month and your transferring will stop until you reach it. Your experience will go into normal leveling again, and each training you will gain additional stat points (140 points x 2n, where n = number of stat doubling items you have). Once you reach the SoS hard cap you will again be able to start transferring. You can still allocate some exp towards skills while transferring but the process will be slower than if you put all your exp into it.





## Gold


There are several ways to make gold in the game. The simplest way, and the one that gives the least amount, is by killing creatures. Every time you kill a critter you get some experience and some amount of gold. The amount you get depends on what you kill, and where you are located. Higher-level creatures, harder ones further down the fight list, give more gold. On the surface, the hardest creature is the Yoro La HiHi Samurai and in normal kingdoms killing one gets you 24,500 gold. Magician�s Apprentices are the easiest creatures to kill and they give 5 gold per kill. You can find them all listed on Cyril�s web site if you�re curious. Some locations give more gold per kill. On Surface, both the Pub (150,Sur,145) and Puddle (140,Sur, 190 and you need aqualungs to enter it) give 5x normal surface gold. The Forbidden Mines (162,Sur,159 you need a Scroll of Forbidden Rights to enter) give much more gold, and when imbued by a player will give up to 2 billion gold from a kill (if you get a gold rush, which is a random event and won�t happen with most kills). Other planes also give more gold: Dungeon 2x Surface and Heaven 3x. However, for the most part killing creatures isn�t going to make you wealthy in RWK.


New players can usually make more gold by selling their drops, items with attached relics you randomly find after a kill, to other players buying them to make ash . You can�t do this until reaching level 10 but then you can start profiting from the useless drops you collect up to that point. Keep in mind you can�t trade, sell or destroy any items listed in your inventory under "Others", those are all used for quests . Use the actions menu, trade, to offer an item to another player. You can put someone on your trade menu list by either clicking on his name or using the command "/add playername" where playername is replaced by the name of the player you want to trade with. The price will vary but you can count on getting between 50 and 100 million gold for any crappy item with a relic on it, and nearly all the drops you get will be crappy items with relics attached. On average, you can get a drop every 100 kills or so and selling to ash buyers gives you a decent way to begin making gold. Items with relics on them show as an item name with the relic shown between asterisks. For example, a Rusty Dagger*Strong Arm* would be a weapon (rusty dagger) with relic (strong arm) attached and could be sold to an ash buyer. Pure relics can�t be traded to other players but they can be sold to the "store" (actions menu, sell) or destroyed (actions menu, destroy). Other pure item drops are for quests, they will be in your inventory under "Others" and you can�t sell, trade or destroy them. See the quests section for details about those.


You can also earn some gold by playing another Glitchless game, Dragon Wizard (thanks to Captain Anarchy for that suggestion), which isn�t terribly interesting but might help get you started. You can only get one payout per day from Dragon Wizard. It�s not much of a game and I suggest only playing it once to earn some startup gold, 200k is more than enough, then don�t waste time on the game unless you genuinely like it for some reason. For players below level 10 having more gold won�t help you level faster, and above level 10 you can earn gold faster in RWK than by playing Dragon Wizard.



## Movement


You have three ways of moving around the RWK maps: walk, teleport or set sail. To walk, click on the word "Map" on your screen and a small map will appear on the right side with arrows below it. Those arrows will allow you to move East, West, North and South. The slanted arrows are for moving up and down between the planes if you have access to them. When you walk you move one kingdom at a time.


You can teleport across several kingdoms by using the actions, teleport menu. When you start playing, the distance you can teleport will depend on your base (not modified) Ntl stat. With a Wand of Transportation (quest item requiring Dungeon access) you can teleport an unlimited distance regardless of your Ntl. You can�t directly teleport between planes, just across the plane in which you are currently located.



On the Surface, you can use the Actions, Set Sail menu to sail between ports. This method allows players without Aqualungs to travel across water kingdoms to different landmasses. There are 30 ports scattered across Sur, they show as gray squares on the map, and once you are in one of them you can sail to any other. You can see a list of them at any time by using the Actions menu, set sail. I also have an annotated Sur map showing all the ports and their locations, along with the special Sur kingdoms. Setting sail costs some gold, the price increases with the distance you want to travel. � It also has a considerable delay, but for players without Lungs or a Wand of Transportation it�s the only option for moving between certain places.


Overall, there is little reason to move around on the surface. All the normal kingdoms are the same; the fight list, experience and drops are as good in one as in another. Working on quests or visiting the special kingdoms are the primary reasons to move.




## Useful Commands


You can click on the word " Actions " to see a list of most of the game�s commands, it opens the web page www.racewarkingdoms.com/actions.htm. They begin with a slash, then the command, then the argument for that command. So, sending a pm would be /m Name: message, where "Name" is the name of the player or NPC you wish to send the message to. You can add names to your trade and pm list using the command "/add Name" where "Name" is the account you want to add. The game has its own time, although it is synchronized with real world time, and you can see it by typing /t. A very useful command to remember is to begin your auction posts with /a, which will always put the text which follows into auction, and keep it out of shout. Using the pulldown menu to send your auction posts into auction is less reliable since it will often switch back to shout without you realizing it. Posting auctions in shout can get you banned, so it�s something you really do want to avoid.



## Special Locations


The game has several " special " kingdom locations. The ones on the surface are all shown on my Sur map . Stallion�s Pub is accessible to all players and located at 150,Sur,145. Andal is the most convenient port to get there. Le Puddle Deep is equivalent to the Pub but it�s a water kingdom at 140,Sur,190 and you need Aqualungs to get there. The Forbidden Mines are at 162,Sur,159 and requires a Scroll of Forbidden Rights to enter. Compared to normal kingdoms, The Puddle and Pub both have harder critters (they have defensive skills and relics so all your damage in those locations will be lower then normal), higher drop rates and give 5x normal Sur gold. The creatures in the Mines are the same as in regular kingdoms but they give more gold than other kingdoms, and when the mines are laced, saturated or imbued by players you can get up to 2 bil gold from a single kill. The Temple is nothing special, just a location you need to enter to complete parts of some quests . Players cannot own the Puddle, Pub or Mines.



## NPCs


The game has several Non Player Characters , normally referred to as NPCs, which are involved in quests . Players cannot fight these computer characters at any level. If you try to duel one, you just get a message that you are "far too weak". Unlike players, you can trade pure relics and quest items to an NPC and that action is required to complete the steps in several quests . You chat with an NPC by using a private message (pm) the same way you would contact another player. So, if you want to see the Rune Keeper dialog you can start it with something like "/m Rune Keeper: hello".



## Storing Gold


Players at all levels are limited to holding a maximum of 2 billion gold at a time. The same limit applies to kingdom treasuries, 2 bil gold. You quickly notice that people are offering trades for much more than that, so how is it done? You can store up to 1 bil gold at a time in the form of crafted items which cost 1 bil to make and will sell to the "store" for 1 bil when you need the gold. Often called "specials" or just "specs" they are items like Durability Timeless Fortitude armor; Light, Heavy or Precise Rustless Lifeceasers; Rapid, Major or Accurate Lifeless Growls; Timeless Fortitude Bows and Arrows. All cost 1 bil to craft and, when pure, sell for 1 bil. Items that cost more then 1 bil to craft can also be used as specials, but they will only sell back to the store for 1 billion gold. You can see how crafting something for 25 billion and selling it for 1 bil is not an efficient way to store your gold. You can also use gold to buy runes for your kingdom, then use the /na command to convert 1 rune into 999,999,999 gold. The danger with this approach is that if someone wages war on your kingdom and overthrows it, you lose all the runes and gold you put into it. Kingdoms can have up to 999 runes so each kingdom can be used to store over 1 trillion gold.


Large amounts of gold can be stored on characters and traded using Treasure Elements. An Essence Element of Treasure (an EEoT) is made from 50 runes, 50 ash and 1 mil gold, and can be converted back into 50 runes for free using the Rune Keeper, an NPC. An Essence Element of Great Treasure (an EEoGT) is made from 250 runes, 250 ash and 2.5 mil gold, and can be converted back into 250 runes for free using the Rune Keeper. An Essence Element of Superior Treasure (EEoST) is made from 999 runes, 999 gold and 999 ash and can be converted back into 999 runes for free using the Rune Keeper.. To make any treasure, use /add Rune Keeper to put that NPC on your trade list, then give him the appropriate amount of gold while you are in YOUR kingdom with at least 50, 250 or 999 runes. The Keeper takes the gold and runes and gives you the appropriate treasure element. Trade the treasure back to him from any location where you want to put the 50, 250 or 999 runes. Be very careful where you are located when trading a treasure to the Rune Keeper. If you are in someone else's kingdom THAT is where the runes will appear and they will then belong to the king of that kingdom, not you. Treasures can be freely traded between characters and are not at risk like kingdom runes. Players with high crafting skills can make treasures directly from runes without using the Rune Keeper or any ash.


Mules are characters you create for special purposes such as storing more items than will fit on a single character. You can make as many characters as you like and can log them all in at the same time. In the past we routinely created mules to store items like specials or equipment that doesn�t get used often. When you needed the items you just traded them back from the mule to your main account. In April 2006 this process was changed when Glitchless imposed a level 10 minimum for trading to other characters. This change means you need to play a character long enough for it to reach level 10 before you can use it as a mule. On the surface you need to make at least 3 attacks to train your stats, and it takes 100 trainings to gain 1 level. Since mules can�t accept any trades below level 10 you can�t trade one an apex or any items to speed the process, the best you can do is to buy a light weapon or rapid spell. Figuring around 20 completed kills per minute on such an account and needing at least 3,000 kills to reach level 10 you are looking at about 2.5 hours of playing time per mule before you can trade items to it. Along the way you are most likely going to find a few quest items you can�t use so the total storage space available will be less than the 50 slots you begin with.



## Travel


We have 3 ways to move in the game: "walking" by using the arrows under the mini-map (click on the word "MAP" in the center-right portion of the game page), sailing from one surface port to another, and teleporting directly to another location. I have a modified surface map showing all the ports and special kingdom locations, along with a grid overlay to make it easier to estimate where you are. If you do not have aqualungs, you can�t enter or walk over surface water kingdoms. This restriction includes not being able to enter Le Puddle Deep at 190,Sur,140. Sailing will get you to most landmasses where you can then walk on the land kingdoms to reach your final destination. Your base Ntl stat determines teleporting distance when you do not have a Wand of Transportation . The more Ntl you have, the farther you can teleport in one step. Long trips will involve several shorter segments.



## Storage Limits


Players start out with a storage limit of 50 Items, which includes all equipped or unequipped armor, weapons, spells, relics and also the quest drops under "others". Once you reach that limit you can�t add anything more, including any useful drops you might find. The storage limit can be increased to 75 with a backpack , which is a quest item requiring access to hell and templar status. Players with a Goblin Hide Backpack , another quest item, can hold 90 items.



## Upgrades


You can improve your character in fundamental ways by getting upgrades. Many of these are included on the quests page and are available by either paying money (US dollars, not game gold) or collecting large amounts of ash . The money route is favored by those who don�t have the patience to collect ash and are willing to spend some real cash for virtual items in a game. Using ash is free in monetary terms but you will need a lot of time to save enough to get even the cheapest upgrade. The Ash keeper is an NPC who takes your ash and gives upgrades. The easiest one to get is an Ocean Bubble for 20,000 ash. That becomes Aqualungs which allow you to move in water kingdoms on Surface. There are several other upgrades for 50,000 and 100,000 ash each that will give you access off the surface, upgrade your race to Templar , and provide quest items to make it easier to fight beasts . If you don�t want to spend the time for getting ash and have money to spend, you can purchase the same upgrades using PayPal or a money order (MO). For either of these options, just click on "Buy Items" in the middle of your browser page while in RWK, a new window will pop up letting you choose your payment method and enter all the other details of your purchase. PayPal purchases are usually processed within a few minutes so you should receive your item while still logged in. Money orders need to be physically mailed to Glitchless to they take one or more days to reach them. You can also use PayPal to purchase a temporary character upgrade for only $15.00. It lasts 10 days and gives you Templar status and all planes access. At the end of the 10 days you revert back to a non-Templar race and are stuck on Surface again, but in that time you can get some very useful quest items and gain a lot of levels in hell, or collect a decent amount of ash in the Heaven Keep (66,Hev,66). You can�t summon beasts with trial access alone (that requires permanent Templar status) and without a Scroll of Forbidden Rights you can�t enter the Sky Shrine (100,Sky,250).


The character upgrades in RWK used to be available only by paying money, so adding the Ash Keeper and letting players earn upgrades using ash opened up the game tremendously for those who like to play but don�t want to pay. Although collecting several hundred thousand ash is a long and tedious process it IS possible, so if you are going to be spending a lot of time in the game anyway you now have a way to use it to significantly improve your character. As far as what to get in concerned, that will depend on your goals, patience and to some extent what you may have randomly found as drops. Personally, I went with templar first, then sky access, then dungeon, then various other items. I don�t think that sky or dungeon alone are as useful as templar for a permanent upgrade but others will have different opinions about that, and what is right for me is not right for everyone. Whatever you get using ash, the option to improve your character without needing to pay any actual money for it was a very nice addition to the game.



## Things NOT to do


Don�t play in a location with any tax, that is gold being taken away from each of your kills and put in the king�s treasury. If you�re new you want to keep all your gold, so just move until you find a location with 0% tax and play there. Some players who own the new character starting locations have set the tax in them to 99%, meaning that you only get to keep 1% of the gold from each of your kills.


Some players just like to destroy newbie kingdoms. Up to level 500 your location is hidden, but starting at level 500 all your posts in shout and auction will clearly show your location to everyone in the game unless you are a Templar. Once your location shows, never post a message in shout that indicates you own a kingdom while you are located in your kingdom. Don�t ask questions about selling or alching runes, or how treasuries work when your kingdom location is revealed. Your questions can attract players who will look for your kingdom and destroy it, for profit or just for fun.


The game has an official help site, you can reach it in game by typing /help. If you ask a lot of questions about topics which are clearly answered on that help site, it is likely some players will get pissed at you and come to either destroy any kingdoms you own, or just kill you over and over as fast as you revive (known as chainkilling). Spending time reading this site, /help, and /faq can save you some grief in the game.


If you followed the advice at the top of this page you have already read the rules. If you beg for gold or items or spam messages in the game there really is a chance you will get banned. It doesn�t always happen and you might not get punished, but you need to understand that players are regularly banned for breaking those rules. You take a risk each time you break a rule. If you want to "pretend" to beg as a joke, keep in mind you can still get banned for it, in which case the joke is on you.


Don�t trust anyone with access to your account . For one thing you have no control over what someone else logged into your account may do. He or she could give away or sell all your equipment or spam chat until the account is banned. It is now also against the rules to share accounts and offering to buy or sell an account, even in auction, can potentially get you banned. If you do share, buy or sell an account and get scammed or banned, Glitchless will do nothing to help you. The best security is to keep your account to yourself and use a password that no one, including your friends, can guess.


Don�t think that everyone in the game is honest. It�s actually full of scammers who will try to cheat you if given any chance. Treat other players with the same skepticism you would any stranger on the street asking to borrow your wallet.


Also be aware that if someone buys an upgrade for your account using PayPal, that player can subsequently cancel the payment and make you liable for getting banned as a result. I don�t know whether that has been done intentionally with malicious intent but it is possible so you should keep in mind. Glitchless has a rule against letting others buy items for your account using PayPal and I believe this is the reason why. If someone buys you an upgrade without your permission, email Glitchless about it immediately if you want to protect your account.


Back to home

pages.title: The Forge
pages.text:
## A Guide To The Forge


Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright.

Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.


Here is the guide written by Niizandar/Sarenrae and Obsidian Night/Cagedangel . More recently, Archerofsm00thness added his findings about the costs of forging an 11th item. This guide is to help you understand everything about the most recent plane added to RWK and what comes with it.



This lowest plane named "The Forge" was added to the game on 30 June 2017. It is located below Hell.



The official Announcement of this new location was:



***********************************************************

A new plane now exists: The Forge.

On this new plane the mechanics of kingdom ownership are very different. Here are the differences we are willing to elaborate on:

-All kingdom assets (other than Runes) are worth 1 million times more than normal.


-All kingdom assets cost more to purchase as their total goes up.


-Kingdoms here cannot be traded.


-Each hour when interest is gained, there is a chance to harvest a very rare element. This chance increases slightly based on the amount of teasury.


-Certain locations here that are closer to the source of riches have bonuses toward interest or element harvesting.


-Kingmanship increases the interest bonus if present, but does not affect the kingdom in any other way.


-Three new skills exist which increase element harvesting bonuses.


-Interest gained here is not affected by normal bonuses. It starts out very high but decreases as teasury increases, making them more profitable than normal kingdoms only for players without extreme wealth.


-Settle kingdoms WISELY. Attempting to settle costs gold, and the chances of being successful decrease exponentially each time you successfully settle a kingdom PERMANENTLY, making it extremely difficult to own more than a handful of kingdoms on this plane.


-Populations consume less food.


-Due to the nature of the higher and varying resource costs, many kingdom management / commands will not function in the Forge


-Less morale is lost in the Forge from guard slayings. Kings should still take care to rune up.


-Forge kingdoms cannot be quaked directly, however, they will still be considered under major assault and have army and fortification penalties if attacked by someone who has quaked.


-Paying serfs is 20x more costly, bribing them is 50x times so.


-Overthrowing a kingdom via war will cause the KD to be occupied by NOBODY as opposed to the attacker to prevent using it as a settling exploit. Recovering the conquered kingdom's resources will require you to settle it, which in itself is expensive.


-War on this plane is designed to occur mainly between neighbors battling over high bonus territory. Fighting on foreign soil is expensive.


-All war activity in the Forge will be announced server wide, regardless of girlfriend bonuses.


-There is a significant gold and experience bonus from creatures in the Forge. It has been buffed from its original amount at release - Exp bonus is greater than Hel and gold bonus is greater than Hev.

***********************************************************



Here is a map of this plane you can view if you are curious. On the map you will see things that look like gold deposits or Gems .


Those locations showing gold have an enhanced Trs interest rate, while the Gem graphics show locations with a higher drop rate than normal kingdoms for Gems of the same color as the crystalline graphic.


Gems can drop in your kingdom every hour, they work the same as Trs interest so it stops stacking at either 24h or 48 hrs depending if you have a DoD,DwD relic combo equipped.



Bonuses

With The Forge, a new feature was added to the game where some kingdoms show a property called "Bonus". Bonuses only occur in Forge kingdoms and not all of kingdoms get them.


They range from 0% which is no bonus to 7%. Those percentages are related to the type of kingdom you settled.
The bonus is either for a higher chance to find gems (Ruby,Sapphire,Emerald) or a Trs interest rate increase of 0% to 7% per hour.



In the Forge, Kingmanship has the effect of increasing the interest bonus innate to some locations by +1% per skill point, up to +125%. If 0 bonus to start, there is no benefit.



3 skills were also added to the game:



Ruby/Sapphire/Emerald Hoarding - In the Forge, certain locations provide an innate bonus to finding rare essences.



These skills enhance that bonus by +1% per skill point, up to +125%. If 0 bonus to start, there is no benefit.


For most part if you settle a kingdom in the Ruby area you will only find Ruby gems. On a very very rare occasion you may find other type gems but it’s very rare.



Kingdoms settled in a Trs bonus area can drop any type gem, but the chance of them dropping is lower than others.

In the 0% kingdoms you may find any type gem but it’s not suggested to settle those unless there is absolutely no better location available.


Gems

There is 3 types of Gems : Green=Emerald, Red= Ruby, Blue=Sapphire



Gems are used the same way as Unborn Torment and Dragon Eater elements except their boosts are stackable until Rank 9.



You can then push to Rank 13 and gain different stats called gems resist which allow you to resist another player's gems proc (programmed random occurrence) when dueling.



Different Gems have different proc effects:



Ruby/Sapphire have a % chance to kill without the target having any chance of reviving. It bypasses/ignores every revive factor in the game so if those gems proc for your dueling opponent, you are killed.



Emerald gives you a % chance to revive yourself.



Ruby also gives a small bonus to First Attack and Second Attack, but the % is not even 1% per rank you equip.



Sapphire also gives a small bonus to First Cast and Second Cast, the % is not even 1% per rank Gem you equip.



Each of those also doubles your armor defense stats in /st, same as Dragon Essence.



Gem Rank

As you add more gems to a given equipment slot their benefits stack and the rank increases.



As more gems are added to the same slot the required number to reach the next rank doubles. Each addition of gems also consumes 999 runes.



To add gems to your character go to one of YOUR kingdoms that has 999 runes and trade one gem to the Surface Hermit.



The Hermit will accept the trade, take however more Gems are needed along with all 999 runes, and your Gem rank for that equipment slot will be increased by 1.





Rank | Gems Needed |

1 | 1 |

2 | 2 |

3 | 4 |

4 | 8 |

5 | 16 |

6 | 32 |

7 | 64 |

8 | 128 |

9 | 256 |

10 | 512 |

11 | 1024 |

12 | 2048 |

13 | 4096 |




When activating gems you need all gems to be the same type for that rank.


You could have 3 different types equipped on one character if you wish but, say you want to reach rank 8 for the head slot, you will need 128 gems of the same type.



As you add Gems to your toon the game will start in the Left Weapon/Shield position and successively add 1 gem to each slot in order, left to right then down and left to right again, etc.



So the first Gem attaches to the Left Weapon/Shield position, second Gem to the Helm, then in the order Right Weapon/Shield, Gauntlet, Mantle, Sleeves, Left Spell, Leggings, Boots, Right Spell.



If you do not have a full set of 10 Dragon Essences equipped the Gems will cycle among however many DEs are on your toon.



You can overwrite an equipped Gem with a differnt type but the number of new Gems needed will still increase.


So, if you have a slot with a rank 4 Sapphire and want to overwrite it with Ruby to rank 5 you will need to have 16 Rubies in your inventory.



You will notice that starting at rank 8 it becomes impossible to equip enough ordinary gems due to requiring more than your inventory can hold, that's why Great Gems were added to the game.



To make Great Gems you need to have at least 32 regular Gems of the same type (Ruby, Sapphire or Emerald) and at least 999 ash on your character and be in a kingdom with 999 runes where your character is the king.


Give the Surface Hermit 32 gold and it will take 32 gems, all 999 runes and 999 ash and in return will give you 1 Great Gem.



Forged Gear


Forged gear was also added as part of the Forge update. Like previous endgame gear in RWK it’s an upgrade of the gear you have, so you upgrade your Shadow Scale gear into Forge gear.



Forge gear requires more work than shadow since you need to be able to craft the almost DPP variant of that piece on the character you want to upgrade. Like Shadow Scale items, Forged gear cannot be traded or destroyed.



It requires a craft skill of 765 minimum and we’re assuming trivial of 815 but to our knowledge you can’t fail it.



Forging equipment requires a lot of Trs gold in the Forge kingdom you will be using for the upgrade. It also takes many days to craft, and a lot of Great Gems. Each successive Forge item requires more Trs, more days, and more Gems to craft.


Here a list of the cost for each piece


# of Forged Piece | Forge Trs Gold Needed | # of Great Gems Needed | Days required to Forge |

First | 10,000,000 | 5 | 5 |

Second | 20,000,000 | 10 | 10 |

Third | 28,000,000 | 15 | 15 |

Fourth | 39,200,000 | 20 | 20 |

Fifth | 54,880,000 | 25 | 25 |

Sixth | 76,832,000 | 30 | 30 |

Seventh | 107,564,800 | 35 | 35 |

Eighth | 150,590,720 | 40 | 40 |

Ninth | 210,827,008 | 45 | 45 |

Tenth | 295,157,811 | 50 | 50 |

11th | 413,220,936 | 50 | 50 |





You can also find all the information at the bottom of this page 


Be warned: IF your kingdoms get nuked while crafting a Forged item we are ASSUMING you lose the piece since the Shadow Scale item it is made from disappears from your inventory when you begin crafting it. So, make sure you have strong kingdoms.



Forged Gear Bonuses


Gear Bonuses: Forged gear comes with different bonuses and some bonuses are specific to which piece it is. Here is a list of each Item and its effects:




Item Type | Stat Boosts |


Weapon | +10% chance to Ambush, 10% Ambush Resist for dueling, 1% Sneak Attack, 1% Ambush chance when waging war, +5% Void Resist, +44% Ntl, +44% Str, +25% Cnt, +25% Agi, +100% Dur, 15% Cripple and Crippling Resist

|


Damage Spell | +10% chance to Ambush, 10% Ambush Resist for dueling, 1% Sneak Attack, 1% Ambush chance when waging war, +5% Void Resist, +44% Ntl, +44% Str, +25% Cnt, +25% Agi, +100% Dur, 15% Cripple and Crippling Resist

|


Arrow | +10% chance to Ambush, 10% Ambush Resist for dueling, 1% Sneak Attack, 1% Ambush chance when waging war, +5% Void Resist, +44% Ntl, +44% Str, +25% Cnt, +25% Agi, +100% Dur, 15% Cripple and Crippling Resist

|


Bow | +10% chance to Ambush, 10% Ambush Resist for dueling, 1% Sneak Attack, 1% Ambush chance when waging war, +5% Void Resist, +44% Ntl, +44% Str, +25% Cnt, +25% Agi, +100% Dur, 15% Cripple and Crippling Resist

|


Shield | +10% chance to Ambush, 10% Ambush Resist for dueling, 1% Sneak Attack, 1% Ambush chance when waging war, +5% Void Resist, +44% Ntl, +44% Str, +25% Cnt, +25% Agi, +130% Dur, 15% Cripple and Crippling Resist, 10% Damage Mitigation

|


Armor | +10% chance to Ambush, 10% Ambush Resist for dueling, 1% Sneak Attack, 1% Ambush chance when waging war, +5% Void Resist, +44% Ntl, +44% Str, +25% Cnt, +25% Agi, +45% Dur, 15% Cripple and Crippling Resist

|


Heal Spell | +10% chance to Ambush, 10% Ambush Resist for dueling, 1% Sneak Attack, 1% Ambush chance when waging war, +5% Void Resist, +44% Ntl, +44% Str, +25% Cnt, +25% Agi, +100% Dur, 15% Cripple and Crippling Resist, 25% Damage Mitigation

|





Cripple: All stats are reduced by 80%, including attack and heal ratings. The effect of all ruby, sapphire, emerald, and void resists innate to armors is halved.



There is a less important side effect that will permanently reduce your stats by 1,000 each. This will always happen in Inquest, but will only happen outside of Inquest if you are above the hard cap and have been active for that hour



Settling Forge kingdoms


After settling your first Forge kingdom each additional kingdom you attempt to settle has a % chance to succeed, with that % getting reduced with each increase in your Forge kingdom count. You can see how many times you've settled using the /settle chat command.



First kingdoms or if you have zero settles, it is 100% success chance, additional kingdoms will be 50% then 25% and so on for each attempt.



Since each attempt costs 2 billion gold the calculation for averaged expected gold cost is ((2^n)*10^9) where n is the number of your settled kingdoms + 1.



For example, settling your 12th forge kingdom would be estimated as:

((2^12)*10^9) = 4,096,000,000,000

4,096,000,000,000 / 1,000,000,000 = 4,096 billion or ~4.1 trillion gold



On a brand new character you can easily settle 10-12 kingdoms with a few trillion.
You can also reduce your “settled count“ (command /settled) by breaking a black ashen rock in your inventory.



However the count will only decrease if you break your Ashen Rock with a real money transaction, it doesn’t work if you break it with ash.



There are also 2 other options in the "Buy Items" menu shop that reduce your forge settled number by a certain amount: The "DDD, 20x EEoST and Forge Settle Reduction" for $199.99 and the "-11 Forge Settled Count" also for $199.99.



Building Forge Kingdoms

To build kingdom assets will cost 1,000,000x as much gold as in a normal kingdom in the other planes.



If you buy an army component with 2,000,000,000 (2 billion) gold the kingdom value will increase by only 2,000 units.



As you increase the value of kingdom assets, you will get slightly fewer units per gold each time.



The diminishing return moves towards getting only 50% of the army or kingdom defenses you would get on the other 5 planes.



Generally, when you want to aim for a target value, plan a 50% diminishing return and double your expected gold cost.


*********************************************************


NOTE: I (Jimcav) have found the cost of settling Forge kingdoms has enough randomness to give very nonlinear costs. Presenting a formula may give players the mistaken impression that each settlement has a predictable cost. It could be a rude surprise for some.


On S2 my kingdom settling costs were:


KD Count | Cost to settle next KD (Billion) |

Server 1 | |

8 | 1612 |

9 | 1292 |

10 | 914 |

11 | 942 |

12 | 3650 |

13 | 1670 |

14 | 33200 |





Server 2 | |

0 | 2 |

1 | 2 |

2 | 4 |

3 | 6 |

4 | 16 |

5 | 8 |

6 | 96 |

7 | 408 |

8 | 84 |

9 | 2714 |

10 | 1062 |

7 | 132 |

8 | 284 |

9 | 388 |

10 | 4362 |

11 | 1918 |

12 | 13750 |

10 | 1402 |

11 | 3000 |

11 | 2500 |

12 | 2750 |

12 | 4250 |

13 | 7750 |




*********************************************************


This page last updated 9 February 2026
