pages.title: Actions
pages.text:
Actions

## Actions

These are the various options you can choose under the Actions menu. The are also explained on the Actions web page .

New Fight

Pick a fight with an inactive monster. Once this is done, the monster will appear in the battle option for others to attack as well.

Battle

Attack an active monster. This allows groups of people to fight the same monster killing things that they could not on their own.

Duel

Target another player so you can attack them. You can also use this feature just to check another player's current life total.

Move

Travel North, South, East, West, Up, and Down. This allows you to change your character's location and explore new areas.

Skills

Skills are an additional way for a character to build power. In order to train skills you must divert a certain percentage of your experience toward them. Although skill increases are random, your chances of having a skill increase is proportional to the amount of experience diverted. RWK does not provide detailed information on skills; it is up to the player to determine their true effects. The game offers the "/stat" and "/relic" commands (see Chat Shortcuts below) to help players in determining their effects, however, only some of the skills affect stats directly.

Teleport

Warp to a new location directly, without moving through intermediate locations. The distance you can teleport depends on your Intelligence (NTL) stat.

Set Sail

Travel from one port to another via boat. The price of the trip will depend on the distance between the ports.

Buy

Acquire new items, paying for them with gold. This allows you to upgrade your equipment and become a more powerful player.

Sell

Trade your items for gold. You will receive 50% of the value of your items when you sell them. You cannot sell enchanted items, they can only be destroyed.

DESTROY

This feature is used to destroy enchanted items...you will only receive one gold for these items since they are considered cursed by vendors. If you accidentally destroy an uncursed item it will default to sell and give you the proper amount of gold. Any item that has a relic attached is cursed.

Equip

Put your items on your body for use in battle. You must equip your items in order to have them take effect in battle.

Craft

Crafting gives you the ability to create items far superior to store bought items, or make items in the "Buy" menu for half the cost. Initially crafting items will cost exactly one half "Buy" price and will sell back for the same amount, however, making very high level items that are not normally available in the game will require the use of expensive runes and those items will never sell back to the game for more than 1 billion gold.

In theory you could raise your crafting to a very high level without spending a dime, however failures in craft attempts are bound to cost you in the long run. The chance of succeeding a craft attempt is dependent on the crafting skills associated with that item, and likewise so is the chance of improving those crafting skills. Making harder items will result in more failures, however, they will also give you a greater chance of increasing in skill when you do succeed. Eventually some items become trivial for you to create and will no longer fail or give you any further skill increases. You will receive a message telling you when an item you craft is trivial to make.

Enchant/Disenchant

This feature allows players to add/remove relics from items. The cost of doing so is 1 or 2 runes for low quality items (the relic doesn�t matter) and can be up to 250 runes per chant for the best quality items in the game.� You must be in your own kingdom to do any chanting. The difficulty of chanting depends solely on the item being chanted, not the relic.

Give Gold

Give gold to the target player. Using the shift key you can select multiple people to give gold to at once.

Give Kingdom

Give the kingdom you are currently in (granted you are the king) to the selected target. This action does not work for Forge kingdoms

Trade

Propose a safe trade to someone. They will either accept or reject your item and price. If they accept the transaction will take place automatically, you do not have to rely on them to pay you.

View Tops

See a list of the top ranking players in various categories.

Options

Chat Size

This determines the amount of text lines in your chat display box. Although it is called your "chat" box, other important information such as fight details are found in this area.

Relic Display

Verbose- When your relics shimmer it tells you in detail what has occurred. This option should be used when testing relics and their effects.

Brief - You are only notified as to whether or not your relics have taken effect. This option should be used to cut down of chat lines that your fight actions take up. This does not affect the actual effects of your relics, just what you see.

Graphic Window Update

Automatic - Your graphic window automatically changes to display the map when you move, your enemy when starting a fight, etc.

Manual - You will have to manually change your graphic
window mode when you want to see your face, target, inventory, or map.

Auction/Emote/Shout Text

This option allows players to toggle whether to see or not see Auction, Shout, and Emote text. Note: Disabling any or all of these can increase game speed by reducing lag. You can still send text from NCL (no chat land) with them disabled but you cannot receive it.

Button Enlargement

This allows to enlarge the button of a given statistic making it easier to click it while leveling.

Preferred Blessing/Approbation

When slaying certain special beasts and completing some quests , players sometimes have actual body parts or items enchanted. This will allow you to pre-select the enchantment of choice. Failure to do this may mean getting the wrong enchantment permanently.

War Message Filtering

This can disable the yellow messages that appear informing you of another player attacking someone's kingdom.

Kingdom Actions

Settle

If no one owns the land you are currently on, you may claim it for yourself becoming the king.

Bribe/Pay Serfs

Players can bribe or pay the serfs in the kingdom in order to sway their likelihood of instigating a coup. Bribing the serfs lowers morale, hurting the kingdom's stability and paying the serfs increases morale. It is considerably more difficult to lower morale than it is to increase it in this fashion.

Incite Coup

If the morale of the peasants is below 5% you may incite a coup, an attempt to overthrow the kingdom via peasant rebellion. You may only do this once an hour, however.

Guard

All kingdoms start out with the weakest guard possible. By hiring a better guard you lower the chances of him being killed which is catastrophic for the morale of your peasants. Guards are permanent and are revived once an hour if killed. Killing the guard (actions menu, battle, guard) will lower morale in the kingdom.

Tax

The tax is the percentage of money from each kill that goes into the kingdom's treasury. Only the king can manipulate this setting.

Runes

This allows a king to purchase a rune for his kingdom. For more information on runes see the Info page.

Buy Food

Kings use this feature to purchase food to feed their peasants. If there is no food the serfs will begin to starve and morale will decrease each hour.

Embezzle

Kings use this feature to withdraw funds from the kingdom's treasury. Only the king can embezzle the Trs this way.

Deposit

This feature is used to add funds to the kingdom's treasury and anyone can add gold, not just the king.

Build Army

Kings may use this feature to
build up an army with which they can both attack and defend.

Fortify

Fortifications are erected by kings as a means of kingdom defense. They can be built on the N orth, S outh, E ast, and W est borders of your kingdom.

Wage War

This feature allows you to attack nearby enemy kingdoms. Choose your enemies wisely, however. War is very costly and not always beneficial.

Blind Charge

Some special quest items give you the ability to attack more than one kingdom at a time, plowing through your target kingdom into the kingdoms that follow. Blind War differs from Wage War in that it does not check to make sure the king of the kingdoms that follow is the same as the king of the initial target kingdom. It does, however, still prevent you from attacking yourself which means you can blind charge through your own kingdoms to do damage to further enemy kingdoms if desired. When attacking X amount of kingdoms you take 1/X the normal amount of damage in each kingdom.

Transport

This feature allows kings to move their army and guards to and from their adjacent kingdoms. You can only perform this action once per hour for each unit type.

Chat Actions

SHOUT

Shouting displays your message to everyone currently playing the game no matter their location. Only use this feature when you intend to speak to the entire gaming population; use private messages (click player names to send privates messages) and KINGDOM chatting for personal conversations or hunts. You may NOT attempt to sell items or advertise items with SHOUT . You will be muted if you do this.

KINGDOM

Kingdom messages are heard only by the players currently in your zone (i.e. at the same location as you). This type of message mode is preferred, and you should encourage other players to use kingdom chat instead of shouting when applicable.

AUCTION

Auctions are seen by everyone currently playing the game. They are also colored green so they are discernible among the other messages. Players are limited to 4 auctions
per hour. You may NOT attempt to sell or advertise items with SHOUT You will be muted if you do this.

Chat Shortcuts (in-game commands)

/help

Automatically opens the official RWK help site .

/info

Displays a list of topics you can get further info on.

/faq

Opens the Frequently Asked Questions page.

/bb

Beast Bane . Using this command requires the Beast Bane item which is only available through purchase. It will teleport you directly to the last spawned beast.

/bnb

Beast Noobane . Teleports even surface noobs to the last surface beast with a small delay.

/search (command is now disabled)

Occasionally a beast will go dormant and remain in a zone but not be on the battle list. This command will find and revive and such beast but can only be used once an hour in each
kingdom.

/pe LOCATION (as in /pe 123,Sky,012)

Public Embezzle . You need a have a pure Leather of Delegation relic in your inventory, but it does not need to be equipped. Public Embezzle is a complicated feature that requires you to be standing in a kingdom with full treasury and specify a kingdom owned by the same king (other than yourself) that has a public embezzle amount set (using /pea, see entry below). This will automatically embezzle and send that specified amount of gold to the target kingdom's treasury. If this would cause the treasury to exceed 2 billion then 1 billion of the treasury is automatically used to purchase a rune.

This method allows kings to have other players move through their complexes and easily embezzle a specified amount of gold from each kingdom. The kingdom owner sets the amount of gold to embezzle, the embezzler does not. Note that using this method there is never a need to use a well rounded number for collection, you can specify the exact amount of gold you would be able to make back in 24 or 48 hours making each point in kingmanship count.

Public embezzle does not work for Forge kingsoms.

/pea AMOUNT

Only players with the Scepter of Delegation can use this command. This sets the Public Embezzle Amount for the kingdom you are standing in. You may set this value to 0 in order to disallow public embezzling to this kingdom. Note that this value is set to 0 whenever a kingdom changes hands.

/br 123,Sky,012

Buy runes in a distant kingdom. Using /br alone assumes current kingdom.

/sr 123,Sky,012

Single rune purchase in distant kingdom. Using /sr alone assumes current kingdom.

/p character

View that character's linked picture.

/s character

View that character's linked swimsuit picture so you can vote for it.

/swim

View how many Sexy! votes you have received for your swimsuit picture.

/alch

Alchemize 1 rune into 1,000,000,000 gold, requires Scepter of Unending Repulsion .

/re

Stats as Affected Only By Relics

/na

Newbie version of Alchemize which converts 1 rune into 999,999,999 gold.

/da 123,Sky,012

Double alchemize, converts 2 runes into 2,000,000,000 gold, requires Harbinger's Scepter of Oppression . Using /da alone assumes current kingdom.

/sa xxx,Plane,yyy

Alchemize 1 rune into 1 billion gold, requires Harbringer's Scepter of Oppression , DaD and DsD . Using /sa alone assumes current kingdom.

/freefort

Adds 250 million to all fortifications and to trs (but not to any armies) at no cost, however, it requires you have a pure Free Fortify relic in your inventory which has a very random chance of breaking (it disappears) each time it is used.

/fortify

Repairs damage to your fortifications as much as possible with your existing gold without any waste.

/repair

Repairs damage to your armies as much as possible with your existing gold without any waste.

/nrage

Enrages your armies to do double damage. Costs 10 runes.

/chance

Adds 10 runes to whatever kingdom you are in with a 1 in 5 chance of burning an EEoT .

/bf

Buys as much food as possible with your existing gold without any waste.

/ref

Repairs and buys as much food as possible without any waste.

/t

Returns the time in Race War Kingdom units.

/ann

Opens the announcements page automatically.

/ignore character

Adds character to your ignore list until you relog. You can repeat the command to remove a character from the list and return to seeing all his messages.

/inv

Outputs your inventory in chat so you can copy and paste it on webpages and the like.

/skills

Shows you all your skill proficiencies as affected by equipped relics.

/rawskills

Shows your raw skills without any bonuses due to items.

/relics

Shows you your stats modified only by relics .

/stats

Shows you your stats modified by all your relics , items, special abilities, and skills .

/trades or /crafts

Shows you your tradeskill proficiencies.

/add character

Add the character to your hotlist for trading purposes or for sending private messages .

/m character: message

Sends a private message to another player. This is much more easily accomplished by clicking on the players name.

/a message

The /a command is merely a shortcut for making auctions. All the same rules apply. Using this is a sure way to keep your auctions out of shout.

/e message

Rather than displaying a normal message, this will display your message as an emote and will appear in italics.

/z Race:Sex

Templars have a special ability allowing them to morph into another Race, keeping all the positive aspects of being a Templar such as double leveling speed, hidden location, and increased skill advancement, but acquiring the statistical and physical characteristics of their chosen race. YOU CAN ONLY DO THIS ONCE for free so don't mess with this feature until you are sure you have chosen the Race and Sex that you want to keep. You will not be able to change again unless you re-purchase the Templar upgrade or use 5,000 ash and the Ash Keeper . Misleading other players into wrongly using this feature will get you banned immediately. You have been warned! To view the stats of all the races type /help while in the game and click "Choosing a Race".

/ins character

For the cost of 1 million gold you can see 1 random item in another player's inventory granted you are in the same kingdom and you have a Scepter of Specter Sight .

/shh character

Disables battle text to help from getting spammed while nuking or defending a plex.

/girlfriend

Displays an info page about girlfriends .

/gf

Displays your girlfriend photo album and current girlfriend points.

/gf character

Displays the character's girlfriend photo album and current girlfriend points.

/dump accountkey

Starts the girlfriend process over from scratch, removing all girlfriends and points. You must enter your account key to prove you're not only the owner of the account but that you seriously want to do such a crazy thing.

Back to home

pages.title: Inquest
pages.text:
## The Acumen Inquest


Copyright 2018 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved


Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is prohibited.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.




I was recently asked to offer some guidance for new players on the Acumen Inquest. I will try to post some of the commonly-known useful information here. My thanks to Anubis for his insightful contributions to this page.


I'll start by pointing out, before others do, that I have never won an Inquest and haven't participated in one for years. I think the best I ever managed was three rounds in an inquest many years ago, clearly due to the luck of being matched with even less prepared characters than mine. I don't have any tips on winning or secret information to get more out of them, but new players may find just the basics helpful.


The Acumen Inquest is a monthly tournament where players duel each other for prizes. It is usually scheduled for 6pm Eastern US time on the last Saturday of the month. You can find the date for the next one on the Announcements page.


Participation is restricted to the top 128 players (by level) who relog during a roll call at the start of the event. All participants win some ash and the overall winner is awarded a $200 credit to use for purchases from Glitchless. The month's kingdom complex winner is announced after the Inquest and wins a $100 game credit.


The Inquest runs concurrently on both servers but I imagine some players manage to participate in both with two browser windows open, quick reflexes, and some luck with the timing of their duels. If your characters on both servers are picked at the same time it would seem that one is going to be neglected and lose.


The incentive to participate, even if you have no chance of winning even the first duel, is that every participant wins at least 2,500 ash. If there are not many players logging in it's possible to even get some low-level mules into the top 128 list for that night and pick up some ash on them. You can use poker to move ash between characters if there happens to be nobody else playing at the same time. Log in both (or any number you can control) in separate browser windows, max the bets, then fold all the ones you want to move ash from so the one you want to move it onto will win. If a character gets a bonus hand let it win, then move the ash during other, non-bonus, hands.


For those interested in participating, log into the game about 30 minutes before the scheduled start time. There should be a system announcement (yellow text) telling you when to relog to repopulate the tops list. In the past Glitchless suggested logging a couple of times to be sure you are added. If your character is in the top 128 you will be matched randomly with another player and when your turn comes will be pulled into the Brawl arena (40,Sur,140)to start a duel. Players are locked out of the Arena during normal gameplay and it is only opened by Glitchless during the Acumen.


The URL for a list of the initial fighting pairs is announced and it's worth viewing it to see the order in which the duels will be played. If you are on that list you will know what pairs preceed you so you can try to be ready when your own time arrives.


When your turn comes you will be teleported to the Brawl arena and you will get a message to "Fight". That's the signal for you to, as quickly as possible, select "Duel" from the "Actions" menu, select your opponent (it will be the only other player at that location) and attack. Keep attacking until either you or your opponent dies. The winners advance to the next round with half the players eliminated each time until there is a final overall winner. 

In the last Inquests where I participated it seemed like I saw the "Fight" message at the same time as the one indicating I had been killed. For some reason, despite having a fast broadband connection and reasonably current hardware, I seemed to lag behind my opponents and get killed before I had a chance to fight at all. After having that happen several times the Inquest lost its appeal for me, but my experience may not have been typical and things may be different today.


If you plan to participate you may want to equip differently than for leveling, skilling or fighting beasts. Here I have to speculate. Being able to void or devoid your opponent, and avoid being voided, devoided or annulled yourself, is helpful. Probably more than inflicting the most damage your character is capable of. Having high dur may also help, which I've been told figures into the popularity of Emeralds for upgrading Dragon Eater approbations. Getting in the fight before your opponent should help. From my own experience I'd say that being slower is not a good thing.


There are some quests that help with dueling, and sleeved cards from poker can come into play to increase your chances of winning. Anubis pointed out to that you can trigger two cards, a red (defensive) and black (offensive) card, to have both active during a duel and get the benefits of each. The various upgrades, high-end armor, weapons, spells and relics and high skills all improve your chances to win duels. However, as mentioned above, just getting into that 128 player group will get you at least some ash regardless of whether you have a snowball's chance in hell of winning even a single bout.


If you happen to log into RWK when the tops list is being reset for an Inquest, and you are within the top 128 players, your character will be pulled into a duel even if you log out before the fighting begins. At least that was true some time ago when I forgot about the Inquest and logged in to see it was being set up. When I logged back into the game hours later I saw my character had been killed in a duel while I was logged out. The interesting part for a Vampire is that you might even win in that situation against the right opponent since relics can do damage without the player taking any action.


The event also features a free-for-all Brawl, with everyone in the same location picking opponents and dueling them until only one player remains alive as the winner, who gets a $50 game credit.


After the dueling is completed Glitchless has a beast invasion where many different public beasts are spawned in rapid succession and players scramble to reach them and try getting the kill. It's handy to keep track of the locations since some survive the spawn period and can be battled later. After that the player monthly beasts lists (type /beast in game to see what you've killed in the current month) are reset so all beast types are available to be fought again.


I'll update this page as other players remind me of things I've forgotten about the Inquest or inform me about useful aspects I don't know. This page is not going to become a tutorial about how to win the duels, I'll leave that to players who actually know how to do it. As mentioned at the top, I just want to help new players get an initial understanding the event. They will get the best insights by participating.







This page last updated 10 July 2018




Return to home

pages.title: ATTACK DELAY EFFECTS
pages.text:
## Attack Delay Effects


Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden. Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.


These calculations were done before the game had hotkeys so all the focus changes between triggering an enemy and killing it were done with mouse movements. Since the implimentation of hotkeys, which was a great addition, we can switch between spawn and fight much more quickly. My calculations and conclusions are now likely incorrect in the current (January 2024) game. I'll leave this page up for a while in case someone finds value in it, but I think it's utility was significantly reduced once hotkeys were added.


Each time you attack a critter in RWK you get a certain amount of experience, a random chance at getting a skill increase (if you have exp for skills set above 0%) and a random chance for receiving a drop. It therefore makes sense that playing faster, which allows you to make more attacks per minute, will help you develop your character more quickly. Working against this player goal is the Barney Bar, that purple delay bar moving across your screen after every action. This feature is there to give players with dialup a chance to compete with those who use faster, broadband connections. Compounding these effects are your haste skill and several items which affect both your delay bar and some other aspect of the game, like your odds for skills (Sacrificial Skillation relics , usually just referred to as SS) or number of levels you receive at a time (the Apex relic ). These items illustrate the principle of Glitchless giveth and Glitchless taketh away. The question is, just how much giving and taking away is really going on when you play? How much do these items actually help, and who do they help most?



In general terms, players really do get an overall benefit from using an apex or SS relics. Players with low haste get less help from these items than those with high haste, but everyone gets a net increase in skilling rate by using them. Assuming you are already using a light weapon or rapid damage spell, which you should always do if your goal is fast progress, a player with 0 haste will level around 20% to 30% faster with an apex while someone with 99 haste will level about twice as fast. I did some testing with a level 20 mule that had 6 haste, it was as close to 0 as I could get with my available characters. Training 10 times each with and without an Apex, using a rapid damage spell in each case for what should be a standard speed boost everyone equips to get. Without an Apex it took an average of 73 seconds for 10 trainings, so figure 730 seconds to train 10 times and gain a level with no other leveling aids equipped. Adding an Apex increased the time for 10 trainings to 119 sec, which would translate to 595 sec for 50 trainings and gaining a level. The relative time for leveling the character with and without Apex was 595/730 = 0.82. So, in this case, using an Apex allowed me to gain a level in 82% of the time it took without one. That is a nice 18% leveling speed boost for a character with almost no haste and at a very low level. I�d have guessed a larger speed boost from the timing calculations below, but even this result makes using an Apex worthwhile in my opinion.



Using an SS will give around a 5% increase in your skilling rate with 0 haste and 6% with 99 haste. Using 16 SS will increase your skilling rate by about 64% with 0 haste and 209% with 99 haste. Players with haste above 99 will do proportionately better while those not using a light weapon or rapid spell will do worse.

Please note: For both leveling
with an apex and skilling with SS relics the benefits will probably be
significantly less for those who use slow vamping . That technique will make attacking a higher percentage of your overall playing time, and the action delay will have much less non-delayed component to offset it. The analysis here assumes players are getting a kill with each attack, as would be the case for someone playing to collect ash.


NOTE: only one of either a light weapon or
rapid spell will give you a speed increase. �
Equipping a second rapid or light, or using two rapids or lights, won�t
make you any faster than a single light weapon or rapid spell.



## Calculations

Once I made the time to look into delay effects I found the
question wasn't as complicated as I had expected. The total amount of time it
takes you to complete an attack, lets represent it with the symbol T, can be
divided into two components. First, there is the delay imposed by the Barney Bar, the time between clicking the attack icon and having the bar disappear completely so you can begin another action. Let's call this time the delay time, and represent it using Td. The second component of your attack is the time it takes you to do all the other things NOT controlled by the Barney Bar. These would include moving your mouse between �submit� and the attack icon, clicking on the icons, clicking on anything else, like "destroy" when you get a drop, etc. Let's lump these all together and call them the Other delay time, and use To to represent their contribution to your overall delay. We now have a simple
equation for the total time it takes you to complete an attack:


T = Td + To (equation 1)


Factors affecting your �other� delay (To) might include your
connection speed, graphics card, processor and RAM, general mouse habits
(moving your mouse quickly or slowly) and other local factors not controlled by
the game server. Some of these variables probably also affect your Barney Bar, but for a given computer and connection method they should be relatively constant.


Variables we'll factor into the Td value are your haste skill, speed modifying items (light weapons and rapid spells), and most importantly the Sacrificial Skillation and Apex relics. We know these relics affect the delay bar, and the amount of that effect. We also
know they don't affect the other speed factors like mouse movement and Internet
latency. We can now use these known delay effects to run some calculations.


A rapid spell or light weapon cuts the Barney bar delay (Td)
in half but it won't change the factors included in To. If we assume equation 1 is correct, we can modify it to account for the effect of equipping a rapid or light item and show this second delay time as:


T2 = 0.5*Td + To (equation 2)


Using the two equations and rearranging terms we get:


T = Td + To


T2 = 0.5*Td + To




T - Td = To


T2 � 0.5*Td = To




T - Td = T2 � 0.5*Td


T � T2 = Td � 0.5*Td




T - T2 = 0.5*Td


2*(T � T2) = Td




T � Td = To

The last two equations tell us how to use a rapid/light item
find our individual values for To and Td. An apex could be used similarly with a slight rearrangement of the equations. Either way, once we have the
values for To and Td we can get an approximation of how much items like SS and
Apex will really help. Let's use some actual numbers to put it all in perspective.


I used a low level character having nothing to modify its speed (0 haste skill) and timed how long I needed to make 50 kills under unmodified conditions, meaning no Apex or rapid/light items equipped. This is taken as the base case, a character with nothing modifying its speed either way. I then made 50 kills with a rapid spell equipped, 50 kills with an Apex AND rapid spell equipped, and 50 kills with an Apex but NO rapid spell
equipped. I took 50 kills as sufficient to level out some of the kill to kill variations in how I move my mouse, any variations in connection lag, etc. More kills under each condition might give more accurate data, but this IS just a pointless game and not a moon shot, so 50 was enough for my purposes.

2*(T � T2) = Td


2*(4.2 � 2.8) = 2*(1.4) = 2.8 sec = Td


T � Td = To


4.2 � 2.8 = 1.4 sec = To


Comparing effects, we see that there is more benefit in just
equipping a rapid spell or light weapon than in using an apex alone. � Any player can equip a light weapon or rapid
spell for as little as 20 gold, so that should be your default configuration if
progressing quickly is your goal. � This
is true even when you can�t afford an apex to go along with the light or rapid
item. � Notice that you would get a 12%
leveling benefit from using an apex alone, with no light or rapid item, but I
can�t see why anyone would want to take this approach. � If you pair the apex with a light weapon or
rapid spell, the speed boost cancels the apex delay and you should get the most
benefit. � Archers without an Archer�s Aid will suffer a big cnc drop using a rapid
spell, but you don�t need max damage to level or skill, while speed is your
friend. � Other classes can all use one
of these speed boosting items with little or no negative side effects, so the
light or rapid setup should be your normal one and will be used as the base for
comparison here. � Taking that (equipping
a light weapon or rapid spell) as our new base scenario, the benefit of adding
an apex is an additional 33% leveling speed increase. � All this is with 0 haste skill, so it can apply to a newly
created character.


How are players with more haste or different connection
variables (the To factor) affected? � We
can use the values for To and Td to create a "what if" table showing
how various factors change the benefit of using an Apex. � Haste and rapid/light items all change the
value of � Td so we can treat them all
as � Td parameters.

Looking at variables not controlled by the game server,
Table 3 shows how player-side changes might affect someone�s overall leveling
speed.


Table 3. � Effect
of connection variables (things that change To, not Td) like a faster or slower
connection, less lag, etc.

From the tables you can see that equipping an apex alone
doesn�t help as much as just using a light or rapid item, so the incentive for
always using a light or rapid is clear. �
Combining the two lets you level even faster than with either
alone. � Lowering your other delays, the
To factors (Table 3), helps but not as much as an equivalent drop in Td since
the Td factor here is higher and so gives a more dominant effect on the overall
delay. � If your connection is much
slower than mine, with a higher To value, you may see a bigger improvement from
a faster connection than using any in-game items. � Individual variations in computer and connection might change the
details for you but the trends should stay the same.



## Overall Benefit of SS Relics


Please note: � If your
goal is to gain skills you do not want to equip an apex
relic. � It will only slow you down
without giving any boost to your chances of gaining a skill.


The data for Td and To also let us calculate an
"effective" value for the benefit of equipping SS relics. � Each SS increases your odds of gaining a
skill by 10%, but it also increased your delay bar time (Td) by 10%. � These two factors combine to give you an
effective improvement of less than 10% since the delay means you get in fewer
attacks per minute, thus countering part of the 10% odds improvement.


Using the values for my test attacks and factoring in both
the 10% improvement in odds and the 10% increase in the Td component of overall
attack delay, I came up with this table. �
The values are all relative to the starting condition of a character
using no SS relics.


I am assuming that these relics only affect the Td component
of overall delay and have no effect on the �
To component.


To = 1.4 sec


Td = 1.4 sec with 0 haste, light or rapid; 2.8 with 0 haste,
no light or rapid; 0.70 with 99 haste, light or rapid





Time calculation: � T
= To + Td*[(1.1)exp(n)] where n = number of SS relics equipped


Raw odds = (1.1)exp(n) = relative to starting point (with no
SS relics)


Corrected odds = (raw odds)/(relative time)

spell, equipping 16 SS relics would be expected to help gain skills 35% faster
over time than using no SS at all. �

greater benefit in their skilling odds. �

We can see that more speed does indeed make those SS relics
more effective. � The simple act of
adding a light weapon or rapid spell, something players should be using anyway,
increases the effectiveness of 16 SS relics from a 35% boost to 64%, relative
to the same player using no SS. � With
both 99 haste AND the light or rapid item it goes up to 209% over using no
SS. � In all cases, it�s interesting to
compare the raw odds, what you might expect just using the 10% odds boost, to
the values corrected for the delay effects. �
That delay really does cut into the benefits of using SS relics but not
enough to completely cancel them.


Players who want to see how their own characters would be
affected by these sorts of changes can get the values of � Td and �
To easily enough and then just plug them into the appropriate equations
to see how much adding an apex or some number of SS relics would be expected to
help. � I wouldn�t put too fine a point
on the results, I believe all tests done in game have a fairly large
experimental error, but the relative values should give you a ballpark figure.

pages.title: Arrow Speed
pages.text:
Arrow Speed 



## Arrow Speed



Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved 

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is prohibited.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.

Page last updated 15 February 2022



Arrow Quality And Action Delay


RWK originally had no bows or arrows. These were added on 4/6/02 and are in the announcements on www.racewarkingdoms.com/oldann.htm for anyone who wants to take a stroll down memory lane. The description is:


"Bows and Arrows added. Those who use bows and arrows will deal 5 times the damage with 5 times the accuracy at the cost of a greater delay. Purchasing better arrows will lower this delay, however. Arrows are infinite. Damage dealt depends on your bow and Cnc and accuracy depends on Dex."


I used to equip as an archer for beasts, which was an easy change from my normal theurgust since the old beasties didn't have any agi and so didn't require much dex to hit. I hated the slow attacks of an archer compared to theurgs, especially since I used a light weapon as a theurg and had nothing similar as an archer. At the time I just made a DT arrow and used it, but I have wondered just how much more expensive arrows help with speed. After my crafting skills developed I did some controlled testing to see how archer attack speed varies with the quality of arrow going from Dirty Rag up to Protectant Prevailation and the results surprised me.


All attacks were made using the same equipment other than the arrow, a DT bow, and the time was measured using a stopwatch. My procedure was to attack the same critter 10 or 20 times while keeping track of the total time required for the full set of attacks. If anything caused an additional delay, such as a server glitch, need to level, or mistake on my part, the test was started over and repeated until a valid set of 10 or 20 uninterrupted attacks could be completed with the arrow being tested. My haste skill was 99 at the time so your mileage may vary with less haste but I think the trends would be the same. I numbered each level of arrow quality starting with Dirty Rag at 1 and continuing up through Tecton Scaled Platemail at 81, although I only had PP (79) at the top end to test with. Just for comparison I timed my attack equipped as a theurg using a light weapon.


Overall, I don't see an incentive to use any arrow above TF unless there is some damage increase as you move up. Within experimental error, I think the results from TF to PP are probably the same although I'm sure some will insist that there is a huge improvement moving from TF to PP, with demonic and tectonic being "uber":) Whatever your views, good hunting!


13 Jan 2007: Caged Tiger (Server 2) did some testing comparing a DA arrow with a Demonic and found the demo to be around 20% faster, which is probably also the relative speed difference compared to PP. He found DA took 4.0 sec per attack compared to 3.3 sec with demonic. It's possible you do get a step change increase in speed with demonic, which would be fair given the much greater cost compared with godly.


More recently (May 2018) Spacta contacted me with some very interesting results of his own experiments with arrows. He has some way to monitor the traffic between his PC and the game server that shows the server-side delay time is in milliseconds, when attacking with various arrows. His approach eliminates all the Internet latency or PC delays that are included in my more empirical approach. He was also able to repeat his testing both before (83 haste) and after (121 haste) getting SoSe on his character and see the extra boost that upgrade provides.


Most recently (June 2021) Obsidian Night used the same methodology as Spactra to determine the speeds of Shadow Scale and Forged arrows with characters having 125 Haste skill. He determined those quality arrows continue the same trend as lower quality ones. His base speed for a comparable Fighter was 444 ms, so that is the value to compare the Archer speed against. Obsidian Night confirmed that using that value gave a relative delay of .99 for a Crystal arrow, the same as Spactra.

Relative values are the time or delay for attacks with a bow and arrow compared to attacks without them. So, for the mathematically inclined, [Relative (time or delay)] = [(value attacking as an archer) ÷ (value attacking as another class)]. This approach allows us to collect data with different characters and different methodology while still determining the overall trends as arrow quality changes. My non-archer attacks were made as a vampire, I don't know what class Spacta equipped as for his tests.

This table combines my old results with the new data from Spacta and Obsidian Night. The text color changes to red where the SoSe speed boost comes into play starting with DT arrows.

The new results from Spacta and Obsidian Night are consistent with my old ones as far as we overlapped, and with both of us being pre-SoSe. Spacta's method is cleaner and gives a smoother trend since it's not affected by connection artifacts.


The really interesting part is when Spacta repeated his testing after getting the SoSe upgrade. You can see the clear break to lower delay for Divine Transcention arrows and better, which are highlighted in red in the table. With a Crystal arrow there was no delay penalty at all for playing as an Archer and with a Shadow Scale or Forged arrows the Archer is even faster than other classes. The value of higher quality arrows is very clear to see in the graphs. SoSe Archers can be as fast as other classes when using Earthen arrows, and may become even faster with Crystal, Shadow Scale and Forged ones depending on variables like Haste skill and connection speed.


Archers without SoSe do not see much benefit from better quality arrows at the high end and will always be attacking more slowly than the other classes. 


I'm grateful to Spacta and Obsidian Nightfor sharing their results and thank them for allowing me to use their data to update this page.



















Back to home

pages.title: Ash
pages.text:
## Ash 101





## Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved


Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.







New players often ask "what is ash and how do I use it?" This is not surprising since ash is, as far as I know, something unique to RWK. If you pay attention to chat for a while you can see clues about what ash does, but even a diligent player is probably not going to understand it by that method alone. Ash can be used by experienced players to control beast spawning and upgrade high-end items Large amounts of ash (20,000 to 250,000) can be used by all players to get free upgrades which otherwise would cost money to buy, or to make Treasure Elements for storing runes outside kingdoms.


Each time you destroy (actions menu, destroy) an item with a relic on it (which covers most drops) you get one ash and one gold. The ash does not take up any space or show in your inventory. You need to type /ash or be playing poker to see how ash much you currently have. The type of relic or base value of the item destroyed does not matter, one item becomes one ash. Actual ash, what you see using the /ash command, can't be sold, traded or destroyed.


Ash is used to summon beasts , but only players who are Templars and have access to hell can reach the Ash Collector to summon anything. It can also be used to combine devoid and D*D relics into D*Devoids, but again you need Hell access, must be a Templar, and need to already own the devoids and D*D ultra relics. Ash is more generally useful for making Treasure elements via the Rune Keeper with ash (50 ash for an EEoT or 250 for an EEoGT ), runes (50 runes for an EEoT or 250 for an EEoGT) and a little gold (1 mil gold for an EEoT or 2.5 mil for an EEoGT). Surface players above level 10 can capitalize on the uses of ash to make gold by selling their useless drops to access players who will then destroy the items and use the resulting ash to summon beasts, etc. When ash was added to the game the value of routine, useless drops went from 0 up to 100 mil gold each in a very short time. Players commonly use the word "ash" to mean both drops being sold for destruction and the ash itself (what you see using the /ash command). Two meanings for one word can confuse new players, but like any slang you pick it up quickly and learn what is meant by the context of its use.


You can also earn ash through swimsuit votes, being a mentor in the game, or having someone name you as their patron when making a money order purchase. You can also win the daily ash lottery (potential 10,000 ash jackpot) once you reach level 5. Entry is automatic.



A more recent change to the game was the addition of the Ash Keeper , an NPC at 150,Sur,150. It is now possible to use ash to obtain items previously only available by paying real money. It takes a LOT of ash, 20,000 and up, but it IS possible to get several nice upgrades this way. The amounts needed are as follows:




Item | 
Ash Needed | 
Give Ash Keeper | 


Ocean Bubble (becomes Aqualungs giving access to Sur water kingdoms | 
20k | 
1 gold |

50k | 
2 gold | 



Beastbane

|

Fyre

|

50k |

12 gold | 



A Mask of Enshroudment (allows Templars who already morphed to morph again) | 
5k | 
13 gold | 



Goblin Hide (for Goblin Hide Backpack ) | 
| 
20 gold (you will need to already have a backpack ) | 



Ash Essence Element

($50 money order purchase from Glitchless) | 
GIVES YOU 50,000 ash when traded to the Ash Keeper
| 
Trade the ash element to the Ash Keeper
(Use /add Ash Keeper to put him on your trade list.) | 






To use the Ash Keeper you can visit his location and start chatting with him, he will tell you what to do. I have the entire dialog with him shown here if you want to save yourself the trip.


As of 26 March 2006 ash can now be used to upgrade the highest quality items in the game using the Heaven
Citizen quest . You can upgrade Demonic items to Earthen for 6,000 ash, Earthen to Crystal for 50,000 ash and Crystal to the new Shadow Scale for 5,000 ash. Shadow Scale cannot be traded, so to move it between characters you must first downgrade it to Crystal for 100,000 ash and 999 runes (ouch!) then upgrade back to Shadow Scale after the trade.


You can also now use 2,000 ash to convert one D*Dv relic into another using the Shrine Keeper. I have the whole dialog with him here and also include it on my quests page .


Ashen rocks are random drops added to the game to encourage players to spend more money. If you get one it will provide you with either ash or Essence Elements of Great Treasure when you spend $50 or more with Glitchless, or use 50k ash to get an upgrade.


A character can hold up to 1 million ash as of 6 November 2017. It is possible to use the Citizen quest to get a Devil's Deliverance relic for 250,000 ash. The Citizen can also convert one UT element (Ice, Fire, Lightning) into another. Glitchless has also added an Ash Essence to its Buy menu for $50. It can be converted into 50,000 ash using the Ash Keeper .


The various items are mostly explained on my quests page , while a DD is explained in the relics section .


In summary:


You get ash by destroying items with relics attached (most drops), playing poker (and winning), being a mentor in the game, being someone's patron, getting swimsuit votes, and the daily ash lottery.


Ash is used to summon beasts, make treasure elements, attach devoids to D*D super relics, play poker , re-morph a templar, or obtain quest items previously only available by paying real money. With 250k ash you can now even get a DD relic from A Citizen at 69,Sky,157 .


There is currently a cap of 1 million on ash capacity. That is the maximum amount any one player can hold. If you destroy items or play poker at that point your additional ash will simply not register and the items you destroy or hands you win will simply be wasted.


The Ash Collector (141,Hell,174) summons beasts and attaches devoids, but you need to visit him at that location, which requires Templar and Hell access.


The Ash Keeper is at 150,Sur,150 and anyone can reach his location to get the items he provides.


A Citizen is at 69,Sky,157 for turning ash into DD relics or interconverting UT essences. You need sky access to reach him in order to do his quests.


A Citizen at 150,Hev,150 will upgrade pure Demonic, Earthen and Crystal items for 6k, 50k and 5k ash, respectively.


The Rune Keeper can use your ash and some gold to convert runes in your kingdom into treasure elements (you 50 or 250 runes per element). You can just pm him (/m Rune Keeper: hello) to get instructions on how it is done, no face-to-face visit is needed.


The Shrine Keeper can change one pure D*D or D*Devoid relic into another using 2,000 ash per transformation.

Last updated 3 September 2024


Back to home

pages.title: Ash Collector
pages.text:
## The Ash Collector





## Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved


Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.








The Ash Collector (we will call him the AC) is an NPC (non player character, a computer personality) located at 141,Hell,174 who can make use of the ash players collect in the game (type /ash to see how much ash is on your character). You must visit the AC at his location to make use of him, and since he is on Hell magma that requires you to have all planes access , Templar status and a Templar's wand . The AC is useless to Surface bound players. Do not confuse the Ash Collector with his relative the Ash Keeper who is located at 150,Sur,150. The AC is used to summon beasts, public ones or private beasts, and to attach devoids to D*D relics .



## Summoning Beasts


The difference between public and private beasts is that public ones use less ash and also prompt a system message that every player sees announcing their locations. It appears in yellow text and you see it even when you have all chat types disabled. Since every player in the game sees when and where a public beast spawns, anyone might move to that location and kill it. This means that the player who pays gold and ash to summon it may not get the kills.


Public beasts come in two types; guaranteed and not guaranteed. A 'guaranteed' public beast means that the AC will definitely summon a beast and you have no risk of your ash and gold being wasted. These cost 1.5 bil gold and 2,250 ash. There is no guarantee that the summoner will kill it, but at least some beast, and you have no control of what type or what location, will appear. The cheapest beast summoning is public but not guaranteed. They cost 1 bil gold and 1,000 ash. This type uses the least amount of gold and ash, but they also seem to fail about half the time. When it fails, the AC laughs at the summoner as being a fool and no beast spawns. When it succeeds, a random beast appears at a random location and you again have no control of what or where.


Private beasts use a lot more ash, 7,000 to 8,500 ash for a private vs. 1,000 or 2,250 ash for a public, but the private beast's location is only shown to the player who summons it. This feature is what makes them popular, you get to fight the beast all by yourself without someone else seeing its location and killing it before you do. Private beasts are the only way players who need a lot of time to kill a beast are ever likely to get one.


To summon a beast you visit the AC location and give him some gold. He will take the required amount of ash from your character and summon the beast. The amount of gold determines what type of beast will be summoned. Give him the
wrong amount and you get the wrong beast, and the AC does not give refunds for mistakes. NOTE that if you summon a 2 bil gold beast (7,500 ash used) you can get any one of the three possible beasts, you do not get to choose which will appear . All three give an approbation, but if you have already killed one of them and that same beast is the one you summon, you will not be able to fight it. You can check which beasts you have already killed that monty using the /beast command.

## Devoid Relics


The Ash Collector can also add a devoid relic to a D*D relic . (Devil's*Deliverance relics where * = Mutilating, Lithe, Putrid, Resistant, Skillful, Accelerated, Warring or Opulent).


This will consume 1,000 ash.


The procedure is simple, you need to have a pure devoid, the D*D relic, and enough ash. You go to 141,Hell,174 and trade the Ash Collector the D*D relic. He will take a devoid and 1k ash and return a D*Devoid (D*Dv) relic to you.




## Ash Collector Dialog



The Ash Collector dialog is shown here for those who might be curious but who can't visit the old codger themselves. He doesn't have much to say.



Message sent to Ash Collector: hello


Ash Collector Away with you...weak fool. I'm busy [collecting] ash...


Message sent to Ash Collector: collecting


Ash Collector Perhaps if you were to give me 1 billion gold and 1000 ash I could...possibly [summon] a beast.



Message sent to Ash Collector: summon


Ash Collector For 1.5 billion gold and 2250 ash I would summon a [public] beast with no risk to your investment.



Message sent to Ash Collector: public


Ash Collector But for 2 billion and 7500 ash I could definitely summon a [private] approbation yielding beast just for you!



Message sent to Ash Collector: private


Ash Collector For slightly less than 2 billion and a hunk of ash I could summon different types of beasts. Offer me an amount and I'll tell you what I can do.

Last updated 3 January 2026


Return to home

pages.title: Ash Keeper
pages.text:
Ash Keeper 





## The Ash Keeper



Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright.

Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is prohibited.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.



Page last updated 15 February 2022



Ash Keeper Dialog To Obtain Various Upgrades In Return For Your Ash


You must visit the Ash Keeper in his location at 150,Sur,150 (the center of Surface) to use your ash for upgrades. The dialog is shown from top to bottom, on your chat screen in game it will be reversed with the last message at the top, first at the bottom. Player response is in blue, Keeper�s text is in red. To get a specific item you give the Ash Keeper the gold specifies for that item. Give the wrong amount and you will get a different item from what you wanted, SO PAY ATTENTION to what he says.


If you have an Ashen Rock when you get any upgrade using 50,000 or 100,000 ash the upgrade will crack your Rock and you will get either treasure elements or ash, depending on your poker payout setting (actions menu). If you have the payout set for ash, you get ash when the Rock breaks. If you have the payout set to runes, you will get Treasure Elements . The exact amount of ash or number of EEoGT you get will vary randomly within a range that depends on whether your Rock is white, grey or black.


Summary


Item | Ash Needed | Give the Keeper |

Ocean Bubble | 20,000 | 1 gold |

Scepter of Annulment | 50,000 | 2 gold |

Beast Bane | 100,000 | 3 gold |

Skill Potencifier | 100,000 | 4 gold |

Fyre Rock (dungeon access) | 50,000 | 10 gold |

Platinum Ore (sky access) | 50,000 | 11 gold |

Templar (permanent) | 50,000 | 12 gold |

Mask of Enshroudment
(allows Templars to change race again) | 5,000 | 13 gold |

Goblin Hide
(for a Goblin Hide Backpack) | 50,000 | 20 gold
(you need to have a Backpack first) |







Start by sending a message to the Keeper like "/m Ash Keeper: hello"


Message sent to Ash Keeper: hello


Ash Keeper: You found me...wow good for you. What's your [name]?



Message sent to Ash Keeper: : Jimcav


Ash Keeper: �Well hello nice to meet you. Now go away unless you have a ton of [ash].


Message sent to Ash Keeper: : ash


Ash Keeper: �Yeah ASH, I really like the stuff...my brother collects it, I keep it...do you understand the [difference]?


Message sent to Ash Keeper: :yes


Ash Keeper: � [NO]! You don't understand the difference otherwise we wouldn't be having this ridiculous conversation...


Message sent to Ash Keeper: : difference


Ash Keeper: �Are you just going to repeat everything I say? I asked you if you understood the difference, now answer me!


Message sent to Ash Keeper: :no


Ash Keeper: �That's right you don't understand the difference. Which is why you might just be [stupid] enough to deal with.


Message sent to Ash Keeper: : stupid


Ash Keeper: �OK Stupid, here's the deal. For 20k Ash I'll give you an Ocean Bubble. I don't know what it does but it's [pretty].


Message sent to Ash Keeper: : pretty


Ash Keeper Thank you, you're pretty too. For 50k Ash I'll give you an Scepter of Annulment, not that you'd have that [much]. Or maybe you have a [50k] Ash element?


Message sent to Ash Keeper: 50k


Ash Keeper: Well hand me the 50k Ash element and I'll convert it to 50k ash for you free of charge. I love the color it turns the clouds!


Message sent to Ash Keeper: : much


Ash Keeper: �Oh really? Well for 100k I'll give you a Beast Bane or Skill Potencifier. And if you have a backpack 50k ash gets you a Goblin Hide. [Well]?


Message sent to Ash Keeper: :well


Ash Keeper: �Hand me 1 gold for the OB, 2 for the SoA, 3 for the BB, 4 for the SP or 20 for the Goblin Hide. Unless you want something [else].


Message sent to Ash Keeper: : else


Ash Keeper: �For 50k Ash I would give you any of these three, hand me 10 gold for Fyre Rock, 11 for Platinum Ore or 12 for [Templar].


Message sent to Ash Keeper: :templar


Ash Keeper: �I suppose if you're already a Templar or at least have the Mask of Enshroudment I could renew yours for 13 gold and 5k Ash.


Back to home

pages.title: Beast Drops

## Beast Drops

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.




26 Jan 2018 - Under construction, revising the format. Tables are currently incomplete.


The data below are
courtesy of Buldermar (Server 2) who has been tracking beast drops for nearly two years. He's got an impressive 220 beasts in total, with some appearing enough to give some meaningful trends.


I have not included the guaranteed drops in the percentage calculations.Those are sure things and would give a misleadingly high result if included in the drop percentages. Guaranteed drops would include UT elements from a UT,
freefort relics from a Goblin King, great treasures from a Goblin Thief, and palace elements from a PLA.



I also did not include trivial items like Rusty Dagger*DotB* or apex because I don't think anyone cares about them.They just aren't worth anything to most players.


Ancients drop an average of 4 PE but most actually seem to only drop 2.In this set only Ancients and Goblin Miners dropped Demo items (other beasts can drop them) and only GMT and Dragon Eaters dropped Earthen or Crystal.Out of 7 DE kills only 2 did not drop any Dragon Elements.


I never kept close track of what dropped from my own beast kills but did track 39 GMT kills while working on my crafting skills.Out of that set I got a total of 2 Crystal items (both spells) and 1 Demo (I think it was armor). Putting in those numbers we get 39 GMT in total with 3% Demo and 13% Earthen/Crystal dropped.


You can pick through the table to find the items that interest you and what dropped them.Have fun :)

pages.title: DD for Palace Elements Quest
pages.text:
## Citizen DD Quest





You can get the details for this quest by traveling to
069,Sky,157 and private messaging A Citizen there. � If you pm A Citizen in another location, it tells you to go to
069,Sky,157. � The dialog is as follows,
it reads from top to bottom.





Message sent to A Citizen:
hello


A
Citizen My friend who lives in 69 Sky fifty pi has the ability to
surprise your eyes.





Message sent to A Citizen:
hello


A Citizen 
Hi! Hi! Hi! Hi! Hi! ... *pulls down pants and defacates in the street*





Message sent to A Citizen:
hello


A
Citizen Guards Guards! This womanly man is harassing me!





Message sent to A Citizen:
hello


A Citizen 
Did my friend send you?





Message sent to A Citizen:
yes


A Citizen 
Alright then here's the deal, you give me a fire, ice, or lightning and I'll
give you something [random] in return. [Or]...





Message sent to A Citizen:
random


A
Citizen By random I mean I'll give you back either one of those 3,
or just some old poker card I found lying around granted you're not in a
special attack mode already.





Message sent to A Citizen:
or


A Citizen 
Or give me [200] Palace elements (Storm, Lava, or Blood) over time and I'll
give you a shiny new Devil's Deliverance.





Message sent to A Citizen:
200


A Citizen 
Don't cry about never getting 200 there are other [ways], and at anytime you
can ask me to give one back in the form of 1,000 ash. Say [give it back] if
that's what you want.





Message sent to A Citizen:
ways


A Citizen Well if you give me an upgraded
super relic I'll give you credit for 25 Palace Elements. Or say [take 50k] if
you want to give me 50k ash for 40 credits. 




So if you want to get a DD entirely with ash you pm the Citizen "take 50k" five times to transfer a total of 250k ash and he will give you the relic. If you use some combination of ash, palace elements and/or supers you need to figure out how to mix them to reach the 200 palace element (250k ash) total.

Death From Above (Server 2) added
that you can get an update of how many PEs you�ve given the Citizen by asking
him (while at 069,Sky,157):





Message sent to A Citizen: how many?


A Citizen I'm now holding 0 of your Palace Elements.





Back to home




�

�

pages.title: https://www.jimcav.com/hev_citizen.htm
pages.text:
## Heaven Citizen Upgrade Quest



Copyright 2005 - 2026 jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden. Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.





Go to 150,Hev,150 and chat up the Citizen there. � He will upgrade pure (no relics attached) Demonic, Earthen and Crystal items in exchange for some of your ash. � The cost is 6,000 ash to upgrade Demonic to Earthen, 50,000 ash for Earthen to Crystal and 5,000 ash for Crystal to the new Shadow Scale Invisibility items. � Those new items are not expensive to get, that really is just 5k ash if you already have the Crystal.





Enchanting or disenchanting shadow items is cheap (10 runes), it only requires a chantcraft skill of 75 to attempt and 125 to be trivial (Manson/BT confirmed they�re trivial at 125). � The rub is that Shadow Scale items can�t be traded between characters, either pure or with relics attached. � If you want to move them you must first down grade the Shadow Scale to Crystal at a cost of � 999 runes and 100,000 ash. � They can then be traded and the character receiving the item must spend another 5,000 ash up grading it back to Shadow Scale. � This means the cost of trading a Shadow Scale item is 999 runes and 105k ash in total.





Shadow items have the same stat bonuses as Crystal (+5% Void Resist, +44% Ntl, +44% Str, +25% Cnt, +25% Agi, Armor +45% Dur, Weps and Spells +100% Dur,+70% theurg casting damage boost) but add a 10% Ambush and 10% Prevent Ambush chance in combat, 1% Sneak Attack and 1% Ambush chance per piece when waging war. � As with other increases in item quality the armor will have more armor defense, weapons will increase first/second attack/cast values.





The Citizen dialog is shown below reading from top to
bottom.




� Message sent
to A Citizen: hello


A Citizen: So you've heard about my
item [upgrading] abilities?





Message sent to A Citizen:
upgrading


A Citizen: If you hand me a pure
Demonic I'll take it and 6k ash and give you an [Earthen].





Message sent to A Citizen: earthen


A Citizen: If you hand me a pure
Earthen I'll take it and 50k ash and give you a [Crystalline]. 




Message sent to A Citizen:
crystalline

A Citizen: Oh wow if you've got a Crystalline I'll only
take 5k ash and I'll give you something I think is [better].





Message sent to A Citizen: better


A Citizen: Well yeah it's much
better, it helps your alertness and stealth allowing you to better ambush and
avoid ambushes by 10% per piece. [But]...





Message sent to A Citizen: but


A Citizen: But you can't trade it
with others. Even a fool could enchant and disenchant the pieces though so
that's no problem. And if you really [want]...





Message sent to A Citizen: want


A Citizen: You can hand me the item
and I'll downgrade it back to what it was so you can trade it, but that's
[expensive].





Message sent to A Citizen:
expensive


A Citizen: Yeah expensive, like 999
runes and 100k ash expensive.











Back to home

pages.title: Chantcrafting
pages.text:
## Using and Developing Your Chantcraft Skill


Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright.

Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is prohibited.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.



Most of these values are taken off Cyril's site at http://www.angelfire.com/rpg2/rwk4/ and are used here with his permission.


General Information

Chantcraft is the crafting skill that includs enchanting and disenchanting items. That is, adding or removing relics from spells, armor, weapons, bows and arrows. When chanting items you use the skills of chantcraft and rune working. The same skill level is required to either enchant or disenchant an item, and it is based on the value of the item you are chanting, NOT on the relic. All relics are the same for chanting purposes. The base value is what that item would cost if you buy it from the �store�, meaning the actions, buy menu. So, a Rusty Dagger has a base value of 10 gold, a Rustless Lifeceaser a base value of 1 billion gold. The highest quality items are not available from the store but are either crafted by players or drop off beasts.


Requirements

Enchanting requires you to be the king of a kingdom and for that kingdom to have at least 1 rune in it, which you will consume in the process of chanting. You use the rune or runes each time whether you succeed or fail, but the item and relic are never in any danger. As item base value increases you reach a point where you move from using 1 rune per attempt to using 2, so the skill becomes more expensive to train. For the 3 highest quality classes of item, meaning demonic, earthen and crystalline, you will use 250 runes per attempt, succeed or fail. High level chanting becomes very expensive very quickly.


Chantcraft skill is capped at 10 for players who do not have a Tradeskill Manifesto (TSM) and at 999 for those with a TSM. If you browse the tables below you will notice that no items are trivial to chant with a skill of only 10, so without a TSM you will always have a chance of failure even when working with the cheapest items. The TSM is a quest item .



How It�s Done

To use chantcraft you use the actions menu, choose either enchant or disenchant as appropriate, select the item and relic to enchant or the item to disenchant, then hit submit. If you succeed in enchanting, your item and relic will be combined. If you succeed in disenchanting, your item will be split into the �pure� item and a pure relic. If you fail, you lose the rune or runes but nothing else happens.


You can�t enchant a new relic onto an item that already has a relic on it. If you want to change what relic is on an item, you first need to disenchant it and then re-enchant it with the new relic. This means, that switching the relic on any item would cost a minimum of 2 runes, meaning 2 bil gold. For this reason, it�s obvious that it would not be cost effective to change relics on cheap (low base) items and even more so when low value relics are involved.



Getting Started

You can lower the cost of getting started in chanting by disenchanting drops that have relics you can sell to the store for 1 bil each when the disenchanting succeeds. hantcraft covers both enchanting and disenchanting, so both processes train the skill. You would collect low base value items with relics on them like Cara, Death Spike, Blood Bath, Vamp Leech, Stream Of Conscious or Dex Hoist to get back the 1 bil cost when you sell one relic. You can also craft (or buy, but it�s not as cost efficient that way) 5 of the 20k base relics (Confusion, Life Roulette, Annulment, Damage Shield, Damage Capacitor) then disenchant items with that same relic on them. Once you have 6 pure relics of that type in inventory, the first one will sell back to the store for 1 bil, recouping your disenchanting cost. This approach works until you get enough skill to need 2 runes per chant, then at best you only get back half your cost in a successful chant. Still, it can save you billions when you are starting and isn�t much trouble to do. I find it helps to have a mule to store the items on while collecting them, and then disenchant several with the same relic in a one session so I get more 1 bil sales from the same 5 �primer� relics.



Skill Requirements

The tables below are divided into �normal items� and �special items� using the conventional terms of RWK. Normal items are just that, rusty daggers, timeless fortitude helmets, meteor swarm damage spells, etc. with nothing preceding the name. Special items are things like precise rusty daggers, durability timeless fortitude helmets, rapid meteor swarm spells. Special items cost twice as much to craft as their normal equivalents, and also need more skill to chant. The first table shows the minimum skill levels (skill available) when you can start chanting normal items and also the skill at which chanting them becomes trivial. When it�s trivial, you will never fail in a chanting attempt but you will also never gain any further skills chanting that item. If you want to progress, you need to work with more expensive items. The second table shows the skill levels for chanting special items, and you will see how they require a lot more skill.

Forge Risen | Forge Talon | Expurgation of the Forge | Upgraded from Shadow Scale

Requires SoMC ) and a Forge kingdom. First item consumes 10 million Forge Trs gold and 5 Great Gem Essence Elements. Each additional item consumes increasing amounts of gold and twice as many Great Gems as the previous one as explained here .

It's likely the Forge heal Forgeneration (Calcium Enriched) is the only "Normal" item players would make, the others being "Specials".

They cannot be traded | 75? | 125 to chant and 95 to disenchant? | These items retain their Shadow Scale boosts and add the following:

Mantle - 15% Cripple and Crippling Resist (Courtesy of Evincarvolrath)

Shield - 15% Cripple and Crippling Resist, 10% Damage Mitigation (Courtesy of Evincarvolrath)


From the RWK Forum: "The effects of cripple are severe for a round of a fight. All stats are reduced by 80%, including attack and heal ratings. The effect of all ruby, sapphire, emerald, and void resists innate to armors is halved.

There is a less important side effect that will permanently reduce your stats by 1,000 each. This will always happen in Inquest, but will only happen outside of Inquest if you are above the hard cap and have been active for that hour."


Weps and Spells +100% Dur


70% theurg casting damage boost, 10% chance for second theurg cast


NOTE: These items can NOT be traded between players |








Most of the pre-Demonic item values are taken off Cyril's site at
http://www.angelfire.com/rpg2/rwk4/ and are used here with his permission.



Last update 31 March 2025

pages.title: RWK Relic Crafting Skills
pages.text:
RWK Relic Crafting Skills

## Crafting Relics

Values up to Denial of the Believer are taken off Cyril's site , the ones requiring skills above that are additions based on my experience and input from other players.

The data below show the skill levels you need to attempt to make each relic and when they become trivial. If you attempt to make a relic with the minimum skill at which it is available you can, and most likely often will, fail in the attempt and you will just lose your gold. When the relic is trivial it will never fail and you will succeed every time, but you also will never gain any additional crafting skills making that item. To grow in skill you must risk failure.

Note that the relics shown in red text have a special cost structure. If you do not have any of those relics in pure form, that is relics unattached to items, the cost will be 5k or 10k gold as shown. If you already have a pure relic and attempt to craft another of that same relic the cost will increase by a factor of 10 for each additional relic you try to make. For example, you have no pure strong arm relics (strong arm attached to items like weapons, armor, spells don't count here) and try to craft one. It will cost you 5,000 gold. If you succeed and get the relic, then try to craft another strong arm, that second attempt will cost you 50,000 gold. Both relics do exactly the same thing, the increase in cost is just how Glitchless makes crafting relics more challenging. If your second attempt succeeds, the third one will cost you 500,000 gold and the cost keeps increasing up to 500 million gold for the 6th relic. The same cost increase occurs for the 10k relics up to a cost of 1 billion gold for crafting the 6th pure one.

If you want to avoid this steep cost increase you need to frequently sell your relics back to the store before you craft more of the same one. The price for the 500 mil and 1 bil relics does not change, they start out expensive enough. Other items in the game, armor, weps, spells, bows and arrows, do not increase in cost as you craft more of the same thing.
