pages.title: Embezzlement Calculator
pages.text:
Embezzlement Calculator 





## Embezzlement Calculator


After editing a field, click somewhere outside of that field and the page will automatically update.


Please Note: It is highly recommend to subtract at least 1000 from the listed Embezzlement Amount; failure to do so could result in your kingdoms not being ready to bezz. The forumlas used by this calculator are not the exact same ones RWK uses. Also, I suggest you use the calculator as a starting point to see how close it gets to the result you want, then make fine tuning adjustments to the embezzled amount as needed to achieve your goal.







Number of Kingdoms: | 
| 
Kingmanship: | 
| 



Number of Hours: | 
| 
Collectia: | 
(Tick for "Yes") | 










Your Corruption: | 
0% | 
Embezzlement Amount: | 
0 | 






## Here is a short introduction to how public embezzling works.

When public embezzling was originally added to the game it was possible for a character to embezzle another character's kingdoms. A sub-economy developed where players with large complexes would arrange for others to help embezzle their kingdoms and in return they would pay those embezzlers some amount that had agreed upon in advance. After some years with that function the process was changed and restricted to only working for a character embezzling its own kingdoms. In its current form a better name would be "remote embezzling" since it's not accessible to the public anymore but does allow us to send money from one location to another far away and have the embezzled gold automatically converted to runes in the destination as its treasury becomes full. The process is still very useful, just different from what it was at the start.

To use this feature in your kingdoms you first need a Harbingers Scepter of Delegation . Once you have that quest item on your character pick a kingdom you own where you want embezzled gold to be deposited in the treasury and use the command /pea "amount", where ,"amount" is how much gold you want to move from each remote kingdom into the recipient location. So, if I want to embezzle 250,000,000 gold from my other kingdoms I'd type /pea 250000000 while I am in the location where I want it to be collected.



After preparing the recipient kingdom, you will need a pure Leather of Delegation (LoD) relic to embezzle remotely. These are crafted by players with enough tradeskill. It has to be pure but does not need to be equipped. With an LoD in your inventory move to a remote kingdom of yours that has a full treasury (2 billion gold) and use the command /pe "destination" where "destination" is the kingdom you set up with the /pea command. If my pea location was 140,Sur,190 I would type /pe 140,Sur,19 and 250,000,000 gold would be transferred from my current location to the treasury at 140,Sur,190. Move through the kingdoms from which you want to embezzle and repeat the /pe command in each one. If you have hotkeys enabled you just need to tap the corresponding number each time and the process will move along very quickly.



When the destination Trs reaches 2 billion gold (and is then full) runes will be automatically be purchased for that location. You will see how many runes are there each time you use the /pe command. When the destination gets to 999 runes you cannot embezzle to it anymore since it's full. Either remove some runes or set up another destination to use. The /pe command can only be used to embezzle your own kingdoms, a change from it's original operation (thanks to Lord Asrock for pointing that out, I had missed noting that change).


All this remote embezzling works on any plane EXCEPT the Forge. You can't embezzle to or from Forge kingdoms using the /pe or /pea commands.



Interest will only compound for 24 hours with no player activity except for characters who have the special relic combo of a Devil's Opulent Deliverance and Devil's Warring Deliverance equipped. They will have up to 48 hours of interest accrued as opposed to the standard 24




Page last modified 22 November 2023

pages.title: Drop Rates
pages.text:
Drop Rates 





## Kingdom Drop Rates



Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright.

Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is prohibited.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.



Page last updated 31 August 2018



Drop Rates


Broadly speaking, drop rates in RWK are not the same for every creature or every location, although the vast majority of locations in the game are the same. As you kill harder enemies on the "New Fight" menu, moving from Magician�s Apprentices to Yoro Hi Hi Samurais on the Surface, the drop rate declines overall. This means that, on average, you get fewer drops when killing Yoros than when killing Magician�s Apprentices. The drop rate does not vary with the plane where you fight. Normal kingdoms in Heaven and Hell have the same drop rate from a given level creature (determined by its stats) as that enemy gives on Surface. A few locations do have higher drop rates than normal. Surface has Stallion's Pub (150,Sur,145) and Le Puddle Deep (140,Sur,190 but requires Aqualungs to reach) which both have higher drop rates than other Surface kingdoms. A few kingdoms located on other planes have even higher drop rates than the Puddle and Pub.


Quite a few years ago I collected data on the number of drops I received from about 16,000 kills spread among the easiest 30 enemies in 4 kingdoms with the highest drop rates (Heaven and Hell Keeps, Sky Shrine, Insane Dungeon). The overall average I found was 4.8 kills/drop with a standard deviation (1 sigma) of 0.63 (13% of the average). So, about 5 kills per drop in any of the highest drop rate kingdoms was a reasonable expectation for these enemies. My guess was that the Forbidden Palace has the same rates as these kingdoms but I did not confirm that idea at the time.


I recently expanded the data to include normal Surface and Forge kingdoms, Stallion's Pub (150,Sur,145), Le Puddle Deep (90,Sur,140), The Forbidden Mines (162,Sur,159), and the Forbidden Palace (162,Hev,159). The method I generally used was to kill either 250 (old data) or 300 (new data) enemies and count the drops I received. This approach works well in the high drop rate locations but not as well in normal kingdoms or anywhere with a drop frequency that is low enough for even 300 kills to not give a single dropped item. The results for the Insane Dungeon are truncated because I can't kill anything harder than what is shown in one attack and the process is just too slow if I have to make several attacks per kill. In some cases I made a lot more then 300 kills either because I was on a roll or I wanted to get more relevant data from a location that was not dropping much. Overall, I've logged the results for a total of about 48,000 kills and 4,000 drops so far.



There are 70 enemies in the "New Fight" menu so I've numbered them with the easiest (at the top) as 1 and the hardest (at the bottom) as 70. The �Creature Stats� column shows the value of that target�s stats (all stats are the same for any given enemy). You can find that value by using the �Kingdom� menu, and scrolling down to �Guard�. The cost of that enemy as a kingdom guard is also the value for each of its stats and also the base value of all its drops. For example, a Yoro costs 1 bil gold to buy as a guard, all its stats (dur, dex, cnt, etc.) are 1 billion and all its drops will be 1 bil gold base items, like Rustless Lifeceasers, Timeless Fortitude armor, etc.



Given the variability in the results it looks like at least the easiest 30 enemies or so on the fight list, 23,000 base and lower, give the same drop rates in the off-surface special kingdoms. Those would be Heaven Keep (66,Hev,66), Hell Keep (66,Hell,66), Sky Shrine (100,Sky,250), Forbidden Palace (162,Hev,159) and the Insane Dungeon (100,Dun,200). Somewhere between enemy 30 and 44 (23k and 960 k) the drops become less frequent but I can�t tell from these data exactly what that transition looks like. In any case, I�d say it doesn�t matter which of those 30 easiest enemies you fight in those locations, you can expect the same average drop rate from all of them. The Palace takes a little longer to collect these data because the enemies there void my relics about every 10 fights. As a pure vamp that stops my damage cold even with an easy enemy.


Drops the Puddle (140,Sur,190), Stallion's Pub (150,Sur,145), the Forbidden Mines (162,Sur,159), and normal kingdoms follow a similar trend but the numbers are so much lower that the results are less clear. It was commonly believed that drop frequency in the Puddle is the same as the Pub and that is what I found. Overall I'd say that there are no kingdoms on Surface or the Forge that come close to the drop frequency of the five special locations off-Sur. On Surface the Puddle and Pub will have more frequent drops than other locations, but they will still not be what I'd call "frequent" compared to the high-drop, off-surface locations. I think low drop frequency is why the data for these locations don't show a clear or consistent trend. Too few results to smooth out the randomness. I'd like to collect more drop numbers for the low frequency locations to see if the results smooth out and give more insight into what is reasonable to expect there. I will update this page if I get around to doing that.


Low Drop-Rate Locations: Puddle=Le Puddle Deep (140,Sur,190) ; Pub=Stallion's Pub (150,Sur,145) ; Forge=The Forge Kingdoms ; Mines=The Forbidden Mines (162,Sur,159) ; Surface=Normal Surface Kingdoms.

After consolidating all the new and old data I graphed the results to show the trends in the high drop frequency locations and you can clearly see how flat it is for the lowest 30 creatures and then the curve begins to slop upwards in what looks like a linear progression. Some of the data in the graph were left out of the table to keep it from getting too busy. Given the scatter I would say that the drop frequency for a given enemy is the same in the Keeps, Shrine and ID and all those locations give drops much more often than the other locations on any plane.












back to home

pages.title: Forge Hermit
pages.text:
## The Forge Hermit










The Forge Hermit, located at 24,For,32, was added to the game in June 2018. In order to use it, or get most of the chat dialog, you need to have access to The Forge plane so you can go to its location. Forge access requires a Sanction of Serenity . This Hermit offers several quest items that waive the stat requirements to qualify as a master of class and they also offer some character boosts. For Fighters, Casters, and Vampires the cost of each quest item is 25,000 ash.


For Theurgists and Monks the price is much higher, you must give up 2 DaD or DaDev relics (thanks to Tris on Server 1 for pointing this out to me). The upside is that the hermit offers a refund if a player is not happy with these items. That refund will cost 50,000 ash for the Friar's Staff and presumably the same for the Theurg one. Thanks to Evincarvolrath for reminding me about the refunds and providing the ash cost and Niizandar for pointing out I had forgotten to add the refund information here.


At this time (24 June 2018) Archers do not have a quest item in this category.


The properties are given on the quests page.


The dialog I was able to trigger from the Fore Hermit is shown below. There may be more available but these were the only responses for which I could find triggers. The dialog is shown in the order they appeared, which is opposite to how they look within the in-game chat window. I reversed them to make them easier to follow.


Message sent to Forge Hermit: hello

Forge Hermit You're as ugly as you sounded. What do you want


Message sent to Forge Hermit: want

Forge Hermit I have things that might interest Theurgists or Monks. Fighters, Casters, and Vampires, too. Not mouthy ones.


Message sent to Forge Hermit: vampire

Message sent to Forge Hermit: caster

Message sent to Forge Hermit: Fighters

Same reply each time:

Forge Hermit Those each could benefit from a different item I could make for say... 25k ash. Be the type you want and give me 25 gold.


Message sent to Forge Hermit: monk

Forge Hermit Ya, Monk or Theurgist, either or. A staff that shines in darkness. But it'd have a hefty cost.


Message sent to Forge Hermit: staff

Forge Hermit Well, I never had a dad. So I'd want two of yours. The staff would have great powers. I'll refund it if you don't like it.


Message sent to Forge Hermit: refund

Forge Hermit That's right. If you don't like it, give it back... along with some ash. Otherwise, give me the damn relics.



18 March 2020

The Forge Hermit can also help settle Forge kingdoms with less clicking. See the Announcements (type /ann in game). Chesty Puller was good enough to explain. If you visit the Forge Hermit at 024,For,032 and pm "settle" you can get a short dialog from him "Ya, toss me some treasures if you want to try and settle here faster." You don't need to do this, it's just informational.


Move to the Forge location you want to settle and trade treasure elements to the Hermit for your attempts. An EEoT=25 chances to settle, EEoGT=125, EEoST=500. When the attempt fails the Hermit pm's you "Sucks to be you, haha!" and "*deep breath* No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No.No." That was 25 No's for an EEoT. When your attempt succeeds you get a "Yes" and the kingdom is yours.


This method consumes an entire treasure element even if the settlement succeeds on the first attempt. Kingrico on S1 used an EEoST to settle a kingdom and it took less than 25 tries to succeed (less than 50 billion gold) but the entire treasure was still lost in the process.













This page last updated 30 August 2020

pages.title: Free Cards
pages.text:
## Tracking Quest Free Card Drops


Copyright 2023 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved


Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is prohibited.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.




Players who Kill a Nightmare Fly or Dragonling Zombie beast begin a tracking quest that ends with the addition of a new stat called "Free Red Card" or "Free Black Card" to their /st list. These values show your percentage chance to not consume a black or red sleeved card when you trigger one before fighting. The value stacks as you repeat the quests. I've only done these quests a few times because I think they are pretty awful, but Seiyakou on S2 had enough fortitude to do them 20 times and then generously shared the details of how the stat increased after each kill.

As is often the case in RWK there is a dimishing return on the benefit you get by repeating the quest. The results are wshown in this table. You asymtotically approach a 60% chance of not losing a card but never actually reach 60%. There seems to be little reason to do these quests more than 10 times each unless you enjoy it and are repeating for the entertainment value.

Here are the same data presented graphically, which show clearly how the free card chance levels off and approaches 60%.

My thanks to Seiyakou for collecting these data and generously sharing them with me to present to the RWK community.

pages.title: Gems
pages.text:
Gems 







## Gems







Copyright 2017 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved




Gems are elements that randomly drop in The Forge for players who have kingdoms on that plane. The drops can occur when a Forge kingdom gains interest. The information I have here is a mix of what I've done firsthand and what is very well explained in more detail in a Reddit post by amnislupus you can find here .


Gems come in 3 flavors (Ruby, Emerald and Sapphire) and are used to upgrade Dragon Essences. Three new skills were also added with these elements; Ruby, Emerald and Sapphire Hording. As you train each skill your chance of getting that gem to drop increases provided you are in a kingdom that has a boost for that particular gem. For example, if your kingdom has a boost for Sapphires you will find them more often at that location as your Sapphire Hording skill increases. However, Sapphire is the only gem you will ever find in that kingdom, so your Ruby or Emerald Hording skill level won't come into play there.


The hording skills apparently don't help in locations with no gem boost, so interest boosted kingdoms and locations with no boost at all give a random mix of gem types regardless of your hording skills.


The basic process of adding gems to your already equipped Dragon Element is to move to a kingdom of yours that has 999 runes and trade one of the gem type you want to equip to the Surface Hermit. He will take the gem(s) and the runes and then your gem(s) will be equipped to your equipment slot(s). The graphic display shows gems as a colored graphic overlay and hovering your cursor over that slot will show you its rank, based on how many gems were added, as an r value. Adding one gem to a slot raises it to rank 1, which shows when you hover as r1. The second gem added to that same slot brings it to r2, etc.



If you can get to gem rank 10 the system message changes to "....commands powers beyond that of known gems" and you start seeing a value in the stat "Gem Resist" but I don't know what the stat does. I thank Mav73, Obsidian Night and Pro for sharing that information.


As you use gems they are added to each DE you have equipped starting with the lowest rank position. If you have 10 DE's and add gems each will upgrade a single slot starting with your left hand and moving across and down. Adding 2 gems will upgrade your left hand and head each to r1. Adding 10 gems will bring all your slots to r1. Every rank increase for a slot will double the cost of the next one. So getting an item to r1 only consumes 1 gem, but getting it to r5 takes 31 (1+2+4+8+16).At some point, I think after r5, you start needing Great Gems which are made by condensing 32 ordinary gems into a single item.


Since any gem will overwrite all previously equipped ones you can use "cheaper" gems to get an item up in rank and then finish with the gems you actually want to use at the end, provided you have enough gems available to complete the upgrade. For example, if you have 1 DE and want to upgrade it to r5 with emeralds you could do it all with 31 emeralds or raise it to r4 with 15 sapphires and then finish with just 16 emeralds. This approach lets you use all your gems and still get to the flavor you want at the end, or buy cheaper ones for the preliminary upgrades and only use the more expensive ones for the final step.






GREAT GEMS 

Great gems or 32 regular ones can be used to upgrade character gem boosts to r6. It is likely that r7 and higher require Great gems only and they are also required to make all forged items.
Great gems are made by "condensing" 32 regular gems into one item, similar to the way 50, 250 or 999 runes can be combined to make treasure elements. Unlike treasures, you can't reverse the process to convert Greats back into regular gems.

To make Great Gems you need to have at least 32 regular Gems of the same type (ruby, sapphire or emerald) and at least 999 ash on your character and be in a kingdom with 999 runes where your character is the king. Give the Surface Hermit 32 gold and it will take 32 gems, all 999 runes and 999 ash and in return will give you 1 Great Gem. You can add the hermit to your trade list with the command /add Surface Hermit.


Forging
Forge Crafted items were added on 22 Feb 2019. Making them requires having a Forge kingdom with enough gold in the Trs, enough Great Gems, and a pure (no relic attached) Shadow Scale item to upgrade. The skill required is currently (26 Feb 19) in dispute but seems to start at either 765 or 785. Either way the cost doubles with each item and the time required also increases. The first Forged item costs 10 mil gold (from your Trs) and 5 Great Gems, the second item consumes 20 mil gold and 10 Great Gems, and the cost keeps increasing as shown below. You will find the forged items at the very bottom of your crafting list. If you have the required Great Gems on your character and are in your kingdom with at least the minimum amount of Trs gold, you select the item you want to forge and which is now a pure Shadow Scale on your character, click "Submit", and the process will begin.




The amount of gold and number of Great gems needed to craft a Forged item increases with each one you make on a given character. The numbers are:

Forge Risen Item Crafting Requirements (Courtesy of Peutre).



Item Number | Great Gems Needed | Trs Gold Consumed | Forging Time (days) |

3rd | 15 | 28,000,000 | 15 |

4th | 20 | 39,200,000

pages.title: RWK Girlfriends
pages.text:
RWK Girlfriends 





## Girlfriends in RWK


Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright.

Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.





On 9 Sept 2006 a new feature was added to RWK in celebration of its fifth anniversary � girlfriends! These aren�t intended to be your virtual sex slaves, they are character enhancements you can earn through your kills. The way it works is that the first 100 kills you make each day after you reach level 10 will earn you girlfriend points (GF points) which you can apply towards adding a �girlfriend� to your character to enhance some of its (your character�s, not your girlfriend�s) abilities. Different girlfriends provide different �services� as explained on the Glitchless GF page and summarized in the table below. As I read the explanation, you can eventually have all 8 girlfriends at the same time if you earn enough points, but will take a huge number of points to reach that stage. You get your first with 7,500 points, which will only be 2 days worth of killing 1-bil base critters. The second requires 22,500 points, 3x as many as the first, and the third 67,500, 3x the number for the second. With this progression players killing 1 bil critters can collect 3 in only two weeks and have the fourth within a month, but after that they will take a lot of time to increase. Plan ahead and get the ones you want most first, because you won�t likely be getting them all.





You only get a message showing your points every 10th kill but they do accumulate for every kill that is among your first 100 of the day. You want all your kills to be the hardest critter you can manage so the total points after 10 will be as high as possible. Kill 10 Yoros and you get 700 points in total, kill 9 Magician�s Apprentices then 1 Yoro and the kill message will show the full 70 for your �last kill� but your total for the set of 10 will only be 79.





Beasts give more points than normal critters, all guards only give 1 point, and dueling other players gives no points. All locations give the same points, so there is no point boost in different planes or special locations. There is also a new set of commands on the actions page to use for managing your girlfriends. If you don�t like your choice, or choices, you can type /dump to eliminate all girlfriends you currently have and then you can begin over again. You don�t get back the points, you will be starting from scratch.





When you collect enough points to choose a girlfriend you will receive the following message in purple text:





You now have enough points for a girlfriend! Type /date followed by the name of the girl you wish to date. Or type /girlfriend for the names and abilities of the different girls.

Stop fighting at this point and choose a Girlfriend. You will not collect more Girlfriend points until you choose to "date" one even though you keep fighting, so start collecting towards the next girl and don't waste those kills.





NOTE : Theurgists, Theurgal Divinators and Archer Theurgists have no innate resists, so Rezizthis will not help those classes. Glitchless has confirmed this.

Your Girlfriend choices, taken from the official RWK site.







Girlfriend Name | 
What She Does | 




Revivia | 
10% chance to spontaneously revive you if you die (similar to Tears of the Fallen) | 



Transcend | 
Doubles the number of stat points you get each training, thus speeding your leveling. Doubles the effectiveness of runes for defending your kingdom (does not stack with DRC). | 



Vampeyes | 
Doubles your modified durability stat | 



Rezizthis | 
Doubles innate class resistances to voidance and mesmerization

NOTE: If your class has NO innate resists Rezizthis will do nothing | 



Darkharvest | 
Gives you a 5% chance of getting 10 ash instead of 1 when destroying an item with relic attached.

Overall boost averages to 45% more ash (from destroying items) than without Darkharvest.

Does not affect your ash from other sources. | 



Greenlust | 
Increases the gold reward from Gold rushes by 50x in normal Sur kingdoms and 500x in normal Hell, Dun, Sky and Hev kingdoms.

Both the frequency with which you get rushes and the amount of gold per rush increase for the final, overall boost.

Also increases your odds for gaining a skill by 5%. | 



Collectia | 
Increases interest rate from kingdom treasury by 10% | 



Silence | 
Prevents system messages from showing everyone who and where you are attacking when you wage war, and hides your identity from the king you are attacking (although he or she does see that the kingdom was attacked).

This feature can be disabled if you choose by using the Actions menu, Options, War Message Filtering. |

pages.text:
## The Language Of Race War Kingdoms

Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright.

Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.

File last updated 17 May 2026

Return to home

Term | Meaning | Type |

9x9
9x9x2 | Shorthand for doing 999,999,999 damage (nine 9�s hence 9x9) to an enemy when attacking it This is the max damage a weapon or spell can deliver When you have two weapons or spells each do 9x9 damage it�s referred to as 9x9x2 (just under 2bil total damage). |
gameplay |

/BB | Beast Bane
Quest item, purchased for $100 or available from the Ash Keeper for 100,000 ash , Using the /bb command instantly teleports you to a location one kingdom away from the location of the most recent beast . You need to move that last kingdom manually. Works for all beasts. | quest |

bnb | Beast Noobane
Quest item,random drop found anywhere you can use from level 50 and up.The /bn command instantly teleports you to a location one kingdom away from the location of the most recent Sur a href="http://www.jimcav.com/beasts.htm">beast .You need to move that last kingdom manually. |
quest |

/q |
Quickness
Formerly awarded to Acumen winners, no longer being awarded Using the /q command instantly teleports you to a location one kingdom away from the location of the most recent beast
You need to move that last kingdom manually Works for most, but not all, beasts . |
quest |

AA | Ancient Alchemar
A special beast that can appear randomly on any plane except Sur, you should have an Scepter of Annulment (SoA) to fight them, you need to have both a Scroll of Forbidden Rights and a Beast Bane to attack them Without those two quest items, you will get an error message when you try to attack one. | Beast , gameplay |

AB |
Attacker's Balance
A random quest drop that can greatly enhance a player's damage potential. | quest |

Access | The ability to leave the Surface and access the other planes in the game. In total there are 6 separate planes stacked one atop the other, in order from bottom to top they are: The Forge, Hell, Dungeon, Surface, Sky and Heaven. | gameplay |

Account Key | A secondary password providing extra security that you should set once you log into your character Use the �actions� menu, �options�, �change account key� and create a key for yourself (you create the key, it�s not assigned to you) You will need this key to recover your login password if you ever forget it, and I suggest you write down BOTH passwords, which character they are for, and store it in a safe place that you will remember when you need it Do not use the same password for both your login and account key, it�s poor security. And keep in mind that you do not control the account until you control BOTH passwords. | gameplay |

Accurate Spell | Compared to normal spells, Accurate double your Cnc | items |

Acumen |
The Acumen Inquest is a monthly tournament where players duel each other for prizes Restricted to the top 128 players torelog during a roll call at the start of the acumen. All participants win some ash, the overall winner wins game credit. | Gameplay |

AD | armor defense, more AD means you take less damage when attacked | items |

alch | Short for �alchemize� which basically allows you to convert runes into gold Players with a Scepter of Unending Repulsion or better can type /alch and convert 1rune into 1 billion gold, other players can all use the /na command (newbiealch) to convert 1 rune into 999,999,999 gold Players with a Harbinger�s Scepter of Oppression can alch 2 runes at a time and can do it from distant kingdoms. This method to basically sell runes is handy for using kingdoms as gold storage or to convert poker rune winnings into gold. | gameplay |

Allegiance | Allegiance relic, raises Kingmanship skill by 5 | relics |

"An abomination fails to take flight" | A yellow system message that appears when a Dragonling Zombie beast spawns on Sur | Beast |

Angel's Tears |
Tears of the Fallen relic, 50% chance of being saved from death for 1stTotF equipped, 10% for each one equipped thereafter You make one by giving a super relic to the Pub Keeper (you lose the super relic in the process) |
relics |

annul | Annulment relic, Cancels two of enemies relics/enchantments | relics |

Apex | The Apex relic, Doubles leveling speed but also doubles your delay factor (not stackable, beast drop, crafted, or obtained from the Hell Hermit for 100 runes, pm the Hermit) | relics |

APoS | Archer's Pouch of Secrecy, a quest item | quest |

approbation | Approbations are relics you can see your choices for approbation by using the �Actions� menu, �Options�, �Preferred Approbation� This setting has two functions One is when you use the sky hermits to enchant spells, weapons, shields, bows or arrows.

Your preferred approbation is the relic they will add to the item The second use is when you kill certain beasts

You will get your preferred approbation attached directly to your character's body parts You can have a total of up to 10approbations+blessings added this way Approbations will replace blessings, but blessings will not replace approbations Approbations do not take up any inventory space, they are always equipped, and they do not show in your inventory list You can see how many approbations you have by setting your relic display to verbose (actions menu, options, relic display) and then attacking something Read how the relics fire and the ones that are on body parts instead of equipment are approbations or blessings

You can also enhance them by adding Unborn Torment or Dragon Eater elements to them (see the quests page) Not all beasts give approbations, including the Unborn Torments, Goblin King, Thief and Miner Some that do give approbations include Crucified Corpse, Darkest Widow,Eclyptic Hell Angel, Rogue Goblin, Lord Alchemar. | gameplay, quest |

Ash | Game slang for any useless item with a relic attached, when destroyed these items give 1 gold and 1 ash. | gameplay |

Ash | Destroy 1 item with a relic and you get 1 ash, type /ash to see how much you have Ash is used is to summon beasts, put devoids on D*D relics , make a Sanction of Serenity or make treasure elements. Large amounts of ash (20k to 100k) can get you quest upgrades like aqualungs, dun and sky access, SP, etc You can't sell or trade ash itself, you trade the drops that are used to make ash Players who buy or sell ash in auction actually mean drops. You need access to hell and Templar to use ash to summon beasts . | gameplay |

ash buyer | player buying drops to convert into ash | gameplay |

Ash Collector | an NPC located at 141,Hell,174 who is used to summon beasts . Pay him a visit, give him a gold piece and have a little chat to begin his dialog, you will need all planes access and the Templar upgrade to reach him. | Non Player Character ( NPC ) |

Ash Keeper |
an NPC at 150,Sur,150 who can take your ash and provide quest items : ocean bubble (20,000 ash), Sky access , Dungeon access , Scepter of Annulment , Templar upgrade (50,000 ash each), Skill Pontificator or Beast Bane (100,000 ash each). |
Non Player Character ( NPC ) |

Ash Lottery | A daily contest you are automatically entered into on reaching level 5 Each day a winner is randomly chosen by the server and wins 10,000 ash Everyone wins a small amount, but less than 100 ash unless you get the jackpot.. |

Gameplay |

Ashen Rocks | Random drops that begin with a white ashen rock As you continue playing it transforms into a grey ashen rock and finally a black ashen rock Each rocks will give a bonus, you choose whether it�s in ash or great treasures , when you either spend $50 or more on the game or use the Ash Keeper for a 50,000 or 100,000 ash upgrade. The payouts for each Rock vary randomly within a range that depends on the Rock color:
White
� 4,000 to 6,000 ash or 1 to 2 EEoGT's
Grey
� 6,000 to 8,000 ash or 2 to 3 EEoGT's
Black
- Black Rocks give 8,000 to 10,000 ash or 3 to 4 EEoGT's | Gameplay |

asher | Someone paid to log into another person's character and collect drops This is against the rules of the game (see the 7/5/2005 announcement ) and can get both players banned. | gameplay |

Assail | An ability that comes with the Scepter of Harnessed Hatred and its upgrades If you have the ability to attack 2 or 3 kingdoms at once and the first kingdom in your path is an enemy kingdom and you own the second kingdom in your path then you will do 10 times normal damage to the first kingdom. | Gameplay quest |

Backpack | Quest item requiring Hell access to obtain, lets you store 75 items instead of 50 on your character. | quest |

base |
An item's "base" is what that item (weapon, spell, armor, etc.) costs in pure form if you buy it from the store (actions menu, buy) So, a Rusty Dagger has a base of 10 gold, aRustlessLifeceaser (RLC) a base of 1 billion gold. |
items |

/bb |
Beastbane quest item, available by purchase for $100 or from the Ash Keeper for 100,000 ash . |
quest |

BB |
Blood Bath relic, Lowers enemy health by 60% OR by 60% of your durability, whichever is lower. |
relics |

beast | Special creatures that can drop special items, and are usually much harder to kill than normal ones Most beasts have 2bilDur, high defensive skill, and require up to 13 annuls to fight, or an SoA. Many give approbations and they can drop the best items in the game. |
Beasts , gameplay |

beetle |
Polymorphic
Beetle , a special beast that can appear randomly on any plane except Sur, you need an
SoA or 8 annuls to fight them. |
beast |

beg |
begging is asking for free items or gold, it's against the rules , and can get you muted or banned. |
gameplay |

Bellows alongside the Mine Keeper | Part of the system messages (yellow text) for a few quests: [Playername] bellows alongside the Mine Keeper...All shall tremble before him� shows when a player gets a Scepter of Unending Repulsion [Playername]bellows alongside the Mine Keeper...For he now commands the power of true oppression!� shows when a player gets a Harbinger�s Scepter of Oppression | quest |

besiege | An ability that comes with the Scepter of Unending Repulsion and its upgrades. If you have the ability to attack 3 kingdoms at once and the first kingdom in your path is an enemy kingdom and you own the second and third kingdom in your path then you will do 25 times normal damage to the first kingdom. | Gameplay, quest |

Bez ,bezzler | Short for embezzle or embezzler Players with a pure leather of delegation (LoD) relic can use it to help kings with a scepter of delegation embezzle the gold from their kingdom treasuries The embezzler usually gets paid a percentage of what he embezzles. | ameplay |

blessing | Blessings are relics you can see your choices for blessing by using the �Actions� menu, �Options�, �Preferred Blessing� This setting has two functions One is when you use the Dungeon hermits to enchant spells, weapons, shields, bows or arrows Your preferred blessing is the relic they will add to the item The second use is when you kill a Polymorphic Beetle beast You will get your preferred blessing attached directly to your character's body parts You can have a total of up to 10blessings+approbations added this way Approbations will replace blessings, but blessings will not replace approbations Blessings do not take up any inventory space, they are always equipped, and they do not show in your inventory list You can see how many blessings you have by setting your relic display to verbose (actions menu, options, relic display) and then attacking something Read how the relics fire and the ones that are on body parts instead of equipment are approbations or blessings. | Gameplay |

blood | Essence of Blood quest item, drops off Ice LordAlchemar beasts | quest |

BP

Blood Pinch A relic that steals health from your enemy in the amount of 20% of your durability.

Available from the in-game Actions>Buy>relics menu, player craftable and a common relic on drops. |
relics |

BS | Brain Swell relic, Doubles your Intelligence (only dropped off beasts) | relics |

BT | Blood Tyrant, higher level vampire You become a BT if you are a vamp with 67% of your base stats inDur This requires you to reach the level cap and transfer some stats, you can�t get 67% intoDur while leveling | gameplay |

burn | destroy an item using the Actions menu, pull down to destroy This is used to eliminate items with relics, drops, and in the process you get 1 gold and 1 ash Also used to destroy high level pure relics, like voidance, in exchange for 1bil gold but no ash. | gameplay |

Burns the flesh of the living |

System message (yellow text) �[Playername] burns the flesh of the living for the power of the dead� when
a player uses the Ash Collector to add a Devoidance relic to a D*D relic and create a D*Devoid relic. |

relics |

cap | Highest you can reach in the game, there are caps on level , skills, ash, etc When used as a verb, as in �Hey, I capped this month� it means to have reached the level cap. | gameplay |

cara | The Calling of Cara relic | relics |

cassius | The Calling of Cassius relic | relics |

causes all his kingdom walls to solidify | System message (yellow text) �[Playername] causes all his kingdom walls to solidify and his attackers become wrought with fear` is displayed when a player triggers an Essence Element of Stone (by trading it to the Quake Keeper) to increase the defenses of his kingdoms for whatever remains of one game hour (compliments of The Raven Reborn) | quest |

Causes the ground to shake | A system message �[Player] causes the ground to shake as his enemies fall to their knees and his troops fight with fervor.� = dropped an essence element ofTecton, an item for waging war� shows in yellow text when a player uses an Essence Element ofTecton to attack a kingdom. | gameplay |

Causes the oceans to boil | A system message �[Player] causes the oceans to boil red with blood, finding new and mysterious powers� appears when a player displays when someone upgrades a DDD relic to a D*D using a dark essence . | relics |

CC | Crucified Corpse , a special beast that can appear randomly on any plane except Sur, you need at least 1 annul to fight them. | Beast , gameplay |

CER | Caster's Eternal Revenge relic, it is an upgraded CR relic | relics |

Chained Libertine | NPC located at 69,Sur,69, she's involved in some quests You can pm using �/m Chained Libertine: message� | NPC |

chainkill | Repeatedly killing a player, ideally as quickly as he can revive This can prevent the player being killed from do anything in the game other than chat since you can't fight or move when deadChainkilling is one way players show their disapproval of someone. | gameplay |

chance | Using the /chance command, for players who have a Captain�s Staff of Valor (CSoV, quest item) and at least one Essence Element of Treasure (EEoT) Each time you use /chance the kingdom you are in at the time gains 10 runes (the good part) while each use also has a 20% chance of destroying anEEoT (worth 50bil gold, the bad part) The gain and chance for loss balance out, so over time the odds predict you will break even It�s a convenient way to quickly rune up kingdoms, provided you have enough treasure elements and can afford to lose some in the process. | gameplay |

A Citizen hands over a Devil's Deliverance to a very worthy recipient | A player used 250,000 ash to get a Devil�s Deliverance relic from the Sky Citizen | quests |

A Citizen rolls a xx | A player is trying to get a Sash of the Blood Rogue , each roll costs 250bil and the number wanted is 10 | quests |

class | Basically, your character�s fighting style The game has several different classes to play, each needs different stats and will use different items The choices are two types each of Fighter and Spellcaster , Vampire , Archer , Monk , and Theurgist A Hybrid has some characteristics for more than one class (I don�t recommend that approach) | classes |

clicker | Someone paid to log into another person's character and make kills to gain levels. This is against the rules of the game (see the 7/5/2005 announcement ) and can get both players banned. | gameplay |

The clouds darken as
ash fills the sky | A system message (yellow text) seen by all players when someone converts an Essence Element of Ash into 50,000 ash on his character. | quest |

Combines the powers of many into one | The system message (yellow text) �[Playername] combines the
powers of many into one� means a player completed the quest for an Omega Sanction | quest |

commands powers beyond that of known gems | System message (yellow test) "[Playername] commands powers beyond that of known gems" = A player has upgraded an equipped gem to rank 10 or higher (thanks to Kathstandsalone and Mav73 for that information). | quest |

Commands the power of Dragon Shards | System message (yellow text) �[Playername] Commands the power of Dragon Shards` = someone activated a Dragon Shard element (compliments of The Raven Reborn) | quest |

commands the power of a theurgal god. | System message (yellow text) �[Playername] commands the power of a theurgal god` = someone obtained the Theurgist`s Staff of Domination (compliments Driknar S1) | quest |

Consumed by Dark Light | System message (yellow text) "[Playername] is consumed by dark light..." = someone found a Dark Essence , a very rare drop | quest |

corruption | In RWK, corruption is when the amount of interest earned by your kingdom treasury is reduced, displayed as theprecentage of reduction The message you get looks like this:
�Your
treasury atxxx,Yyy,xxx has gained 7,262,437 interest in the last 16 hours. 89% lost to corruption. Total: 680,826,368�
Corruption
is triggered by owning more than1,000 kingdoms This effect was added on 7/20/2006 and modified on 30 May 2007, see the announcements
After you own more than 1,000 kingdoms, your interest loss will become greater as you add more kingdoms Similar to corruption was over-runing , which started in any kingdom having more than 5 runes Overruning was eliminated on 9 September 2007 so is no longer a factor in the game
For historical interest, how it worked is explained in several announcements from 31 Mat to 3 June 2007 and the values are shown here . | gameplay |

CR | Caster's Revenge relic, Calling of Cara + 50% concentration boost + 50% intelligence boost | relics |

creature stats | You can find the stats for any normal creature by using the "Kingdom" menu, expand it to "Guard" and see what the monster in question costs That is the same as all its stats, and the base value of all items it drops | gameplay |

Crystal | 1. Crystal of Lars , a quest item or
2. Crystalline items (armor,weps, spells), the third best quality available in the game Crystal items are beast drop or by upgrading earthen items with ash and the Heaven Citizen | quest |

D*D | Upgraded DDD relics | relics |

D*Dv | Any of the various D*D relics that has been upgraded by the Ash Collector with the addition of a Devoid . | relics |

DAoD | Double Axe of Durability, 2.2bil base weapon that doubles durability | items |

Dark Essence (DE) | Quest items that are rare, random drops and can also be made by trading super relics to the Puddle Keeper (who may just take the Super and give nothing in return) Dark Essences are used to upgrade DDD relics to various D*D types.

When one drops all players see a system message (yellow text) "PLAYER is consumed by dark light" (thanks to Alundra Mischef and Seiyakou for sharing that information). | relics |

DCC | Durability Clarified Creation armor, bow or arrow | items |

DD | Devil's Deliverance relic It is a Denial of the Believer + 50% boost to all stats + 10 Death Spikes + **Double Defense + Voidance + Annulment + Doubles your damage output | relics |

DDD | Devil's Deleterious Deliverance relic, Upgraded DD relic | relics |

DDLD | Demonic armor items, Durability Devouring Lifeless Demise | items |

DE |

Dragon Eater , a high level beast | gameplay |

Demonic , demo | Fourth best quality items in the game Beast drop crcraftable for 28 runes and a palace element. | items |

Devoid | Devoidance relic, Each yields a 10% chance to void all enemy enchantments + Confusion + 10%mesmerization resistance. Only dropped off beasts . |
relics |

DH | Dexterous Hoist relic, Boosts dexterity by 60% but can be annulled | relics |

DotB | Denial of the Believer relic, Calling of Cara + lowers enemy health by 90% OR by 90% of your durability, whichever is lower | relics |

DPP | Durability Protectant Prevailation armor, costs 1 billion gold and 24 runes to craft and boosts Dur by 21%,Str and Ntl by 20%. | items |

Dragon Eater | Any of three types of beast , that drop three different quest items , Dragon Shards, Flames and Storm which are upgrades to Unborn Torment elements . | Beast , quest |

Dragon Wizard | Another game by Glitchless, very simple and reminiscent of something from an old Atari console Primarily of interest to very new player, Dragon Wizard points can be converted into RWK gold. This can be done by players of any level, but the amounts earned will likely only appeal to new players. | gameplay |

DRC | Depending on who you talk to, it's either the DD Relic Combo or the Double Rune Combo A player with Devil's Opulent Deliverance , Devil's Warring Deliverance , Devil's Skillful Deliverance , and Devil's Accelerated Deliverance equipped doubles the effectiveness of all your kingdom's runes | relics |

Drives his Harbinger`s Scepter of Oppression into the beetle's
skull | System message (yellow text) �[Playername]
Wierdone drives his Harbinger`s Scepter of Oppression into the beetle's
skull and it begins to glow and change form� displays when someone upgrades a Harbinger`sScepter of Oppression to a Harbinger`s
Scepter of Delegation by killing a Polymorphic Beetle . |
quest |

drop | any item obtained by killing a game critter or from other in-game activity, such as moving around the map | in game activity |

DS | Death Spike relic, 1 milunannullable damage | relic |

Dungeon | One of the 5 planes in RWK, Dungeon is just below Surface and above Hell Access to Dun requires a Lava Splicer , Transcendental Sanction , Omega Sanction , or Trial Access You get 2x Surface gold in Dungeon and it�s needed for several quests . | gameplay |

Dungeon Hermit |
any of 4 NPC's in Dungeon who can add a blessing to any spell, weapon, shield, bow or arrow for 1bil gold Each hermit chants a different item slot, so you want to visit the correct one First spell 0,Dun,0; second spell 299,Dun,0; first weapon 0,Dun,299; second weapon 299,Dun,299 You can pm using �/m Dungeon Hermit: your message here� | NPC |

Dur | Durability armor, it adds 15% to yourDur stat and 15% more armor defense | items |

DW |
Darkest Widow , a special beast that
can appear randomly on any plane except Sur, you need anSoA or 3 annuls to fight them, plus you must have a RDADMSlessonbook . |
Beast , gameplay |

earthen |

Third best quality items in the game, same as tectonic Beast drop
or by upgrading Demonic items with ash and the Heaven Citizen | items |

ED | Encephalon Decay relic, 2 milunannullable damage | relics |

EEoA |
Essence Element of Ash, an item that can be converted into 50,000 ash These are available for $50 fromGlitchless and are used as prizes for winners of the monthly Acumen | items |

EEoGT | Essence Element of Great Treasure , tradable item that stores 250 runes Obtained from the Rune Keeper for 2.5 mil gold and 250 ash, or can be crafted with very high tradeskills. To convert runes to anEEoGT, use the command �/add Rune Keeper� to put that NPC onto your trade list Then use the actions menu, give gold, to give him 2.5 million gold He takes the gold and 250 ash, gives you 1EEoGT To convert the treasure back to runes simply trade it to the Rune Keeper. | quest |

EEoT | Essence Element of Treasure , tradable item that stores 50 runes Obtained from the Rune Keeper for 1 mil gold and 50 ash, or can be crafted with very hightradeskills. To convert runes to anEEoT, use the command �/add Rune Keeper� to put that NPC onto your trade list Then use the actions menu, give gold, to give him 1 million gold He takes the gold and 50 ash, gives you 1EEoT. To convert the treasure back to runes simply trade it to the Rune Keeper. | quest |

EHA |
Eclyptic Hell Angel , a special beast
that can appear randomly on any plane except Sur. You need anSoA or 12 or 13 annuls to fight them These drop theTradeskill Manifesto (TSM) |
Beast , quest |

embezzle |

The
process in RWL where gold is removed from a kingdom treasury (Trs) If you are the king you just use the Kingdom menu, embezzle to choose how much gold to remove from the kingdom where you are located If you are embezzling for another player who has a scepter
of delegation you will need a pure leather of delegation (LoD) relic in your inventory The king you embezzle for will set some of his kingdoms to receive embezzled gold You then move through his kingdoms using the /pe command in each one to send gold from the location you are in to the one set to receive embezzled gold The embezzler never gets to keep any of the embezzled gold, he or she needs to be paid in a totally separate process.

So,
if 150,Sur,150 is set by its owner to receive gold and you are located in another kingdom at 66,Hell,66 owned by that same king, you would type the command /pe 150,Sur,150 to send gold from the 66.Hell,66Trs to150,Sur,150 You then repeat this command in every kingdom you are embezzling until you are either finished, or the receiving kingdom is full (999 runes) at which point you would need to change the destination to another kingdom able to receive more gold. |

gameplay |

embezzler | No longer possible, but in the past it was someone who uses a leather of delegation (LoD) relic to help kings with a scepter of delegation embezzle the gold from their kingdom treasuries | gameplay |

Emerald | A Gem that drops in . The Forge | Gameplay |

Fangs | Fangs of theBloodletter , quest item for Blood Tyrants | quest |

FF | Freefortifier relic, see below | Relic , quest |

Finds paths to new lands |

The system message �[Playername] finds paths to new lands� shows in yellow text when a player completes the quest for a Transcendental Sanction | quest |

Finds the power to change worlds and command legions! | The system message �[Playername] finds the power to change worlds and command legions!� shows in yellow text when a player has found or made an Essence Element ofTecton | quest |

Fire | Essence Element of Fire , quest item that drops off Unborn Torment beasts | quest |

Fists | Any of the quests Enduring Fists, Fists of Scale, Fists of Flames, all for monks. |
quest |

Forbidden Mines | 162,Sur,159 you need a Scroll of Forbidden Rights to enter (very rare and random drop or $50 purchase) | gameplay |

Forbidden Palace | A special kingdom at 162,Hev,159 you need a Scroll of Forbidden Rights to enter (very rare and random drop or $50 purchase) Palace critters use relics to fight back and will annul, so player relics that can be annulled are useless. | gameplay |

Found a powerful relic | A system message (yellow text) of �[Playername] found a powerful relic in the cracked earth!!!� displays when a player finds a Devil�s Deliverance relic after dropping a Tecton , or something like � (Player) found a powerful relic while digging trenches around his kingdom!" after dropping s stone (courtesy ofEhlmaris on S1)
Very uncommon. | Relic |

Freefort | Freefortifier relic, drops off the Goblin King Sur beast, gives free improvements to kingdoms With each use of the /freefort command each kingdom fortification (moats, cannon, walls) plus trs is increased by 250 million (thanks to Dragon Eater on S1 for telling me the amount), so you can get 3.25 billion worth of improvements each use But, every time it's used, afreefort can break be lost Breakage is random, so it may happen on the first use, the 100th, or the 1,0000th If you pay an EEoGT (250bil) for one you break even with 77 uses. |

Relic , quest |

GC |

Game
credit, an amount Glitchless has credited to your account that can be used to purchase items from the company Winners of the acumen
can get game credit as a prize. |

gameplay |

GHB |

Goblin Hide Backpack , a quest item made using a Goblin

Hide , which drops off Goblin

The last 10 levels for players with SoS is a sort of upper soft cap where it takes 100 kills to train stats. The Announcements on 7/21/2007 included a vague notice that �The amount of ash you get from swimsuit votes after obtaining a certain amount has been reduced.� I don�t know what the �certain amount� is or how much it�s been reduced Swimsuit voting has been abused by players several times and eGlitchless keeps making it less valuable as a result. | gameplay |

taming a beast | Sometimes a beast doesn't die when attacked, it flees the kingdom and becomes a guard in a different location The player who makes it flee gets the same benefits as killing it, and beast guards don't give any special drops when they are subsequently killed. | gameplay |

TC | Theurgal Conquest relic, upgraded TE relic. Raises Theurgist damage by a factor of 6.25 + Voidance (Not including Confusion) | relics |

TE | Theurgal Equalizer relic, Raises Theurgist damage by a factor of 2.5 | relics |

tecton | Essence Element ofTecton, quest item to help wage war, talk to the Quake Keeper | quest |

Tectonic, or Earthen | Fourth best quality items in the game, same as earthen. Beast drop or by upgrading Demonic items with ash and the Heaven Citizen | items |

Templar | Templar is a race upgrade you can make after you create a character It costs either $50 as a purchase from Glitchless, or 50,000 ash in-game using the Ash Keeper. | races |

Temple | A special location at 40,Sur,225 that is part of some quests It differs from many special locations in that it can be owned by players. | Gameplay, quest |

Thief |
Goblin Thief, easy Sur beast that any player can kill if he or she can reach it in time. | Beast, gameplay |

titus |
The Calling of Titus relic | relics |

ToTD |
Touch of the Damned relic, Lowers enemy health by 40% OR by 40% of your durability, whichever is lower. | relics |

ToTF
Tears of the Fallen | A relic, 50% chance of being saved from death for 1st TotF equipped, 10% for each one equipped thereafter | relics |

Transfer (xfer) | Transferring points from the stats you don�t use into the stat or stats useful for your class Players can begin to transfer when they reach the hard level cap. The process works exactly like leveling where you kill critters until you get to choose a stat to increase The difference is that you do not get any additional total stat points, you simply move some into one stat from all the others each time you train. Players without an sos will be able to start transferring at level 1,000 and will still need the full number of soft cap kills to train. Players with an SoS can transfer some points after each kill so the process is much faster. | gameplay |

treasure | either an EEoT (worth 50bil) or an EEoGT (Worth 250bil), or an EEoST (worth 999 billion gold) often used for in-game trade payments | item |

Treasury (Trs) | The kingdom treasury, shown on your screen as Trs, is where you can store gold and have it gain interest every hour. The amount of interest you gain will vary with your kingmanship skill and whether you have Collectia as a girlfriend. It ranges from a low of 0.449%/hr for players with 0kingmanship skill and no Transcend to a high of 0.803%/hr for those with 125 kingmanship and Collectia. | gameplay |

Trial | Trial Access, a $15.00 PayPal purchase from Glitchless that gives you access to all planes and Templar status for 10 days This is a cheap way to complete several quests and see more of the game than you get to play on the surface. The quest items you get during the access period, like a Wand of Transportation and Backpack, remain with your character when the access expires. If you don�t already have permanent Templar status, trial access will not let you get a Templar�s Wand so you can not summon beasts. | gameplay |

trivial |
In crafting, an item becomes trivial when your skills are high enough for crafting that item to never fail In addition, making trivial items will never get you any further skill increases To increase crafting skill, you must make items which are not trivial and thus still have a chance to fail. | gameplay |

TSM | Tradeskill Manifesto, a quest item that raises your crafting caps to 999 | quest |

twink | Used as both a noun and verb Twinking is when you buy or give items to a character that are much better than what that character can earn itself by playing the game Sometimes the items are transferred from a higher level account owned by the same person, other times they may are bought from other people using in-game or real money Atwink would be a character that has beentwinked. | gameplay |

UT | Unborn Torment, a special beast that can appear randomly on any plane except Sur, you need an SoA or 12 annuls to fight them. | Beast, quest |

VV | A Vogar's Vice relic. It does 3 milunannullable damage to normal enemies | relics |

vacation | Yellow text message "Player x has decided to take a 2 day vacation" means someone has been banned from the game for 2 days for breaking the rules No longer part of the game. | gamepla |

VAS | Vampire's Amaranthine Scorn. A relic, upgraded from a VS relic | relics |

VL | Vampiric Leech relic, Steals 50% enemy health OR 50% of your durability, whichever is lower. | relics |

Vogar | An NPC who can makePutrifaction relics from your pure Vogar's Vice and Encephelation Decay relics and 5 runes You can pm using /mVogar: your message here | quest |

Void | Voidance relic, Each gives a 10% chance to void all enemy enchantments + Confusion | relics |

VS | Vampire's Scorn relic, 20% durability boost + 10 Death Spikes + **Double Defense + Confusion + Blood Pinch | relics |

Waking Nightmare | The system message " A waking nightmare fills your vision " shows when a Nightmare Fly beast has appeared on the Surface | Beast |

A wondrous crack is heard in the distance | System message (yellow text) message that appears when a player with an ashen rock makes a purchase of $50 or more (or uses 50,000 or 100,000 ash for an Ash Keeper upgrade) and receives either some ash or treasure elements as a bonus. | Gameplay |

## Skills From Grandmaster Templars

Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden. Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.

I spent a lot of time and gold working on crafting skills on
Server 1 until I couldn�t progress any further by actually making items, then
relied on killing a Grandmaster Templar every month to push them all to being
trivial. � I was curious how much the GMT
kills helped so I kept track of which skills I got from each GMT I killed,
which also included a few for approbations on Server 2. � In case anyone else is curious about what we
can expect to get from killing a GMT I�m posting my observations here.

Overall they�re pretty evenly distributed except for
favoring Gemworking a bit in this set. �
The �relative� row shows the averages normalized relative to the least
common skills. � My guess is that with
enough kills to average in, all the skills would end up being equally probable,
but that�s just a guess. � The average
number of points I got from a GMT was 9 with a high of 12 total points from one
GMT and a low of only 6. � Unfortunately,
these data don�t allow players to do anything about how many points they get or
where they go, that�s all out of our hands.
