pages.title: Hermits and other Non Player Characters (NPCs)
pages.text:
Hermits and other Non Player Characters (NPCs)

## Hermits and other Non-Player Characters (NPCs) Of RWK

Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is prohibited.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.

Page last updated 7 August 2021

Race War Kingdoms has a number of different Non-Player Characters, usually referred to as NPCs, which serve various functions in the game. One thing they are not for is fighting, players can�t attack any NPC. If you try to battle one you get a message �you are far too weak� regardless of your level. The NPCs are there to use for various quests.

You can chat with an NPC by sending it a pm, something like � /m Vogar: hello� will usually start a dialog. If you repeat back to the NPC whatever word it says in brackets, like [power] it will give you the next part of its dialog. So, when Vogar says " Vogar: I am the great Vogar, the bringer of [pain]. " You would pm back " /m Vogar: pain� and then get his next line � Vogar The relics of mine you may have are weak, I can craft those with much greater [power]. � And thus work through his whole spiel.

The first NPCs I recall added to the game were the Hermits. RWK has six different planes (Forge, Hell, Dungeon, Surface, Sky, Heaven), each is a square map 300 kingdoms in each direction, and each has four corners, at 0,plane,0, 0, plane,299, 299,plane,299 and 299,plane,0. There is a Hermit in each corner of each plane (except Forge, which has one hermit at 24,For,32) so there are 4 in 5 planes plus the Forge Hermit for a total of 21. They are simply named after the plane, so you have the Hell Hermit, Surface Hermit, etc. Different hermits have different functions.

A Citizen |
Ash Collector |
Ash Keeper |
Chained Libertine |
Dungeon Hermit |
Forge Hermit |
Heaven Hermit |

Hell Hermit |
Keep Keeper |
Mine Keeper |
Palace Keeper |
Pub Keeper |
Puddle Keeper |
Quake Keeper |

Rune Keeper |
Shrine Keeper |
Sky Hermit |
Surface Hermit |
Vogar |
|
|

The Hell Hermits are part of the Fangs of the Bloodletter, RDADMS Lessonbook quests and can also make you an Apex or Sacrificial Skillation relic in exchange for 100 runes. To get the relics you need to own a kingdom (YOU must be the king) with at least 100 runes in it. From that KD you send a pm to the hell hermit to get the relics and have him take the runes. DO NOT give the kingdom to the hermit. The messages are /m Hell Hermit: purchase Apex or /m Hell Hermit: purchase Skillation. The Hermits are on magma so you need a Templar�s Wand to visit their locations.

The Dungeon Hermits can add a Blessing relic (see actions, preferred blessing for your choices) to any weapon, shield, bow, arrow or spell in exchange for 1 billion gold. They can overwrite existing enchantments without your needing to disenchant the item first, something players can�t do. You set your preferred blessing to what relic you want added, go to the hermit�s location in Dungeon, and once there give him 1 billion gold. You specify which item is enchanted by the location of the hermit you visit. Location 0,Dun,0 (lower left) if for spell 1, location 299,Dun,0 (lower right) is for spell 2, location 0,Dun.299 (upper left) is for weapon slot 1, and location 299,Dun,299 is for weapon slot 2. If you compare the map to your inventory slots, the hermit location for each item is the same as the equipment location he enchants. If you go to the wrong location, you will have the wrong item enchanted so double check you are at the correct place before you give the hermit any gold. Dun Hermits will also enchant Demonic and higher items for only 1 bil gold, not the 250 runes required when players chant items at that level. Unfortunately, your choices for both item and relic are very limited.

The Surface Hermits are part of several quests : they add essence elements of Ice, Fire or Lightning to your approbations for 10 runes, they will use Dark Essences and a super relic to convert DDD relics into D*D ones, they add Dragon Elements to approbations for 999 runes, add Gems to Dragon Elements for 999 runes, convert Goblin Hides into Goblin Hide Backpacks for 999 runes, and converts a Goblin Head into 999 runes. The Goblin Hide Backpack (GHB) allows you to carry 90 items. I believe he also will convert 32 gems into 1 Great Gem as a cost of 32 gold, 999 runes and 999 ash. Give him the gold while in your kingdom with 999 runes and he will take the ash and gems, then give you 1 Great Gem.

The Sky Hermits work just like the Dun hermit (see above) except they add approbations, not blessings (see actions, preferred approbation for your choices), and the cost is 2 bil gold instead of 1. The same location (except in Sky not Dun) apply for these hermits. Dun Hermits will also enchant Demonic and higher items for only 2 bil gold, not the 250 runes required when players chant items at that level. Unfortunately, your choices for both item and relic are very limited. Coal Chamber on S1 has confirmed that the hermits will not overwrite a super relic or higher, so those are protected from player mistakes of that kind.

The Heaven Hermits will convert a Satan Hide you get in Hell into a Backpack for 2 billion gold. The Pack allows you to carry 75 items instead of just 50. See the quests page for the details.

The Forge Hermit There is only one in the Forge plane (located at 24,For,32) and it is used to complete the Master of Class quests. See the quests page for the details.

The Theurgist's Staff of Domination and Friar's Staff of Preeminence can be returned for a refund of your relics, but that refund will cost 50,000 ash for the Friar's Staff and presumably the same for the Theurg one. Thanks to Evincarvolrath for reminding me about the refunds and providing the ash cost.

The Forge Hermit can also speed settlement in the Forge plane by allowing players to use treasure elements for multiple attempts in a single action. Trade an EEoT to this hermit and you will make 25 settle attempts in one action. The dialog from him shows the failed attempts as a string of "No" followed by "Sucks to be you, haha!" If one succeeds the string ends with a "Yes" at the one that worked. You lose the entire treasure regardless of where in the string of 25 attempts you finally succeed so it's best to do this process using just EEoTs and not the higher level treasures in case you get lucky early on. If you succeed on the thrid attempt with an EEoT you only wasted 44 bil, but if it happens with an EEoGT you throw away 244 bil and nearly a trillion if an EEoST is used. I cannot confirm whether EEoST can be used in this process but someone did tell me about using a great treasure which settled after only a few attempts.

Other NPCs

Other non player characters are also in the game and serve various functions in quests.

Chained Libertine at 69,Sur,69 will convert Theurgal Equalizer (TE) relics into the more effective Theurgal Conquest (TC) relic for free. Just trade the pure TE to the Libertine from any location, you do not need to be in the same kingdom as this NPC, and you get back a pure TC.

Vogar (each corner of Sur) will convert two pure relics, a Vogar�s Vice and an Encephalation Decay, to a pure Putrefaction relic for 5 runes. Just trade Vogar the pure VV relic while you are in YOUR kingdom containing at least 5 runes. He takes the VV, Ed and 5 runes and gives you back the Putrefaction.

Rune Keeper (162,Sur,159) will convert your runes into treasure elements, or treasure elements back into runes. Making treasures will cost you the runes, some ash and a little gold. An Essence Element of Treasure (EEoT) costs 50 runes, 50 ash and 1 million gold. You can convert it back into 50 runes for free. An Essence Element of Great Treasure (EEoGT) costs 250 runes, 250 ash and 2.5 million gold. An Essence Element of Superior Treasure (EEoST) is much more expensive, costing 999 runes, 999 ash and 999 gold. You can convert them back into 50, 250 or 999 runes for free simply by trading the element back to the Rune Keeper from the kingdom into which you want the runes to be put . Remember that the location you are in when you make the trade is the location where the runes will appear , so make sure that kingdom where you really want them.

The Rune Keeper also converts the SoS, SP, SoD, GHB, FotB, and BB quest items into the Sanction of Serenity (SoSe) .

Quake Keeper (150,Hel,150) will provide an Essence Element of Tecton for waging war more effectively or an Essence Element of Stone for better kingdom defense. Each costs 999 runes.

Ash Keeper (150,Sur,150) can be used to get upgrades for ash instead of paying cash for them. You can get an Ocean Bubble, Platinum Ore, Fyre Rock, Templar, Skill Potencifier, Beastbane, Scepter of Annulment or Goblin Hide for between 20,000 and 100,000 ash each. You can also trade the Ash Keeper an Essence Element of Ash (EeoA, $50 money order purchase only) and he will convert it into 50,000 ash which you can then use in the game. Anyone with enough ash can use the Ash Keeper.

Ash Collector (141,Hell,174) is used to summon beasts using ash and gold, and can also add Devoid relics to D*Ds and make D*Devoid relics in exchange for 1,000 ash. The Ash Collector is located at 141,Hell,174 which is on Hell Magma, so you need to be a Templar and have a Templar�s Wand to reach him. The collector won�t even chat with you unless you go and visit him at his location.

Pub Keeper (150,Sur,145) will convert a super relic (Melee�s Rage, Caster�s Revenge or Vampire�s Scorn) into Tears of the Fallen , which he refers to as �angel�s tears�. He will also give you some clues about how to complete the Captain`s Staff of Valor quest (just keep sailing between the different ports until it drops).

Puddle Keeper (140,Sur,190) will convert super relics (Melee�s Rage, Caster�s Revenge or Vampire�s Scorn) into Dark Elements. These are quest items for upgrading DDD relics to the various D*D types. The type of Dark Elements you receive is random, and it�s possible to get up to 0 to 3 in return (yes, you may get nothing), so you can�t count on getting what you need. If each possibility is equal it would mean you have a 25% chance of just losing the super relic. He will also take a DD relic and convert it into a random number (4 to 6) and type of super relics. He will not make super relics from a DDD (courtesy of Homer Stokes on S1).

Keep Keeper (66,Hell,66 and 66,Hev,66) will convert a Lord Alchemar�s Skull (drops off a Lord Alchemar beast) into a Quickening , which doubles the stat points you receive each time you train and does so with no reduction in your delay bar. You stand in a kingdom you own that has at least 100 runes and trade the head to the Keeper. He takes the head and 100 runes and you get the quickie.

Mine Keeper (162,Sur,159) is used to obtain the quest items Scepter of Harnessed Hatred, Scepter of Unending Repulsion, and Harbinger�s Scepter of Oppression, which allow players to be more effective when waging war with their kingdoms. To enter the mines you need a Scroll of Forbidden Rights , a very rare, random drop or $50 purchase from Glitchless.

Shrine Keeper (100,Sky,250) interconverts D*D relics (plain D*D or D*Devoid) for 2 k ash each change. So, if you have a D*D relic of one type and want one of a different flavor, you can use the Shrine Keeper to cycle through the types until you get the one you want. The cycle is Mutilating to Lithe to Putrid to Resistant to Skillful to Accelerated to Waring to Opulant to Mutilating . The relics can be swapped whether in pure form or when attached to an item.

So, if you have a Devil�s Lithe Deliverance and want a Devil�s Putrid Deliverance it would be one conversion and only cost you 2,000 ash. If you had a Devil�s Accelerated Deliverance and wanted a Devil�s Skillful Deliverance you would need to go through the entire cycle (A to W to O to M to L to P to R to S) of 7 conversions and use 14,000 ash. The key parts of the Shrine Keeper dialog are:

For 2,000 ash I'll swap a D*D or a D*Dev in the following manner, M to L to P to R to S to A to W to O to M. [Understand]?

If you gave me a DsDev I'd take 2k ash and give you a DaDev. If you gave me the DaDev back I'd take another 2k ash and give you a DwDev. Make [sense]?

Palace Keeper (162,Hev,159)will upgrade a super relic (Melee�s Rage, Caster�s Revenge or Vampire�s Scorn) to the next higher level using a Palace Element (Storm, Lava or Blood) or upgrade a Devil�s Deliverance to a DDD using all 3 Palace Elements. You give the Keeper the relic, he takes the element or elements and you get the upgraded relic. The palace keeper will also reverse a super relic upgrade. If you trade him a pure MUR, CER or VAS he will remove the palace element and return the base super relic (you do not get back the palace element). This seems odd but you need an un-upgraded super to get a tears of the fallen or to convert a DDD into a D*D, so if you only have a MUR, CER or VAS this procedure allows you to get the plain super for completing the ToTF or D*D quests. According to Archer Of Crosis (S1) the Palace Keeper no longer downgrades a DDD back to a DD.

A Citizen is an NPC located in every kingdom. Several Citizens in different ports are part of the Scepter of Specter Sight quest . You travel between various ports and give quest items to the Citizens there.

The Citizen at 069,Sky,157 is part of the Citizen DD Quest which involves using palace elements, ash and upgraded super relics to get DD relics, changing around Ice, Fire and Lightning elements, and potentially converting palace elements into ash.

The Citizen at 150,Hev,150 is part of the Citizen Upgrade Quest and will use your ash to upgrade Demonic, Earthen or Crystal items or downgrade Shadow Scale items to Crystal. Players with only 10-day trial can reach both Citizen locations.

The Citizen at 129,Sur,51 (Port of Carella) is part of the Sash of the Blood Rogue quest. Each time you give him an EEoGT you have a 1 in 20 chance to get the sash. It�s random so you could get lucky and get it in one try, or end up like some players trying many times before either giving up or getting the drop. Good luck!

Back to home

pages.title: The ORIGINAL Jimcav's RWK Help Site Updates
pages.text:
The ORIGINAL Jimcav's RWK Help Site

Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express permission of the author is forbidden. Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.

This page contains
all my updates that are older than I feel need to be on the home page, but
which someone might want to view to find something he or she remembers from the
past. � What is here isn�t on the home
page, and vice versa.

12 Mar 2006

I corrected some mistakes on the relics page ,
especially the details of converting a DDD into a D*D and fixed a lot of typos
that somehow got into the arrow speed page . � I also tinkered with a few other pieces of
the site but I don�t recall which ones, they were just tweaks to make the
information clearer. � I appreciate all
those who tell me when they find mistakes on the site so I can correct them.

Thanks � I�d like
to thank �� Fialchar on Server 2 for making an $11.38 PayPal donation
plus Vampgre , Gerrard
Capashan and Elwan
Myth on Server 1 and Peacefu1
Grem1in and Adi14885 on Server 2 for naming me their mentor. � Thank you for your continuing support.

22 Feb 2006

I added more
information to the relics page , putting in the common
abbreviations for those relics that are often abbreviated in chat. � The jargon of the game confuses new
players. � I also added more information
on relics and the abbreviations used for items to the character
creation guide . � Telling new players
to get DIP shields and DEE armor doesn�t help them when they have no clue what
the acronyms mean.

Thanks � I have
some major thanking to do this time since a few players gave me an unusual
amount of help. � Cypher on Server 2 donated $50 for dungeon so now I have
all planes there, and Stormcajun named me his patron, also on S2. �
Thank you both for the big boosts. �
I also want to thank Grantiss , Cammris Of Moe , Adi14884 and Zippo on Server 2 and Ardablyukey on Server 1 for naming me their mentor. � It all helps and I thank you all.

12 Feb 2006

Not much to update, I tweaked some pages to fix mistakes people reported
and tried to make the content a little easier to understand while I was working
on them. I also added a few sentences to the reliccrafting page explaining how
the cost of crafting the 10k and 20k base relics increases if you already have
some pure ones in your inventory. Someone got burned crafting them because he
didn't realize how the costs change.

Thanks � I want to thank Stickman Bob, Matimeo, Deimus, Dark5800,
Sumimasen, P5ych0, � The Nukers Target,
Jubalkane, Ghoxoster, Water Elf, Gnome King and Chaosdarkdragon on Server 2 and Darth Syd on Server 1 for naming me their
mentor. � I appreciate the help and it�s
moving me closer to getting all planes access on S2.

22 Jan 2006

I rewrote several parts of my relic delay
effects page again after someone asked some good questions about the
conclusions in chat today. � It has taken
me a few iterations to get out the mistakes on the page but I think I have
gotten them all fixed now. � I had not
included the double exp from apex in the first table and the results and
conclusions for that part of the page were wrong. � While I was at it I tried to also clean up the overall
presentation and make it a little clearer.

Thanks � I want to thank Dylan on Server 2 for his generous
$10 PayPal contribution, and Ghost Archer and Seker , also on S2, for naming me their mentor. � It all helps and I appreciate your support.

15 Jan 2006

Transferring the site to its new registrar seems to have gone smoothly,
the www.jimcav.com only stopped working (for me at least) for a few hours. � The new location has a lot more storage
space, 10 GB in total, and includes utilities I can use to manage the site but
which won�t affect visitors. � All the
pages will also be on the jimcav.com domain now, although I�ll keep the
jimcav.nicolejw.com addresses working for a while as well. � The files are actually the same ones, just
different hyperlinks to get to them.

I added a page collecting all the rules for RWK
in one place, or at least all the ones I could find in /faq, /help. /guide and
/ann. � If I missed any please point me
to where they are hiding. � I thought
some folks might want to read them all so they don�t inadvertently break some
and unintentionally get banned.

I also made a few small changes to my beasts
page. � I don�t really know for certain
whether several beasts do or don�t give approbations. � Some are beasts I only killed once a long time ago, or after I
got all my approbations, while others are beasts I never killed at all. � I couldn�t remember the sources for some I
had listed as approbation or no approbation so I changed those to �don�t know�
to avoid misleading anyone. � If people
can let me know for certain, based on first hand experience, which ones give
approbations and which don�t I�ll update the page. � I only want direct, first-hand information from those who have
killed the beast and consciously made note of whether or not it gave an
approbation or approbation message. � I
don�t want speculation, uncertain memories or �
referrals to other sites which may also be speculating on the
answers. � I�ll try to keep track of my
sources this time so they can be appropriately berated if they�re wrong :)

13 Jan 2006

The web host for jimcav.com was finally changed today, so the URL
www.jimcav.com may not work for some people over the next couple of days while
the Web routers are updated with its new IP address. � I hope the transition will not take long but it�s out of my hands
now, so time will tell. � The domain
jimcav.nicolejw.com will continue to point to the same files as jimcav.com will
once it�s all settled.

Thanks � I found out that the patron assignment I got from Glitchless
was most likely from Rego. � I guess
there was a glitch when it went through :) �
IN any case, I want to thank Rego on Server 2 for the
Patron, it�s 10% of what I need for Dungeon. �
I also wans to thank Forza Juve on Server 1 and Rebel Yell , Candalo , Htruman and Badnames on Server 2 for naming me
their mentor. � I really do appreciate
the help.

4 Jan 2006

I plan to change the registrar for jimcav.com and when the transfer
happens there may be a couple days when using the address www.jimcav.com does
not point to the correct location. � It
takes some time for an IP change to be propogated through the many routers that
comprise the Internet. � The location of
these files won�t change, so if you bookmark this page now and use that
favorite to get here you won�t notice any difference. � Once the transfer is complete I will have both this and the
jimcav.com address pointing to the same location, which will also be
transparent to users. � Hopefully, the
whole process will go smoothly and be complete in a week or so.

Thanks � I want to thank Glitchless , of all characters, for
naming me his or her patron on Server 2. �
I�m not sure exactly how that happened but the ash bump was a nice move
forward towards getting dun access there. �
Thank you for the help!

30 Dec 2005

I was asked to add an explanation of how sleeved cards work so I have
put up a short page on it here . � The information is all available in 3 old
announcements (just look in www.racewarkingdoms.com/ann.htm )
but I know some players either don�t know to look there or have trouble finding
information when they do. � I don�t use
the cards for fighting so I can�t add anything beyond what I read in the
announcements. � For more insight about
what they can do in acumens you would do best to ask some duelist in the game,
it�s not my thing.

Thanks � I want to thank The N , Jaydawg and Hells Retributor on Server 2 and � Eagle Wraith and Bloodied Fang on Server 1 for naming me
their mentor. � Thanks for you help and
support.

24 Dec 2005

I edited the delay effects page to try
making it easier to understand, and also to change the baseline for comparison
from a player with 0 haste skill and no speed boosting items to one with 0
haste but using either a rapid spell or light weapon. � I think any sensible player of any class would be using a light
or rapid if his or her first priority is leveling, skilling or getting drops as
quickly as possible. � The numbers don�t
change but the relative benefits will shift in relationship to each other when
the basis for comparison is changed.

Thanks � My
thanks go out to Sir Axe on server 2 and Kiseitai on server 1 for
naming me as their patron. � I also want
to thank Funkymule2 on S2 for naming me his mentor. � I really appreciate the ash, it is
especially useful on S2 since I don�t have a good way to get it via drops there
and I still need a lot to get dun access. �
Thanks again folks.

12 Dec 2005

I added a separate page that collects the stat
boosts for various items in the game. �
The information is still also in the crafting tables, but I think a lot
of people use the crafting tables just to find the boosts and it should be
easier now with them in a separate place, especially without all the items
below 1 bil base filling most of the tables.

I also did some damage tests to expand the scope of my DotB vs. BP tables . �
It looks like relic damage is reduced by the same amount in the Puddle,
Pub, Keeps and Palace. � All those sites
showed a 25x relic damage reduction compared with normal kingdoms. � I was expecting to see more reduction in the
Keeps and Palace so the results surprised me. The reduction factor in the
Insane Dungeon was a whopping 25 million. �
I don�t know how this reduction might relate to damage by weapons,
casting, arrows or monks. � I don�t have
a good way to test those classes so I�m sticking with relic damage and
specifically the choice between DotB and BP.

8 Dec 2005

I am using a different web host for my site so the actual location of
these pages has changed. You may notice the address of the pages is different
from what it was in the past. � It is
possible I will also change the registrar for the jimcav.com domain which may
disrupt access to the site using the www.jimcav.com URL but if that happens it
should only last a day or two and I�ll give notice here at least a week in
advance. � All the content will still be
available here but the jimcav.com address won�t be directing your browser properly
until the information spreads to the various web routers. � For now I�ll just redirect the domain to
this location and see if any problems develop while keeping the old site as it
was.

I added some actual vs. calculated data to my DotB
vs. BP relic damage page . � The
experimental results were right where the calculations predicted, and I also
tracked damage in the Pub for a few critters. �
It was consistently 25x lower than against normal creatures. � I�ll �
try to get similar data in the other special kingdoms to see how they
differ and maybe I can wrap it all into some general guidelines about damage
relic combinations in the various locations.

Thanks � I
haven�t updated in a while and I need to acknowledge a lot of people on both
servers for their support. � My thanks go
to Majikk, Kiseitai, Knight Syd, Benamato, Badooy, Dead Rose, Ddarcher and
Honorsmacked on Server 1 and Ehlmaris, Theurgal Divinator, Parkdzee, Defcon
Three and Version16 on Server 2 for naming me as their mentor. � The ash is a big help folks and I appreciate
it.

11 November 2005

I started to rewrite my drop rate page to
make it clearer what the data mean and I�m expanding it to cover more critters
and locations. � This is pretty dull work
so it will take a while, but I�d like to eventually sketch out how drop rates
vary with creature and location in the game.

9 November 2005

I added a new page explaining the Ash
Collector and how to use him to summon beasts and add Devoids to D*D
relics. � I also updated my character creation guide to give better guidance to Vamps
about using DotB and BP relics, including a new page
showing damage as a function of player dur, relic combination and enemy dur. I
also edited my quests pages to try making them clearer.

7 November 2005

Not much new to add about the game. �
I have found that my old rule of thumb about vamps using DotB as their
relic below a modified dur of 500 mil and BP when it�s above 500 mil is really
too simplistic. � It�s valid when you
compare all DotB vs. all BP and test against a plain 1 bil critter (like
Yoros), but a more practical approach would be to start changing from DotB to
BP at a much lower modded dur, beginning with the last relic slot and working
backwards towards your left hand as you keep gaining more dur. � A mix of both relics gives better results at
durability well below 500 mil. � I�ll try
to work out a better guide for this when I make the time to do some
calculations and tests, but I�d say that vamps really should start changing
gradually from DotB over to BP starting around 75 mil modified dur or so.

Thanks � I need
to thank Terak Noir on Server 2 for
donating $20 via PayPal so I could buy lungs for Jimcav on that server. � It was a very generous gift. � I also want to thank Dirk Diggler , and

Sussymule
(Sussyta) on Server 1 and Therias , Pdxnguyen , Original Eyemredy , and � Ultima Theurgism
on Server 2 naming me as their mentor. � Thank you
all for your support. � I need to also
thank Edmoni for taking my last complex on Server 1. � It prompted me to stop holding land and just embezzle for
others. � Over the summer I earned around
60 tril that way at no risk to myself, maxed my crafting skills, and had 25
tril left for future use. � Much better
than the paltry 35 bil a day I was earning from my little complex. � Thanks to Ed, I have higher crafting skills
than I expected at this time and a lot more gold :)

23 October 2005

I corrected some serious mistakes in my crafting tables that probably
crept in during one of my updates. � I�m
surprised no one caught them sooner. � I
also made some corrections and clarifications to the quests tables. � I appreciate all the feedback on those
errors, player input is the best way for me to get them fixed.

Thanks � I want
to thank Darkknight8416 on
Server 1 for making an $10 donation via PayPal to support this site. � That was a pleasant surprise. � Also Roan on Server 2 naming me as his
mentor.

6 October 2005

I updated my character creation guide to correct a serious error about
archers, and updated my quests page to give a better and more detailed
explanation of the Archer�s Aide. � Also
updated the ash page a bit to include the most recent changes added to the
game.

Thanks � I want
to thank Lord Longshank and Killerkid3 on Server 2 for naming me as their
mentor. � Thanks for the ash, guys :)

3 October 2005

They say that imitation is the sincerest form of flattery, so I suppose
I should be very flattered that Ximor and Red Drag0n have chosen to copy chunks of my content and post it on Ximor's
site as their own. I guess they can't come up with enough original material and
decided to just copy verbatim what looked good without permission or
acknowledgment. It's a sad comment on both their integrity and creativity. Some
of the material on this site is derived from /help and Cyril and it is used with their
permission. I also cite what I used from them and link back to the original
authors.

I updated my dialogs for the Ash Keeper and Citizen since their functions
have changed a bit recently with the DD attempts removed and the Ash Element
added.

Thanks � I want
to thank Flyinghawaiian on Server 2 for naming me his Patron, the extra
ash really helps. � I�d also like to
thank Dadamssr, Guitarsummoner10, Krakilin2 and Livo1012 on
Server 1 for naming me as their mentor.

27 Sept 2005

I need to start by thanking Eldriken on Server 1 and Wafer on Server 2
for helping my characters on those respective servers with a couple of very
generous birthday presents. � Thank you
again guys, I appreciate your help.

I�ve added a bikini link on my Server 2 account, which is
here , to see if I can make more progress towards enough ash for access
there. � I appreciate any �sexy� votes I
get.

Thanks � I
haven�t updated in a while and I need to thank a lot of people on both servers
for their support. � Adamthepig,
Shadowfox42, Wolf79 and Lucia on Server 1 and Friedreich, The
Timeless Line, Empyreal and Renzaway on Server 2 for naming me as
their mentor.

17 Sept 2005

My mini beast invasion was a little disappointing, only 24 beasts in 50
attempts and none dropped anything special. �
Oh well, that�s just the luck of the draw. � I emptied my crafting storage mules and gave away some armor,
weapons, spells, bows and arrows. � Some
of them will probably even be used by the recipients :) � The top place players in the S1 swimsuit
contest have hundreds of votes each, so there is no possibility I could win,
but I�ll keep up my photos and add another girl for every 20 votes just for
fun.

12 Sept 2005

A swimsuit contest, of all things, has been added to the game. � Players above level 1,000 can participate,
see the announcements (type /ann in game) for the details. � If you want to compete, you find or post a
photo on the web and use the actions menu, options, swimsuit picture and paste
in the hyperlink to the photo you are entering. � When you do, a purple message will appear giving you a warning
about suitable content and a hyperlink you then give other people so they can
vote. � It�s in purple text and I missed
it the first time, but you can check your link any time by typing /sw while in
the game. � The general format of the
link is http://rwk1.RaceWarKingdoms.com/cgi-bin/rwk.cgi?swimsuit Playername where the Playername will
be the person you are voting for. � On
Server 2 replace rwk1 with rwk2.

You can help , click on
my Server 1 link here or my Server 2
link here and give my photo a �Sexy� vote. �
I have the various images I�ve used for the contest on this page so they will continue to be available for
viewing. � One more thing, keep those
hands ABOVE the desk where I can see them :).

8 Sept 2005

RWK
Anniversary Updates Begin Early . �

Glitchless has started making the 4 th anniversary updates a
day early. � You can see the announcement
yourself by clicking on �announcements� when you log in or typing /ann in
game. � They include:

A new Citizen DD quest by which players can convert upgraded super
elements (Melee's Undying Rage, Caster's Eternal Revenge or Vampire's
Amaranthine Scorn) and palace elements (Storm, Lava, Blood) into Devil�s
Deliverance relics, potentially get sleeved cards for UT elements (Ice, Fire,
Lightning), turn a palace element into 1,000 ash. � The dialog explaining all this is available by private messaging
A Citizen in 069,Sky,157. � I have the whole dialog here and have added the
information to the Quests page .

Another addition is the ability of crafters to make Demonic items
now. � DDLD armor requires a palace element,
27 runes, 1 bil gold, and enough skill. �
I�m guessing the special demonics are available at 785 and trivial at
835. � I was able to try, and fail, with
816 Armorcrafting. � This raises an
interesting option for Theurgs though, since we should now be able to equip an
Everlasting Aura as our heal spell. �
Only a 45% dur boost but it adds the 70% damage boost and increases the
chance for a second cast. � I estimate
the skill needed to make them should only be 415 to try and 465 for trivial.

Thanks � I'd like
to thank Lyncher and Dark Clown on Server 1 and Quasar and
Alextsh on Server 2 for naming me as their mentor.

30 Aug 2005

Jimcav's
Birthday celebration, 50 beast attempts in a row ON SEPTEMBER 17 ! � I'll turn 50 on Sept 17 and I've been � pondering whether to do something special in RWK to celebrate the
occasion of being more than halfway to the grave. � I�ve decided it would be appropriate to celebrate 50 years by
making 50 public beast summoning attempts in a row on that day on Server 1
(sorry S2 but I have no access or gold there). �
I�d have preferred to make it 50 assured ones, but I don�t have enough
ash, so 50 attempts will have to suffice. �
It�s also a little more suspenseful this way. � I�ll also be giving away a number of the highest craftable items
with DotB relics on them, DPP armor, PP bows and arrows, Special AEP weapons
and Special HR damage spells. � I don�t
know what time this will all happen, I�d guess sometime around noon for me, so
17:00 GMT.

Thanks � I'd like
to thank Sniper1990 and Alex2255 on Server 1 for naming me as
their mentor.

25 August 2005

Hermits

I implemented two suggestions I got from new players over the past
couple of months. � Someone suggested I
add a page explaining all the NPCs in the game. It took me a while but I
finally got one into presentable form. �
If I missed some characters, left out some functions, or just got some
parts wrong I�d appreciate feedback so I can improve the page. � You can find my guide to Hermits and
other NPC�s here .

Surface Quests

I also broke out a separate page showing just the quests players can
perform with only surface access. � I
debated whether to also take out the ones that required Aqualungs but decided
to leave them in since they are the cheapest upgrade and the quests are still
surface ones. � The Surface Quests
page is here .

Thanks � I'd like to thank Catastrophe
on Server 2 for naming me as his mentor.

19 August 2005

Home again! � I was in Germany
for the past 18 days and have finally returned home. � My own house, my own bed, and my own computers at last. � I need to catch up on the site soon, but it
may take a few days.

Thanks � I'd like
to thank Shawnchen on Server 1 for naming me as his patron, and Warpvamp
on Server 2 for naming me his mentor. �
Thank you both for your support.

29 July 2005

Glitchless has added a download
link to an RWK client for players who want to run the graphics off their
own hard drives instead of using the web-based graphics. You will see it at the
bottom of the login page, the yellow C|Net icon, it is a 10.51 MB .exe file.
The executable will create a folder c:\rwk (or whichever drive has Windows
installed on it), install the graphics there, and create a program group and
desktop icon to launch the game. If you want to use custom skins, you would
replace the default files with the new ones, such as the skins you can download
after using the /skins command in the game. If you have a slow web connection,
the game may run faster if you have the graphics on your local drive. If you
like using the web-based graphics you can keep playing that way. It is your
choice.

Thanks � I'd like
to thank Crappy Monk on Server 1 for naming me as his mentor.

21 July 2005

I added a column to the beasts page
showing the gold and ash required to summon them. I'm not certain whether
Dragon Eaters or the newbie sur beasts can be publicly summoned, I was told the
Goblins can't be. I also added a little more to the newbie guide about things not to do in the game.
Some poor newbie got nuked after making the mistake of asking how to convert a
treasure to runes while he was in his KD. Made me think about other mistakes
new players don't even realize they are making.

I have 4 houseguests visiting from Germany so things here
are a bit more active than usual and I�m not updating the site as often. � I made some minor changes to the quests table , nothing major, but it should be a little
more accurate now. � I started a page on
all the NPCs but it�s on hold until the end of the month when I�m back from
Europe and things at home are back to normal. �
Trying to list all the non-player characters made me realize how many
there are now and the variety of functions they fulfill. � I remember when the hermits were first added
now see how much has changed since then.

One thing players often ask is �how do I talk to [NPC name here]?� � You just send a pm, using the same format as
with any player: /m Name: message. � So,
for example, to start a dialog with Vogar you would start with something like
"/m Vogar: hello" and he will reply. �
Respond, using pm, with any words the NPC has in brackets. � That will usually move the conversation to
the next step. � You can also use /add
Name as a command to put the name on both your pm and trade lists.

Thanks � I�m
behind on this so there are a lot of names. �
I�d like to thank Piggyboy9 , Fuzzybunnyslippers , Sir
Rohan , Sk8er17 , Pdxnguyen , The Angry Gypsy , Vacio ,
and Kalburo on Server 1 and Ebb Ibn Ozz , Knightfrost , and
Darken Rahl on Server 2 for naming me as their mentor. � Sorry it took so long to thank some of you
here. � I�d also like to thank the
players on S2 who offered to name me as their patron. � Unfortunately, I can�t be a patron on S2 because I�m not a
Templar there. � I still do appreciate
the thought behind the offers.

4 July 2005

More minor corrections in the quests table, plus I eliminated the link
I used to have here for item boosts. �
All that is included in the crafting tables and it was redundant in the
other location. � I also added a first
pass at a guide for new players which I�ll
try to improve as I think of changes to make and additional topics to add. � Next, I�d like to implement a suggestion
that someone sent about a page devoted to the various NPC�s and their
functions.

Thanks � I�d like
to thank Queen Of Spades and Master Of Worlds on Server 2 and
Qwertyuiop118 , Legolaszac , Dijanna , Roiston Drakeon
and Alex225 on Server 1 for naming me as their mentor. � Thank you all for your support.

21 June 2005

I corrected a few small mistakes some players found and pointed out to
me (thank you for the feedback) in the quests table, and also fixed the skill
requirements for the two highest base items in my crafting table, thanks to a
very handy post by Bahamut
on the Cabal message board .

I�ve been trying to put together a new
player�s guide but it just keeps growing as I think of topics that people ask
about. � If it�s too long no one will
read it, so I need to get a handle on what�s important and whittle it
down. � It�s more difficult than I had
thought.

Thanks � I�d like to thank Connor Maccloud , Mickey Finn , Laorir Immortus on Server 2 and Nikon18 , Spell Slinger , Heideroosjes on Server 1
for naming me as their mentor. � Thank
you for your support, I can certainly use the ash.

15 June
2005

I had a recurring mistake in my character creation guide where the
accuracy and damage skills for most classes were reversed. � I corrected them after another player
pointed out the mistake. � Feedback like
that is the best way for me to fix mistakes on the site since my mind is
sometimes changing what is on screen to what I think should be there. � I added a coded email address at the top of
this page where I can be reached. � It
should display as plain text in a browser window but be useless gibberish to
any spiders trolling the web for email addresses to sell to spammers.

Thanks � I�d like to thank Sim0n16 , Sinful , Surface Theurgist , and Heather , all on Server 1,
for naming me as their mentor. � Thank
you for your support.

10 June 2005

I fixed a mistake in my crafting tables where I had listed the dur
boost for IP and DIP simply as 100%. �
Somewhere along the way I had lost the full story, that IP bow, arrow,
shields and the corresponding weps and spells boost dur by 100%, DIP shields
boost by 130%, but the other DIP armor slot items (helm, mantle, gaunts,
sleeves, leggings, and boots) give only the standard 15% dur boost. � Dur body armor all gives the same 15% boost
from DDR right up to DCC. � From DEE (11
bil to craft pure) through DPP the dur boost of body armor is 21%.

Thanks � I�d like to thank Crunchyboy (Crunchysspecs) on Server
1 and Syladex , Claladhedril , Drakons , Princeofdarkness (Princeofdarkness03), Ubern00b and Snugz on Server 2 for naming
me as their mentor. � Thank you very
much!

7 June 2005 -
Thanks

I'd like to thank Vofs2 on Server 1 and Silent
Monk, Ripfang, Evil Rubber Duckie and Lemmiwinks on Server 2 for
naming me as their mentor.

6 June 2005 -
Thanks

I'd like to thank Crunchyboy and Greater Faydark
on Server 1 and Death And Taxes , Cricket, Hellsbane, Laser Blade,
Fortifiedcorpse and Sith 8 on Server 2 for naming me as their mentor.

2June 2005

I Finally hit level 100 on Server 2 yesterday. I'm trying to
put together a guide for new players and being back on sur without any upgrades
is a good reminder of the issues they face. Putting it into a useful form
will take a while but, hopefully, I'll have something useful in the next few
days.

Thanks - I'd like to thank the players on Server 2
who named me as their mentor: Creel3, Xaftermath, Darkziblet,
Destruction1k, Fayea and Whiskeyjack I appreciate your support.

26 May 2005 -
Thanks

I�d like to thank Holy Crusader for setting me as his mentor and Galileo (S1) for making me his
Patron back in January. Both are on Server 1.

24 May 2005

I updated my chantcrafting page to make it easier for new
players to use. The old version was really aimed at those who knew how to craft
items but just wanted to see the skill values. �
Now it has an introduction to try explaining the process to those still
trying to learn the basics of crafting in general. � I�ll probably be doing the same to the other crafting pages so
they aren�t as cryptic when a newbie tries to make sense of them.

23 May 2005

I updated my quests page with the new ashen rock
quests and added more drop data to the drop rate page . � I also revised the delay
effects page using more data to draw a more accurate conclusion, and
corrected some calculations I believe I did wrong on the first pass.

21 May 2005

Started revising the page to make it more useful to new
players. � Crafting now has a short intro with
a general explanation of how to start crafting. � I�ll eventually offload it to a separate crafting page with more
information and links to the crafting tables. �
I started revising the quick links but that process still needs to
continue. � I edited and expanded my
advice to new players as Getting Started and updated it with the
possibility of getting several upgrades using ash instead of cash.

I also added a page on the delay effects of Apex and SS relics . � It�s
something I�ve thought about doing for a while and finally got around to
working on. � In a nutshell, when you use
an apex or sacrificial skillation you get the benefit of more levels at a time
or higher odds for gaining a skill, but the delay that also is part of the
relic counteracts some of the benefit. �
I ran a quick test to get some numbers and then backed out some
calculations to try correcting for the delay and getting to the overall
benefit. � The results will probably
disappoint some players and I expect some will disagree with my methodology or
conclusions. � Read the page and form
your own opinion.

18 May 2005

I reorganized and revised my quests page in an attempt to make it more
informative and easier for new players to decipher. � The quests are now grouped by type (access quests, vampire
quests, crafting quests, etc.) and color coded to make it easier to see which
ones require what type of access.

16 May 2005

I logged in this morning to find my one remaining complex
on server 1 had been nuked overnight by an asshole named Edmoni. There was no
profit or expansion involved, maybe he just doesn�t like my helping
newbies. � I was initially pissed enough
to take down this site, but after a couple hours I cooled down and put it back
up. � It�s time to give up on trying to
hold complexes and just get a job embezzling for others. � I�ll make a lot more gold that way with no
risk.

12 May 2005

I added a page on Skills
(fighting skills like Haste, Attacking Accuracy, etc., not crafting skills)
after I sent someone to the S1 help URL and then realized that it wasn�t
working. � The skill descriptions I�m
using are shamelessly plagiarized from the official site , there really
isn�t much I can add to that information, but I did substantially change and
expand the comments before listing and describing each individual skill. � Like much of my site, it�s a mix of something
borrowed and something new. � I guess I�m
the something old, not sure about what�s blue :)

15 March 2005

New crafting quest information added to my quests page , information compliments of Ehlmaris�s
site. � The Scepter of Master
Craftsmanship (SoMC) allows us to craft up to DPP armor (and equivalents). The
new scepter also greatly reduces the cost of making the 2 items below the
top. � For example, a major Disparagement
of the Forgotten � costs 151 bil to craft
without an SoMC but only 24 bil with one. �
The top end spells and weapons may find some buyers but I think the
demand will be limited. � At 25 bil to
craft each heavy Angel`s Eye Piercer or major Hellacious Rebirth I do wonder
how much profit will ever be realized from making them, especially when you
factor in the 1 trillion gold initial cost of getting the SoMC. � The DPP armors seem less likely to sell
since they only offer an AD advantage over DDT, and armor defense isn�t
terribly important in RWK. � Time will
tell.

17 March 2005

In RWK, the Shrine Keeper will now convert some D*D and
D*Dv relics into other flavors, send him a pm for the details. � It costs 2k ash per change and the options
are limited.

Attention New Players

Do everyone a huge favor, including yourself, by reading
the rules of RWK , become familiar with the
official help site , and pull down all the
Actions and Kingdom menus in the game and at least look at what�s there. � The pulldown menu options give you enough
clues to figure out most of the game�s basics. �
If you take these simple steps, the short time you spend doing so will
make your entire RWK experience much, much better. � You can also get information within the game by clicking on the
word �Actions�, or any other brown text (Loc, Runes, etc.) and by typing /help
to open the help site or /info to see some terms and items explained within your
chat window. � I also have information
here to help you get started, including a newbie
guide , a character creation guide , glossary of RWK
terms , crafting tables , and other miscellaneous data and advice organized
in the quick links table at the top of this page.

If you take a little time to educate yourself in the
basics of this game you will ask much more informed questions in chat, other
players will respect you more, you will get less abuse from them, and your
start will be a good one. � If you are
too lazy to try helping yourself, your time in the game will be much less
enjoyable. � The choice is yours. � Also keep in mind that violating the rules
can get you banned, in which case everything on your character will be
permanently lost. � The rules are pretty
simple and easy to obey, the consequences of breaking them really are not worth
whatever you think you might gain.

RWK is a business, the owners are adults who need to
support themselves, and as a result the best items in the game can be bought
from Glitchless for real money (US
dollars, not game gold) by players who are willing to invest in RWK. � You can now use ash to get several upgrades
which used to only be available by paying for them. � With enough time (admittedly, quite a LOT of time) you could
theoretically collect enough ash to get yourself aqua lungs (20k ash), a
Scepter of Annulment (50k ash), a Beast Bane (100k ash), a Skill Potencifier
(100k ash), sky access (50k ash), dungeon access (50k ash), templar (50k ash),
and try for a small chance (4% per attempt) at getting a DD relic (10k ash per
try). � Those all require a lot of ash,
but at least the option now exists to develop your character pretty far without
paying any cash to do it. � Good luck :)

One final warning. �
Don�t complain that you can�t do things without paying, don't whine about
the players who have paid for more options, and don�t beg for
handouts. � If you annoy the game�s
owners sufficiently by whining and criticizing the game, they can simply delete
your account without any need to justify their decision to you. � Being in the game is the equivalent of being
on private property. � The owner can
eject you at any time without having to give you a reason. � Behave yourself if you want to stay in the
game.

Ash 101 (19 Feb 2005)

I put up a page trying to explain ash
to new players, see it here .

Surface Map

I
made a downloadable Surface map (JPG file) showing the ports labeled by name
and location, the special sur kingdoms (Pub, Puddle, Mines and Temple), and with
a grid overlay to make estimating locations easier. � You can view/download the
map here .

/info Command

You can
learn about many aspects of the game while playing simply by typing /info. � If you don�t add anything else, you get a
list of the topics available, if you add one of them, for example �/info ash�,
you get a short explanation of that topic. �
You can also see a list of the game commands while playing by clicking
on the word �Actions�.

19 Feb 2005 - Crafting Expanded and High End Raised

The limits for crafting have been raised two
additional � items, so now it�s possible
to make armor up to Demonic Adulation, spells to � Disparagement of the Forgotten, weapons to Avengment of the Fallen,
and heal spells to Omagawad along with their corresponding special
versions. � The crafting cost is 76 bil
for DSU and equivalent and 151 bil for DDA and equivalent. � Nothing like paying 151 bil to craft items
worth perhaps 35 bil.

The top end for items has also been expanded with the
addition of � a �crystalline�
series: � Crystal Dragon Skin armor
(presumably bows and arrows too), Crystal Dragon Fire Axe, Crystalizing Consciousness
damage spell, and Crystaline heal spell which are beast drop only and offer
boosts of � +5% Void Resist, +44% Ntl,
+44% Str, +25% Cnt, +25% Agi, Armor +45% Dur, Weps and Spells +100% Dur. � More toys to help line the pockets of the
game�s enterprising item farmers.

Beasts Page

I added a page with more information on the beasts of RWK since a few people have asked about
them. � It�s not complete and I�ll
probably need to make some corrections and additions, but it should answer some
questions about what beasts are, how to kill them, and what they can do for
you. � I still have the summoning information and a list
of all beasts further down on this page

Ash Keeper

Along with the new server, a new NPC was added on
Christmas. � The Ash Keeper can be found
at 150,Sur,150 and now offers players some new options for using their
ash. � You can visit him and send a pm to
start a dialog, or cut to the chase and just go there and pm �stupid� to get to
the good parts quickly. � For 20k ash you
get an Ocean Bubble, which you convert into Aqualungs by entering a port. � For 50k ash you get you a Scepter of
Annulment, equivalent to having 26 annulment relics equipped, a Templar
upgrade, a Fyre Rock or Platinum Ore (access to dungeon and sky,
respectively). � For 100k ash, the
maximum amount we can hold at one time, you can get either a Beastbane or a
Skill Pontificator. � Yes, it is now
possible, in theory, to actually get access without buying it, provided you can
manage to save up at least 50k ash. �
Templars who want to morph a second time can also use 5k ash to get a
new Mask of Enshroudment and then change their race again. � The whole Ash
Keeper dialog is shown here . � As
usual for RWK chat, the start of the conversation is on the bottom, the end at
the top. � Player messages are red, Ash
Keeper dialog is blue. � I also
summarized the options in a table here for those
who don�t want to figure out what the dialog is offering them.

This is a nice addition, although I think a player can
actually do much better trading purchases by other players in exchange for
private beast summoning. � Still,
Glitchless is giving us some no-risk options for using our ash. � Since I already have the upgrades, I�d
personally like to see some other uses for it, like crafting unique,
valuable, � items so crafters have some
reason to ply their trade and can get a return for the effort. � Right now I think crafting is pretty much a
waste of time since the game is flooded with items which never break or wear
out and prices range from below the crafting cost to only a small amount over
it.

Poker

The �community based mini game� added to RWK is poker and offers
a use for ash by virtually all players, regardless of whether you have access
or not. � Players can now use cards to
alleviate the boredom of endless clicking, and with some luck can also win more
ash. � In order to play you need at least
10 ash and 100 mil gold. � Each hand will
cost you 100 mil gold and up to 10 ash. �
If you win the hand you get whatever is in the pot, and with the best 4
possible hands you also get an ash bonus (5k ash for a royal flush, 1k for a
straight flush, 500 for 4 of a kind, 100 for a full house). � The game is Texas Holdem, if you don�t know
how it�s played you can visit sites offering advice here and here . � You can also use any search engine to get
many more sites using �Texas holdem strategy� as your search terms. � Good luck.

100k Ash (12 Jan 05)

I finally hit the 100k ash cap, which has been my most
recent objective in the game. � I�ll
probably use this ash for summoning a GMT each month to get the crafting
skills, I can do that for 14 months with what I have in hand. � I�ll also see about collecting a bit as I
go, in case some new use for ash is added to the game.

Glossary

I�ve started a glossary of
terms used in the game to help newbies understand the strange language of
RWK. � It should expand as I add more
terms to it. � Some of this table has
been incorporated into the /info command but many entries are not there and
won�t be added.

Character Creation Guide for Newbies

New players ask often enough about how to start a
character and which race is best for which class, what stats, etc. � There is a character creation guide on the
official help site here but for
some reason few new players seem to use it. �
So, I�ve made my own character creation guide here .

Crafting

Players can use the gold they earn in the game to craft a
wide variety of items. � Armor, weapons,
spells, and relics can all be made if you have enough gold to pay for the
process and enough skill to make the item you want. � Begin by making the cheapest version of the type of item you
want, such as Rusty Daggers if you want to learn weapon crafting. � As you will see if you review the crafting
tables linked below, crafting simple Rusty Daggers is possible with 0 skill. � You use the actions menu, craft, weapons to
select the item, then just click on �Submit�. �
When you start, there will be some failures and some successes but if
you keep at it you will gain higher skills and the failures will become less
frequent. � When an item becomes
�trivial� it means that you will never fail when making it. � However, you also can never increase your
crafting skills by making trivial items. �
If you wish to train your skill higher, you need to craft a more
expensive item that is not trivial and still has a chance of failing. � Those failures are the RWK version of the
expression �no pain, no gain�. � You just
keep repeating this process over and over, moving up to more expensive items as
you get close to making something trivially, and you can eventually get to the
crafting skill caps for your character. � A handy tip for spells, weapons and armor is to train your skill
by making �special� versions of those items, such as durability armor, � rapid spells, heavy weapons, etc. � If you check the crafting tables and compare
the skills required and cost per attempt, you will see that it�s much cheaper
to work up skills by making specials.

I mentioned �skill caps� in the preceeding paragraph and
some of you may be confused by the term. �
Those caps are the upper limits of how high your skills can go. � Once at the cap, you can increase no
further. � All characters begin with
crafting caps of 10 for chantcraft (enchanting and disenchanting are both
chantcraft) and 100 for all other crafting skills. � You can increase the caps for everything except chantcraft by collecting
the crafting guides (such as the Balace Armorcraft Guide) shown in the quests table . �
These 5 books are free, just enter the appropriate port and the guide
will drop automatically. � NOTE that these books increase your
crafting skill CAPS , not your actual crafting
skill LEVELS . � You still need to train up the skills to
actually reach the caps, spending the time and gold required. � If you want to reach crafting skills over
400, or a chantcraft skill over 10, you will need another quest item, a Tradeskill Manifesto . �
This is not a simple and free quest. �
You can either buy it from Glitchless for $50 (click on �Buy Items� while
logged into RWK), or get one by killing an Eclyptic Hell Angel (commonly called
an EHA), which is a non-surface beast.

If you want to experiment with crafting it helps to know
what each attempt will cost and what skills are involved. � The crafting tables below are modified from Cyril�s site and updated with
newer items. �� I have extrapolated the
skills for items above 1 bil base. � I�ve
also added the crafting price and the effects, if any, that the items provide
(stat boosts, mez resists, etc.). � Some
of the values are estimates and may be off by a few points, but these tables
worked for me. � Non-craftable items
(earthen and crystalline) are also included to show their boosts. � Note that while we can not craft new
earthen and crystalling items, the two highest quality classes in the game, we can
enchant and disenchant them with enough skill and 250 runes per chant.

I present the skills needed to make items on this site but don�t go
into details about what you should be doing to develop your skills. � A good, detailed crafting guide
written by Bahamut is available on the Cabal web site. � It explains more about how to use the skills
tables to become a crafter in an efficient manner.

Crafting Normal items

Crafting Special items (rapid
spells, heavy weapons, etc.)

Relic Crafting

Chantcrafting (enchanting and
disenchanting are the same skill)

Note that there are no special heal spells, and that bows and arrows are in the specials table since
they follow that pattern for skills.

Drop Rate Data

I have started collecting data on the drop rate in both of
the keeps (66,Hev,66 and 66,Hel,66) and the sky shrine (100,Sky,250). � The data show a lot of scatter, but the
trend shows that all three locations give the same probability of receiving a
drop, and the drop rate remains flat moving from the 10 gold critters up to the
23,000 gold ones. � I�d have to conclude
that you can kill anything in this range and have pretty much the same chance
of getting a drop, so there is little if any incentive for players to fight
over the lowest base critters. � Over
this entire range (based on 12,486 drops) �
I averaged one drop every 4.7 kills. �
The drop rate data can be found here .

Arrows and Attack Speed Testing

When archers and theurgists were added to RWK the
announcement (4/6/02) described � bows
and arrows this way � Those
who use bows and arrows will deal 5 times the damage with 5 times the accuracy
at the cost of a greater delay. Purchasing better arrows will lower this delay,
however. Arrows are infinite.� � I finally ran some tests of just how much the
base cost of an arrow changes the attack delay for me and was surprised at the results . � I�m not
an archer but would be curious to see if arrows also affect damage, since that
could give a better reason to upgrade your arrow than anything I saw in the
attack delay.

Quests

The
official help site has a nice table here explaining all
the quests and including most of the details you need to do them. � I have taken the official table and added
notes to fill some of the gaps. � My version is available here . � If I can get feedback from other on the mistakes and omissions it
contains I�ll work at getting it back in order.

Beast Summoning

NOTE : � The Ash Collector is on magma in Hell, so
you need both hell access and a Templar�s Wand (meaning you need
to be a Templar) to summon beasts.

Regarding the new beasts and our new ability to select
certain ones when dealing with the Ash Collector, here is the thumbnail summary
for those who don�t already know how it�s done. � You can go to the AC at 141,Hel,174 and begin the dialog as usual
by giving him 1 gold. � He eventually
mentions, � For slightly less than 2 billion and a
hunk of ash I could summon different types of beasts. Offer me an amount and
I'll tell you what I can do .� � At
this point you can offer him various amounts of gold just less than 2 bil and
he will summon a specific private beast. �
If you have more than about 7,000 ash do NOT try this just to find out
the details. � If you have enough ash and
offer the right amount of gold the AC just summons the beast, he doesn�t just
�tell you what he can do. � I found this
the hard way but at least I did summon the beast I was interested in and got my
Theurgal Orb. � Anyway, the information
you need to know is what amount of gold summons which specific private beast,
which I show in the table below. � If
there are others I have not included here please update me on them so I can
added them to my table.

Beast to Summon

The complete list of all beasts as
of 20 October 2004 is:

Polymorphic Beetle,
Crucified Corpse, Darkest Widow, Eclyptic Hell Angel, Rogue
Goblin , Unborn Torment (3 flavors, Fire, Lightning and Ice), Lord Alchemar
(2 flavors, plain and Palace), Ancient Alchemar, Master Templar, Grandmaster
Templar, Goblin Shaman, Golem Of Living Rock, Dragon Eater (3 flavors, Fire,
Thunder and Ice), Goblin Thief , Goblin Miner , Goblin King .

The Rogue Goblin, Goblin Miner, Goblin Thief and Goblin King
all appear on the surface and can be reached instantly using /bn. � The Miner and Thief and only have 10 dur and
are easily killed by anyone IF you can reach them on time, the Rogue is
much harder to kill, the King has only 410 HP but has a damage cap of 1 per
attack per attack type (weps or hands, spells and relics can each do 1 point
damage), so the kill is more a matter of good luck than with other beasts.

Keep in mind some come
in different flavors, like Ice, Fire and Lightning Unborn Torments.

pages.title: Item Stat Boosts
pages.text:
## How Equipment Boosts Player Stats

## Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.

Last updated 28 September 2022

The tables below show how various items in the game can
boost your base statistics (stats).

Items with a base value of 1 billion gold or below (Timeless
Fortitude, Rustless Lifeceaser, Lifeless Growls) do not boost any stats.

The &quotSpecial&quot versions of these items can
boost your stats.

Each piece of Durability armor increases your armor defense (AD) and durability (Dur stat) by
15%, each Accurate damage spell doubles concentration (Cnc), each Precise
weapon doubles dexterity (Dex).

Items with a base value above 1 bil gold add some very desirable boosts to player stats, as shown below.

The choice of which item helps you the most will depend on your class, since different equipment boosts different stats. Proper use of these effects can dramatically increase player effectiveness.

Remember, these values are for items above the 1 bil base versions, so more expensive than Timeless Fortitude armor, bows and arrows, Lifeless Growls damage spells, Livagane heal spells, and Rustless Lifeceaser weapons.

Some items help but do not change the stats you see when you type in the /st command.

All light weapons and rapid damage spells, regardless of the base value, will cut the delay bar time in half. This comes close to doubling the speed at which you can play, so it increases your leveling and skilling rates as well as earning you gold and drops faster than if you do not equip one. I think every
player should use either a light weapon (theurgs, wizards, magicians) or rapid spell (vampires, monks, armsmen, berserkers and even archers) for all routine gameplay regardless of class. They just help too much to ignore and it is foolish to pass on the benefit they offer.

Note that for light weapons and
rapid spells the speed boost does not stack with themselves or each other. This means that you only get a speed boost from one equipped item in this class. Using more, like two rapid spells, two light weapons, a rapid spell and light weapon, will not make you any faster than using just one. The second light or rapid will just function like a normal item. No harm is done, you just do not get any more help from the light or rapid feature using more than one.

All Heavy weapons and Major damage spells can do twice the damage as their normal, light, rapid, accurate or precise versions. If you have enough accuracy stat to hit your enemy reliably, using a heavy weapon or major spell should significantly increase the damage you do as a fighter or spell caster.

pages.title: Surface Quests
pages.text:
## SURFACE QUESTS


Most of this information is
taken right off the official
help site , it�s been reformatted and some notes have been added for
clarification.



You can use the �find� feature of
your browser to search for specific items on this page if you don�t know what
category they�re in or if you just can�t seem to find them by eye.



RWK has a number of quests
which players can complete. They give benefits like access to more
locations, more killing power, higher crafting abilities, faster leveling,
etc. Most of the quests will require upgrades to complete, but some can
be performed without access. Since you can now even
quest for access using ash, players who spend enough time in the game could
complete all the quests without paying any real-world money.


The complete list of all 
quests can be found here . For those wanting to
see what they can do without any access to other planes the tables below show
all the quests you can do with only access to surface kingdoms. Quests
requiring Aqualungs are shows in magenta .


**NOTE**:
Quest items show in your inventory under �Others�. They can NOT 
be traded, sold or destroyed. Once you get one you�re stuck with it unless
you can complete some quest that rolls several items together into one. 
Don�t offer to sell your quest drops in auction or you end looking a bit
foolish :)


The
quests below are arranged to show any dependencies, so multiple step quests
show the sequence in which each step must be performed. Prerequisites 
are the items you must have in your possession to complete a multipart
quest. Some �quests� are only one step. The quests are also color
coded:


To
Purchase items on Server 1 click here , to purchase items on Server 2 click here . NOTE that purchasing an item
requires you to spend real US dollars, not game gold.


If a quest requires a particular
location, and the item doesn�t drop automatically when you enter that kingdom,
just start killing any creature and it will drop eventually. If a
specific critter needs to be killed it will be specified as such in these tables.




Jump to Quest Type:





Access

| 

Archer quests

| 
| 

Crafting quests

| 

Essence Quests (Tecton, Stone)

|

|

|

|

|
