Vampire quests

| 








Item

| 

Where To Get It

| 

Details

| 







## ACCESS, TRANSPORTATION AND MOVEMENT QUESTS


Mountain Tamer (access to Sky plane)






## Platinum Ore

| 

Does not drop off
any creature in the game. Either purchase or
get from the Ash Keeper

| 

You need to either buy the ore from Glitchless for $50 or use 50,000 ash
and the Ash Keeper at 150 ,Sur,150 
to get one. Ore is used to Make a Mountain Tamer (see line
below). Once you have the Ore, take it to the Temple (40 ,Sur,225 ) and it will transform into a Mountain Tamer
which gives you access to the Sky plane. The temple is on an island
without any ports so you will need to cross a small distance of water to
reach it. You can get there using Lungs or
teleport from the nearest point on the large landmass with at least 3,600
base Ntl stat. Mememe249 on S2 found you can reach the Temple island
with as little as 1,600 base Ntl by sailing to
Hykra, walk down the narrow island to get close to the temple, then teleport
from the West.

| 





## Mountain Tamer

| 

Take Plat Ore to
the Temple at 40,Sur,225

| 

Once you have taken
the steps above to get a Mountain Tamer you can enter the Sky plane, but only
from the entrance at 078 ,Sur,242 (or a kingdom
around it). You can drop back to Sur from any Sky location, but will
need to use the entrance at 78 ,Sur,242 to get back
to Sky as long as your only access is with the Tamer. Players with a Trans or better can move between planes at any location.

| 





Lava Splicer (access to Dungeon plane)






## Fyre Rock

| 

Does not drop off
any creature in the game. Either purchase or
get from the Ash Keeper

| 

You need to either buy the rock from Glitchless for $50 or use 50,000 ash
and the Ash Keeper at 150 ,Sur,150 
to get it. Once you have the Rock, take it to the Temple (40 ,Sur,225 ) and it will transform into a Lava Splicer which
gives you access to the Dungeon plane. The temple is on an island
without any ports so you will need to cross a small distance of water to
reach it. You can get there using Lungs or
teleport from the nearest point on the large landmass with at least 3,600
base Ntl stat.

| 





## Lava Splicer

| 

Take a Fyre Rock to
the Temple at 40,Sur,225

| 

Once you have taken
the steps above to get a Lava Splicer you can enter the Dungeon plane, but
only from the entrance at 240 ,Sur,60 (or a kingdom
around it). You can pop back to Sur from any Dun location, but will
need to use the entrance at 240 ,Sur,60 to get back
to Dun as long as your only access is with the Splicer. Players with a Trans or better can move between planes at any location.

| 






Aqua Lungs (access to surface water kingdoms)






## Ocean Bubble

| 

Can drop off Polymorphic Beetles , or be Purchased ,
or obtained from the Ash Keeper

| 

Used to make Aqua
Lungs (see line below). This item can drop off Polymorphic
Beetles , can be Purchased from Glitchless for
$20, or obtained for 20,000 ash from the Ash Keeper (150 ,Sur,150 ). Once you have the Bubble take it to any port
and it will transform into Aqua Lungs (see line below). You can see the
locations of all ports by using the Actions menu, Set Sail or check the map here . Just walk and/or teleport to the one
closest to your location.

| 





## Aqua Lungs

(Lungs)

| 

Take an Ocean
Bubble (see line above) to any port

| 

Lungs allow you to
walk on surface water, so once you have them all Sur locations except the Mines (162,Sur,159) and Brawl Arena (40,Sur,140)
become accessible to you.

| 






Demonic Pinions






## Demon Horn

| 

Random drop off any
creature in any location

| 

Used to
make Lava Boots (see line below). You need a Lava
Splicer or Transcendental Sanction (or better) to
use it. If you have the access, just enter Dun with the Horn and it
will transform into Lava boots.



Note: If you can�t enter Dun, you can not use this item. You also can�t
sell, trade or destroy it.

| 







White Wings Note : You will need a Mountain Tamer or Transcendental
Sanction to enter Sky






## Angel Hair

| 

Random drop off any
creature in any location

| 

The Hair
is used to make White Wings (see line below). If you have Sky access
(you need a Mountain Tamer ,
Transcendental Sanction ,
or better) you can convert the Hair into White Wings when you enter Sky.



Note: If you can�t enter Sky, you can not transform this item into White
Wings. You also can�t sell, trade or destroy
it.

| 







Wand of Transportation Note : You will need a Lava Splicer or
Transcendental Sanction to enter Dun





Carbon Encasement

| 

Drops off any
creature in the Temple (40 ,Sur,225 ), just keep
killing something until it drops.

| 

Used to make a Wand of Transportation (see line
below). Once the Encasement drops take it to any Dungeon lava (many
possible locations, 007 ,Dun,225 definitely works)
and it transforms into Wand of Transportation.



Note: If you can�t enter Dun, you can not use this item. You also can�t
sell, trade or destroy it.

| 







Wand of Swift Winds Note : You will need Aqua
Lungs to enter any water kingdoms






## Fish Scales

| 

Drops
in any water kingdom, off any creature

| 

Used
to make a Wand of Swift Winds (see line below). Kill any creature in
any water kingdom until the Scales drop, then take
them to any port. They will transform into a Wand of Swift Winds. 
Use the Actions menu, Set Sail to see all the port locations or check the map
here .



Note: you need Aqualungs to enter any water kingdoms so
you can get the Scales

| 




Wand
of Swift Winds

| 

Take
Fish Scales (see line above) to any port

| 

Eliminates
teleport delay, requires Fish Scales (See line above).

| 







Templars
Wand Prerequisite : Upgrade to Templar
Race and Transcendental Sanction






## Crystal of Lars

| 

Drops off the two
hardest creatures on any plane

| 

Used to
make a Templar�s Wand (see line below). Kill either of the hardest 2
creatures on the New Fight list (any plane seems to be acceptable) until the
Crystal drops. Go to a kingdom on the Hell Shoreline (many possible
locations, try 145 ,Hel,153 ). The Crystal
transforms into a Templars Wand if your character has received the Templar
upgrade. If you are not a permanent Templar, you can�t get the Wand.



Note: You will need a Transcendental Sanction or Omega Sanction to enter
Hell, without those the Crystal is useless. You cannot sell, trade or destroy the Crystal.

| 








Scroll
of Forbidden Rights

(SoFR)

| 

Random drop found
anywhere (very, very rare) or a $50 purchase from
Glitchless.

| 

Allows you to enter
special locations and is required to fight Ancient
Alchemar and Palace Alchemar beasts.

The Scroll allows
you to enter the Forbidden Palace (162, Hev, 159) the Forbidden Mines (162,
Sur, 159), and the Sky Shrine (100 ,Sky,250 ). 
It is also required to fight Ancient and Palace Alchemar beasts 
or to get the Scepter of Harnessed Hatred .

| 






##



## LEVELING, SKILLS AND DROP-FREQUENCY RELATED QUESTS




Experience
Gravitato r 







## Temple Stone

| 

Random
drop found anywhere off any critter

| 

Used to
make an Experience Egg (see line below). If you don�t already have a
Stone it will drop when you reach level 200. Take the Stone to the
Dungeon Spiral (150 ,Dun,230 ) and it transforms into
an Experience Egg.



Note : You will need a Lava Splicer or Transcendental Sanction to enter
Dun. Without Dun access you can�t use the Stone but it also cannot be
sold, traded or destroyed.

| 









## Templar


## ( race upgrade)

| 

Either a $50 Purchase or 50,000 ash from the Ash
Keeper

| 

Either a $50 Purchase or 50,000 ash from the Ash
Keeper (150 ,Sur,150 ). Templars are an
upgraded race .



From /help �Templars have the same stats as humans, but level at twice the
pace. Even at level caps it is beneficial to be a Templar because it will
help you rearrange your stats more quickly. Templars get a 50% skill
bonus making them 50% more likely to go up in skills every fight. Templars
also, when speaking in main chat, have their location blocked from the other
players so they can chat safely from their secret kingdoms without anyone
knowing where they are.�

| 









## Skill Potencifier

(SP)

| 

Either a $100 Purchase or 100,000 ash from the Ash
Keeper

| 

Either a $100 Purchase or 100,000 ash from the Ash
Keeper (150 ,Sur,150 ). This item doubles
your chance of gaining skills and allows you to reach a skill cap of 125
instead of 99. Visit the Keeper and send him a private message (�/m Ash
Keeper: hello� will work) if you want to use 100k ash to get one.

| 





MONK CLASS QUESTS




Fists of Scale






## Enduring Fists

| 

Drops in Stallion�s Pub (150,Sur,145)

| 

Adds 10 points to your Monk Discipline
skill and is used to get Fists of Scale (see line below) 
Kill creatures in Stallion's Pub (150, Sur, 145) until you get the
Fists (I don�t think the creature matters). To
convert them to Fists of Scale go to Le Puddle Deep (140, Sur, 190).



Note : You will need Aqua Lungs or an Omega Sanction to enter Le Puddle
Deep

| 





## Fists of Scale

| 

Drops
in Le Puddle Deep once you have Enduring Fists (see line above)

| 

Adds
20 points to your Monk Discipline skill and requires Enduring Fists (See line
above)

| 







VAMPIRE CLASS QUESTS





Vamp Horns 
Note : You
will need Aqua Lungs or an Omega Sanction to enter Le Puddle Deep






## Vampskin

| 

Drops
in Le Puddle Deep (140, Sur, 190)

| 

Increases
your durability in battle by 50% and is used to make a Vamp Hide (see line
below). Keep killing creatures stronger than Stone Golems until the
Vampskin drops, then take it to Stallion�s Pub (150,
Sur, 145) to upgrade them to a Vamp Hide.



Note : You will need Aqua Lungs or an Omega Sanction to enter Le Puddle
Deep

| 





## Vamp Hide

| 

Drops
in Stallion�s Pub (150,Sur,145) once you have a Vampskin (see line above)

| 

Requires
a Vampskin (See line above). Increases your durability in battle by
75%. Transforms into Vamp Horns (see line below). Once you are in
Stallion�s Pub (150 ,Sur,145 ) kill creatures stronger
than Twin Raiders until the Hide drops. Now go back to Le Puddle Deep
(140 ,Sur,190 ) to transform them into Vamp Horns.

| 





## Vamp Horns

from using relics to get their health above 1,999,999,998 so that you are
sure to kill them with two max damage hits unless they have used a heal spell
or manage to revive.

Also allows
Theurgists to equip a single heal spell.

| 











Sash of the Blood
Rogue

(Sash)

| 

Need at least 1
Essence Element of Great Treasure

| 

Gives some
defensive bonuses if you have sleeved cards. You get it by giving an
EEoGT to A Citizen in the port of Carella, you can
chat up A Citizen there to get some details. Success appears to be
random, with the chance of getting a Sash only 1 in 20 against you. 
Some players have used many treasures trying for a Sash. Good luck :)

Quote �A Citizen:
Think you're luckier than me? Well give me an EEoGT and I'll roll my 20 sided
die, if it rolls a 10 you can have my sash.�

| 








Most of this information is taken right off the official help site , it�s been
reformatted and some notes have been added for clarification.



## Special Locations




Forbidden Palace = 162 ,Hev,159 you need a Scroll of
Forbidden Rights to enter (very rare and random drop or $50 purchase)



Forbidden Mines = 162,Sur,159 you need a Scroll of Forbidden Rights to enter
(very rare and random drop or $50 purchase)



Mine Keeper: If you were to give me an Essence Element of Treasure whilst the
mine's were saturated...imagine the result!

1. lacing the mines = someone buys a rune for the forbidden mines and all
players there get 1 mil gold per kill until the new game hour

2. saturating the mines = players buy 10 runes
for the mines and all players there get huge amounts of gold per kill until the
new game hour

3. imbuing the mines = player trades the Mine Keeper an EEoT, runes go to
999, and players there can make several bil an hour



Sky Shrine = 100,Sky,250 Gives a 10% relative increase in skilling odds,
requires a Scroll of Forbidden Rights to enter (very rare and random drop or
$50 purchase)





Ash Collector 
location is 141 ,Hell,174 , pay him a visit, give him a
gold piece and have a little chat. For various amounts of gold and ash he
can summon beasts on demand.




Trade the Ash Collector your D*D when you have
the 1,000 ash and a pure devoid. He will take the ash and combine the D*D
with the devoid, making you a D*Devoid relic





Ash Keeper 
location is 150 ,Sur,150 . Note the Ash
Keeper and Ash Collector are different 
characters with different functions.





Stallions Pub 150 ,Sur,145 . The pub is a
special KD at 150 ,sur,145 that gives 5x normal Sur
gold, has special drops, is part of some quests, and has harder critters.


Le Puddle Deep 140 ,Sur,190 . 
You have to buy aqualungs ($20) to get there. It has the same
characteristics (harder critters, 5x gold) as
the Pub.




A Citizen at 69 ,Sky,157 
can give you a DD if you give him enough ash (250,000), palace elements (200),
upgraded super relics (8) or some combination of these items.



The Citizen at 150 ,Hev,150 can upgrade Demonic, Earthen and Crystal items
in exchange for ash.





Forbidden Mines 162 ,Sur,159


Forbidden Palace 162 ,Hev,159


Heaven Keep 66 ,Hev,66


Hell Keep 66 ,Hel,66


Insane Dun 100 ,Dun,200


Sky Shrine 100 ,Sky,250


Ash Collector 141 ,Hell,174


heaven lake for SoS is at
256,Hev,256


shores of hell =
145,Hel,153





wand of transportation
quest: 000,Dun,205 is one location that worked to convert a carbon
encasement into a wand





dun mountains = try
207,Dun,188 or the ID at 100,Dun,200





one location for dun
lava is towards the very top of row 009,Dun,256





Northern Port = Kremk


Southern Port = 
Belania


Central Port = 
Andal


Fisherman's Catch = Kremk 047 ,Sur,290 (Northern Port)


Toe = Andal 130 ,Sur,164 
(Central Port)


Finger = Belania 261 ,Sur,020 
(Southern Port)


Coin = Puddle (140, 190)





Brawl arena loc 40 ,Sur,140


Dungeon entrance is at 240 ,sur,060


Sky entrance is at 078 ,sur,242





Innate Class Resists ( These stack with all quest items and relics)





The Master Bowman class now receives an
additional 10% resistance to voidance.


The Grandmaster Bowman class now receives an
additional 20% resistance to voidance.


The Armsman class now receives an additional
10% resistance to voidance.


The Master of Arms class now receives an
additional 20% resistance to voidance.


The Berserker class now receives an
additional 5% resistance to voidance.


The Master Berserker class now receives an
additional 10% resistance to voidance.


The Magician class now receives an additional
5% resistance to voidance.


The Master Magician class now receives an
additional 10% resistance to voidance.


The Wizard class now receives an additional
3% resistance to voidance.


The Master Wizard class now receives an
additional 5% resistance to voidance.


The Blood Tyrant and Bloodletter class now
receives an additional 3% resistance to voidance.





The Master of Fists class now receives an
additional 5% resistance to mesmerization.


The Armsman class now receives
an additional 10% resistance to mesmerization.


The Master of Arms class now receives an
additional 20% resistance to mesmerization.


The Berserker class now receives an additional
5% resistance to mesmerization.


The Master Berserker class now receives an

5% resistance to mesmerization.

Theurgal Divinators get a 10% resistance to
Voidance and Mesmerization





* The only way to get your fists to a power
greater than a Mace of Resurrection is through pure skill training, special

When in Sky, you get DOUBLE the amount of

When in Dungeon, you get DOUBLE the amount of

pages.title: RWK Rules
pages.text:
RWK Rules 



## The Rules of Race War Kingdoms







RWK doesn�t have a lot of rules but finding them all can take a little time and effort. The basic rules are found easily enough by typing /faq while logged into the game. A second set of rules is posted on the official help site , also accessible within the game by typing /help. The two sets are nearly the same, but small differences have crept in over time. There are also some rules added in various announcements which were never added to the /faq or /help rule sets. Below are all the various ones I�ve found, collected here in one place. My objective is to give the players who care about following the rules, and improving their chances of not getting banned, a single page where they can find them all.


Rules From the FAQ


Q: What kind of things am I NOT allowed to say in chat? READ THIS OR BE BANNED!

You may not ask or beg for free items anywhere in the game.

You may not spam the chat with multiple lines in rapid succession.

The buying, selling, and trading of things, be they items, characters, services, etc. is prohibited from shout and must be said in auction.

You may not sell anything or ask for any price check, item, service (clickers, runners, etc), or form of payment (money order, PayPal, etc) unless it is in auction.


Don't ask for votes. Votes are when you say something like: Everyone who likes pizza type 911! This is inciting spam and is grounds for being muted. Don't start lottos. Lottos are things like: Whoever gives me 50k will get 2Mil back! These are considered to be SCAMS. So don't do it.


Don't badmouth RWK. If you don't like the game, then don't play it. Don't advertise other games. Do not thank people who give you trivial amounts of gold. This includes people who give you "yellow spam". Thank them in private messages all you want, but do not pollute chat with it. To define trivial: anything under 100 million gold.


If you purposefully or accidentally break any of these rules or if you are muted/banned and you get on another character before the mute/ban wears off and speak in chat you will be 2 day or permanently banned without question. If you feel you've been unfairly muted your only option is to send an email to help@racewarkingdoms.com and the matter will be investigated. Mute evasion or false claims to unfair muting will result in banning. Complaining about being muted or banned in chat is a great way to get banned.


Q: Is lying and the use of propaganda allowed in RWK?

It is NOT legal to lie in order to scam other players out of their belongings. It is NOT legal to mislead other players about aspects of the game in an effort to discourage them or for any other reason. The one thing you may do safetly is lie about the identity of your character(s). This allows you to have multiple characters with different allegiances. Keep in mind that if you break any game rules with any of your characters then they could all be banned or we may reveal your identity as a punishment.


Q: What are the rules for speaking in emote?

The emote channel is reserved for those who wish to roleplay. It has its own set of moderators that will determine whether you are contributing to the roleplaying discussion or merely causing trouble. Not following the rules of the moderators in this area of chat will result in you being chastised and disallowed to speak there for an hour. As with any rule, repeatedly breaking this can result in banning.


Q: Can I please have some gold, anybody?

Not only will you not receive any gold from begging, but you risk losing your account. Please refrain from begging (you can 'role-play' a beggar elsewhere) and help keep Race War Kingdoms free of annoying spam.




Rules from the Terms of Service

The Glitchless Sites/Services provide an outlet for communication with other visitors. By way of example, and not as a limitation, you agree that when communicating in any way, shape, or form with other site visitors, you will not:



- Defame, abuse, harass, stalk, threaten or otherwise violate the legal rights (such as rights of privacy and publicity) of others.


- Publish, post, upload, distribute or disseminate any inappropriate, profane, defamatory, infringing, obscene, indecent or unlawful topic, name, material or information.


- Upload files that contain software or other material protected by intellectual property laws (or by rights of privacy of publicity) unless you own or control the rights thereto or have received all necessary consents to do the same.


- Upload files that contain viruses, Trojan horses, worms, time bombs, cancelbots, corrupted files, or any other similar software or programs that may damage the operation of another's computer or property of another.


- Advertise or offer to sell or buy any goods or services for any business purpose.


- Restrict or inhibit any other user from using and enjoying the Glitchless Sites/Services.


- Harvest or otherwise collect information about others, including e-mail addresses.


- Violate any applicable laws or regulations.


- Create a false identity for the purpose of misleading others.


- Violate any of the forum or otherwise posted rules specific to the site you are using.


- Attempt in any way shape or form to undermine the authority of site moderators.





Rules From the Official Help Site


The first thing people notice and go to in the game is the chat. So, Glitchless is going to lay out the rules of chat to help make it so newbies don't get banned or muted before they even get to know what the game is all about. Also, just to make sure EVERYONE knows the rules and can't complain that they 'didn't know why they were muted.'. Listed below are the RULES OF CHAT which must be followed and are strictly enforced. When you are muted for bad chat behavior, you cannot chat for up to an hour and if bad behavior persists for a second or third time, you may be banned all together from the game.


1. Don't spam. Don't fill up the chat screen with useless, repeated messages, phrases, letters or numbers.


2. Don't beg. Don't ask people for gold, items, donations, etc. If you want gold, play the game. If you need items, fight to get gold so you can buy them yourself.


3. Don't ask for votes. Votes are when you say something like: Everyone who likes pizza type 911! This is inciting spam and is grounds for being muted. If you REALLY want to know who likes pizza, say something like: Everyone who likes pizza PM me and tell me about it!


4. Don't start lottos. Lottos are things like: Whoever gives me 50k will get 2Mil back! These are considered to be SCAMS. So don't do it.


5. Don't Auction in Shout. There's a reason for all the above mentioned words go directly to Auction (sell, sale, buy and bids); so if someone makes a mistake and forgets to put their message in Auction, it'll automatically go there for them. That's what the Auction channel is there for. Use it!


6. Don't badmouth RWK. If you don't like the game, then
don't play.


7. Don't advertise other games.


8. Do not thank people who give you trivial amounts of gold. This includes people who give you "yellow spam". Thank them in private messages all you want, but do not pollute chat with it. To define trivial: it's anywhere from 1 gold to... let's say.... 100 million.


9. If you wish to buy/sell/trade items from another game you MUST have the templar upgrade. Other players and mods can tell whether you have this upgrade because your location will show as asterisks. The vast majority of low level players offering trades from other games are scammers looking to make a quick buck. This will help prevent scamming when doing deals between other games because each attempt will effectively cost the scammer 50 dollars.


10. It is NOT legal to lie in order to scam other players out of their belongings. It is NOT legal to mislead other players about aspects of the game in an effort to discourage them or for any other reason. The one thing you may do safely is lie about the identity of your character(s). This allows you to have multiple characters with different allegiances. Keep in mind that if you break any game rules with any of your characters then they could all be banned or we may reveal your identity as a punishment.


11. The emote channel is reserved for those who wish to roleplay. It has its own set of moderators that will determine whether you are contributing to the roleplaying discussion or merely causing trouble. Not following the rules of the moderators in this area of chat will result in you being chastised and disallowed to speak there for an hour. As with any rule, repeatedly breaking this can result in banning.


Macroing


Macroing is when a player uses software to let their computer play their character for them. Meaning they would level without having to click the buttons themselves.


Many people have lost their characters because they think that Glitchless will not be able to detect their use of macros and/or other cheat devices. Macro users have been detected within 15 seconds of starting their macro. You lose your account FOR GOOD if you macro. It's not worth the risk.


Rules from the /guide command (just choose Actions, Options, Be A Guide? in game to see this yourself)


You have chosen to see requests made by players under level 1 for assistance. You must be helpful and kind, if you harass players under level 1 you will be banned. If you succeed in helping them get to level 1 by answering their questions and offering any other assistance you wish to give them they will be very grateful. When a player reaches level 1 they are asked to type the command /mentor followed by the name of a player who helped them the most and that player will get 250 ash. There is no limit on the number of new players that you help in this manner, and you can receive countless ash rewards if you are indeed a good guide. Mechanisms exist to prevent you from be able to /mentor your own characters or have friends repeatedly make characters and get them to level 1 just to give you the ash bonus.

You can give items to new players if you wish but you CANNOT advertise in chat or auction or any other way that you are trading items or gold in return for receiving /mentor, nor can new players ask for them. 


TIPS

1. Befriend the person and get to know them. If they are your friend you will surely be the one they give /mentor to and possibly a 5k patron reward.

2. Do not give them links to help sites or tell them to read this or that, this will not encourage them to play and they won't ever get to level 1.

3. Teach them how to move around the map and level effectively, don't drone on about details. Answer their questions that arise via normal gameplay.

4. Do not tell the player that they need to pay to have fun, the fact is you don't. Your goal is to help this person get to level 1, not to win the Inquest.

5. If they are too stupid for you to deal with then kindly tell them you must go and that they should seek another guide by possibly typing /guide again.


Glitchless Staff will randomly make characters and seek guides. The guides that are truly following these tips will receive additional rewards.


Rules are posted in the Announcements , important parts of long announcements are in red (announcement dates are shown, so you can look them up yourself)


4/10/2006

In an effort to prevent mentor buying players under level 10 cannot receive gold and items nor can they receive or settle kingdoms. They can however still give kingdoms and send items in exchange for 1 gold to allow people to retrieve their belongings off of mules. Anyone still attempting to buy mentors off new players (or sell or buy swimsuit votes) will be banned .





9/10/2005

RWK Swimsuit Edition is here! For players above level 10 you can now set a swimsuit image and show the link to people outside of RWK. If your picture receives a "Sexy!" vote then you will get a swimsuit point. The person with the most swimsuit points at the end of each month on each server (resets each month) will receive 100 dollars cash (or credit if preferred). That's right, we'll be sending you a money order instead of the other way around for once. Note the rules, however, or you will risk losing your account. Your swimsuit picture can NOT be vile, illegal, distasteful, nude, or otherwise inappropriate. The object is to post the sexiest female picture you can find - you will disqualified if you do not follow this rule regardless of your number of votes. You can use /s NAME to view people's swimsuit pictures and /sw to check how many votes you have. Repeated votes from the same IP blocks will not count. >You are NOT allowed to post your link in RWK chat.


8/2/2005

Purchasing PayPal items for someone else's character in trade for game assets is strictly forbidden . If seen auctioning PayPal purchases (buying or selling) you will be banned. You can trade PayPal dollars for game assets, but you cannot make purchases for others. If you want to buy an item for a friend then send him the PayPal money and let him purchase it his or herself. This rule has been in effect for years but will now be more strictly enforced. If someone purposefully buys you something in an effort to get you in trouble simply report it to orders@racewarkingdoms.com immediately.


Character sharing and trading is no longer allowed. We will not be actively seeking out and punishing those who break this rule, but if you auction that you are selling a character, or e-mail us complaining that your clicker stole your character or that you were scammed in a character transaction you will be banned for failing to follow the rules. These rules are for your protection.


5/15/2005 (no rules here but an announcement that may interest people)


We have updated our policy on deleting inactive accounts. Any account that has a purchasable quest item or super relic (MR, CR, VS or better) will never be deleted. Accounts that have no purchasable items may be deleted after 6 months of not being accessed, however.


8/23/2003




To help with the confusion over the auction rules we have simplified them: You may not sell anything or ask for any price check, item, service (clickers, runners, etc), or form of payment (money order, Paypal, etc) unless it is in auction.




If you are muted and you get on another character before the mute wears off and speak in chat you will be 3 day or permanently banned without question . If you feel you've been unfairly muted your only option is to send an email to help@racewarkingdoms.com and the matter will be investigated. Mute evasion or false claims to unfair muting will result in banning.






Back to home

pages.title: Skills
pages.text:
## Skills





This information is taken right off the official help site , paraphrased
an expanded a bit with my own comments to make it (hopefully) clearer.





Skills can range from 0 to 99
without a Skill Potencifier , and can be trained up
to 125 with an SP. � They will increase randomly 
as you play if you assign some experience to them (actions menu, skills, %
experience). � That �randomly� part means
you never know when you will get a skill increase, it can�t be predicted, and
there is NO skills exp bar to watch. �
Also note that the experience you put towards skills is NOT 
going towards leveling. � If you allocate
99% of your exp to skills you will never level.





The higher the percentage of
experience you have going to skills the higher your chance of getting a skill
increase. � Betting more exp on skills
does not make the process less random, it just increases your odds. � Think of it like rolling dice and trying to
hit a particular number. � Each roll is
completely random and is not affected by any previous rolls. � However, if you use a 12-sided die your odds
of hitting a given number are 1 in 12 (8.33%) per roll while a 6-sided die
gives you a 1 in 6 chance (16.7%). � You
can only train one skill at a time, but you can become proficient in multiple
skill categories by training several of them one at a time. Skills become more
difficult to improve as they mature. This means it�s harder to advance from 89
to 90 than it was moving from 8 to 9. �
You can type /skills at any time to see the rating of all your skills.





Put another way, the randomness
means skilling is not like a container into which you pour exp until it's
full. � It is like a wheel of fortune you
spin with each fight, more exp put towards skills means fewer numbers on the
wheel and better odds of winning.





Skills can take a long time to
develop. � I think it took me about 20
months to get all 10 of my skills from 0 to 99 as a Templar. � Your mileage may vary, and the game now has
several ways to boost your chances of gaining a skill.





Every creature you fight seems to give
you the same chance for getting a skill, so Magician�s Apprentices are as good
as Yoros from a skills point of view. �
Every kingdom in the game except the Sky Shrine (100,Sky,250)
gives you the same chance of gaining a skill. �
The additional exp in Hell and Sky do not help you skill faster.





Just doing damage gives you a
chance to gain a skill increase, you don�t need to completely kill your enemy
to skill. � Some players take advantage
of this to do minimal damage and keep an enemy alive as long as possible so
they don�t lose the time it takes to click on Submit between attacks and spawn
a new enemy. � This is often done with
relic damage and called �slow vamping� since you are trying to kill your enemy
slowly. � I find it boring as hell and
don�t use the technique but others are quite fond of it.





Fighting faster helps you gain
skills faster. � Each attack gives you a
chance for a skill, so making more attacks per hour means more skilling chances
per hour, and over time more skills gained per hour. � For this reason, the first skill to increase is Haste since it
will then help you increase all the others more quickly. � You can also equip Impetuosity relics to
increase your modified Haste. � As your
raw Haste rises you can replace the Impetuosities with more useful relics.





The Eternal relics can be used to
boost your modified skills up to 99. �
See the relics section for details on which
relic boosts which skill. � Even with an
SP you can�t use relics to increase a skill above 99, only raw skills can be in
the 100 to 125 range.





Skill increases are dramatically
slower above 99. � An SP will let you train in that range but it will be
slower going than when skills are below 100. �
Some players use Sacrificial Skillation 
relics to help them skill faster, although a side effect is increased delay
between actions. � I found the delay
intolerable, but that�s a personal preference, some people seem happy using them.
You can also equip Impetuosity relics to
boost your modified haste skill and reduce the action
delay, which lets you get in more attacks per minute and thus more chances to
skill per unit time. � Each Impetuosity adds 5 to your base haste skill
up to a total modified value of 99. �
Using DsD relics is the most effective way
to skill faster (ideally with one DaD as well for
a combo bonus ) since they add no action delay,
but these are among the most expensive relics in the game (expect to pay real
money for them, not just game gold) so you�d expect them to be useful.




Skill effects are as follows:





1. Attacking Accuracy 
- This raises the chance of your successfully landing a hit on your enemy. � It works by
increasing your modified Dex stat for Berserkers, Armsmen and Archers, modified
Agi for Monks.

At a skill of 99, you are 5 times more likely to hit your enemy.

At a skill of 125, you are 6 times more likely to hit your enemy.




2. Attacking Precision - This increases the chances of you
dealing high or max damage to your enemy.

At a skill of 99, you will always deal at least 77% of max damage.

At a skill of 125, you will always deal at least 97% of max damage.




3. Casting Accuracy - This raises the chance of your
successfully casting a spell. � It works by increasing your modified Cnc stat, which is
accuracy for Wizards, Magicians and Theurgists but translates to higher
damage for Archers.

At a skill of 99, you are 5 times more likely to successfully cast.

At a skill of 125, you are 6 times more likely to successfully cast.




4. Casting Precision - This raises the chance of you dealing
high or max spell damage.

At a skill of 99, you will always deal at least 77% of max damage.

At a skill of 125, you will always deal at least 97% of max damage.




5. Criticality - This increases your chance of executing a
critical attack. This includes critical, crippling, mana surge, mana rush,
euphoric rush, euphoric surge, etc.

At a skill of 99, you are 5 times more likely to score a critical attack .

At a skill of 125, you are 6 times more likely to score a critical attack .




6. Regeneration - This affects the amount of life that you
naturally recover each time you attack. Every skill point you get past 99 gives
you an extra 1% durability boost prior to relic calculations.

At a skill of 99, you will fully regenerate all your hit points after every
attack.

At a skill of 125, you will have a 26% boost to durability.




7. Kingmanship - This allows a kingdom to gain increasing
interest on all kingdom aspects, (except for treasury and guard) every hour,
granted the King is currently present. Kingdom aspects include Population,
Morale, Food, Army, Walls, Moats and Cannons.

At a skill of 99, kingdom aspects (armies,
population, fortifications) will go up by 1% per hour and you will gain
an additional 50% interest (Trs interest is 0.5692%/hr
at a skill of 0 and 0.8504%/hr at a skill of 99) .

At a skill of 125, kingdom aspects (armies, population,
fortifications) will go up by 1.25% per hour and you will gain an
additional 62.5% interest (Trs interest of 0.9242%/hr) .
For more info see the relic combos on the relics
page. 

At a skill of 50 the 5 deposits per hour limit is removed (Thanks to Yoshister on S1 for sharing that information)





Owning
more than 1,000 kingdoms will reduce the Trs interest rate. � More kingdoms = greater reduction, see the Announcement from 7/20/2006
for the details.


At a skill
of 75 you get an additional Kingdom attack per hour, at a skill of 99 you get
two additional attacks.


At a skill
of 50 you get unlimited deposits per 24 hours




8. Haste - This lowers the delay associated with all game
actions.

At a skill of 99, all game action delays will take 50% as much time as
usual.

At a skill of 125, all game action delays will take 44% as much time as usual.




9. Defensive - This increases your ability to withstand
damage. � Higher def skill means higher armor
defense with any given set of armor, which will reduce
enemy healing . � That effect can make
defensive skill useful for killing harder critters.

At a skill 99, you will be able to withstand 25 TIMES more damage.

At a skill 125, you will be able to withstand 832 TIMES more damage.





10. Monk Discipline 
- Players will be able to do damage with just their fists if they have NO
weapons and NO shields in either hand. This skill turns your fists into
weapons: a 0 in the skill is like a rusty dagger, a 69 is like a Rustless
Lifeceaser, a 70 is like a Mace of Ressurection and beyond that is incredible
power (30% more power each time you go up a single point). Unlike other
fighters, Monks do damage based on Cnt and their chance of landing a blow is
dependant on Agi, so basically their Cnt is their Str and their Agi is their
Dex.

At a skill of 99, you'd do approximately 2,500 times more damage than you
would with a Rustless Lifeceaser.

At a skill of 125, you'd do approximately 30,000 times more damage than you
would with a Rustless Lifeceaser.





This information is taken right off the official help site ,
paraphrased an expanded a bit with my own comments to make it (hopefully)
clearer.

pages.title: Sleeved Cards
pages.text:
## Sleeved Cards



Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden. Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.





If you play poker in RWK there is
a possibility that you will collect some sleeved cards. � These are supposed to be the equivalent of a
card shark slipping a card up his shirtsleeve during a game to use for cheating
at a later time. � In RWK it happens
automatically when your pocket cards (the first two you are dealt and which
only you can see) are a same color pair, jacks or higher. � One will be slipped up your sleeve and
appear as miniature cards superimposed over your pocket cards as well as lined
up near your �submit� button. � The
picture below shows sleeved Jacks, Kings and Aces although it may take some
close looking to see.











Without the pocket cards in the
background it�s easier to see how the sleeved cards are positioned while you
are playing poker.











You can have up to 8 sleeved cards
at one time, one each diamond and spade, ace, king, queen and jack.





Poker


If you want to use a sleeved card
to �cheat� at poker, that is to replace a pocket card in your hand with a
sleeved card, you just wait until the last card is dealt and then click on the
sleeved card you wish to use where it�s superimposed over the one you want to
replace. � In the image above, if you
wanted to replace the 2 of spades with the king you would just click on the
king of spades where it is over the 2 of spades, and there is a 50% chance you
will succeed in changing your pocket cards from a 2 and king to a pair of
kings. � If your attempt at cheating
fails, everyone playing cards sees a system message that you were caught
cheating and you just lose the hand and the pocket card you attempted to
use. � If you already have the same card
in your hand you will also lose it and the hand. � If you click on the king of spades where it is superimposed over
your king of diamonds pocket card you will replace the king of diamonds with
the king of spades, so you want to be careful where you click to be sure you
make the substitution you wanted. � You
can try cheating twice in the same hand to replace both your pocket cards with
sleeved ones. � Since each attempt has a
50% chance of failing, I believe your overall chance to succeed with a double
cheat is only 25%, so you probably only want to attempt it to try for a very
good winning hand.





There is an announcement explaining
sleeved cards in poker:




1/3/2005

There has been a new addition to poker: cheating. Getting certain card combos
will result in you automatically slipping a card "up your sleeve"
which you can later use to replace one of your existing two cards to better
your hand. Be careful using this technique as there is always a 50% chance of
being caught and forced to fold. Also if you try to swap a card in that is
already in your hand you will be caught for cheating 100% of the time for
having two of the same card.





Fighting


Sleeved cards can also be used to
enhance your fighting abilities for a short time. � If you click on the card icon next to your �submit� button it
will trigger a special bonus for the duration of the fight. � Different cards give different bonuses, and
if you have a Sash of the Blood Rogue you get more use
from sleeved cards than players without a sash. � These fighting enhancements are probably most useful to players
participating in the acumens, or may be useful against beasts. � Using a sleeved card for some routine battle
against a normal critter would be a real waste. � You lose the card when you use it.





Black card
bonuses:





Jack - ultra resist


Queen � 50% chance to negate
resists (a sort of double negative there)


King � ultra revive (50% chance of
reviving, I presume it�s spontaneous as with a ToTF)


Ace � Ultra crit (2x the chance of
delivering critical damage)





If you have a Sash the bonuses are
apparently stronger, although I don�t know by how much. � It also gives the player the ability to use Red sleeved cards to enter ambush mode, which
gives a 50% chance for you to negate an attack against you. � Without a Sash you do not get
any benefit from using a Red sleeved card in a
fight.





The benefits are explained in 2 announcements shown below:





1/4/2005


The attack bonuses have now been
implemented for all sleeved black cards. The Black Jack gives Ultra Resist (50%
void/mes resist), the Black Queen gives Negate Resist (50% anti void/mes
resist), the Black King gives Ultra Revive (50% Revive), and the Black Ace
gives Ultra Crit (more than doubles all criticality chances). Note that using
these abilities will cause you to lose your sleeved card (which you can later get
back again playing poker) and will last until you successfully kill a creature
or player. These bonuses should add an extra element of strategy to inquests as
players must decide when to use these abilities and may also help with slaying
difficult creatures or beasts. You can only use one of these abilities at a
time since they overwrite each other if used simultaneously.





3/17/2005


Legend has it that there was a
card playing Rogue who bled his opponents of their money mercilessly. He would
bleed them literally as well if they ever confronted him about his cheating.
His sash can now be quested for a hefty and random cost of gold, or purchased
by clicking the buy items link when in the game. The sash greatly increases the
power of the attack bonuses gained from sleeved black cards and also allows you
to gain an Ambush ability from any sleeved red card. The Ambush ability when
activated gives you a 50 percent chance of ambushing someone who attacks you,
causing their attack to be useless (their relics don't fire and they can't
attack) and still allowing you to perform your counter attack as always. Note
that you only leave Ambush mode after it has once successfully fired,
guaranteeing some usage out of every sleeved red card. Having the sash also
allows your Devastating Blow, Crippling Blow, Mana Rush, and Mana Surge to work
on beasts.

pages.title: Special Locations
pages.text:
## Special Locations of RWK



## Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved


Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.







Most of the 450,000 kingdoms in the game are pretty much the same. You have the same list of 70 critters to fight, and although the names change as you move among the planes their stats are the same. Aside from the changes with plane (Dungeon gives 2x Surface gold, Sky gives 2x Surface experience, Hell gives 3x Surface experience and Heaven gives gives 3x Surface gold) there isn't a lot of variety in location. However, there are a handful of kingdoms that are different, usually called "special kingdoms" for their unique properties. They offer things like harder enemies, more gold, better skilling odds, more drops, or figure in various quests. I mention these locations on other pages but I'm also consolidating all of them here as well.


***NOTE***

For these locations, some items will drop or convert automatically as soon as you enter. For other items, you need to kill creatures on the "New Fight" list. If a specific creature or range of creatures is required, fight and kill those. If no specific creature is mentioned, just kill anything you can and keep killing it until the item drops or transforms .





Character, Location Name or Item Name | 
Location | 
Special Properties | 
| 



Ash Collector | 
141,Hell,174 | 
Home of the Ash Collector , one of the game's Non Player Characters involved in various quests. You need access to Hell ( Transcendental Sanction , Omega Sanction , or 10-day trial access ) and a Templar's Wand to reach him. | 



Balace Armorcraft Guide | 
Port of Baneesh (128, Sur, 234)
When you enter the port for the first time the Guide will automatically drop unless you already have a Tradeskill Manifesto | 



Brawl Arena | 
40,Sur,140 | 
Location closed to players except during the monthly Acumen Inquest , a tournament for the highest level 128 players who wish to participate. | 



Carella (port) | 
129,Sur,51 | 
Location where players can risk an EEoGT (or likely several of them) trying to get a Sash of the Blood Rogue
|



Center of Evil | 
141,Hel,146 | 
Entrance to The Forge plane for players who have completed the required quests. From here just move down. | 



Central Port(Andal) | 
130,Sur,164 | 
Location used in the RDADMS Lessonbook quest | 



Chained Libertine | 
69,Sur,69 | 
You never need to actually visit the Chained Libertine but she does have a location. Trade her a pure Theurgal Equalizer relic (just use /add Chained Libertine to put her on your trade list) and you get back a Theurgal Conquest relic. | 



Dungeon Entrance | 
240,Sur,60 | 
Location where players with a Lava Splicer need to go to move from Surface to Dungeon. | 



Dungeon Mountains | 
Various locations in Dun including 207,Dun,188 and the Insane Dungeon at 100,Dun,200 | 
A range of Kingdoms in the Dungeon plane, locations other than those mentioned will also work, probably the regions where the spiral 'mountains' are seen on the Dungeon map . The Mountains are where Satan's Calling will drop randomly off any critter. | 



Dungeon Spiral | 
150,Dun,230 | 
Location where you take a Temple Stone to convert it into an Experience Egg ( Lava Splicer , Transcendental Sanction , Omega Sanction , or 10-day trial access gets you into Dungeon. | 



Dwarven Weaponcraft Guide | 
Port of Perydan (150, Sur, 197) | 
When you enter the port for the first time the Guide will automatically drop unless you already have a Tradeskill
Manifesto . | 



Forbidden Mines | 
162,Sur,159 | 
Location requires a Scroll of Forbidden Rights to enter. It offers much more gold from drops when the Mines are Laced , Saturated or Imbued . If a Tecton is dropped on the Mines when Saturated or Imbued the gold bonus disappears (thanks to Alundra Mischef for that information). One of the "Special" kingdoms that can not be owned by players. You can not coup or wage war against it but you can drop a Tecton there to cause damage. | 



Forbidden Palace | 
162,Hev,159 | 
Location requires a Scroll of Forbidden Rights to enter. It offers harder then normal creatures, a high drop rate, and is the location where the enemies can void or devoid your relics when you attack them. Has a very high drop rate. One of the "Special" kingdoms that can not be owned by players. You can not coup or wage war against it but you can drop a Tecton there to cause damage. | 



Heaven Citizen | 
150,Hev,150 | 
Location of a Citizen (NPC) needed for several quests ( Transcendental Sanction , Omega Sanction , or 10-day trial access gets you into Heaven). | 



Heaven Keep | 
66,Hev, 66 | 
Location has harder than normal creatures and a very high drop rate, requires access to Heaven to reach ( Transcendental Sanction , Omega Sanction , or 10-day trial access gets you into Heaven). One of the "Special" kingdoms that can not be owned by players. You can not coup or wage war against it but you can drop a Tecton there to cause damage. | 



Heaven Steps | 
220,Sky,66 | 
Location to take Lava Boots to convert them into Demonic Pinions , not a "Special" kingdom and can be player owned. | 



Hell Keep | 
66,Hel, 66 | 
Location has harder than normal creatures and a very high drop rate, requires access to Hell and a Templar's
Wand to reach ( Transcendental Sanction , Omega Sanction , or 10-day trial access gets you into Hell). One of the "Special" kingdoms that can not be owned by players. You can not coup or wage war against it but you can drop a Tecton there to cause damage. | 



Hell Shoreline | 
Various locations, including 145,Hell,153 and 132,Hel,146 (I used this one myself) | 
Location where a Crystal of Lars transforms into a Templar's Wand. Various locations should work, look at the map of Hell and the shoreline should be any place where the solid land is bordering on Magma. | 



Hermits (4 per plane) | 
0,Plane,0 and 0,Plane,299 and 299,Plane,299 and 299,Plane,0 | 
Each plane has 4 Hermits on it, one in each corner. Every plane is a 300x300 kingdom square with numbering from 0 to 299. First number in a location is the number of kingdoms East from 0 and second number is kingdoms North from 0. So, the lower left corner is 0,0 and the upper right corner is 299,299.


Hermits in dungeon can enchant blessings (Actions, Options, Preferred Blessing) onto items in your spell and weapon slots for 1 billion gold. Hermits in Sky can do the same with approbations for 2 billion gold (Actions, Options, Preferred Approbation).


The location of the Hermit determines what item will be enchanted. Go to 0,0 for Spell 1; to 299,0 for Spell 2; 0,299 for Left hand weapon; and 299,299 for Right hand weapon. If you look at the map and think of your body superimposed onto it with hands and spells in the various corners the Hermit to use for each item corresponds to which corner that item would fall into. | 



InSaNe Dungeon | 
100,Dun,200 | 
Location that has the hardest creatures in the game, requires access to Dungeon ( Lava Splicer , Transcendental Sanction , Omega Sanction , or 10-day trial access gets you to Dungeon) and Demonic Pinions to enter (it is apparently in the Dungeon Mountains).

Vampire relic damage at this location is 25 million times less than normal. | 



Lake In Heaven | 
256,Hev,256 | 
Location to go to complete the quest for a Sanction of Surpassment . | 



Lava Falls | 
0,Dun,150 | 
Location where Satan's Calling transforms into Haste of Hell , not a "Special" kingdom and can be player owned. | 



Le Puddle Deep | 
140,Sur,190 | 
Location requires Aqualungs to enter since, as the name implies, it is a water kingdom. Has harder creatures than in normal kingdoms (all damage there is reduced by 25x), they give 5x normal Surface gold, and occasionally drop Dur armor and special weapons and spells (Rapid, Major, Accurate spells and Light, Heavy, Precise weapons). Has a higher then normal drop rate. Basically the same as the Pub but on water. The Puddle figures in several quests and is one of the "Special" kingdoms that can not be owned by players. You can not coup or wage war against it but you can drop a Tecton there to cause damage. | 



Mesmer's Book of Mythics | 
Port Dillusia (274,Sur, 158) | 
When you enter the port for the first time the Book will automatically drop unless you already have a Tradeskill Manifesto . | 



Middle of Surface | 
150,Sur,150 | 
Home of the Ash Keeper , one of the game's Non Player Characters involved in various quests. | 



Middle of Heaven | 
150,Hev,150 | 
Location of the Citizen who upgrades Demonic , Earthen and Crystal items using ash. | 



Northern Port (Kremk) | 
047,Sur,290 | 
Location used in the RDADMS Lessonbook quest | 



Ports | 
Various Surface locations.

See map or in game use the Actions menu, Set Sail to see all the Port locations | 
Ports are scattered around the Surface plane to allow players who do not own Aqualungs to travel
across water by setting sail. You can get a listing of ALL port locations at any time by using the Actions menu,
Set Sail while playing RWK.


You can also see all the port locations, their names, and how they relate to other Sur features by viewing the surface map here . To use the ports you must first get to one of them by whatever combination of walking and teleporting works for you. Once there you can sail from one port to any other for a fee in gold. 


There is a fairly long delay while sailing, you might find it faster to set sail, then relog your character rather than wait for the voyage to end of its own accord. Only Sur has ports, they don't exist, and there is no equivalent, on the 4 other planes. Ports are not a "Special" kingdom and can be player owned. | 



Quake Keeper | 
150,Hel,150 | 
You never need to actually visit the Quake Keeper but he does have a location. He will provide you with an Essence Element of Tecton for
waging war more effectively or an Essence Element of Stone to increase your kingdom defense. Each item costs 999 runes. Give him a Rusty Dagger and you get the Tecton, give an Old Knife and you get a Stone. | 



Sailor's Crimp | 
130,Sky,140 | 
Location where the Sailor's Crimp drops | 



Sky Citizen | 
069,Sky,157 | 
Location of a Citizen (NPC) needed for several quests ( Mountain Tamer , Transcendental Sanction , Omega Sanction , or 10-day trial access gets you into Sky). | 



Sky Entrance | 
78,Sur,242 | 
Location where players with a Mountain Tamer need to go to move from Surface to Sky. | 



Sky Shrine | 
100,Sky,250 | 
Location requires a Scroll of Forbidden Rights to enter. It offers a 10% bonus to your chances for gaining a skill, so it is like
equipping one Sacrificial Skillation relic without the delay. Home of the Shrine Keeper , an NPC who can convert D*D relics of one type into another for 2,000 each change. You don't need to visit this location to use the Keeper, just use /add Shrine Keeper to add him to your trade and pm lists. This location also has a very high drop rate and is one of the "Special" kingdoms that can not be owned by players. You can not coup or wage war against it but you can drop a Tecton there to cause damage. | 



Smallest Sky Cloud | 
140,Sky,30 | 
Location where you take an Experience Egg to convert it into an Experience Gravitator ( Mountain Tamer , Transcendental Sanction , Omega Sanction , or 10-day trial access gets you into Sky). | 



Solon's Arcane Booklet | 
Port of Merlana (24, Sur, 61) | 
When you enter the port for the first time the Booklet will automatically drop unless you already have a Tradeskill Manifesto . | 



Southern Port (Belania) | 
261,Sur,020 | 
Location used in the RDADMS Lessonbook quest | 



Stallion's Pub | 
150,Sur,145 | 
Identical to the Puddle except it's on land so any player can enter. Closest port is Andal (130,Sur,164). Has harder creatures than in normal kingdoms (all damage there is reduced by 25x), they give 5x normal Surface gold, and occasionally drop Dur armor and special weapons and spells (Rapid, Major, Accurate spells and Light, Heavy, Precise weapons). Has a higher then normal drop rate. The Pub figures in several quests and is one of the "Special" kingdoms that can not be owned by players. You can not coup or wage war against it but you can drop a Tecton there to cause damage. | 



Sylvain Fletcher's Workbook | 
Port of Carella (129, Sur, 51) | 
When you enter the port for the first time the Workbook will automatically drop unless you already have a Tradeskill Manifesto . | 



The Temple | 
40,Sur,225 | 
Required location for several quests. Location where Platinum Ore converts into a Mountain Tamer , Fyre Rock into a Lava Splicer , Mountain Tamer and Lava Splicer combine into a Transcendental Sanction , 9 items combine into an Omega Sanction , and where the Carbon Encasement drops. The Temple can be owned by players, in that sense it's not a "special" kingdom like the Pub, Keeps, etc. | 



Vogar | 
Each corner of Sur | 
You never need to actually visit Vogar but he does have a location (4 actually). If you want to make a Putrefaction relic you need a pure Vogar's Vice relic, a pure Encephalon Decay relic, and a kingdom with at least 5 runes. You trade the Vogar's Vice to Vogar (just use /add Vogar to put him on your trade list) and he takes the ED and 5 runes, gives you a pure Putrefaction in return. | 






Page updated 23 March 2023

Thank You! 





## Thank You For Your Support!



I�ve been helping players in RWK since the current version came up in September 2001. This web site began as just a place for me to sell the items I was crafting but it evolved into a help site instead. I have never explicitly received anything from Glitchless for helping players learn about RWK other than the special mentor messages , it�s something I have been doing on my own. With the addition of patrons and now Mentors to the game, players have two ways to acknowledge those who have helped them. Some folks have been kind enough to name me as their Mentor or patron and I, in turn, would like to thank them for their support by publicly acknowledging it.


Names are shown in reverse order, newer ones first then older ones after. Unless otherwise noted the player named me his/her mentor.

pages.text:
## Miscellaneous Advice

## Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.

Read the login message sometime: Type /faq and read the game rules or you will be banned! Type /help for game details!

Read the rules: If you wish to buy/sell/trade items from another game you MUST have the templar upgrade.

## Special Locations

Forbidden Palace = 162,Hev,159 you need a Scroll of Forbidden Rights to enter (very rare and random drop or $50 purchase)

Forbidden Mines = 162,Sur,159 you need a Scroll of Forbidden Rights to enter (very rare and random drop or $50 purchase)

Mine Keeper: If you were to give me an Essence Element of Treasure whilst the mine's were saturated...imagine the result!

lacing the mines = someone buys a
rune for the forbidden mines and all players there get 1 mil gold per kill
until the new game hour

saturating the mines = players buy 10 runes for the mines and all players there get huge amounts of gold per kill until the new game hour.

imbuing the mines = player gives the Mine Keeper an EEoT, runes go to 999, and players there can make several bil an hour

The Ash Collector location is 141,Hell,174, pay him a visit, give him a gold piece and have a little chat.

Ash Collector: Perhaps if you were to give me 1 billion gold and 1000 ash I could...possibly [summon] a beast.

Ash Collector: For 1.5 billion gold and 2250 ash I would summon a [public] beast with no risk to your investment.

Ash Collector: But for 2 billion and 7500 ash I could definitely summon a private approbation yielding beast just for you!

The Ash Collector can also add a Devoid to your D*D if you have 1000 ash and a pure devoid relic. Trade him the D*D and he will take a devoid and you will "find" a D*Devoid relic.

Stallions Pub at 150,Sur,145 is a special KD that gives 5x normal sur gold, has special drops, is part of some quests, and has harder critters.

Le Puddle Deep at 140,Sur,190 is like the Pub except you need aqualungs ($20 purchase from Glitchless) to get there since it's a water kingdom.

Heaven Keep 66,Hev,66

Hell Keep 66,Hel,66

Insane Dun 100,Dun,200 Sky Shrine 100,Sky,250

## Crafting Workbooks And The Ports Where They Drop

Sylvain Fletcher's Workbook - 129,Sur,51 (Carella)

Dwarven Weaponcraft Guide - 150,Sur,197 (Perydan)

Mesmer's Book of Mythics - 274,Sur,158 (Dillusia)

Solon's Arcane Booklet - 24,SUR,61 (Merlana)

Balace Armorcraft - Guide 128,234 (Baneesh)

## Quest Locations

Wand of Transportation - 000,Dun,205

Heaven Lake for SoS - 256,Hev,256

Dungeon Mountains = 140,Dun,130 or 000,Dun,150 or 240,Dun,90 or 240,Dun,060

Dungeon lava is towards the very top of row 009,Dun,256

Shores of Hell = 145,Hel,153

Kremk = Northern Port, Belania = Southern Port, Andal = Central Port

Fisherman's Catch = Kremk 047,Sur,290 (Northern Port)

Toe = Andal 130,Sur,164 (Central Port)

Finger = Belania 261,Sur,020 (Southern Port)

Coin = Puddle (140, 190)

Brawl arena loc = 40,Sur,140

Dungeon entrance (for Lava Splicer owners only) is at 240,Sur,060

Sky entrance (for Mountain Tamer owners only) is at 078,Sur,242

## General Advice

Your relics, skills and equipment are often more important than character level in determining what you're able to kill. More levels give more stat points and those will certainly help, but a very well equipped character can kill the hardest normal enemy at a surprisingly low level.

The "right" monster to kill at any given level will depend on what your goals are at the time. On Surface and without an Experience Egg or Experience Gravitator you will need at least 3 fights before you can level. Once you are fighting a creature that gives you enough experience to do this you will be leveling as quickly as the plane allows. If you want to earn the most gold from fights, attack the highest critter you can kill with 1 hit most of the time.

If you want to maximize your drops without sacrificing leveling rate, fight the lowest level enemy you can kill in one attack while still getting enough exp to train every 3 attacks.

If you just want the most drops, fight any of the easiest 20 enemies on the "New Fight" list.

Most of the game is explained on the Official help site , just type /help in-game or visit www.racewarkingdoms.com/help/

Don't confuse doing well in RWK with having achieved something meaningful in life. This is a game and it is fun, but realistically speaking it is mostly a waste of time. Becoming cocky about your level in this obscure game can make you look foolish. If you want to brag, do something with genuine substance in real life to earn that attitude.

Some folks want to have a better character, but don't like the game enough to build it themselves. If you don't want to actually play it yourself, perhaps you should reassess why you even have an account in RWK.

## Access and Paying

The only access you get for free is surface land kingdoms, anything more costs real money.

All planes access (incl. Hev, Hell) is $100 but does not include Sur water kingdoms.
Water kingdoms require Aqualungs, which cost $20.
Click on "Buy Items" in-game for details.

## Random Bits

Runes increase KD defense, they are used in high end crafting for items above 1 bil base, and are always used (and consumed) when enchanting or disenchanting items.

"Nuke" is game slang for waging war to destroy a KD (kingdom menu, wage war). The game has no nuclear weapons, although Tectons come close.

Quest items cannot be sold, traded or destroyed. Anything under "Other" in your inventory (type /inv in game to get a list) is a quest item.

You enchant or disenchant to raise chantcraft. To do this you need a kingdom and will consume at least 1 rune per attempt. Your chantcraft skill is capped at 10 without a Tradeskill Manifesto (quest item).

Spending 1 bil (for a rune) per attempt at the start makes chantcraft an expensive hobby. The highest level items consume 250 runes per attempt, succeed or fail so at that point it beomes a Very expensive hobby.

You can block someone's posts in shout using the command /ignore "name" (without the quotes), where "name" is the player to block.

Rune Keeper dialog for treasure elements:

Rune Keeper: I have the power to turn rune to essence and [essence] to rune. I also have the ability to combine the power of [six].

Rune Keeper: Give me 1 million gold, 50 ash and 50 runes for Treasure, Give me 2.5 million gold, 250 ash and 250 runes for Great [Treasure].

Rune Keeper: I'll change Treasure to 50 runes, and Great Treasure to 250 runes for free. [Also]...

Rune Keeper: Perhaps handing me 999 gold, 999 ash and 999 runes might make something.

Rune Keeper: SoS, SP, SoD, GHB, FotB, and BB can be unified into the [SoSe] ... the powers of which would defy your belief.

Templar is a $50 upgrade, you can't get it for free

GOD (in-game handle for RWK's owner/developer) on clickers: "It's legal to share accounts, just don't expect compensation when they steal your stuff"

The "skill machine" is the personification of the random chances for getting a skill increase, there is no actual machine to physically kick. It's just slang for an in-game joke

NCL = No Chat Land = actions menu, options, shout disabled. Not an actual location.

Compared to normal weapons, Light are twice as fast, Heavy can do double damage, and Precise double your modified Dex stat (type /st to see the modified values).

Compared to normal spells, Rapid are twice as fast, Major can do double damage, Accurate double your Cnc stat (type /st to see the modified values).

Clickers, skillers, ashers = other people paid to play your account for you, sometimes they also sell your equipment without your permission. See GOD's comment above.

The price of a critter as guard (kingdom menu) is the same as all its stats, and the base value of all its drops.

If you pull down the "actions" and "kingdom" menus you'll see most of your options in the game, do some exploring and you will learn a lot about RWK.

If you pull down 'set sail' in the 'actions' menu you can see the coordinates for all the ports. You can view them even when you are not located in a port.

Modified Cnc = base*((1.2)^x)*((1.5)^y))where x=# titus, cara, or mental focus and y=#SoC or Caster's Revenge relics you have equipped.

Modified Dex = base*((1.2)^x)*((1.5)^y))where x=# titus, cara, or Archer's eye and y=#Dex Hoist or Melee's Rage relics you have equipped.

Kingdom Population increases every hour in which the King is present.

Access Plane Bonuses compared to Surface:

Hell = 3x exp

Dungeon = 2x gold

Sky = 2x exp

Heaven = 3x gold

Forge = Both more gold and more exp.
