Beast Kill Messages (yellow system text):

Beast = PB, Super = CC, Mega = DW, Ultra = EHA, Horrid = UT

Damage Relics

1. Touch of the Damned,
2. *Blood Pinch, 3. *The Calling of Lestat, 4. *Blood Bath, 5. *Vampiric Leech, 6. *Death Spike , 7. *Denial of the Believer, 8. *Vampire's Scorn, 9. *Devil's Deliverance, 10. *Encephalon Decay, 11. *Vogar's Vice, 12. *Vampire's Amaranthine Scorn, 13. Devil's Deleterious Deliverance, 14. Various D*D Relics

## What Is This?

Skills

Actions menu-->skills. Choose the skill to work on, set some exp towards it, then play normally and hope for an increase.

Skills are random, so skilling is not like a container into which you pour exp until it's full. It is more like a wheel of fortune you spin with each fight, more exp lost means fewer numbers on the wheel and a greater chance of winning.

Ash

When you destroy an item with a relic (meaning virtually all drops) and you get 1 gold and 1 ash.

You can check how much ash you have by typing /ash.

Ash has many uses, including getting access quest items, adding a Devoid to DxD relics, summoning beasts, making treasure elements, modifying high-end relics, playing poker (type /poker).

You can't trade ash, you trade the drops that are used to make ash. Players offering to buy or sell ash actually mean armor, weapon or spell drops with attached relics.

If you don't have Templar and access to hell, OR a KD with at least 50 runes, ash is useless to you other than for playing poker or something you are saving for an upgrade. Just sell your drops in auction to get some gold.

Items showing as "Other" in your inventory list is part of a quest, they cannot be sold, traded to other players or destroyed. You are stuck with them. Type /help or visit www.racewarkingdoms.com/help/ for more details.

All relics are explained on the help site, type /help in game or visit www.racewarkingdoms.com/help/rwkrelics.htm for details.

All skills are explained on the help site, type /help in game or visit
www.racewarkingdoms.com/help/rwkskills.htm for details.

All quests are explained on the help site, type /help in game or visit
www.racewarkingdoms.com/help/rwkquest.htm for details.

The help site has downloadable maps of all 5 planes, type /help or visit www.racewarkingdoms.com/help/ for details.

The rules are available by typing /faq in game.

The help site is your friend, learn to use it and you will look less foolish in chat.

## Obeying The Rules

Begging can get you banned from this game. Type /faq and read the rules while you still have an account.

Buying or selling in shout can get you a 2 day ban.

Offering or seeking services in shout can get you a 2 day ban

Price checks in shout can get you a 2 day ban.

## Begging

Beg = To ask for as charity, To ask earnestly for or of; entreat.

Keep in mind that begging makes enemies, can get you muted, and could ultimately get you banned. Overall, it's a very bad idea. Other players will chain kill you and they will destroy any kingdoms you build.

## Rules of Chat (from the official help site, differs from the rules in /faq)

The first thing people notice and go to in the game is the chat. So, Glitchless is going to lay out the rules of chat to help make it so newbies don't get banned or muted before they even get to know what the game is all about. Also, just to make sure EVERYONE knows the rules and can't complain that they 'didn't know why they were muted.'. Listed below are the RULES OF CHAT which must be followed and are strictly enforced. When you are muted for bad chat behavior, you cannot chat for up to an hour and if bad behavior persists for a second or third time, you may be banned all together from the game.

1. Don't spam. Don't fill up the chat screen with useless, repeated messages, phrases, letters or numbers.

2. Don't beg. Don't ask people for gold, items, donations, etc. If you want gold, play the game. If you need items, fight to get gold so you can buy them yourself.

3. Don't ask for votes. Votes are when you say something like: Everyone who likes pizza type 911! This is
inciting spam and is grounds for being muted. If you REALLY want to know who likes pizza, say something like: Everyone who likes pizza PM me and tell me about it!

4. Don't start lottos. Lottos are things like: Whoever gives me 50k will get 2Mil back! These are considered to be SCAMS. So don't do it.

5. Don't Auction in Shout. There's a reason for all the above mentioned words go directly to Auction (sell, sale, buy and bids); so if someone makes a mistake and forgets to put their message in Auction, it'll automatically go there for them. That's what the Auction channel is there for. Use it!

6. Don't badmouth RWK. If you don't like the game, then don't play.

7. Don't advertise other games.

8. Do not thank people who give you trivial amounts of gold. This includes people who give you "yellow
spam". Thank them in private messages all you want, but do not pollute chat with it. To define trivial: it's anywhere from 1 gold to... let's say.... 100 Mil.

## Class Specific Resists

The Master Bowman class now receives an additional 10% resistance to voidance.

The Grandmaster Bowman class now recieves an additional 20% resistance to voidance.

The Armsman class now recieves an additional 10% resistance to voidance.

The Master of Arms class now recieves an additional 20% resistance to voidance.

The Berserker class now receives an additional 5% resistance to voidance.

The Master Berserker class now receives an additional 10% resistance to voidance.

The Magician class now recieves an additional 5% resistance to voidance.

The Master Magician class now recieves an additional 10% resistance to voidance.

The Wizard class now receives an additional 3% resistance to voidance.

The Master Wizard class now receives an additional 5% resistance to voidance.

The Blood Tyrant class now receives an additional 3% resistance to voidance.

The Master of Fists class now receives an additional 5% resistance to mesmerization.

The Armsman class now recieves an additional 10% resistance to mesmerization.

The Master of Arms class now recieves an additional 20% resistance to mesmerization.

The Berserker class now receives an additional 5% resistance to mesmerization.

The Master Berserker class now receives an additional 10% resistance to mesmerization.

The Magician class now recieves an additional 5% resistance to mesmerization.

The Master Magician class now recieves an additional 10% resistance to mesmerization.

The Wizard class now receives an additional 3% resistance to mesmerization.

The Master Wizard class now receives an additional 5% resistance to mesmerization.

Back to home

pages.title: Quests on the $20 Trial
pages.text:
Quests on the $20 Trial 






## Quests on the $20 Trial



Revised 27 July 2023


Players who get the $20 access trial for 10 days can complete a number of quests. If you also have Aqualungs you can do several more, and if you are a permanent Templar you can even summon beasts. Some of these quests require killing specific creatures that may be too tough for you to handle but try it and see what happens. It is possible to complete some additional quests that I�m not showing here but which you can find on my main quests page , but exactly what you can kill will vary with your level and how twinked your character may be. Here I�m aiming to help the average player who doesn�t have anything to fight with other than items he�s found/crafted/bought in the game and using the trial as his or her only upgrade. I�m not listing the quests you can do with only surface access here since you don�t need the temporary access to work on those. Surface only quests are listed here .


Aside from quests, you can also use the trial period to level faster by parking in Hell. As a Templar (and if you are still below the soft cap 500 levels under the max) you can train a stat every fight in Hell, which is 6x faster than you will level on Surface. If you want more drops (and hence more ash) you can get into some special locations with Trial, like Heaven Keep (66,Hev,66) or the Insane Dungeon (100,Dun,250), although damage in the ID is much, much lower than in other kingdoms (it�s 25 million times lower then normal there for Vamps). If you also have a Scroll of Forbidden Rights you can get into the Forbidden Palace (162,Hev,159) which has a drop rate comparable to the Keeps (although the Palace enemies have devoids) or the Sky Shrine (100,Sky,250), which would give you a high drop rate plus a 10% boost to your chances to skill (like an SS relic without the delay). Permanent Templars who buy trial access can get a Templar�s Wand which allows them to enter Hell Keep (66,Hel,66) for high drop rates and 3x Sur experience at the same time, meaning training every fight up to level 1,000, which will be a hard stop unless you get a Sanction of Surpassment.


I have reordered the quests to match the order in some need to be completed. I also suggest completing all the Surface prerequisites for some (like getting a Temple Stone) before you buy trial access so you don't have to spend time on Sur getting items while the clock is ticking away on your access.


Quests you can do with only the trial features (no Lungs or permanent Templar) are:


Aqua Lungs (access to surface water kingdoms) This is probably unlikely for a trial character, but it is possible in theory if you have the equipment to kill a beetle so I added it here.




Ocean Bubble 
| 
May drop off Polymorphic Beetles Purchased directly from Glitchless, or obtained from the Ash Keeper . | 
Used to make Aqua Lungs (see line below). This item can drop off Polymorphic Beetles (confirmed by Metalg316 on S2) but the drop isn�t a sure thing (Swp Devil on S2), or can be Purchased from Glitchless for $20, or obtained for 20,000 ash from the Ash Keeper (150,Sur,150). Once you have the Bubble take it to any port and it will transform into Aqua Lungs (see line below). You can see the locations of all ports by using the Actions menu, Set Sail or check the map here . Just walk and/or teleport to the one closest to your location. | 



Aqua Lungs
(Lungs) | 
Take an Ocean Bubble (see line above) to any port | 
Lungs allow you to walk on surface water, so once you have them all Sur locations except the Mines (162,Sur,159) and Brawl Arena (40,Sur,140) become accessible to you. | 





Start with the Surface quests you can do before buying the Trial access.



Carbon Encasement | 
Drops off any creature in the Temple (40,Sur,225), just keep killing something until it drops. | 
Used to make a Wand of Transportation (see line below). Once the Encasement drops take it to any Dungeon lava (many possible locations, 007,Dun,225 definitely works) and it transforms into Wand of Transportation. Once it drops you can move down in to Dungeon and just move West until you hit lava. You may need to detour around some Mountains kingdoms but it�s not a big deal. | 



Demon Horn | 
Random drop off any creature in any location | 
Used to make Lava Boots (see line below). Just enter Dun with the Horn and it will transform into Lava boots. | 



Angel Hair | 
Random drop off any creature in any location | 
The Hair is used to make White Wings (see line below).You can convert the Hair into White Wings when you enter Sky. | 



Temple Stone | 
Random drop found anywhere off any critter | 
Used to make an Experience Egg (see line below). If you dont already have a Stone it will drop when you reach level 200. Take the Stone to the Dungeon Spiral (150,Dun,230) and it transforms into an Experience Egg.


Note : You will need a Lava Splicer or Transcendental Sanction to enter Dun. Without Dun access you cant use the Stone but it also cannot be sold, traded or destroyed.
| 





After you buy the Trial access you can do the parts on other planes.



Lava Boots | 
Take a Demon Horn (see line above) into the Dungeon plane | 
The Boots Allow you to walk on Dun lava, and are also used to make Demonic Pinions (see line below). You can convert the Boots into Pinions by going to the Heaven Steps at 220,Sky,66. | 



Demonic Pinions | 
Take Lava Boots (see line above) to 220,Sky,66 | 
Pinions allow you to walk on Dungeon Lava and the Dungeon Mountains. You will need them to enter the Insane Dungeon (courtesy of Balbusta on S1). Once you have Lava Boots and take them to the Heaven Steps they will transform into Pinions.

You will need access to the Mountains to
get the Haste of Hell (see below) | 



Wand of Transportation | 
Take a Carbon Encasement (see line above) to any Dungeon lava, (007,Dun,225 definitely works). | 
Allows you to teleport any distance, regardless of Intelligence. Once you have the Wand, just teleport normally. There will be no restrictions on distance. It is a very handy item to have. | 



White Wings | 
Take Angel Hair (see line above) into the Sky plane and it becomes WHite Wings. | 
Allows you to walk in between clouds in the sky. Once you transform the Hair into Wings, you are done. | 



Satan's Calling | 
Drops off any creatures in the Dungeon Mountains | 
Used to make the Haste of Hell (see line below). Kill any creatures in the Dungeon Mountains (try 207,Dun,188 or 068,Dun,225 (confirmed), or the ID at 100,Dun,200) until the Calling drops. Then take it to Lava falls (0,Dun,150) and it transforms into a Haste of Hell (locations courtesy of Lord Longshank) | 



Haste of Hell | 
Take a Satan�s Calling (see line above) to 0,Dun,150 | 
Shaves 1 second off of the delay time for attacking, casting, defending, etc. Requires a Satan�s Calling, See line above. | 



Experience Egg | 
Take a Temple Stone (see line above) to 150,Dun,239 | 
Gives you a 20% experience boost. Also used to make an Experience
Gravitator (see line below).

Once you have the Experience Egg, take it to the Smallest Sky Cloud (140,Sky,30) and it transforms into an Experience Gravitator. | 



Experience Gravitator | 
Take an Experience Egg (see line above) to 140,Sky,30 | 
Gives you 20% experience boost and doubles drop percentage. Requires an Experience Egg, See line above | 





Skill Binder



Skill Binder | 
Kill any of the hardest 4 creatures in Heaven (Feline Floater and harder) until the Binder drops | 
Increases skill level chance by 5% | 




Totem of Second Chances




Sailor�s Crimp 


(requires Mountain Tamer or Transcendental Sanction) | 
Specific Sky cloud (130,Sky,140) | 
Take the Crimp to any port on Surface, it Transforms into a Totem of Second Chances


Note : You will need a Mountain Tamer or Transcendental Sanction to enter Sky


Despite what is on /help , Forsaken Dream (S2) mentioned finding one at 85,Sky,84 and Xxzeroxx (S2) at 66,Sky,66 so several locations seem to work. Perhaps anywhere in Sky will do the trick.
| 



Totem of Second Chances
(Totem)
| 
Requires a Sailors Crimp, See line above | 
Makes it impossible to be fatally wounded by relics when attacking | 






Backpack Prerequisite : Transcendental Sanction




Satans Hide | 
Drops off the Satans in Hell. I dont really think these are rare in spite of what it has on /help | 
Once you get the Hide go to any kingdom in Heaven and give 2 billion gold to the Heaven Hermit (use /add Heaven Hermit to add him to your trade list). He takes the hide and gives you a backpack.


After the Heaven Hermit receives the gold and the Hide, he will use his power to bind it into a magical backpack. The price may seem high, but the backpack is a necessary part of the Sanction of Surpassment quest.


Note : You will need a Transcendental Sanction or Omega Sanction to enter Hell
| 



Backpack 
| 
Requires a Satans Hide, See line above | 
Allows you to carry 75 items instead of 50. | 






Detangler




Detangler | 
Kill an Almighty Avatar in Heaven Keep
(66, Hev, 66) | 
Halves your chance of being affected by your enemys confusion relics.


Note : You will need a Transcendental Sanction or Omega Sanction to enter Heaven
| 






Coif of Refulgence



Coif of Confidence | 
Drops anywhere in Dun off Fissile Fearman Guard and harder | 
Doubles dexterity for Armsman and Master of Arms classes (you only get the benefit of this item when equipping one weapon).


Transforms into Coif of the Calling (see line below). Kill any of the hardest 9 creatures in the Dungeon (Fissile Fearman Guard and harder) until the Coif drops. Once you get the Coif of Confidence move up to killing Drifting Bottomscalers.


The Fissile Fearman Guard has 110 mil for every stat, so you need enough Dex to hit against it�s 110 mil Agi. 
| 



Coif of the Calling | 
Drops anywhere in Dun off Drifting Bottomscalers once you have a Coif of Confidence (see line above) | 
Triples Dexterity for Armsman and Master of Arms classes (you only get the benefit of this item when equipping one weapon).


Transforms into the Coif of Refulgence (see line below). Requires a Coif of Confidence (see line above). Keep killing Drifting Bottomscalers in Dun until the Coif of Calling drops, then move up to killing Owners of Infinite Darkness.


Drifting Bottomscalers have 440 mil for every stat. | 



Coif of Refulgence | 
Drops anywhere in Dun off Owners of Infinite Darkness once you have a Coif of the Calling (see line above) | 
Quadruples Dexterity for Armsman and Master of Arms classes (you only get the benefit of this item when equipping one weapon).


Requires a Coif of Calling(see line above). Keep killing Owners of Infinite Darkness in Dun until the last Coif drops.


Owners of Infinite Darkness have 1 billion for every stat. | 







Warrior�s Companion | 
Drops in the InSaNe Dungeon (100, Dun, 200) off the Damned Dragon and harder creatures. | 
25x boost to Str, 2.25x boost to Dex and 10% resistance to Voidance and Mesmerization.


Only Master Berserkers and Masters of Arms are able to get this drop and its effects. This means that you need to show as one of those classes in /st when you make your kills. Go to the InSaNe Dungeon (100, Dun, 200) and kill any of the hardest 39 creatures(Damned Dragon and harder) until the Companion drops.


You will need Demonic Pinions to reach the ID. | 







Warrior�s Warder | 
Drops in the InSaNe Dungeon (100, Dun, 200) off the Gaia Harpy and harder creatures. | 
50x boost to Str, 3x boost to Dex and 10% resistance to Voidance and Mesmerization.


Only Master Berserkers and Masters of Arms are able to get this drop and its effects. This means that you need to show as one of those classes in /st when you make your kills. Go to the InSaNe Dungeon (100, Dun, 200) and kill any of the hardest 10 creatures(Gaia Harpy and harder) until the Warder drops.


I�m not sure whether you need to first get a Warrior�s Companion, but having one should help your damage against the Harpies.


You will need Demonic Pinions to reach the ID. | 





Cloak of Debilitation



Cloak of Concentration | 
Drops off the hardest 9 creatures in Sky (Effaceton Angel and harder) | 
Doubles concentration for Magician and Master Magician classes. Transforms into Cloak of Conceit (see line below). Kill any of the hardest 9 creatures in Sky (Effaceton Angel and harder) until the Cloak drops, then start killing Cloud Scrappers. | 



Cloak of Conceit | 
Drops off Cloud Scrappers in Sky once you have a Cloak of Concentration (see line above) | 
Triples Cnc for Magician and Master Magician classes.


Transforms into Cloak of Debilitation (see line below). You need to get a Cloak of Concentration first (see line above), then start killing Cloud Scrappers to transform it into a Cloak of Conceit.


Once that happens, begin killing Templars of Light. | 



Cloak of Debilitation | 
Drops off Templars of Light in Sky once you have a Cloak of Conceit | 
Quadruples Cnc for Magician and Master Magician classes.

You need to get a Cloak of Conceit first (see line above) then start killing Templars of Light until it transforms into a Cloak of Debilitation. | 





Caster�s Minion



Caster�s Minion | 
Drops off Gaia Harpy or harder creatures in the InSaNe Dungeon (100,Dun,200) | 
25x boost to Ntl, 2x boost to Cnc.


Only Master Magicians and Master Wizards are able to get this drop and its effects. This means you need to show as a Master when you check your stats with /st while you make your kills. Kill Gaia Harpy or harder creatures in the InSaNe Dungeon (100,Dun,200) until the Minion drops.


You will need Demonic Pinions to reach the ID. | 






If you also have Aqualungs you can add


Wand of Swift Winds Note : You will need Aqua Lungs to enter any water kingdoms)




Fish Scales | 
Drops in any water kingdom, off any creature | 
Used to make a Wand of Swift Winds (see line below). Kill any creature in any water kingdom until the Scales drop, then take them to any port. They will transform into a Wand of Swift Winds. Use the Actions menu, Set Sail to see all the port locations or check the map here .


Note: you need Aqualungs to enter any water kingdoms so you can get the Scales
| 



Wand of Swift Winds | 
Take Fish Scales (see line above) to any port | 
Eliminates teleport delay, requires Fish Scales (See line above). | 






Fists of Flames




Enduring Fists | 
Drops in Stallions Pub (150,Sur,145) | 
Adds 10 points to your Monk Discipline skill and is used to get Fists of Scale (see line below) Kill creatures in Stallion�s Pub (150, Sur, 145) until you get the Fists (I dont think the creature matters). To convert them to Fists of Scale go to Le Puddle Deep (140, Sur, 190).


Note : You will need Aqua Lungs or an Omega Sanction to enter Le Puddle Deep
| 



Fists of Scale | 
Drops in Le Puddle Deep once you have Enduring Fists (see line above) | 
Adds 20 points to your Monk Discipline skill and requires Enduring Fists (See line above).
Take the Fists of Scale to Hell and they will transform into Fists of Flames.


Note : You will need a Transcendental Sanction or Omega Sanction to enter Hell
| 



Fists of Flames | 
Take Fists of Scale (see line above) into Hell | 
Adds 30 points to your Monk Discipline skill and requires Fists of Scale (See line above). The 30 point MD skill boost upgrades your fists to a weapon 30 items higher than they would be without them.


Note: two players have told me they went a week without getting this drop until they moved up to harder critters, then it dropped immediately. One got it on a Vampire Vereer (10 mil) and the other when he moved from the Vereers to a Death Solicitor. The /help site doesnt specify a particular critter so that usually means anything can drop, but if you have trouble see if moving up to these creatures helps.
| 






Theurgal Damnation Staff (requires all planes access)




Theurgal Rune Staff | 
Drops off anything inStallions Pub (150,Sur,145) | 
Raises a Theurgists minimum random casting attack to that of the spell Elemental Invocation.

Transforms into Theurgal Focus Staff (see line below). Kill anything in Stallions Pub (150, Sur, 145) until the Staff drops, then take it to Le Puddle Deep (140,Sur,190) and begin killing there.
Note : You will need Aqua Lungs or an Omega Sanction to enter Le Puddle Deep | 



Theurgal Focus Staff | 
Drops off anything in Le Puddle Deep (140,Sur,190) once you have a Theurgal Rune Staff (see line above) | 
Raises a Theurgists minimum random casting attack to that of the spell Fire Burst.

Transforms into Theurgal Damnation Staff (see line below). Requires a Theurgal Rune Staff (see line above). Kill critters in Le Puddle Deep (140,Sur,190) until the Rune Staff transforms into a Focus Staff , then drop down into Hell.
Note : You will need a Transcendental Sanction or Omega Sanction to enter Hell | 



Theurgal Damnation Staff | 
Drops off anything in Hell once you have a Theurgal Focus Staff (see line above) | 
Raises a Theurgists minimum random casting attack to that of the spell Meteor Swarm . Requires a Theurgal Focus Staff (see line above). Once you get a Focus Staff enter Hell and kill anything until it transforms into the Damnation Staff. | 






If you are a permanent Templar you can add:





Templar's Wand Prerequisite : Upgrade to Templar Race and Transcendental Sanction




Crystal of Lars | 
Drops off the two hardest creatures on any plane & Goblin Theif | 
Used to make a Templars Wand (see line below). Kill either of the hardest 2 creatures on the New Fight list (any plane seems to be acceptable) until the Crystal drops. Go to a kingdom on the Hell Shoreline (many possible locations, try 145,Hel,153 or the locations within a few kingdoms of there, one will work). The Crystal transforms into a Templars Wand if your character has received the Templar upgrade. If you are not a permanent Templar, you cannot get the Wand.


Note: You will need a Transcendental Sanction or Omega Sanction to enter Hell, without those the Crystal is useless. You cannot sell, trade or destroy the Crystal.
| 



Templar's Wand

(Must be a permanent Templar to complete this quest) | 
Take a Crystal of Lars (see line above) to the Hell shoreline | 
Allows you to walk on Hell magma and air in Heaven , requires a Crystal of Lars (See line above).


You need the Wand to summon beasts using the Ash Collector .
|

pages.title: Kingdom Basics
pages.text:
## Kingdom Basics

Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright.

Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden.

Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.

Last updated 24 September 2024

NOTE: This guide is not comprehensive or for advanced kingdom management, but I keep getting requests for it so I am presenting the basics to help new players start their reigns as Kings.

All the opinions here are my own and are offered "as is" with no guarantees that it�s all perfectly correct or the best course available. I try to avoid mistakes and offer what I think is good advice, but I�m human and fallible. Kingdoms are a very contentious topic in the game, with some people holding strong and inflexible viewpoints. If you disagree with me that�s fine, but I don�t intend to get into a debate on this in the game. If some of this is inaccurate (wrong numbers for something for example) I�ll correct any mistakes when I learn of them, as I do with the rest of my site.

One aspect of RWK is the ownership of kingdoms (usually abbreviated simply as KD) and the things you can do with them. At this point in the game�s development kingdoms generate most of the gold in the game�s economy. Owning kingdoms can produce in-game wealth but only if you can make them strong enough to last and be productive. Weak kingdoms usually don�t last long, and making them strong can get expensive. You can own as many as you like, but the more you have the more time you need to spend building and maintaining them and once you own more than 1,000 kingdoms they become start producing less gold each as you increase the number you own. See the 5/31/2007 announcement .

## Quick Facts About Kingdoms

- Once you own a kingdom, don�t mention it in shout after you reach level 500 if you are not a templar . Your location will show when you use chat, and if some players know you have a kingdom, or that there may be gold or runes in one, they will possibly overthrow you as king. Letting everyone know you own a weak kingdom is like walking down the street with a sign saying �carrying money, rob me� and the results of both behaviors will be similar. When everyone can see your location in chat, do NOT mention your gold, runes, interest or ask questions that can indicate you have gold or runes available to be stolen.

- The King owns the kingdom and everything in it. Upper left corner of your screen shows the King�s name.

- You must be level 10 or higher to settle kingdoms. However, another player who is king can give his or her kingdom to anyone, even a level 0 character, provided the recipient has his trivial filter set to receive it. Use the Actions menu, Options, Trivial Gold/Kingdom Filtering menus and set to "No Filtering".

- Kings can be overthrown by couping the kingdom (kingdom menu, incite coup) or successfully waging war on it from a neighboring kingdom (kingdom menu, wage war). Morale must be below 5% before you can attempt a coup, and the higher it is the more likely you are to fail.

- The king gets a message when you incite a coup or wage war, so if you do these hostile things expect to make an enemy of another player in the game.

- When you are overthrown, anything left in the kingdom belongs to the new king. You lose everything you spent developing it when YOU were king.

- Except in the Forge plane, every unit of food, armies and fortifications costs 1 gold to buy, morale costs 1 million gold to increase (pay serfs or bribe serfs if you are the king) 1%, 10 million gold to reduce 1% (bribe serfs when you are not the king of that location). Bringing all armies and fortifications to their max value of 2 billion would cost 32 billion gold if you simply buy it.

- The maximum for Trs (treasury), each army type and each fortification type is 2 billion (2,000,000,000) except in the Forge plane where all kingdom properties are 1,000X their displayed values.

- You can bribe serfs in enemy kingdoms at 10 million gold per 1% morale drop but can only reduce it to 50% this way. To lower morale below 50% you need to kill the guard.

- Killing the guard lowers kingdom morale, 4% per guard death if there are no runes, less if the kingdom has some runes. With around 4 runes the morale will drop less from killing the guard than it will increase in the next hour, so you can never gain a lasting morale drop. Killing all guards has the same effect on morale, but higher level guards are harder to kill so offer more protection. All guards can be killed by many players, so the protection of harder ones is mostly against low-level players.

- When the population (pop) is below 10k (10,000) the serfs will consume food every hour there is player activity. If they run out of food they will begin to starve, and morale will begin to drop. Buy enough food to always have some there until pop reaches 10k (2 million food will do it).

- When the total value of all improvements in a kingdom is below 10 billion it will decay, and lose morale, unless the king visits it at least one time every 24 hours.

- Gold in the treasury (Trs, the kingdom�s bank) gains interest every hour and so will grow over time. The amount of interest depends on the king�s Kingmanship Skill, and the values can be found here . Interest will stop collecting after a certain amount of time with no player activity at that location, 24 hours by default or 48 hours for kings with a certain relic combo . Any player entering the kingdom, not just the king, will suffice.

- Runes make a kingdom more resistant to attack, they are also used for crafting items costing more than 1 bil gold to make as well as for all enchanting.

- Aside from Trs, the other kingdom properties (armies, fortifications, population, morale, food) will increase each hour there is some player activity in the kingdom (any player will trigger it, not just the king).

- Tax in a kingdom is the percentage of gold from each kill put into the Trs (and thus not given to the killer). If you are the only player in the kingdom, and you are also its king, you are taxing yourself. If any other player is there, you get a piece of his kills too but with 90,000 kingdoms on each plane (450,000 total in the game) having more than one player in any location at the same time becomes unlikely. � Keep in mind that some players will be offended by a kingdom with a tax and may attack and overthrow it as a result.

- You remove gold from the Trs of your own kingdoms using the menu Kingdom, Embezzle. You can�t remove gold from kingdoms of other players, but you can help some kings move it to another location using a Leather of Delegation .

- You can attack neighboring kingdoms using your own kingdom�s armies using the menu Kingdom, Wage War. Attacking another kingdom can reduce your armies as well as the target kingdom�s (they enemy does fight back) and depending on many variables you may lose all your armies without doing much damage to your target. � The king of the kingdom you attack will get messages that you attacked him.

## Getting Kingdoms

For a start, you can�t settle kingdoms until you reach level 10. You can however be given them below that level. Once you are level 10 you can settle kingdoms as long as they don�t already have kings. Some kingdoms are not owned by any players, those will show NOBODY (all caps) as the King. You can simply settle them using the Kingdom menu (below Actions), and settle.

If the kingdom already has a king, you will need to overthrow him before you can settle that location yourself. Assuming you don�t have any kingdoms yet to use for waging war, usurping the king means using the Kingdom menu, Incite Coup option. The KD morale needs to be below 5% to attempt a coup, and my own experience has been that if it isn�t below 3% you are more likely to fail than to succeed. When the king has been uncooperative and not left his morale below 5% you can lower it by killing the Guard (Actions menu, Battle, Guard). The list of available guards is the same as the New Fight list, but you will find the guard version of a critter is a lot harder to kill than the New Fight version.

Guards have relics for one thing, so they can annul YOUR relics and will do relic damage to you. They also take less damage than normal critters, 25x less due to their defensive skill. If you manage to kill the guard the morale will drop by 4% (assuming the KD has no runes, which will decrease the amount killing lowers morale). If the morale is still too high, you will need to wait for the new game hour (type /t to see game time) when a new guard will appear and you can kill it again. You just keep up this killing cycle until you can attempt a coup, or you get tired and give up, or the owner shows up and starts paying the serfs to increase morale faster than you can lower it. If the morale is above 50% you can use the Kingdom menu, Bribe Serfs to lower it down as far as 50% at a cost of 10 mil gold per 1% morale drop.

For example, if the morale is 90% you can bribe the serfs with 400 million gold to lower morale to 50%, then kill the guard every hour for around 12 hours to bring the morale below 5%, then attempt coups until you succeed in overthrowing the king and NOBODY shows as the king, then settle the location yourself. If the kingdom has enough runes (I don�t know the magic number needed but it�s not many) the morale will drop less with each guard killing than it increases each hour from your being in the KD, so you can never get a net drop in morale to attempt a coup. Also, if you do coup a KD the runes in it will remain intact, but any gold in the Trs will disappear as will all its armies.

## Keeping Kingdoms

I mentioned that weak kingdoms don�t usually survive long, so if you want to keep yours it needs to have its defenses built up. I personally don�t add any gold to a kingdom Trs until the armies and forts are all maxed. Your armies (soldiers, archers, catapults and trebuchets) defend your kingdom when it�s attacked and the fortifications (walls, moats and cannons) protect the armies from damage. You also need to prevent your KD morale from dropping to 0 or you will lose it and the king will revert to NOBODY. The morale part is easy and cheap to cover. Use the Kingdom menu to buy 2 million food (it will cost you 2 million gold) and also to pay or bribe the serfs at least 50 million gold to bring morale to 50%. The food is enough to prevent your serfs from starving (which lowers morale) and 50% morale will make it tedious for someone to coup you after killing the guard. You can also buy more food (up to 2 billion) or spend 100 mil to take morale up to it�s max of 99.99% if you like, but 2 mil and 50% will be enough to cover your basic needs.

Next you should buy army units, the more you buy the stronger the kingdom will become. The max for each type is 2 billion, so if you have 8 billion to spend you can maximize all the armies at once. When you are attacked from a neighboring kingdom the relative numbers of attacker army units to your defending army units will determine how much damage you suffer. If you have more food than your enemy you get some extra defense, if he has more he does extra damage. Once you have taken care of your armies, you want to build defenses. Each side of your kingdom has walls, moats and cannons and each can have up to 2 billion units of each type (costing 1 gold for each unit). That makes 6 billion gold to maximize each side and 24 billion gold to maximize all forts on all sides. If you are keeping track you have noticed that getting your armies and forts maxed costs 32 billion gold, or 34 billion if you want to also max your food. You can add in another 1 bil for the best guard for a total of 35 bil to have everything maxed but no runes in place (the 100 mil for full morale is pretty trivial in comparison).

With your armies and forts all maximized your kingdom is still vulnerable to being overthrown, it will always be possible regardless of what you do to it, but you have at least made it something that won�t be too cheap or too quick to destroy. You can increase the defense by buying runes, at 1 billion gold each, up to a maximum of 999 runes. More runes mean more protection, but people seem to settle on 20 to 50 runes in a maxed kingdom to make it reasonably safe. Unfortunately, as of 5/29/2007 runes reduce the amount of gold you get from interest when you put more than 5 into a kingdom, so now we have to choose between profitability and security.

## Using Kingdoms

Sometime after you finish your armies and forts and have added some runes it will become safe enough to put gold in the treasury (Trs) and start earning back something from your kingdom. The gold in the Trs earns interest every hour as long as you have some player activity every 24 hours (more if you own the DRC). The amount of interest will vary with your Kingmanship skill, but it starts at 0.449% per hour. The interest compounds, so the gold you earn in the first hour will then earn interest in the second, and that in the third, etc. The Trs can hold a maximum of 2 billion gold. Once you get that much there is no room for additional gold so interest will be lost if you don�t regularly withdraw gold to make room for more (use the Kingdom menu, embezzle, to remove gold). Without any Kingmanship skill you are limited to a total of 5 deposits per hour, but when kingmanship skill reaches 50 (either raw skill or modified with relics) that limit is eliminated and it's possible to make as many deposits as you like (Thanks to Yoshister on S1 for sharing that information).

You can store the gold you earn as interest in several ways. You can craft items and store them on mules, then sell them to the �store� as needed to recover the gold you spent making them. You can buy runes in your kingdoms for 1 bil gold each, then convert them back into 999,999,999 gold (basically 1 bil) using the /na command. You can also convert runes into treasure elements using a little gold and some ash. An EEoT is made from 50 runes, 50 ash and costs 1 million gold if you use the Rune Keeper (a game NPC) while great treasures (EEoGT) can be made with the Rune Keeper using 2.5 million gold, 250 ash and 250 runes. If you want the maximum conversion of runes to treasure you can use 999 runes, 999 gold and 999 ash to have the Rune Keeper make an EEoST. You convert treasures back into runes by simply trading them to the Rune Keeper (that is free, doesn�t cost you any gold or ash).

You can also use your kingdoms to wage war on your neighbors using the Kingdom menu, Wage War option. You pick the direction in which to attack and can use each army type (soldiers, archers, catapults and trebuchets) separately or attack with all at the same time. Softening up your enemy with cats and trbs before attacking with archers and soldiers is a better technique than waging war with all at the same time. Weakening enemy fortifications first makes your archers and soldiers more effective. Soldiers, archers and cats can only attack kingdoms directly adjacent to yours, while trbs can attack 2 kingdoms away and can attack diagonally. You can attack 2 or 3 kingdoms deep at one time if you collect some quest items that increase your war waging ability.

Players initially can only use each army type once per hour to wage war. With kingmanship skill at 75 you gain a second attack per hour and at a skill of 99 get three attacks per hour. Other items like the quest Scepters and some D*D relics along with kingmanship skills above 100 can bring your total attacks up to something like 9 per hour as well as increasing the damage you do per attack. These all add up to meaning that players serious about waging war and willing to spend a lot on the game can reduce very strong kingdoms in a short period of time. Damage can also be increased for up to 1 hour by dropping a Tecton , or reduced by a defending king dropping a Stone . Both of these items nominally cost 999 runes to obtain so waging war with them can get expensive. If you don�t invest in all the war waging toys, keep in mind that regardless of how much you spend on defenses you will only hold kingdoms at the pleasure of those who have them. Even the strongest kingdoms can be destroyed in a surprising short time by some well-equipped players, especially if more than one is involved in the destruction.

## Waging War

The �War� part of the game�s name comes from the ability to use your kingdoms to wage war on your neighbors. Your armies, soldiers, archers, catapults (cat) and trebuchet (trb), can all attack the kingdoms adjacent to yours. Trbs can also attack 2 kingdoms away and diagonally, while the other army types are limited to only attacking in the cardinal directions (north, south, east or west). Only army units can attack, but both army units and fortifications (walls, moats and cannons) defend a kingdom when it�s attacked. Food also is a factor in waging war. If the attacker has more food than the defending kingdom the attack does 20% more damage, while if the defender has more food it gets a 20% defense boost. If both have the same amount of food it�s presumably a wash, with neither side getting any war damage changes.

Stronger fortifications will reduce the amount of damage done to the kingdom's armies, and during an attack both attacker and defender can take damage. A kingdom is overthrown when its armies all drop to 0, but some fortifications can remain and then become the property of the new king. Forts are built and take damage separately on all 4 sides of a kingdom, so the fortifications not facing the attacker can survive an attack with little or no damage, depending how the attacks were made and the random luck of the attacker and defender. Waging war can give highly variable results so the outcome of an attack between closely matches kingdoms and players is not predictable.

With some quest items you can attack more than 1 kingdom deep but for new players using what they find in the game it�s limited to attacking 1 kingdom at a time. You start with only being able to attack with each army type once per hour per kingdom, but that number increases with kingmanship skill and certain relics and quest items . The cheapest increases are to 2 attacks per hour per army at 75 kingmanship and then 3 attacks per hour at 99 kingmanship. With all the war-waging boosts it�s possible to make 8 attacks per hour per kingdom per army type. Additional help to attackers comes from the ability to enrage their armies using the /nrage command, at a cost of 10 runes. Use of an Essence Element of Tecton (commonly called a tec) causes massive damage to the kingdom onto which it is �dropped� and gives the player using it increased damage from his attacks for the remainder of the game hour (type /t to see the game time, it coincides with Eastern US time). When taken together, all the attacking boosts make it possible for even the strongest kingdom to fall in a surprisingly short time.

Defense also has some options in RWK. More armies and fortifications make your kingdom harder to overthrow. Getting the girlfriend Transcend doubles the effectiveness of runes in defense as does finding a Captain�s Staff of Valor quest item. Defenders also can use an Essence Element of Stone , which is the defender�s version of a Tecton.

When a kingdom is overthrown by waging war 80% of any runes in it are lost if the defender has 1500 or fewer total kingdoms(thanks to Chaos Heretic for pointing out that was reduced from 90% in 2016), so in that case if a kingdom has fewer than 10 runes the attacker will get none of them. If the defender has more than 1500 kingdoms no runes are lost, so you can "profit nuke" (thank you Cagedangel for pointing that out). The kingdom Trs survives intact and the victorious attacker gets all the gold it contains. So the effect on runes and trs is the opposite for couping and waging war to overthrow a kingdom. A coup preserves all the runes but destroys the armies and trs, while waging war will destroy all the armies and 90% of the runes unless the defender has more than 1500 total kingdoms, but preserve the trs for the new king.

Return to home page.

pages.title: RWK Quests
pages.text:
## RWK Quests

This page last updated 30 My 2026

Most of this information is taken right off the official help site (type /help in the game), I have reformatted the material to make it easier (for me at least) to read and added my own notes to some items for clarification.

You can use the find feature of your browser to search for specific items on this page if you dont know what category they're in or if you just can't seem to find them by eye.

Quests that seem to belong in more than one category are repeated in each one. For example, the APoS is a combinational quest that also offers Archers some specific help so it's in both sections. The same is true for the Attacker's Balance and Theurgs. A separate table showing only surface quests is available here.

RWK has a number of quests that players can complete. They give benefits like access to more locations, more killing power, higher crafting abilities, faster leveling, etc. Most of the quests will require upgrades to complete, but some can be performed without access. Since you can now even quest for access using ash, players who spend enough time in the game could complete all the quests without paying any real-world money.

**NOTE**: Quest items show in your inventory under "Others". They can NOT be traded to other players, sold or destroyed. Once you get one you're stuck with it unless you can complete some quest that rolls several items together into one. Don't offer to sell your quest drops in auction or you end looking a bit foolish :)

The quests below are arranged to show any dependencies, so multiple step quests show the sequence in which each step must be performed. Prerequisites are the items you must have in your possession to complete a multipart quest. Some quests are only one step. The quests are also color-coded:

Blue quests require you to have Sky access ($50 purchase or 50,000 ash)

Red quests require you to have Dungeon access ($50 purchase or 50,000 ash)

Purple quests require both Sky and Dungeon access, or all planes access

Green quests require all planes access (you must have a Transcendental Sanction, Omega Sanction, Sanction of Surpassment or Sanction of Serenity)

Magenta quests require Aqua Lungs ($20 purchase or 20,000 ash) or an Omega Sanction to complete, they require entering Surface water kingdoms

To Purchase items from Glitchless log into your character and click on "Buy Items" . NOTE that purchasing an item requires you to spend real US dollars, not game gold.

Some quests will require multiple access items (like Lungs plus Dun access) but you'll have to do without a separate color for that or the tables will get way too confusing. If you are colorblind and can't see the differences in colors, just read carefully and you will be fine :)

If a quest requires a particular location, and the item doesn't drop automatically when you enter that kingdom, just start killing any creature and it will drop eventually. If a specific critter needs to be killed it will be specified as such in these tables.

Jump to Quest Type:

Access |
Afelikian Coin, SoMC |
Archer quests |
Citizen Quests |
Combinational quests |
Crafting quests |
Essence Quests (Ice, Blood, Etc.) |
Fighter quests |

Kingdom and War quests |
Leveling & Skills |
Masters of Class Quests |
Miscellaneous quests |
Monk quests |
Omega Sanction |
RDADMS Lessonbook |
Sanction of Serenity (SoSe) |

Sanction of Surpassment (SoS) |
Shadow Scale |
Special Locations |
Spellcaster quests |
Stats & Damage |
Theurgist quests |
Tracking quests |
Transcendental Sanction |

Treasures and Ash |
Vampire quests |
|
Convenience Items
(Cash only, not available using ash) |
|
|
|
|

Item |
Where To Get It |
Details |

ACCESS, TRANSPORTATION AND MOVEMENT QUESTS

Mountain Tamer (access to Sky plane)

Platinum Ore
|
Does not drop off any creature in the game. Either purchase or get from the Ash Keeper |
You need to either buy the ore from Glitchless for $50 or use 50,000 ash and the Ash Keeper at 150,Sur,150 to get one. Ore is used to Make a Mountain Tamer (see line below).

Once you have the Ore, take it to the Temple (40,Sur,225) and it will transform into a Mountain Tamer which gives you access to the Sky plane. The temple is on an island without any ports so you will need to cross a small distance of water to reach it.

You can get there using Lungs or teleport from the nearest point on the large landmass with at least 3,600 base Ntl stat.

Mememe249 on S2 found you can reach the Temple island with as little as 1,600 base Ntl by sailing to Hykra, walk down the narrow island to get close to the temple, then teleport from the West. |

Mountain Tamer |
Take Plat Ore to the Temple at 40,Sur,225 |
Once you have taken the steps above to get a Mountain Tamer you can enter the Sky plane, but only from the entrance at 078,Sur,242 (or a kingdom around it).

You can drop back to Sur from any Sky location, but will need to use the entrance at 78,Sur,242 to get back to Sky as long as your only access is with the Tamer. Players with a Trans or better can move between planes at any location. |

Lava Splicer (access to Dungeon plane)

Fyre Rock |
Does not drop off any creature in the game. Either purchase or get from the Ash Keeper |
You need to either buy the rock from Glitchless for $50 or use 50,000 ash and the Ash Keeper at 150,Sur,150 to get it.

Once you have the Rock, take it to the Temple (40,Sur,225) and it will transform into a Lava Splicer which gives you access to the Dungeon plane.

The temple is on an island without any ports so you will need to cross a small distance of water to reach it. You can get there using Lungs or teleport from the nearest point on the large landmass with at least 3,600 base Ntl stat. |

Lava Splicer |
Take a Fyre Rock to the Temple at 40,Sur,225 |
Once you have taken the steps above to get a Lava Splicer you can enter the Dungeon plane, but only from the entrance at 240,Sur,60 (or a kingdom around it).

You can pop back to Sur from any Dun location, but will need to use the entrance at 240,Sur,60 to get back to Dun as long as your only access is with the Splicer. Players with a Trans or better can move between planes at any location. |

Transcendental Sanction (access to all planes). Prerequisites – Mountain Tamer and Lava Splicer

Transcendental Sanction
(Trans)
|
Take both a Lava Splicer and Mountain Tamer (see above) to the Temple at 40,Sur,225 |
A Trans gives you access to all 5 planes in the game. You first need to get both a Mountain Tamer and Lava Splicer . Then go to the Temple at 40,Sur,225 and they will combine to create a Trans.

This new item now grants you access to ALL the planes: Hell, Dungeon, Surface, Sky, and Heaven. You also no longer have to access the planes from a specific location, you can move up and down from anywhere. |

Aqua Lungs (access to surface water kingdoms)

Ocean Bubble |
May drop off a Polymorphic Beetles or purchased directly from Glitchless, or obtained from the Ash Keeper . |
Used to make Aqua Lungs (see line below). This item can drop off Polymorphic Beetles (confirmed by Metalg316 on S2). It may be a high-probability drop (Toli on S1 got 10 drops in 10 kills on different characters, Mav73 got one after 5 kills) but it's not guaranteed (confirmed by Seith on S2). Can also be Purchased from Glitchless for $20, or obtained for 20,000 ash from the Ash Keeper (150,Sur,150).

Once you have the Bubble take it to any port and it will transform into Aqua Lungs (see line below). You can see the locations of all ports by using the Actions menu, Set Sail or check the map here . Just walk and/or teleport to the one closest to your location. |

Aqua Lungs
(Lungs)
|
Take an Ocean Bubble (see line above) to any port |
Lungs allow you to walk on surface water, so once you have them all Sur locations except the Mines (162,Sur,159) and Brawl Arena (40,Sur,140) become accessible to you. |

Demonic Pinions

Demon Horn |
Random drop off any creature in any location |
Used to make Lava Boots (see line below). You need a Lava Splicer or Transcendental Sanction (or better) to use it. If you have the access, just enter Dun with the Horn and it will transform into Lava boots.

Note: If you can't enter Dun, you can not use this item. You also can't sell, trade or destroy it.
|

Lava Boots |
Take a Demon Horn (see line above) into the Dungeon plane |
The Boots Allow you to walk on Dun lava, and are also used to make Demonic Pinions (see line below).If you have Sky access (you need a Mountain Tamer , Transcendental Sanction , or better) you can convert the Boots into Pinions by going to the Heaven Steps at 220,Sky,66.

Note: If you can't enter Sky, you can not transform this item into Pinions. You also can't sell, trade or destroy it.
|

Demonic Pinions |
Take Lava Boots (see line above) to 220,Sky,66 |
Pinions allow you to walk on Dungeon Lava and the Dungeon Mountains. You will need them to enter the Insane Dungeon (courtesy of Balbusta on S1). Once you have Lava Boots and take them to the Heaven Steps they will transform into Pinions. You will need access to the Mountains to get the Haste of Hell (see below). |

White Wings Note : You will need a Mountain Tamer or Transcendental Sanction to enter Sky)

Angel Hair |
Random drop off any creature in any location |
The Hair is used to make White Wings (see line below). If you have Sky access (you need a Mountain Tamer , Transcendental Sanction , or better) you can convert the Hair into White Wings when you enter Sky.

Note: If you can't enter Sky, you can not transform this item into White Wings. You also can't sell, trade or destroy it.
|

White Wings |
Take Angel Hair (see line above) into the Sky plane |
Allows you to walk in between clouds in the sky. Once you transform the Hair into Wings, you are done. |

Wand of Transportation Note : You will need a Lava Splicer or Transcendental Sanction to enter Dun)

Carbon Encasement |
Drops off any creature in the Temple (40,Sur,225), just keep killing something until it drops. |
Used to make a Wand of Transportation (see line below). Once the Encasement drops take it to any Dungeon lava (many possible locations, 007,Dun,225 definitely works) and it transforms into Wand of Transportation.

Note: If you can't enter Dun, you can not use this item. You also can't sell, trade or destroy it.
|

Wand of Transportation |
Take a Carbon Encasement (see line above) to any Dungeon lava |
Allows you to teleport any distance, regardless of Intelligence. Once you have the Wand, just teleport normally. There will be no restrictions on distance. |

Haste of Hell Note : You will need all planes access to do this since you must have Demonic Pinions to reach the Lava Falls)

Satan’s Calling |
Drops off any creatures in the Dungeon Mountains |
Used to make the Haste of Hell (see line below). Kill any creatures in the Dungeon Mountains (try 207,Dun,188 or the ID at 100,Dun,200) until the Calling drops. Then take it to Lava falls (0,Dun,150) and it transforms into a Haste of Hell (locations courtesy of Lord Longshank) |

Haste of Hell
Take a Satans Calling (see line above) to 0,Dun,150 |
Shaves 1 second off of the delay time for attacking, casting, defending, etc. Requires a Satans Calling, See line above.

Locations 0,Dun,179 (Megadeth502 on Server 1), 18,Dun,188 (Punkkidd on Server 1) and 11,Dun,150 (Nyteshademaster, Server 1) are also reported to work for transforming the Calling
|

Wand of Swift Winds Note : You will need Aqua Lungs to enter any water kingdoms)

Fish Scales |
Drops in any water kingdom, off any creature |
Used to make a Wand of Swift Winds (see line below). Kill any creature in any water kingdom until the Scales drop, then take them to any port. They will transform into a Wand of Swift Winds. Use the Actions menu, Set Sail to see all the port locations or check the map here .

Note: you need Aqualungs to enter any water kingdoms so you can get the Scales
|

Wand of Swift Winds |
Take Fish Scales (see line above) to any port |
Eliminates teleport delay, requires Fish Scales (See line above). |

Templar's Wand Prerequisite: Upgrade to Templar Race and Transcendental Sanction

Crystal of Lars |
Drops off the two hardest creatures on any plane & Goblin Thief |
Used to make a Templars Wand (see line below). Kill either of the hardest 2 creatures on the New Fight list (any plane seems to be acceptable) until the Crystal drops. Go to a kingdom on the Hell Shoreline (many possible locations, try 145,Hel,153 or the locations within a few kingdoms of there, one will work). The Crystal transforms into a Templars Wand if your character has received the Templar upgrade. If you are not a permanent Templar, you can't get the Wand.

Note: You will need a Transcendental Sanction or Omega Sanction to enter Hell, without those the Crystal is useless. You cannot sell, trade or destroy the Crystal.
|

Templar's Wand |
Take a Crystal of Lars (see line above) to the Hell shoreline |
Allows you to walk on Hell magma and air in Heaven , requires a Crystal of Lars (See line above).

You need the Wand to summon beasts using the Ash Collector .
|

Scroll of Forbidden Rights
(SoFR)
|
Random drop found anywhere (very, very rare) or a $50 purchase from Glitchless. |
Allows you to enter special locations and is required to fight Ancient Alchemar and Palace Alchemar beasts.

The Scroll allows you to enter the Forbidden Palace (162,Hev,159) the Forbidden Mines (162,Sur,159), and the Sky Shrine (100,Sky,250). It is also required to fight Ancient and Palace Alchemar beasts or to get the Scepter of Harnessed Hatred
|

LEVELING, SKILLS AND DROP-FREQUENCY RELATED QUESTS

Experience Gravitator (Grav)

Temple Stone |
Random drop found anywhere off any critter |
Used to make an Experience Egg (see line below). If you dont already have a Stone it will drop when you reach level 200. Take the Stone to the Dungeon Spiral (150,Dun,230) and it transforms into an Experience Egg.

Note : You will need a Lava Splicer or Transcendental Sanction to enter Dun. Without Dun access you can't use the Stone but it also cannot be sold, traded or destroyed.
|

Experience Egg |
Take a Temple Stone (see line above) to 150,Dun,239 |
Gives you a 20% experience boost. Also used to make an Experience Gravitator (see line below).
Once you have the Experience Egg, take it to the Smallest Sky Cloud (140,Sky,30) and it transforms into an Experience Gravitator.

Note: You will need a Mountain Tamer or Transcendental Sanction (or better) to enter Sky
|

Experience Gravitator
(Grav)
|
Take an Experience Egg (see line above) to 140,Sky,30 |
Gives you 20% experience boost and doubles drop percentage. Requires an Experience Egg, See line above |

Skill Binder

Skill Binder |
Kill any of the hardest 4 creatures in Heaven (Feline Floater and harder) until the Binder drops |
Increases skill level chance by 5%

Note : You will need a Transcendental Sanction or Omega Sanction to enter Heaven
|

Quickening Prerequisite: You will need a Scroll of Forbidden Rights to kill a Lord Alchemar for this quest. Without that item you cannot attack them.

Lord Alchemar's Skull |
Kill a Lord Alchemar
(non-surface beast, SoFR required)

|
Used to make a Quickening (see line below). Kill a Lord Alchemar (non-surface beast) and it will drop a Skull unless you already have one (or a Quickening). Then go to a Kingdom you own with 100 runes and trade the skull to the Keep Keeper (use /add Keep Keeper to get him on your trade list). You lose the runes and the Skull transforms into a Quickening.

Note : You will need access to at least one plane (off Surface) to reach a Lord Alchemar. They never appear on Surface.
|

Quickening
(Quickie) |
Obtained from the Keep Keeper for 100 runes and a Lord Alchemars Skull (see line above) |
Doubles level and skilling speed. Requires a Lord Alchemars Skull (See line above) or is purchased from Glitchless for $100. With a Quickening you will get twice as many stat points each time you train, so you will level or transfer stats twice as fast as you would without one. From the entry on the official RWK help site , I gather it also doubles your chances for a skill increase. |

Templar
(Race Upgrade) |
Either a $50 Purchase or 50,000 ash from the Ash Keeper |
Either a $50 Purchase or 50,000 ash from the Ash Keeper (150,Sur,150). Templars are an upgraded race .

From /help "Templars have the same stats as humans, but level at twice the pace. Even at level caps it is beneficial to be a Templar because it will help you rearrange your stats more quickly. Templars get a 50% skill bonus making them 50% more likely to go up in skills every fight. Templars also, when speaking in main chat, have their location blocked from the other players so they can chat safely from their secret kingdoms without anyone knowing where they are."
|

Skill Potencifier
(SP)
|
Either a $100 Purchase or 100,000 ash from the Ash Keeper |
Either a $100 Purchase or 100,000 ash from the Ash Keeper (150,Sur,150). This item doubles your chance of gaining skills and allows you to reach a skill cap of 125 instead of 99. Visit the Keeper and send him a private message (/m Ash Keeper: hello will work) if you want to use 100k ash to get one. |

MONK CLASS QUESTS

Fists of Flames

Enduring Fists |
Drops in Stallions Pub (150,Sur,145) |
Adds 10 points to your Monk Discipline skill and is used to get Fists of Scale (see line below) Kill creatures in Stallion’s Pub (150, Sur, 145) until you get the Fists (I dont think the creature matters). To convert them to Fists of Scale go to Le Puddle Deep (140, Sur, 190).

Note : You will need Aqua Lungs or an Omega Sanction to enter Le Puddle Deep
|

Fists of Scale |
Drops in Le Puddle Deep once you have Enduring Fists (see line above) |
Adds 20 points to your Monk Discipline skill and requires Enduring Fists (See line above).
Take the Fists of Scale to Hell and they will transform into Fists of Flames.

Note : You will need a Transcendental Sanction or Omega Sanction to enter Hell
|

Fists of Flames |
Take Fists of Scale (see line above) into Hell |
Adds 30 points to your Monk Discipline skill and requires Fists of Scale (See line above). The 30 point MD skill boost upgrades your fists to a weapon 30 items higher than they would be without them.

Note: two players have told me they went a week without getting this drop until they moved up to harder critters, then it dropped immediately. One got it on a Vampire Vereer (10 mil) and the other when he moved from the Vereers to a Death Solicitor. The /help site doesnt specify a particular critter so that usually means anything can drop, but if you have trouble see if moving up to these creatures helps.
|

VAMPIRE CLASS QUESTS

Vamp Horns Note : You will need Aqua Lungs or an Omega Sanction to enter Le Puddle Deep)

Vampskin |
Drops in Le Puddle Deep (140, Sur, 190) |
Increases your durability in battle by 50% and is used to make a Vamp Hide (see line below). Keep killing creatures stronger than Stone Golems until the Vampskin drops, then take it to Stallions Pub (150, Sur, 145) to upgrade them to a Vamp Hide.

Note : You will need Aqua Lungs or an Omega Sanction to enter Le Puddle Deep
|

Vamp Hide |
Drops in Stallion's Pub (150,Sur,145) once you have a Vampskin (see line above) |
Requires a Vampskin (See line above). Increases your durability in battle by 75%. Transforms into Vamp Horns (see line below). Once you are in Stallions Pub (150,Sur,145) kill creatures stronger than Twin Raiders until the Hide drops. Now go back to Le Puddle Deep (140,Sur,190) to transform them into Vamp Horns.

Note: Ive been told (Lucivar on S2) that a Grunt Workhorse (weaker than a Twin Raider) will also cause the transformation, and Blevi (S2) got his off a Twin Raider, not harder enemy. The /help information may be a bit off on what you need to kill to do this quest.
|

Vamp Horns |
Drops in Le Puddle Deep (140, Sur, 190) once you have a Vamp Hide (see line above) |
Increases your durability in battle by 100%. And requires a Vamp Hide (See line above). Once you are in Le Puddle Deep (140,Sur,190) kill Stifled Arguers or harder enemies (Blevi on S2 found SAs work fine, not necessary to kill harder as shown on /help) to transform the Hide into Horns. |

Fangs of the Bloodletter
(Fangs)
|
Buy from the Hell Hermit for 999 runes |
This item changes a Blood Tyrant into a Bloodletter. It lowers beast resistance to relic damage, allowing Bloodletters to do up to 10 times more damage to beasts than Blood Tyrants.. Any player can get these from the Hell Hermit for 999 runes, but you must first be a Blood Tyrants to change into a Bloodletter, and only Bloodletters get the extra relic damage boost against beasts. The Fangs are also requires for all classes getting the Sanction of Serenity . You just pm the Hell Hermit the message fangs (/m Hell Hermit: fangs will work) from your kingdom with 999 runes in it and he will take the runes and give you the Fangs in return. |

BERSERKER AND ARMSMAN CLASS QUESTS

(APoS)
|
Drops on Sur off Yoro La HiHi Samurais if you have the required items |
Gives archers a 75% of having 2 attacks, and doubles the speed of chanting and crafting. Archers Aide |
Drops off Golem of Living Rock (1,999,999,997 gold and 8,500 ash to summon ) |
You need to be a Master Bowmen, Grandmaster Bowmen or Archer Theurgist (when you type /st) are able to get this drop and its effects and you need to already have an APoS. Gives a 500x boost to Cnc and 10% resistance to Voidance and Mesmerization when at least one spell is equipped. Also increases your chances of attacking twice to 90% as opposed to 75% with the Archers Pouch. Drops off Golem of Living Rock (1,999,999,997 gold and 8,500 ash to summon ).

It Allows Grandmaster Bowmen to equip spells without suffering a Cnc loss, so archers can use rapid damage spells for greater speed and accurate damage spells for the 2x Cnc boost they give.

Emeralds |
May drop randomly for the king in a Forge kingdom when the location is entered by any player. The probability of a drop increases with hoarding skill and some locations in the Forge offer a boost for a specific gem. |
Upgrade to any Dragon Eater essence at a cost of 999 runes per addition. Emeralds will make any DE essence function like Dragon Shards and each Emerald will boost durability and armor defense up through Rank 9. Essence Element of Treasure
(EEoT) |
Obtain from the Rune Keeper, also Player Craftable with high skills (800 to 850 Reliccrafting, Metalworking and Runeworking) |
Converts 50 runes into a single tradable item.

Note : You will need access to at least one plane (off Surface) to reach a Goblin Shaman or someone with permanent Templar , Aqualungs , and Hell access to summon a private one for you. The random spawns do not appear on Surface but all private beasts do. Remember, a private beast might appear in a water kingdom so having Aqualungs is advised if you want to fight them.

Goblin Hide Backpack
(GHB)
|
Requires Goblin Hide, see line above.Requires a kingdom with 999 runes |
Allows you to hold up to 90 items. Also allows you to obtain the non-combat benefits from DaD, DoD, DsD, and DwD (standard and devoid upgraded) relics if they are in pure form in your inventory

The one exception is that you do not gain the DsDs skill enhancement bonus but it is included on the list so that you can have those 4 relics in pure form in your inventory to gain the 4 relic combo bonuses.

Swp Devil (Server 2) asked Glitchless about whether the rune defense reduction still applied when the DwD is in a Goblin Hide Backpack and was told it must be equipped for that benefit to apply. So it looks like you can have the rest of the DRC in your GHB but need to equip the DwD to reduce enemy rune defense when attacking.

Drops off Goblin Shaman and Rogue Goblin but not every time |

Note : You will need access to at least one plane (off Surface) to reach a Goblin Shaman or someone with permanent Templar , Aqualungs , and Hell access to summon a private one for you. (Noobane)
|
Random Drop Found Anywhere |

You usually are teleported to a kingdom neighboring the beast location, however sometimes you are teleported directly to the beast's location so you need to pay attention to the "Battle" list to know when you are in right place and can begin attacking.

Shrine Keeper D*D Conversion Quest

D*Dv Relics |
You can be anywhere.You Provide the D*D or D*Devoid relic |
For 2,000 ash the Shrine Keeper will convert one type of D*Dv relic into another. DMDv to DLDv to DPDv to DRDv to DSDv to DADv to DWDv to DODv to DMDv

You can pm the Shrine Keeper ( /m Shrine Keeper: hello ) to see his dialog about converting your D*D relics. Tyraelthefallen was good enough to point out that you can do the conversions with relics still attached to items, no need to first disenchant them.

Sky Citizen Quest

Citizen DD Quest |
Go to 069,Sky,157 and pm A Citizen there |
Trade the citizen 200 palace elements Storm, Lava, Blood) or 8 upgraded Super relics and you get a DD relic in return.

When you trade him an upgraded Super relics (MUR, CER, VAS) he will count each as 25 palace elements, or you can have him take 50,000 ash and it will count as 40 palace elements.

Trade a UT element (Ice, Fire, Lightning) and you may get a different one back OR you can get a sleeved card in return.

Heaven Citizen Quest

Citizen Upgrade Quests |
Go to 150,Hev,150 and pm A Citizen there |

Demonic becomes Earthen (6k ash), Earthen becomes Crystal (50k ash), and Crystal becomes Shadow Scale (5k ash).

Shadow Scale is cheap to chant, 10 runes, available at a chantcraft skill of 75 and trivial at 125.

Afelikian Coin |

KINGDOM RELATED QUESTS

Scepter of Harnessed Hatred
(First Scepter)
|
Requires a Scroll of Forbidden Rights |
Trade a Rusty Dagger to the Mine Keeper from your KD with 999 runes

(you lose the runes in the process). Your SoFR becomes part of the Scepter so it will not show as a separate item in your inventory anymore.

When waging war, you will simultaneously attack the next 2 kingdoms (as opposed to 1) in the chosen direction doing full damage to each and taking half damage from each.

You are granted 1 extra attack/transport turn.

Allows you to assail enemy kingdoms (see /ann for 2/13/2004)

Also gives the ability to use some kingdom commands :

/rep = Repairs damage to your armies as much as possible with your existing gold without any waste.

/fort = Repairs damage to your fortifications as much as possible with your existing gold without any waste.

/bf = Buys as much food as possible with your existing gold without any waste.

/ref = Repairs and buys as much food as possible without any waste.

Also used to get the Scepter of Unending Repulsion.
|

Scepter of Unending Repulsion
(Second Scepter)
|
Requires a Scepter of Harnessed Hatred, (see line above) and a pure DDD or D*D relic. A D*Devoid will not work. |
Trade a pure DDD or D*D relic to the Mine Keeper. You lose the relic and get the upgraded Scepter.

Has all the powers of the Scepter of Harnessed Hatred plus:

When waging war, you will simultaneously attack the next 3 kingdoms (as opposed to 2) in the chosen direction doing full damage to each and taking half damage from each.

Allows you to assail and besiege enemy kingdoms (see /ann for 2/13/2004)

You are granted 1 more extra attack/transport turn (for a total of 2 extra attacks from having the Scepters)

You gain the /alch ability which converts runes back into gold (1 rune to 1 bil gold).

When you are defending you do 50% more damage to your attacker(s).
|

(Third Scepter)
|
Requires a Scepter of Unending Repulsion (see line above) and 2 Tears of the Fallen |
You need to have 2 pure Tears Of The Fallen , trade one of them to the Mine Keeper. He converts both ToTF and your Scepter of Unending Repulsion (see above) into a Harbingers Scepter of Oppression.

Has all the powers of Scepter of Unending Repulsion plus is the equivalent of having 2 Tears of the Fallen equipped, without taking up relic slots.

You are granted an extra attack/transport turn (for a total of 3 extra attacks from having the Scepters)

Also gives you more commands , the double alchemize ability, which grants you the ability to alchemize from distant kingdoms, 2 runes at a time (/da xxx,plane,xxx) or if you also have a DaD and DsD you can alch 1 rune at a time (/sa xxx,plane,xxx)

drops off a Polymorphic Beetle |

Note : You will need access to at least one plane (off Surface) to reach a Polymorphic Beetle (PB) or someone with permanent Templar , Aqualungs , and Hell access to summon a private one for you.

Captains Staff of Valor
(CSoV)
|
Sail the ports and it will eventually drop randomly.

It may take many trips to get it. See note on right |
The Staff doubles the defensive effectiveness of Runes. This quest also gives attackers the ability to enrage the armies of their kingdoms resulting in double the war damage. It costs 10 runes to /nrage a kingdoms armies which will last 1 hour.

Also grants the /chance ability. If you have an EEoT you can type /chance and the kingdom where you are located gets 10 runes. However, there is a 20% chance of losing the EEoT in the process. The odds balance out so over time you would expect to break even, no net gain or loss.

Note: Crazy Gizmo (S2) pointed out you can enter a port and keep sailing TO that same one. It costs 0 gold and you will eventually find the staff.

I tried this with a mule and got it in 5 voyages but tried again more recently (August 2018) and the staff still did not drop after 30 "voyages".

Wizardofhatred (S1) has suggested its quicker and cheaper to just keep teleporting to the same port. Its faster than sailing, costs no gold, and he managed to get his staff that way in 4 or 5 trips. I tried it with two mules and didn't get the drop after 200 trips, so I can't verify that it works but Darklordbladeflame on S1 said he also got one by teleporting, so perhaps I was just unlucky.
|

Free Fortifier Relic
(Freefort)
|
Drops off a Goblin King BEAST on Surface |
The King has only 410 dur, but each attack can only do 1 damage per attack type, i.e. if you cast twice and also do some relic damage your total damage in that attack is only 3 points.

Allows a player to use the /freefort command and add 250 million to all fortifications at no cost. It requires you have a pure Free Fortify relic in your inventory, which has a small and very random chance of breaking with every use. Killing a King is a matter of luck.

The Goblin King which drops freeforts is a Beast, not the normal Goblin King you can create using the New Fight menu. Gob King beasts are random Sur spawns.
