SR |
Sinner's Rebirth damage spell | item |

SS | SacrificialSkillation relic, Increases skill chance by 10% and increases delay by 10% (only dropped off beasts or gotten from the Hell Hermit for 100 runes, pm the Hermit) | relics |

Stallion's Pub | A special kingdom at 150,sur,145 gives 5x normalsur gold, has harder critters, special drops, a high drop rate, and is part of some quests Pub critters will annul so player relics that can be annulled are useless. | gameplay |

stats | Short for player statistics, the inherent values for a character that factor into its ability to fight There are 7 stats you can build as your character grows,Str (strength),Dex (dexterity),Agi (agility),Dur (durability),Ntl (intelligence),Cnc (concentration) andCnt (contravention) Different stats are used by different player classes. | gameplay |

"The stench of rotting flesh fills the air" | Yellow system message when a Goblin Shaman beast appears near the indicated location | Beast |

storm | Essence of Storm quest item, drops off Lightning LordAlchemar beasts | quest |

stone | Essence Element of Stone is a quest item obtained using 999 runes and the Quake Keeper It vastly increases the defense of all your Kingdoms for the remainder of the game hour The Stone is the defensive answer to aTecton. | quest |

Stumbles onto forbidden rights | The system message (yellow text) �[Playername] stumbles onto forbidden rights� is displayed when someone randomly finds a Scroll of Forbidden Rights, quest item | quest |

Surface(Sur) | One of 6 planes in the game, Surface is the starting location for all characters, and requires nothing to play on It is centered between The Forge, Hell and Dungeon below and Sky and Heaven above. |
|

Surface Hermit | any of 4 NPC's on Sur who are involved in some quests. You can pm using �/m Surface Hermit: your message here� | NPC |

Super relics | Among the best relics in the game, there are 3 Super Relics: Melee's Rage (MR), Caster's Revenge (CR) and Vampire's Scorn (VS). | relics |

Swimsuit picture | A contest designed to have players advertise the game and bring in new people For the first 50 votes people give your picture each month you get 50 ash Starting with 50 total votes you only get 1 ash per vote for the rest of the month. The vote totals are set back to 0 after the monthly acumen. You link to a picture on the Web using the Actions menu, Options, Swimsuit Picture. People can go to your link (http://rwk#.RaceWarKingdoms.com/cgi-bin/rwk.cgi?swimsuitPlayername, changePlayername and # = 1 or 2 depending on the server you play) and vote whether your picture is �Sexy� which gets you some ash.

The Announcements on 7/21/2007 included a vague notice that �The amount of ash you get from swimsuit votes after obtaining a certain amount has been reduced.� I don�t know what the �certain amount� is or how much it�s been reduced Swimsuit voting has been abused by players several times and eGlitchless keeps making it less valuable as a result. | gameplay |

taming a beast | Sometimes a beast doesn't die when attacked, it flees the kingdom and becomes a guard in a different location The player who makes it flee gets the same benefits as killing it, and beast guards don't give any special drops when they are subsequently killed. | gameplay |

TC | Theurgal Conquest relic, upgraded TE relic. Raises Theurgist damage by a factor of 6.25 + Voidance (Not including Confusion) | relics |

TE | Theurgal Equalizer relic, Raises Theurgist damage by a factor of 2.5 | relics |

tecton | Essence Element ofTecton, quest item to help wage war, talk to the Quake Keeper | quest |

Tectonic, or Earthen | Fourth best quality items in the game, same as earthen. Beast drop or by upgrading Demonic items with ash and the Heaven Citizen | items |

Templar | Templar is a race upgrade you can make after you create a character It costs either $50 as a purchase from Glitchless, or 50,000 ash in-game using the Ash Keeper. | races |

Temple | A special location at 40,Sur,225 that is part of some quests It differs from many special locations in that it can be owned by players. | Gameplay, quest |

Thief |
Goblin Thief, easy Sur beast that any player can kill if he or she can reach it in time. | Beast, gameplay |

titus |
The Calling of Titus relic | relics |

ToTD |
Touch of the Damned relic, Lowers enemy health by 40% OR by 40% of your durability, whichever is lower. | relics |

ToTF
Tears of the Fallen | A relic, 50% chance of being saved from death for 1st TotF equipped, 10% for each one equipped thereafter | relics |

Transfer (xfer) | Transferring points from the stats you don�t use into the stat or stats useful for your class Players can begin to transfer when they reach the hard level cap. The process works exactly like leveling where you kill critters until you get to choose a stat to increase The difference is that you do not get any additional total stat points, you simply move some into one stat from all the others each time you train. Players without an sos will be able to start transferring at level 1,000 and will still need the full number of soft cap kills to train. Players with an SoS can transfer some points after each kill so the process is much faster. | gameplay |

treasure | either an EEoT (worth 50bil) or an EEoGT (Worth 250bil), or an EEoST (worth 999 billion gold) often used for in-game trade payments | item |

Treasury (Trs) | The kingdom treasury, shown on your screen as Trs, is where you can store gold and have it gain interest every hour. The amount of interest you gain will vary with your kingmanship skill and whether you have Collectia as a girlfriend. It ranges from a low of 0.449%/hr for players with 0kingmanship skill and no Transcend to a high of 0.803%/hr for those with 125 kingmanship and Collectia. | gameplay |

Trial | Trial Access, a $15.00 PayPal purchase from Glitchless that gives you access to all planes and Templar status for 10 days This is a cheap way to complete several quests and see more of the game than you get to play on the surface. The quest items you get during the access period, like a Wand of Transportation and Backpack, remain with your character when the access expires. If you don�t already have permanent Templar status, trial access will not let you get a Templar�s Wand so you can not summon beasts. | gameplay |

trivial |
In crafting, an item becomes trivial when your skills are high enough for crafting that item to never fail In addition, making trivial items will never get you any further skill increases To increase crafting skill, you must make items which are not trivial and thus still have a chance to fail. | gameplay |

TSM | Tradeskill Manifesto, a quest item that raises your crafting caps to 999 | quest |

twink | Used as both a noun and verb Twinking is when you buy or give items to a character that are much better than what that character can earn itself by playing the game Sometimes the items are transferred from a higher level account owned by the same person, other times they may are bought from other people using in-game or real money Atwink would be a character that has beentwinked. | gameplay |

UT | Unborn Torment, a special beast that can appear randomly on any plane except Sur, you need an SoA or 12 annuls to fight them. | Beast, quest |

VV | A Vogar's Vice relic. It does 3 milunannullable damage to normal enemies | relics |

vacation | Yellow text message "Player x has decided to take a 2 day vacation" means someone has been banned from the game for 2 days for breaking the rules No longer part of the game. | gamepla |

VAS | Vampire's Amaranthine Scorn. A relic, upgraded from a VS relic | relics |

VL | Vampiric Leech relic, Steals 50% enemy health OR 50% of your durability, whichever is lower. | relics |

Vogar | An NPC who can makePutrifaction relics from your pure Vogar's Vice and Encephelation Decay relics and 5 runes You can pm using /mVogar: your message here | quest |

Void | Voidance relic, Each gives a 10% chance to void all enemy enchantments + Confusion | relics |

VS | Vampire's Scorn relic, 20% durability boost + 10 Death Spikes + **Double Defense + Confusion + Blood Pinch | relics |

Waking Nightmare | The system message " A waking nightmare fills your vision " shows when a Nightmare Fly beast has appeared on the Surface | Beast |

A wondrous crack is heard in the distance | System message (yellow text) message that appears when a player with an ashen rock makes a purchase of $50 or more (or uses 50,000 or 100,000 ash for an Ash Keeper upgrade) and receives either some ash or treasure elements as a bonus. | Gameplay |

pages.title: Shrine Keeper Dialog To Convert One Type If D*Dv Into Another
pages.text:
Shrine Keeper Dialog
To Convert One Type Of D*Dv Relic Into Another





The sequence is shows from
top to bottom, on your chat screen in game it will be reversed with the last
message at the top, first at the bottom. �
Player response is in blue, Keeper�s text is in red.




Start by sending message - /m Shrine Keeper: hello





Message sent to Shrine+Keeper:
hello


Shrine Keeper: I've given away so much ash in trade for
D*D's... and quite frankly I want my [ash] back.





Message sent to Shrine+Keeper: ash


Shrine Keeper: Yeah I want my ash back, but I don't
feel like giving back the [relics].





Message sent to Shrine+Keeper:
relics


Shrine Keeper: Relics, right. I want to keep all my
D*D's and D*Dev's but I'd also like some more ash... so here's my [deal].





Message sent to Shrine+Keeper: deal


Shrine Keeper: For 2,000 ash I'll swap a D*D or a D*Dev
in the following manner, M to L to P to R to S to A to W to O to M.
[Understand]?





Message sent to Shrine+Keeper:
understand


Shrine Keeper: If you gave me a DsDev I'd take 2k ash
and give you a DaDev. If you gave me the DaDev back I'd take another 2k ash and
give you a DwDev. Make [sense]?





Message sent to Shrine+Keeper:
sense


Shrine Keeper: Well then you probably don't have any
D*D's or D*Dev's cause you're dumb!








Back to home

pages.title: DotB vs BP
pages.text:
## DotB vs. BP Relic Combinations for Vampires


Copyright 2005 - 2026 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved

Use of this content without permission of jimcav.com is a violation of copyright. Reproduction of the material on this site, in whole or in part, for use on another public location without the express written permission of the author is forbidden. Hyperlinks to any page on this site are allowed without restriction.


NOTE: On December 27,2014 Blood Pinch was changed from an un-annullable relic to one with a 50% chance of being annulled. It works as described below against normal enemies but those with annuls, such as in the Keeps, will block your BP from firing in almost every fight so the damage you see in those locations, and against some beasts, will be much lower than reported here.


Two popular damage relics in RWK are Blood Pinch (BP) and Denial of the Believer (DotB). � Both of these relics cause damage based on durability but they operate in different ways. � BP is simple, each one does damage equal to 20% of your modified durability (dur) stat. � Type /st to see your modded dur.



DotB is more complicated than BP because it does damage based on 90% of the lower of your modded dur OR your enemy�s dur. � This means that the damage it does when you attack something with more dur than you is 90% of your modified dur, but when you attack something with less dur than you the damage is based on 90% of your target�s durability. � You can find the durability of creatures in the game using the Kingdom menu, Guard to see how much that critter would cost if you bought it as a guard. � It turns out that the price is the same as every one of its stats, and also the base value of all its drops. � So, you click on Kingdom, scroll to the Guard menu choice, and see what a Jailer�s Kinsman costs on the surface, which is 40 million gold. � That means it has 40 mil dur, 40 mil dex, 40 mil agi, etc. �


As each of your damage relics fire (they don�t all fire at the same time) your target will have less dur and so the amount of damage can change when using DotB. � It is a change you should keep in mind when choosing which relic to use and the order in which you equip them. � It also explains why DotB is not a �kill� relic. � When your enemy is down to 1 hitpoint, DotB will only do 0.9 HP of damage to it, so it doesn�t die. � A relic like BP will kill because the damage it delivers doesn�t change with dropping enemy health.


In general terms this means you want to use DotB when your enemy has a lot more dur than you since it will do 90% of your dur in damage, compared to 20% using BP. � When your enemy has a lot less dur than you, whether at the start of the attack or because your previously firing relics have weakened it, you will do better using BP since it does a consistent 20% of your modified dur in damage. � In some situations a combination of DotB firing first while your target has a lot of health, followed by BP firing after it becomes weakened, will do the most damage. � In general you always want all your DotB relics to fire before any BP or fixed damage relics (like Vogar�s Vice, Death Spike) come into action. � Relics fire in order beginning with your left hand slot and ending with your number 6 pure relic slot (left hand item, helmet, right hand item, gauntlets, mantle, sleeves, spell 1, leggings, boots, spell 2, relic slot 1, relic slot 2, �relic slot 6. � All your approbations and blessings fire before any equipped relics and they fire in the same order.)


The first 3 tables below show the best combination of Denial of the Believer (DotB) and Blood Pinch (BP) relics to use if you are a pure vampire and attacking with only those two damage relics. � I used a few different critter durability values and various player durs for examples.



As you develop more durability, the best choice of relic changes from those based on minimum dur, like DotB, to those based on only your dur, like BP. � If you want to maximize the damage you do, you should combine both relics in ways that lead to killing your enemy soonest. � Low dur vamps will be using 15 DotB and 1 BP (BP is the kill relic) for best results against critters with more dur than they have, while very high dur vamps will choose all BP. � The tables shows how to transition between the two relics as your stats change. � The first tables are against creatures in normal kingdoms, not in places like the Puddle, Pub, Keeps, etc.


Creatures in those locations have defensive skills which reduce all damage. � Against relics, these creatures take 25x less damage than those in normal kingdoms. � This often means you end up fighting critters with much less dur than you have and using all BP works better than equipping any DotB. � If you want to get your Vamp Horns in the Puddle and Pub you will find that using BP works best.


The first table shows the best combination of DotB and BP to use against a normal Yoro with increasing modified player dur (type /st to see your modded dur stat). � At low dur, you won�t kill a Yoro in a single attack so the 4 th column shows how much dur it will have left when your attack is done. � At 75 mil modified dur you can kill Yoros in one attack, so the last column shows when it dies, which relic firing delivers the kill. � Sooner is better, although dead is dead so I�m not sure how significant which relic makes the kill really is. � When you fight critters with lower dur, you will want to use more BP than against Yoros because they will get below your own dur faster. � The next two tables so similar calculations for fighting creatures with less dur than Yoros.

Sur)

I did some experiments comparing the calculated damage against actual damage and the results were surprisingly good. � I also did some experiments in the Pub, Keeps, and Palace and found that my relic damage was 25x lower than in normal kingdoms against all the critters I attacked. � The damage in the Insane Dungeon was 25 million times less! � It was so little I�m not sure how accurate the values are, but suffice it to say that the ID critters have a huge resistance to relic damage compared to normal ones. � The high relic damage resistance of these critters also meant that using all BP relics gave the best results since the ones I could kill in a single attack had much less durability than my character. Unfortunately, the 2014 change making BP annullable invalidates my findings in the special kingdoms. I'm leaving the results here in case Glitchless ever makes BP annullable again. The relative results for relic damage are still useful since they show that enemies in these locations are innately more resistant to relic damage than those in normal locations and that effect will be seen when using unannullable relics.

So, now you plan to equip 16 BP relics and go to the Pub or Puddle
and kill things, how much modified dur do you need to kill each creature there
in a single attack? � I tried different
combinations of relics and changed the player dur value until I found the best
combination and the lowest player dur needed to get a one-shot kill. � Turns out you should be able to kill these
creatures (using 16 BP relics) if your modified dur is 7.8125x the enemy
durability. � This is what you would
expect based on using 16 BP relics, damage being reduced 25x � and BP doing damage that�s 20% of your own
durability. � Your required dur � is 25/(16 x 0.2) = 7.8125.



So if you want to kill a Jailer�s Kinsman in the Pub in a
single attack, it has a durability of 40 million and you should be able to kill
it with a modified dur of 313 million.



7.8125 x (40,000,000) �
= � 312,500,000

pages.title: Rune Corruption
pages.text:
Rune Corruption (Over-Runing)


Copyright 2007 by jimcav.com,
all rights reserved





On 29 May 2007 Glitchless made some significant changes to RWK,
including over runing, which is a corruption factor that reduces kingdom
treasury interest for any locations having 5 or more runes. � The announcement states, among
other things, �Hoarding more than 5 runes in a kingdom now contributes greatly
to corruption meaning you will get less interest in that kingdom. A kingdom
with 6 runes will get 80% normal interest and a kingdom with 50 runes will get
10%, etc�.





Players now have to choose between security, more runes make
a kingdom stronger, and income. � There
is no exclusion for small numbers of kingdoms owned, so it affects every player
who owns land in the game.





I did some testing and got input from other players to
compile a table of how much corruption you get with various numbers of
runes. � It looks like the effect follows
two different formulas, much like corruption based on owning more than 2,500
kingdoms, with the break around 20 runes. �
Interest loss is very steep, so the price for �hoarding� more than 5
runes kicks in right at 6. � The results
are shown below.

pages.title: Heaven Citizen Upgrade Quest
pages.text:
Sash of the Blood Rogue Quest


Copyright
2010 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved





Go to 129,Sur,51 (Port of Carella) and chat up the Citizen
there. � He will offer you a chance to
get a Sash of the Blood Rogue . � It�s a quest item that allows you to get
more use from sleeved cards.





The Citizen dialog for this quest is shown below reading
from top to bottom.




Message sent to A Citizen: hello


A Citizen Heard the mysterious
whisper eh? Bet you'd like to know what that's [about].





Message sent to A Citizen: about


A Citizen Well I'll tell you...
there was once a Rogue who lived here, he was a gambling man. Cards, he loved
[cards].





Message sent to A Citizen: cards


A Citizen Yeah, the red ones
especially.. He seemed to always have a lot of them... up his sleeve or so they
[said].





Message sent to A Citizen: said


A Citizen Oh no, you wouldn't say it
to him though, once someone accused him of cheating and he slit the man's
[throat] right then and there.





Message sent to A Citizen: throat


A Citizen Yeah he bled like you
wouldn't believe, I know too cause I was there, close enough to get [blood] on
me myself!





Message sent to A Citizen: blood


A Citizen Funny you should ask, I
still do have the bloody sash from that day, never washed it cause I think it's
[lucky].





Message sent to A Citizen: lucky


A Citizen Think you're luckier than
me? Well give me an EEoGT and I'll roll my 20 sided die, if it rolls a 10 you
can have my sash.

pages.title: Ash Table
pages.text:
The
Ash Keeper


Copyright
2005 - 2006 by jimcav.com, all rights reserved





The Ash Keeper (AK) is a Non Player
Character (NPC) located at 150,Sur,150 (nearest port is Andal). � He was added on 25 Dec 2004 to give players
an option for getting upgrades (like access off the surface, templar, and
several others) without having to pay in dollars, as was the case before the AK
was added. � The catch is that the AK
requires large amounts of ash to deliver anything new, at least 20k
(that�s 20,000 ash) for an Ocean Bubble so you can get Aqua Lungs. � However, with enough time and some luck at
poker it is now possible to leave the surface without needing to pay a
dime. � IF you have the patience to get
both sky and dungeon access, that means a total of 100,000 ash, you can reach
Heaven Keep (66,Hev,66) where the drop rate is
very high. � That would help you collect
enough ash for many other upgrades.





If you visit 150,Sur,150 and give
the Ash Keeper the following amounts of gold for each upgrade he will take the
gold and the appropriate amount of ash, and give you the item in return. � His entire dialog is here .

pages.title: Relic Crafting Skills
pages.text:
Relic Crafting Skills





Values up to Denial of the Believer are taken off Cyril's site , the ones requiring
skills above that are additions based on my experience and input from other
players.


The data below show the skill levels you need to attempt to
make each relic and when they become trivial. If you attempt to make a relic with the minimum skill at which it is available you can, and most likely often will, fail in the attempt and you will
just lose your gold. When the relic is trivial it will never fail and you will succeed every time, but you also will never gain any additional crafting skills making that item. To grow in skill you must risk failure.



have a special cost structure. If you do not have any of those relics in pure form, that is relics unattached to items, the cost will be 5k or 10k gold as shown. If you already have a pure relic and attempt to craft another of that same relic the cost will increase by a factor of 10 for each additional
relic you try to make. For example, you have no pure strong arm relics (strong arm attached to items like weapons, armor, spells don�t count here) and try to craft one. It will cost you 5,000 gold.�If you succeed and get the relic, then try to craft another strong arm, that second attempt will cost you 50,000 gold. Both relics do exactly the same thing, the increase in cost is just how Glitchless makes crafting relics more challenging. If your second attempt succeeds, the third one will cost you 500,000 gold and the cost keeps increasing up to 500 million gold for the 6th relic. The same cost increase occurs for the 10k relics up to a cost of 1 billion gold for crafting the 6th pure one.


If you want to avoid this steep cost increase you need to
frequently sell your relics back to the store before you craft more of the same
one. The price for the 500 mil and 1 bil relics does not change, they start out expensive enough. Other items in the game, armor, weps, spells, bows and arrows, do not increase in cost as you craft more of the same thing.

Crafting treasure elements (EEoT and EEoGT) requires the component
skills of Reliccrafting, Metalworking and Runeworking. � The other relics use the skills Reliccrafting,
Gemworking , and Runeworking. � If
you plan to make treasure elements, you will need to craft weapons or armor
first to train your skill in Metalworking. �
Leather of Delegation is an oddball relic in that is uses Leatherworking 
and Metalworking , so to make those you�ll need to first make armor or
bows and weapons to gain skill in Leatherworking and Metalworking. � Thanks to Xxdeathxx on S2 for pointing out
that people working just on relics will run into problems with LoD and
Treasures if they don�t make other types of items first, and to Macca123 (S2)
for noticing that Metalworking is a skill required for LoD.
